title: fln-env: implement executable extension checkpoint and restore semantics
description:
Missing parent feature: CheckpointSemantics is stored and now enters logical identity, but no checkpoint/restore API consults it. The registry contract is therefore descriptive only, so branch snapshots cannot actually honor JournalSuffix versus FullJournal. Scope: define typed versioned checkpoint values and restore/apply operations. JournalSuffix checkpoints must bind the exact base extension identity, descriptor, journal position, and content/root digest and carry only the deterministic suffix; FullJournal checkpoints must carry the complete ordered raw journal and require no ambient base. Applying a suffix to the wrong name, contract, base length, base digest, or divergent history must fail atomically with a typed diagnostic; replayed checkpoints must preserve payload bytes/order, provenance, logical roots, and merge behavior. Define empty/no-change checkpoints, nested checkpoints, repeated restore, stale checkpoints, cross-branch use, resource limits, and forward schema-version rejection. Use the persistent journal representation from fln-amv.5 rather than flattening or copying the full history for suffix mode. Verification requirements: model/property tests generate fork/checkpoint/restore chains for both modes and compare exact states/roots; negative tables cover every binding mismatch, truncation/version/resource boundary, and recovery; instrumentation proves suffix size/work is proportional to the suffix and FullJournal is explicitly linear. Extend the shared real-path environment E2E lane with detailed schema-versioned logs containing mode, base/checkpoint/restored ids and roots, entry counts, expected/actual outcome, resources, and final state. Run fmt, all fln-env tests, clippy -D warnings, UBS, and parent gates. No advisory enum with ignored behavior and no silent fallback from suffix to full capture.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every scope, behavior, verification requirement, dependency, and prohibited shortcut above is mandatory. Closure requires the named deterministic unit/property/model coverage, the shared real-path E2E success plus negative/recovery scenario with detailed schema-versioned logs, all stated performance or evidence proofs, and green fmt/test/clippy/UBS and parent gates. No unclassified skip, mock-only evidence, partial implementation, or deferred listed feature satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Versioned JournalSuffix and FullJournal checkpoints have executable capture/apply/restore semantics. Suffix checkpoints bind exact name, descriptor, base identity/length/digest/root and carry only deterministic suffix data through the persistent journal; full checkpoints are self-contained and reject ambient bases. Payload order/bytes, provenance, roots, repeat restore, nesting, branch behavior, and every merge mode are preserved. 2. Wrong name, contract, base length/digest/history, schema version, truncation, stale/cross-branch use, resource excess, and inappropriate ambient base each fail atomically with a typed diagnostic; empty/no-change and exact-boundary cases succeed, and there is no silent suffix-to-full fallback. 3. Named unit/property/model tests generate fork/checkpoint/restore chains for both modes, compare exact state/root against an independent model, cover every positive merge/provenance combination and complete negative/resource tables, and prove suffix work/size is proportional to the suffix while FullJournal work is explicitly linear in its carried history. 4. The real shared no-mock environment E2E covers suffix, full, wrong-base or equivalent planted failure, and pristine recovery. It emits schema-versioned line-oriented records with mode, base/checkpoint/restored IDs and roots, entry counts, resources/operation facts, expected/actual outcome and diagnostic class, artifact paths, cleanup, and final state. 5. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, warnings-denied Clippy, E2E shell syntax/ShellCheck, UBS under the documented user-required scanner exclusion, and parent gates are green.
close_reason: Executable versioned checkpoint/restore semantics and all named positive, negative, model, resource, E2E, logging, and gate obligations are verified.

title: fln-env: add the pinned attribute-state substrate without executing upstream code
description:
W2 aggregation for the complete pin-compatible attribute-state substrate without executing upstream implementation code. This epic preserves every original feature while splitting extraction, native storage/query behavior, combined module/provenance lifecycle, and real differential fixtures into independently auditable contracts.

The four mandatory children are: fln-attribute-state-census-h14, the mechanically generated pin census and shared row-ID namespace; fln-attribute-native-state-m7l, immutable definitions/assignments and typed data queries; franken_lean-attribute-combined-state-x74, atomic integration with the single module/provenance/checkpoint/merge-plan truth; and fln-attribute-real-fixtures-epj, real decoded Reference-produced fixture validation through the same module adapter as fln-amv.9.4. The epic depends only on the terminal fixture child; explicit child dependency edges preserve the sequence without parent-child cycles.

Definitions and assignments preserve registration identity, target, payload, priority and tie order, scope, persistence, import replay, removal/replacement, duplicate/conflict behavior, epoch semantics, and unknown/custom data byte-exactly. Root participation is generated per field. Semantic attribute state enters logical_root exactly where the canonical contract declares it; module ownership/evidence enters ModuleProvenanceRootV1; operational facts enter neither. Attribute state reuses extension/module/provenance identities and never creates a disconnected registry or import truth.

For one fixed ordered input closure, equivalent construction-map insertion permutations and productive scheduler/thread partitions 1/8/32 yield byte-identical state, diagnostics, roots, and evidence. Reordering assignment or import rows that the pin treats as observable is a distinct input and must reproduce the corresponding distinct Reference result. Only census-declared commutative or independent reorderings are metamorphic invariants.

Rows whose observable behavior invokes Lean code are never mislabeled data-only. W2 provides lossless state, typed data queries, and generated dispatch metadata. Such a query returns typed RequiresHandler with its census row and honest provisional grade until W6 franken_lean-epx discharges the same row through the native/library-source partition. No feature is dropped or mocked, and fln-env does not violate layering by embedding elaborator/VM handlers.

## Acceptance Criteria

The four explicit child beads—generated census, native state/data queries, combined module/provenance/checkpoint/merge state, and real decoded fixtures—must all close without narrowed scope. Their terminal evidence must cover every census row and observable order, opaque and RequiresHandler boundaries, roots/authority, collision/resource/cancellation laws, models/mutants, productive 1-8-32 schedules, publish-once recovery, and strict no-mock bundles. The structured acceptance field remains fully mandatory.
design: Closure topology: census extraction begins after W1 generated contracts and runs independently of the module-provenance implementation. Native data state joins the census with the already-closed pinned effective-import contract. Combined-state integration then joins native attributes, fln-amv.9.3 provenance, and collision-safe checkpoint identity. Real fixture validation finally joins that state with fln-amv.9.4 so there is one decoder-to-transaction and ownership path. This epic closes only on that terminal child; fln-amv.11 remains the sole global Environment merge join. A related edge to franken_lean-epx records the row-ID/RequiresHandler execution handoff without imposing an invalid W2-to-W6 build dependency.

Combined state publishes attributes with Environment and ModuleProvenanceRootV1 atomically, shares unchanged structures, grades opaque/partial state honestly, and supplies the revalidated child plan consumed by fln-amv.11. Named unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation suites cover every census row and observable query, fixed-order schedule determinism, explicitly commutative reorders, duplicate/conflict/removal, priority/scope/import order, opaque data, exact resource/cancellation boundaries, snapshots/checkpoints/restore/merge/invalidation, root transitions, nonpublication, and recovery. title: fln-env: implement the environment module-import graph and provenance contract
description:
W2 integration epic for the complete native immutable module-import graph promised by plan section 7.1. Module identity is bound to exact epoch and artifact-resolution evidence. Every direct Import row preserves module, importAll, isExported, isMeta, source order, and duplicates. Effective closure reproduces the pinned visibility/data/IR fixed point. Exact module contributions bind actual declarations, extra generated declarations, and ordered extension entries without executing upstream code.

Four mandatory stages remain: the closed lossless persistent module-record/DAG substrate; the closed pinned effective-import fixed point and queries; the fln-amv.9.3 provenance epic, decomposed into canonical identity, atomic payload application, bounded invalidation, and lifecycle/merge-plan children; and fln-amv.9.4 real decoded-fixture integration plus the single shared no-mock E2E matrix. fln-amv.9.3 closes on exhaustive local children and a stable handoff. fln-amv.9.4 consumes that result. This outer epic closes only after fln-amv.9.4, eliminating any downstream-evidence cycle.

Root law is exact. Module topology, artifact evidence, ownership, completeness, and provenance metadata never change Environment logical_root. Actual declaration, extension, or attribute semantic publication changes logical_root only through the canonical Environment root builder. The single aggregate ModuleProvenanceRootV1 covers module topology and contribution identity, optionally composed from named subdigests, and is neither operational metadata nor silently folded into logical_root. Any query, cache entry, demand key, or receipt whose result reads topology/provenance binds ModuleProvenanceRootV1 plus the complete request/profile; pure logical queries that do not read it need not. Omitting the root/profile is a stale-cache defect, not a future option.

No name-only graph, generic graph substitute, inferred import flags, flattened opaque payload, parallel ownership truth, ambiguous graph/provenance roots, hidden logical-root change, partial child closure, or deferred listed behavior satisfies this epic.

## Success Criteria

The closed module DAG and effective-import engine, all four local fln-amv.9.3 children, and fln-amv.9.4 real decoded integration must close without narrowed scope. One aggregate ModuleProvenanceRootV1, exact ordered inputs and actual payload binding, honest completeness/transparency/authority, atomic publication, witnessed invalidation, lifecycle plans, collision/resource/cancellation laws, mutation proof, productive 1-8-32 determinism, and strict no-mock evidence are mandatory.

title: fln-env: build the lossless persistent module-record DAG core
description:
Build the pin-anchored immutable module-record and ordered DAG substrate for fln-amv.9. The input contract is Lean v4.32.0 Import(module, importAll, isExported, isMeta) and ModuleData(isModule, imports, contributions). This bead depends on fln-20n.1 so the environment types must represent every decoded field without invention or normalization.

Define typed ModuleId/ModuleRecord/DirectImport values. Module identity is not stored inside ModuleData, so registration must bind the caller-supplied Name to explicit artifact-resolution evidence (epoch, artifact content identity, producer/source grade) rather than deriving it from a path string or assuming the first import. Preserve each direct-import array exactly, including duplicate rows and all flags. Provide an immutable PMap-backed graph whose clone is O(1), whose module insertion path-copies only bounded structure, and whose direct_imports query returns Reference order. Define exact idempotent re-registration versus conflicting module-record behavior, reflexive-edge and cycle refusal, duplicate-edge classification without deduplication, missing-node/incomplete-closure state, maximum modules/edges/name depth/payload bytes, and atomic failure.

Register deterministic policies separately: ReferenceDirectOrder for observable arrays; ReferenceDiscoveryOrder for import replay; CanonicalNameOrder for semantically free diagnostics/sets. Do not let PMap hash/bucket order become user-visible. The graph must expose typed completeness, conflict dimensions, and deterministic operation/resource facts suitable for later effective-closure and provenance layers.

Crate direction is load-bearing: fln-olean already depends on fln-env, so fln-env must not depend back on fln-olean merely to prove the seam. The concrete ModuleDataView to ModuleRecord adapter and shared real-fixture/no-mock proof belong to fln-amv.9.4. This bead instead proves the environment-side schema field-for-field and leaves no representation in which that adapter could silently discard an import flag, row, identity, or artifact-evidence field.

Verification: exhaustive record-field tables; random DAG model tests against a Vec/BTreeMap oracle; duplicate-row, missing-node, conflicting-record, self-edge, multi-node cycle, malformed identity, and every resource boundary; insertion-order and thread-partition metamorphic checks; pointer/allocation instrumentation for O(1) snapshots and bounded path copying; large sparse/dense DAG operation-count evidence rather than timing alone. Add named mutations that drop and reorder direct rows and require both to be killed. The shared real-fixture/no-mock evidence remains mandatory in fln-amv.9.4 for epic closure. Run scoped format, all fln-env tests, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and contract checks.

No adjacency set that loses duplicates/order, name-only Import, path-derived identity, reverse fln-env to fln-olean dependency, unbounded traversal, partial insertion, silent cycle repair, or generic graph abstraction that hides the Reference contract.

## Acceptance Criteria

The exact persistent module-record/DAG contract above is implemented with complete model, negative, resource, determinism, mutation, operation-count, and sharing evidence. The fln-20n.1 schema is represented field-for-field on the environment side; fln-amv.9.4 retains the concrete decoder adapter plus shared real-fixture/E2E obligation. All scoped gates pass. No partial or mock-only implementation satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ModuleId, DirectImport, ModuleRecord, and registration evidence represent Lean v4.32.0 ModuleData and every Import field exactly, preserving structural Name identity, row order, duplicates, isModule, epoch, content identity, producer, and source grade without path-derived or inferred values. 2. The immutable PMap-backed graph provides O(1) snapshot clone, bounded path-copy insertion, exact idempotence, field-specific conflict diagnostics, typed self-edge/cycle/missing-node/duplicate-row/resource outcomes, atomic failure, and separately registered ReferenceDirectOrder, ReferenceDiscoveryOrder, and CanonicalNameOrder policies; map bucket order is never user-visible. 3. Named unit/property/model/metamorphic tests cover all record fields and flags, duplicate classifications, malformed identity, conflicts, cycles, incomplete closure, every exact/cumulative resource boundary, insertion permutations, thread partitions, snapshot sharing, sparse/dense operation counts, and atomic recovery. Named drop-row and reorder-row mutations are killed with schema-versioned structured evidence. 4. The environment-side schema is field-for-field compatible with fln-20n.1 without adding a forbidden fln-env to fln-olean dependency. The concrete decoder adapter and no-mock real-fixture positive/failure/recovery E2E with detailed schema-versioned logs remain mandatory in fln-amv.9.4 and must block epic closure; this bead does not weaken or duplicate that integration boundary. 5. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, warnings-denied Clippy, UBS under the documented user-required scanner exclusion, and applicable dependency/contract gates are green.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Implemented the environment-side lossless module-record DAG contract in crates/fln-env/src/modules.rs and exposed it from fln-env. The core preserves every direct Import row, all eight flag combinations, Reference row order, duplicate rows, isModule, exact Name identity, epoch/content/producer/grade evidence, and immutable PMap snapshots. Registration is atomic and distinguishes exact idempotence from field-specific conflicts; anonymous/overflowing identities, malformed epochs, self edges, late cycles, and module/row/name-depth/payload limits are typed refusals. Missing targets remain explicit rather than being guessed or repaired. Cycle detection is one deterministic canonical multi-source BFS with operation-count facts.

title: fln-env: reproduce the pinned effective-import fixed point and queries
description:
Implement the pinned v4.32.0 importModulesCore fixed point and query semantics over fln-amv.9.1, derived from vendor/lean4-src/src/Lean/Environment.lean:2057-2187. This is not ordinary reachability: the Reference propagates data visibility and IR requirements separately, revisits a module when later paths upgrade effective state, and records first discovery in recursive postorder.

Model the exposure lattice explicitly as none, private, privateAll, public, and all. privateAll and public are incomparable; their join is all. Preserve the exact two-flag Reference representation and equations for each row i: needsData := parent.needsData && (i.isExported || parent.importAll); importAll := globalLevel == private || (parent.importAll && i.importAll); isExported := parent.isExported && i.isExported; needsIRTrans := parent.needsIRTrans || (needsData && i.isMeta); needsIR := needsIRTrans || importAll || globalLevel > exported. Initial root context is importAll=true, needsData=true, needsIRTrans=false, and isExported defaults to (globalLevel < private); the explicit importModulesCore isExported override is supported and is part of request identity.

Represent OLean global levels exported/server/private and IRPhases runtime/comptime/all exactly. A fresh candidate has phases all when importAll, comptime when needsIRTrans, otherwise runtime. On an existing module, importAll/isExported/needsData/needsIRTrans join by OR and unequal IRPhases join to all. Server mode can request an IR artifact while leaving IRPhases runtime; keep IR request, transitive IR need, and phase distinct. Actual optional IR-file availability/loading belongs to the artifact integration in fln-amv.9.4 and must not be invented in fln-env. EffectiveImport.isMeta remains the first-discovery row value because the Reference does not join it during upgrades. The update condition is exactly the five effective fields above; a genuine upgrade replays the entire stored direct-import array.

Preserve direct row order and recursive postorder discovery: on first discovery, traverse the module direct rows before appending the module; on an upgrade, replay its closure without appending it again. Repeated and duplicate imports remain distinct work inputs and can join state. Missing records become row-indexed typed Incomplete findings with an honest partial closure and canonical diagnostic order. Cancellation and work/witness/resource exhaustion are typed Inconclusive outcomes under FL-INV-07; impossible engine invariants are typed InternalFault outcomes, never user rejection and never panic. Anonymous or overflowing root module identities are definitive typed invalid requests before traversal. A direct non-module import from exported/server module mode is the Reference-defined definitive invalid-request outcome and must remain distinct from Incomplete/Inconclusive. Cyclic graphs are refused by fln-amv.9.1 and must be tested at that boundary rather than reintroduced here.

APIs must support direct imports, exact effective state per module, Reference discovery sequence, canonical transitive set, exact reverse import rows plus canonical reverse importers, and witnesses explaining each joined visibility/IR component. Witness steps identify root-or-module source, direct-row index, target, and triggering flags so duplicates remain explainable. Every outcome carries status, partial-or-complete data, completeness findings, and deterministic operation/resource facts. Do not add a cache until its key includes durable graph identity plus the entire root request/profile; pointer-only identities with ABA risk and request-incomplete keys are prohibited. fln-amv.9.3 owns the durable graph-root domain.

Verification: implement an independent worklist/reference model from the pinned equations and compare every flag combination, every exposure and IR join, first-discovery isMeta retention, server IR-load/runtime-phase distinction, and update condition. Generated random DAGs compare exact effective maps, Reference postorder, row-indexed witnesses, reverse queries, and incomplete findings. Cover duplicate/diamond/upgrade-late/missing/non-module/resource/cancellation cases; insertion permutations and thread partitions prove schedule independence while direct-row permutations remain observable when the Reference makes them observable. Named mutations invert each propagation equation, append before recursion, join isMeta, suppress an upgrade replay, or conflate server IR load with phases, and each must be killed. Operation counts prove bounded work in modules plus rows plus monotone state upgrades and witness budget. Cross-check small pinned source/real artifact fixtures only at integration layer fln-amv.9.4. Run scoped format, all fln-env tests, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and parent checks.

No plain DFS that ignores flags, early visited set that suppresses upgrades, traversal-order diagnostics, boolean accepted/rejected collapse for partial data, invented isMeta join, server-phase conflation, pointer-only stale cache key, cycle acceptance, or hidden decoder dependency.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every pinned equation, global-level/IR distinction, postorder/first-edge behavior, deterministic query, row-indexed witness, typed complete/incomplete/inconclusive/invalid outcome, independent-model comparison, metamorphic schedule proof, named mutation, and operation bound above is implemented and green. fln-amv.9.4 retains the real-fixture/no-mock E2E obligation. No simplified reachability substitute or deferred rule satisfies this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Effective import computation matches every pinned importModulesCore equation, the five-element exposure lattice, exported/server/private global levels, runtime/comptime/all phases, explicit root-export override, first-discovery isMeta retention, server IR-request versus phase distinction, exact five-field upgrade condition, replay-on-upgrade, recursive postorder, direct-row order, and duplicate work inputs. 2. Reports preserve exact effective state, Reference discovery sequence, canonical transitive/reverse queries, exact reverse rows, row-indexed component witnesses, deterministic operation/resource facts, and distinct Complete, Incomplete, Inconclusive, Invalid, and InternalFault outcomes. Missing/non-module/malformed/cancel/resource/invariant cases never collapse, panic, or silently repair. 3. An independently implemented pinned-equation model and named unit/property/metamorphic tests cover every Import flag combination, exposure/phase join, root override, late upgrade, duplicate/diamond/missing cases, server and meta behavior, witness provenance, reverse queries, generated DAGs, construction permutations, thread partitions, cancellation, every resource bound, and bounded dense-work counts. Individually discriminating mutations invert each propagation equation/order/replay/server/isMeta rule and are killed with schema-versioned structured evidence. 4. No cache is added without durable graph identity and the complete request/profile key. Real artifact availability, decoder cross-checks, and the no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E with detailed schema-versioned logs remain mandatory in fln-amv.9.4 and block epic closure; no mock-only substitute or simplified DFS satisfies the integration obligation. 5. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env tests, warnings-denied Clippy, UBS under the documented user-required scanner exclusion, and applicable parent/contract gates are green.
close_reason: Pinned effective-import fixed point, exact queries/witnesses, typed outcomes, independent model, mutation matrix, determinism matrix, and operation bounds are implemented and green; real artifact E2E remains in fln-amv.9.4.

title: fln-env: bind module contributions, provenance roots, and invalidation
description:
Completion epic for module contribution provenance and invalidation over the persistent module graph from fln-amv.9.1 and executable extension checkpoints from fln-amv.7. No scope is removed: every module accounts for imported declarations, extra generated declarations, and exact ordered extension contributions; forward and reverse provenance are one immutable truth; partial and opaque completeness remain honest; import application is atomic; graph identity remains separate from the trusted environment logical root; invalidation is deterministic, bounded, witnessed, and conservative at opaque boundaries; snapshots, paired checkpoints, restores, and provenance-state merge plans preserve identity and sharing; conflicting origins are never last-writer-wins.

The design intentionally separates identity from application payload. The canonical schema/root stores module topology, declaration names, extension entry ordinals/digests, evidence, and completeness without copying payload bytes. Atomic application therefore consumes a separately Arc-backed payload transaction carrying actual ConstantInfo values and raw extension entries, and preflights a one-to-one value/name/digest/range/artifact/epoch binding before publishing the combined Environment plus provenance/index state. Digest equality never replaces actual value equality. Metadata-only provenance cannot change logical_root; declaration/extension semantic publication changes logical_root only through existing Environment canonical rules. Every downstream checkpoint, invalidation request, restore, and merge plan binds exact logical and provenance root preconditions.

The work is decomposed into a shared dependency spine followed by two parallel implementation branches: franken_lean-module-provenance-schema-cxn defines the versioned identity schema, dedicated hash domain, canonical root, resource model, and metadata-only logical-root noninterference; franken_lean-module-provenance-atomic-apply-as7 defines the payload transaction and preflighted all-or-nothing combined state plus bidirectional indexes; franken_lean-module-provenance-invalidation-1qv implements typed bounded module cones and canonical witnesses for artifact, topology, declaration, extension, missing, collision, and opaque changes with FL-INV-07 authority rules; franken_lean-module-provenance-lifecycle-vk2 integrates O(1) snapshots, paired provenance checkpoints, atomic restore, and the revalidated provenance-state merge plan consumed later by fln-amv.11. Schema freezes first, atomic apply freezes second, then invalidation and lifecycle proceed independently from the same committed-state contract. This parent directly joins both branches; neither can close or substitute for the other. Real decoded-fixture and shared no-mock E2E coverage remains in fln-amv.9.4, avoiding duplicate fixture lanes.

Acceptance requires every child closed with its full unit, property/model, negative/collision/resource/cancellation, schedule-metamorphic, sharing/allocation, mutation, schema-versioned logging, fln-env/fln-hash/domain-registry, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor evidence. Module metadata never changes logical_root; semantic declaration, extension, or attribute publication delegates exactly to the canonical Environment root builder. One aggregate ModuleProvenanceRootV1 covers topology and contribution identity and is never operational metadata or silently folded into logical identity. Every query, cache, demand key, or receipt that reads topology/provenance binds ModuleProvenanceRootV1 plus its complete request/profile; pure logical queries need not. No disconnected second truth, flattened opaque payloads, name-or-digest-only application, inferred ownership, partial publication, nondeterministic traversal, hidden logical-root change, advisory-only lifecycle, unpaired checkpoint provenance, global merge duplication, authoritative inconclusive result, or deferred listed feature satisfies this epic.

## Acceptance Criteria

All four dependency-ordered children are closed with every retained original feature plus the explicit identity/payload binding, combined committed state, logical/provenance root transition law, FL-INV-07 authority handling, paired checkpoint, and merge-plan contracts verified. The parent closes only after canonical separate identity, actual-value atomic bidirectional provenance, bounded witnessed invalidation, and snapshot/checkpoint/restore/provenance-plan behavior are complete and all fln-env/fln-hash/domain, model, mutation, collision, sharing, fmt, clippy, and UBS gates are green. fln-amv.9.4 retains the real decoded-fixture/no-mock matrix. No partial child closure or deferred listed feature satisfies this epic.

## Success Criteria

All four local provenance children close: canonical schema/root, actual-value atomic apply/indexes, bounded read-only invalidation, and snapshot/checkpoint/restore/merge-plan lifecycle. Their evidence join contains one validated child reference each and preserves exact root, authority, collision, resource, cancellation, mutation, recovery, and sharing laws. This epic does not wait on fln-amv.9.4; the outer fln-amv.9 epic owns that real-fixture join.
acceptance_criteria:
This epic closes on four local children only: canonical schema/root; actual-value atomic application and indexes; bounded witnessed invalidation; and snapshot/checkpoint/restore/provenance-plan lifecycle. Each child closes on exhaustive local unit/model/mutation/collision/resource/cancellation/sharing/nonpublication/recovery proof plus a stable handoff. The downstream fln-amv.9.4 real fixture matrix remains mandatory before the outer fln-amv.9 epic closes, but it is not a prerequisite for this epic and cannot create a semantic closure cycle.

The canonical contract freezes one aggregate ModuleProvenanceRootV1 and exact payload/identity binding. Module metadata alone never changes logical_root; semantic declaration, extension, or attribute publication delegates to the canonical Environment builder. Any topology/provenance-sensitive query/cache/receipt binds ModuleProvenanceRootV1 and its complete request/profile. Atomic application uses corrected declaration and module-graph admission plans, publishes authoritative complete state only, and preserves partial inspection as non-authoritative. Invalidation is bounded/read-only with canonical witnesses. Lifecycle preserves collision-safe checkpoint identity and returns a revalidated bounded plan for fln-amv.11 rather than publishing the global merge.

The parent evidence join contains exactly one validated reference per child bundle and rejects missing, duplicate, stale, contradictory, cross-input, or aggregate-substitution rows. All fln-env/hash/domain/model/mutation/collision/resource/cancellation/sharing/fmt/check/test/warnings-denied-clippy/UBS gates pass. No child waits on .9.4; no partial closure, second truth, hidden root change, name/digest-only payload binding, authoritative Inconclusive state, or deferred behavior satisfies closure.

No --parent edges were used because br parent links are blocking and previously caused semantic deadlocks.

title: fln-env: integrate real module fixtures and the import-graph E2E matrix
description:
Integrate the lossless fln-20n.1 decoder with fln-amv.9.1/.2/.3 and close the module-import graph epic against real pinned artifacts. This bead owns the single shared no-mock environment import-graph E2E lane so component beads do not duplicate or weaken final evidence.

Build the direct adapter from decoded ModuleData plus resolver-supplied module identity and artifact evidence into both layers required by the design: the identity-only canonical ModuleContributionRecord and the payload-bearing ModuleApplyTransaction. Carry actual Arc-backed ConstantInfo declarations, extra generated declarations, and exact raw extension payload entries; derive names, canonical identities, payload digests, descriptors, ordinals, ranges, epoch, artifact, and completeness once and prove the transaction binds one-to-one to the canonical manifest before atomic application. Exercise a separately labeled bounded-model equal-digest unequal-value synthetic through the owned test hash seam so no digest-only application or idempotence path can hide; this seam is not counted as a real Reference fixture or production-hash collision.

Exercise real Reference-produced modules covering all import flag dimensions, exported/private/import-all/meta propagation, exact duplicate direct rows and same-module rows with differing flags, generating pinned Tribunal fixtures when the corpus lacks them, diamonds and late state upgrades, non-module restrictions, declaration and extension contributions, opaque custom extensions, extra generated declarations, missing artifacts and targets, corrupt/truncated rows, stale manifests and checkpoint roots, cycle/reflexive synthetic negatives, resource exhaustion, cancellation/inconclusive behavior, failed-transaction nonpublication, and clean recovery. Compare exact ordered direct rows, Reference discovery order, effective state and IR phases, actual declaration/extension values, forward/reverse contribution indexes, graph/provenance roots, and invalidation grades/witnesses to independent oracle manifests generated only in the Tribunal fixture path.

Logical-root expectations are scenario-specific and explicit. Topology, artifact, completeness, index, and provenance metadata changes alone must leave logical_root unchanged. Successfully applied declaration or extension semantic bytes must change or preserve logical_root exactly as the existing Environment canonical rules predict. Every log records before/after logical and provenance roots and the reason each is expected to change or remain stable; the phrase unchanged logical root is never applied to a semantic payload update.

Provide a deterministic schema-versioned E2E script with retained artifacts and line-oriented detailed logs: schema/epoch, fixture/module/transaction ids, artifact/contract/manifest digests and evidence grade, ordered import index/name/importAll/isExported/isMeta, graph nodes/rows/completeness, effective exposure/data/IR state, discovery/canonical traversals, declaration and extension payload/contribution ids, ordinals/ranges and opaque boundaries, forward/reverse index facts, before/after logical and graph/provenance roots with transition reason, expected/actual outcome and grade, authority/cacheability/publication flags, typed diagnostic, resource budget/usage, recovery action, and final state. Positive, negative, mutation, collision, inconclusive, nonpublication, and recovery scenarios are mandatory.

Fixture provenance and reproduction instructions must name the exact SUITE.lock epoch, generated ABI/OLEAN contracts, Reference producer command confined to the Tribunal, content digests, and offline replay command. The lane refuses stale pin/contract/manifest drift before application. Deterministic repetitions and thread partitions must produce byte-identical authoritative logs after normalizing only registered run ids; no timestamps, host paths, or scheduler facts enter semantic expectations.

Run generated contract drift checks, fln-olean and fln-env unit/integration/property suites, the real E2E lane, deterministic repetitions/thread partitions, clippy with warnings denied, format, UBS, and applicable W2 and parent gates. Record evidence links and claim grades; no release claim closes on mocks.

No hand-authored oracle answer, network dependency, upstream runtime component, name-only or digest-only adapter, skipped non-default flag, opaque-data loss, logical-root topology leak, missing semantic logical-root transition, nondeterministic logs, partial publication, or success-only E2E.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every decoder/graph/effective/provenance component and the payload-bearing atomic transaction are integrated one-to-one against real pinned fixtures. The positive plus negative/mutation/collision/inconclusive/nonpublication/recovery matrix must produce the detailed deterministic retained logs above; actual payloads, both index directions, scenario-specific logical-root transitions, provenance roots, grades, and oracle comparisons pass; stale fixtures/contracts/manifests are rejected; and every listed quality/contract/parent gate is green. No mock-only, name/digest-only, partial, unclassified skip, or deferred scenario satisfies closure.
design:
Legal adapter and evidence composition boundary.

The ModuleDataView-to-environment adapter lives in an existing permitted integration/conformance layer that can depend on both fln-olean and fln-env, or behind a neutral fln-olean outward data API consumed above both. It never introduces fln-env to fln-olean, fln-olean to fln-env, or another forbidden/cyclic crate edge. The crate owner and dependency proof are frozen before implementation.

The closed fln-20n.1 manifest is reused as immutable baseline evidence by exact digest. Any newly required fixture is described by an additive manifest referencing that baseline; the original is never edited, relabeled, or regenerated in place. Oracle generation stays in the Tribunal. Module identity, ordered rows, actual values, and evidence are derived once into canonical records and nonpublishing per-module plans.

ModuleBatchApplyPlan stages the entire exact fln-amv.9.2 closure privately in discovery/replay order. Later modules may depend on prior staged results, but no authoritative Environment, cache, receipt, watcher, or query sees a prefix. After final closure-wide root, reservation, plan-integrity, and cancellation revalidation, one publication exposes all modules and indexes; publication has no later input-sized allocation or failure point. A failure at module N exposes none of modules 1 through N-minus-1.

Evidence ownership is nonduplicative. This bead owns the legal adapter, module-only semantic scenarios, batch coordinator, and strict module bundle. It validates one digest reference per fln-amv.9.3 child bundle rather than copying child rows. The downstream attribute bundle references this module bundle once and adds only attribute semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
Every decoder/graph/effective/provenance component and the payload-bearing atomic transaction are integrated one-to-one against real pinned fixtures. The positive plus negative/mutation/collision/inconclusive/nonpublication/recovery matrix must produce the detailed deterministic retained logs above; actual payloads, both index directions, scenario-specific logical-root transitions, provenance roots, grades, and oracle comparisons pass; stale fixtures/contracts/manifests are rejected; and every listed quality/contract/parent gate is green. No mock-only, name/digest-only, partial, unclassified skip, or deferred scenario satisfies closure.

Mandatory real-fixture coverage contains all eight import-flag triples, exact duplicate rows, same-module duplicates with differing flags, structural Name cases, positive and missing-target closures, and every other listed scenario. The phrase where fixtures permit is not a waiver: the Tribunal generator must create a pinned fixture or the bead remains blocked.

Injected equal-digest unequal-value tests are a separately labeled bounded_model seam. They prove exact collision-disambiguation behavior, not occurrence or impossibility of a production-hash collision, and are not counted as no-mock Reference evidence.

Composite budgets and usage separately cover decoder, graph registration, declaration admission, extension/history comparison, provenance/index construction, batch coordination, output/evidence bytes, and cumulative checked totals. Each child fact is charged once. Outcome precedence and cancellation checkpoints are frozen; failure yields zero visible prefix and a clean adequate-budget replay.

Productive fixed-input 1/8/32 runs perform actual partitioned preparation/staging with replayable seeds and byte-identical semantic output. Semantic NDJSON excludes timestamp, duration, host path, allocator, and scheduler telemetry; optional telemetry is a separate bounded non-authoritative stream referenced by digest.

The strict additionalProperties=false bundle schema requires exactly one terminal record per scenario, separates streams, stages then validates then promotes evidence atomically, binds baseline/additive manifests and every governed hash, constrains relative artifact paths, and supports offline read-only revalidation. Validator mutants target missing/duplicate child references, manifest substitution, stream merging, path escape, double-charged usage, fake threads, wrong collision claim class, partial-prefix publication, missing terminal, and stale governed hashes. No surviving validator mutant or aggregate green bar satisfies closure.

No fixture-availability waiver remains.

title: fln-kernel K1: defeq-eta slice — KR-903 structure eta + KR-315 unit-like eta (residual eliminator-family probe)
description: Follow-up to franken_lean-5p2. After the recursor-reduction slice, the Init.Prelude replay still false-rejects 343 declarations; the dominant family (244 eliminators) is concentrated in typeclass-structure casesOn/recOn/noConfusionType/below (e.g. Membership.casesOn, HDiv.noConfusionType, Lean.Name.below), all TypeMismatch/DefinitionTypeMismatch. HYPOTHESIS (typed, unverified): these need KR-903 structure eta in defeq (try_eta_struct_core: t ≟ mk as fs for a one-constructor, non-recursive, index-free structure holds when the types agree and every field fi is defeq to t.i, both directions) and possibly KR-315 unit-like eta (two terms of the same one-constructor zero-field structure type are defeq when their types are). FIRST TASK: root-cause ONE case from each residual sub-family from the real olean (decode Membership.casesOn, replay with instrumentation, capture the exact defeq query that fails) — no implementation before the gap is evidence-anchored; if the probe refutes the KR-903 hypothesis, re-triage before coding. Implement per pin anchor vendor/lean4-src/src/kernel/type_checker.cpp:793 (try_eta_struct_core) and :1044 (is_def_eq_unit_like), wired into the is_def_eq ladder at the pin's position. Verification: mutation-killed unit tests per rule branch (eta disabled, one-direction-only, Prop-structure exclusion if pin has one, wrong-field-index), kernel replay re-run with before/after census recorded (expect the 244-family to convert if hypothesis holds), fmt/clippy/UBS/structure-guard green, covenant intact.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Real decoded residual probes from at least one member of every identified family establish the actual first divergent defeq query before implementation; any refuted hypothesis is recorded and the bead is re-triaged rather than forced. 2. The post-whnf and lazy-delta quick-defeq ladder, frontier-transitive replay dependencies, KR-903 structure eta, and KR-315 unit-like eta match the pinned contract and preserve every no-fire/over-acceptance guard. 3. Named public-authority unit and mutation tests cover decoded Sort/Pi shapes, one- and two-direction eta, wrong-field indices, type mismatch, recursive/indexed/non-structure exclusions, unit-like eligibility, and over-acceptance seeds; every planted mutant is killed. 4. The real no-mock kernel replay records exact before/after census, residual-family reclassification, zero inconclusive regression, failure artifacts, and clean recovery in schema-versioned NDJSON with fixture/hash, contract rule, budget, expected/actual verdict and diagnostic, consumption, artifact, cleanup, and final state. 5. Determinism assertions compare semantic replay output for repeated identical inputs while excluding only declared monotonic timing/path fields. fln-kernel tests, replay, format, warnings-denied Clippy, UBS, and the kernel covenant/structure guard are green.
close_reason: Landed as three commits (6fbdb26, 538dff7, 1efcf23). The mandated probe REFUTED the KR-903 hypothesis for the 244-member residual family and uncovered two different defects: (1) KERNEL: the defeq ladder ran its head rules (KR-301/302/303) only on the un-reduced pair — pairs becoming Sort≟Sort (equivalent-not-identical levels) or Pi≟Pi only after beta/delta fell through and false-rejected; fixed pin-shaped via quick_def_eq_rules re-run after whnf_core and lazy delta (3 reproduction tests from decoded shapes; mutants M8/M9 killed). (2) HARNESS: replay topo edges ignored references reachable only through frontier declaration types (env outParam = ABSENT at Membership.casesOn); fixed with frontier-transitive dependency expansion. Census across the arc: 1412/1755 → 1744/1755 accepted, 343 → 11 rejected, 0 Inconclusive. KR-903/KR-315 then implemented per pin anchors as ladder-parity completeness (49/49 tests, mutants M10-M13 killed incl. two over-acceptance gate seeds) with the honest note that Prelude shows zero demand for them. All 11 residuals decoded and reassigned to franken_lean-irm (OfNat.ofNat..lit..≟Nat.zero for 6 match auxiliaries; literal arithmetic for 5 ofNatLT/ofNatAux). Probe lanes FLN_REPLAY_PROBE / FLN_REPLAY_PROBE_ENV + bounded expected/actual kernel mismatch messages are permanent triage infrastructure.

title: W3: Certificates, receipts, transparency log — auditable checking
description:
Implement certificates, receipts, and the transparency log (§8.6): every checked declaration can emit a certificate — the compressed, structure-shared elaborated term DAG plus reduction hints that make independent rechecking cheap (unfold order, literal-arithmetic transcripts so a tiny verifier replays accelerated ops without its own bignum trust). Certificates attach to olean-next records, feed fln export (speaking the community established export format for cross-prover checkers), and may ship a warm defeq-cache cartridge (memoized reduction history — third-party rechecking becomes verification rather than search).

The certificate law (D20): the checker must be SIMPLER than recomputation, must reject unknown versions, and must fall back to recomputation on any failure — an accelerator, never a wider TCB. Receipts bind kernel + checker build hashes, the consensus policy, fuel profiles, and both verdicts. Attested receipts append to a transparency log (Merkle Mountain Range over receipt roots, signed checkpoints): third parties verify inclusion and consistency without trusting the logger; equivocation is detectable — "checked" becomes an artifact anyone can audit, not a verb someone witnessed.

Acceptance: certificate emit + independent replay on the corpus with the checker measurably simpler (line count, dependency count) than the engines; fln export output accepted by at least one external checker; MMR inclusion/consistency proofs verified by an independent implementation of the verifier side; PG-1b receipt-emission overhead <= 1 ms/decl amortized. Tests: certificate-version rejection fixtures; corrupt-certificate fallback-to-recompute fixtures; log equivocation detection test (forked checkpoint must be caught).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

## Success Criteria

All five exact children close with machine-validated roots; certificate format and cartridges are versioned and bounded; the simple verifier and full checker remain independent; disagreements halt; receipts/signatures/checkpoints never grant declaration authority; all mutation/fuzz/no-mock/recovery and semantic-evidence gates pass.
design:
OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this bead owns OQ-8 and OQ-13. Freeze the versioned certificate/export schema and its alignment with at least one existing cross-prover checker; separately specify the warm defeq-cache cartridge schema, admissible hints, size/resource bounds, receipt attachment policy, version rejection, privacy/redaction rules, and recomputation fallback. Update the decision log and add compatibility/tamper/resource fixtures for both formats.

TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are fln-7zrh certificate schema/OQ-8, franken_lean-eikp cartridge/OQ-13, fln-eeyn simple verifier/fallback, franken_lean-d2h5 receipt/consensus binding, and franken_lean-nix9 transparency/checkpoints. This epic closes only after a machine-validated join names every child implementation/evidence root, confirms verifier/checker independence, and proves signatures/checkpoints never grant declaration authority.
acceptance_criteria:
1. OQ-8 is resolved before certificate/export freeze by a versioned reviewed format decision that specifies canonical declaration-certificate framing, structure sharing, replay hints, version negotiation, resource bounds, semantic and byte identities, olean-next binding, and row-by-row alignment or explicit mapping to existing cross-prover checker formats. A real external checker round-trip validates the chosen export; unknown or lossy mappings block the decision. 2. OQ-13 is resolved jointly with K2 by a separate versioned warm defeq-cache cartridge schema and receipt-attachment policy: exact key domain, environment/transparency/epoch/build binding, entry and expanded-weight limits, validation, optionality, privacy/portability, cacheability, expiry, and safe discard/recompute behavior are explicit. Cartridges are accelerators only and cannot carry verdict authority. 3. Emit versioned, structure-shared declaration certificates containing the elaborated term DAG and replay hints, optionally including a conforming warm cartridge; bind certificates to olean-next records and support the reviewed community export format without expanding trusted authority. 4. The independent checker rejects unknown versions and malformed, truncated, non-canonical, mismatched-hash, stale, foreign-build, or over-budget certificates/cartridges. Any certificate or cartridge failure falls back to ordinary recomputation with the same verdict, never acceptance or rejection by the accelerator alone; the verifier remains measurably simpler than recomputation by recorded KLOC and dependency-closure evidence. 5. Receipts bind declaration/environment roots, kernel and checker build hashes, consensus policy, epoch/mode/profile/fuel, both verdicts, certificate version, optional cartridge identity/disposition, and evidence ids. Attested receipt roots append to an MMR transparency log with independently verifiable inclusion and consistency proofs, signed checkpoints, and detectable fork/equivocation. 6. Named unit/property/model/mutation suites cover certificate and cartridge encode/decode/replay, structure sharing, every OQ-8 mapping, version rejection, corrupt/stale/foreign hint and cache fallback, deterministic export, receipt attachment/detachment, MMR append/inclusion/consistency, fork detection, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and failure atomicity. Planted certificate-trust, cartridge-authority, stale-key, receipt-rebinding, and checkpoint-fork mutants must be killed. 7. A shared no-mock E2E lane checks a real corpus declaration, replays its certificate independently, submits export output to at least one real external checker, attaches and discards a real K2 cartridge with identical verdict, verifies inclusion and consistency from a fresh verifier process, demonstrates corrupt-certificate/cartridge recomputation and fork detection, and proves clean recovery. Human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON record ids, OQ-8/OQ-13 decisions, exact redacted argv/cwd, pin/build hashes, declaration/environment/certificate/cartridge/receipt/log roots, versions, expected/actual verdicts, fallback reason, proof/checkpoint data, resources/timings/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state. 8. Reproducible benchmark evidence on the declared corpus/machine shows PG-1b receipt emission at no more than 1 ms per declaration amortized and reports distributions and sampling method. Close evidence archives both reviewed decisions, manifests, certificates, exports, cartridges, receipts, checkpoints, proofs, mutant results, benchmark data, and all named test/E2E artifacts.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — distinguish the bounded simple certificate verifier from the complete independent declaration checker. Malformed, unsupported, stale, or unknown certificate versions reject the certificate and trigger governed recomputation where policy permits; if recomputation lacks budget or authority, the declaration outcome is typed Inconclusive rather than a promised identical verdict. Receipt signatures, transparency entries, checkpoints, and consensus state attest evidence but never grant declaration authority. Tests cover certificate decoder fuzzing, verifier/checker independence, fallback boundaries, tampering, cancellation, resource exhaustion, and exact nonpublication/recovery.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative output is strict schema-versioned canonical semantic NDJSON containing normalized inputs, roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, wall time, temporary paths, cache/performance facts, and scheduler details live only in a separately linked bounded telemetry stream and cannot enter authoritative digests. Validators reject merged streams, unknown versions, extra or missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, or telemetry-root drift. Productive 1/8/32 runs prove nonempty completed work and deterministic reduction rather than thread-count labels.

TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are fln-7zrh certificate schema/OQ-8, franken_lean-eikp cartridge/OQ-13, fln-eeyn simple verifier/fallback, franken_lean-d2h5 receipt/consensus binding, and franken_lean-nix9 transparency/checkpoints. This epic closes only after a machine-validated join names every child implementation/evidence root, confirms verifier/checker independence, and proves signatures/checkpoints never grant declaration authority.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W6: Synod instance engine — exact semantics, SearchCards, keyed negative caches
description:
Implement Synod, the instance engine (§10.4): the Reference semantics — priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, default instances, tabling/caching behavior, the results the ecosystem diamond structures depend on — behind a rebuilt engine: instance search compiled to an indexed relational form (discrimination-automaton candidate retrieval, subsumption-checked tabling, cycle detection with franken_networkx diagnostics), with provenance recorded per resolution: every attempted instance, why it failed (unification residue, priority loss, depth), as a Palimpsest tree.

Every search emits a SearchCard (explored candidates, priorities, unification residues, cycle decisions, cache hits, selection) — provenance and negative-cache key in one artifact, with negative entries valid ONLY under their exact dependency key (instance-table identity, transparency, options, local-context fingerprint). Faithful mode pins search order to the Reference; sound mode may reorder only within proven-confluent regions (same result, better time), CGSE-registered. Name resolution, coercion graphs, and discrimination indexes are immutable content-addressed derivations of the environment commit, incrementally maintained and Ledger-cached — faithful iteration order preserved even where internals are faster.

Acceptance: instance-search outcomes match the oracle across the Corpus instance-trace corpus (instrumented-oracle instance traces); SearchCards complete per perturbation; the sound-mode "why is there no instance" answer (§15.3) renders from this engine exhaust with no second implementation. Tests: golden instance-trace replay; diamond/priority fixtures; negative-cache validity mutation tests (a stale negative entry must be impossible under the dependency key); cycle-diagnostic fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
Synod preserves pinned instance priorities, local/global ordering, outParam/semiOutParam, defaults, tabling, cycles, depth, transparency, failure and diagnostic behavior. Candidate retrieval/indexing and tabling are immutable content-addressed derivations but faithful iteration/selection matches Reference traces; sound-mode reordering occurs only in registered proven-confluent regions. Every search emits a complete SearchCard with candidates, priorities, residues, failures, cycles, cache facts and selection plus provenance tree. Negative entries bind exact instance-table identity, transparency, options, local-context fingerprint, epoch and relevant dependencies; stale or incomplete keys cannot hit. Name resolution, coercion graphs and discrimination indexes share the same environment truth, and why-no-instance renders from recorded exhaust rather than a second engine. Unit/property/model/trace/metamorphic/mutation suites cover diamonds, priorities, defaults, cycles, local shadowing, cache keys, incremental changes, budgets/cancellation and recovery; named stale-negative, candidate-drop/reorder, wrong-priority, false-cycle, incomplete-card and unsound-reorder mutants are killed. Real no-mock Corpus instance E2E replays golden traces, perturbs tables/contexts/options, compares outcomes/diagnostics, restores, and retains strict SearchCard/key/root/final-state bundles. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, trace/Mirror/perturbation/mutation lanes pass. No stale negative cache, hidden second resolver, faithful search reorder, incomplete provenance, mock-only oracle, guessed instance, or deferred listed semantics satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — InstanceSearchKeyV1 binds canonical goal, environment and instance-extension roots, local-instance order, mvar assignment revision and closure, transparency, every search-affecting option/config, epoch/schema, and read dependencies. Only complete authoritative exhaustion can form a negative entry; Inconclusive, cancellation, exhaustion, InternalFault, or incomplete SearchCard cannot. Sound-mode reordering requires registered confluence evidence and shadow validation; otherwise faithful order applies. Full SearchCards are authoritative artifacts and bounded logs reference them without truncation. Named suites: synod_cache_key_model, search_card_completeness, synod_instance_no_mock_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative evidence is canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON validated independently; telemetry is a separate non-authoritative stream and cannot affect hashes, comparison, admission, cache, publication, or promotion. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/duplicate ids, broken step order, unbound artifacts, silent truncation, merged streams, and cleanup/final-state mismatch. Cancellation, exhaustion, and InternalFault never enter cache, negative storage, proof admission, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 records prove useful overlap, completed work, retries, and schedule identity. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: W1: Tribunal bootstrap — epoch lab, differential harness, Parity Ledger schema
description:
## Description

Bootstrap the Tribunal before the subsystems it judges (§18): the pinned epoch laboratory, oracle authority/precedence model, typed bounded comparison classes, complete corpus-family and official-test inventory, Parity Ledger schema, planted-divergence self-tests, G0 decision schema, and Oracle-Only release reachability checks. The Reference runs only inside the designated Tribunal boundary as differential oracle, fixture generator, or census source; it never becomes a FrankenLean runtime component.

Epoch labs bind official archive/binary/tree hashes, executable/symbol/header inventories, CLI/LSP transcripts, artifact fixtures, quirk/defect ledgers, environment normalization, exact reproduction commands, platform, and expected outcomes. Revisions are immutable append-only hash chains. Candidate generation is failure-atomic: interruption or invalidity leaves the prior revision authoritative. C0-C9 schemas are total; C1 imports the complete pinned official-test inventory with expected outcome and explicit exclusion/unsupported mapping. A small smoke slice demonstrates execution but cannot substitute for inventory completeness.

Oracle kind, authority, process outcome, comparison class, normalizer id/version, D7 claim type, evidence kind/state, L-level, mode, determinism class, freshness, and platform are separate closed vocabularies. Reference timeout, cancellation, resource refusal, unavailable platform, or crash is Inconclusive/InternalFault, never a semantic reject or FrankenLean divergence. Typed normalizers are total, bounded, deterministic, idempotent, versioned, preserve distinct semantic errors and ordered diagnostics, and cannot expand adversarial inputs beyond budget.

Every Parity row names a real fixture, exact roots, oracle/comparison, D7 claim type (or mandatory RUR claim-row identity), evidence state, L-level, mode/platform, limitations, and freshness. Claim type, claim state, and L-level are distinct. Missing/duplicate/stale/unknown/root-mismatched/mock-only/overclaimed rows block.

Define G0SpikeDecisionV1. Each G0 spike emits exactly one row with spike id and exact §22.1 question; outcome Ratified, Amended, or NoGo; D7 claim type separately from witness evidence state; exact epoch/corpus/platform/mode scope; fixture, generated-contract, and implementation roots; oracle kind/comparison class; resource contract/usage; mutation and real no-mock E2E roots; known limitations; and affected downstream interfaces. Amended requires exact §25 wording, rationale, blast radius, owners/dependency updates, and green amended acceptance tests. Failed/absent/unresolved/NoGo evidence cannot be normalized into amendment.

Oracle source/reachability checks permit entry points only inside the poisoned development Tribunal boundary. Every oracle-derived product is poison-typed and gate/cache-inadmissible. Development-only Tribunal targets may contain that machinery. Separately, release checks enumerate every shippable target/profile/feature set and prove no oracle symbol/path/capability or unpoisoned product is reachable. Platform population is honest: this epic closes a fully populated primary lab and platform schema; fln-wgz owns real Windows population, and later certified hosts retain BLOCKED rows until run.

Canonical semantic evidence excludes timestamps/duration/PID/absolute paths/scheduler/allocator/performance facts, which live in bounded linked telemetry. Fixed semantic inputs run productively at 1/8/32 where safely parallel, with nonempty partitions and deterministic reduction; intrinsically serial steps stay ordered and are not relabeled parallel.

## Success Criteria

Named suites epoch_lab_hash_chain, official_test_inventory_completeness, oracle_outcome_authority, typed_normalizer_model, parity_row_authority, oracle_only_reachability, g0_spike_decision_model, semantic_telemetry_separation, and reference_reference_no_mock_e2e cover all vocabularies, C0-C9/C1 inventories, revisions/publication, comparator laws/budgets, process outcomes, row freshness/roots/claims, poison boundaries, release profiles, G0 decisions/amendments, productive thread partitions, platform BLOCKED rows, recovery, and independent validation. Plant divergences at line/sub-line/diagnostic/exit/artifact levels and mutations for normalized-away bodies, reordered ordered diagnostics, normalizer expansion, stale/duplicate row, claim/L-level conflation, oracle crash as reject, partial epoch publication, mutable revision, incomplete C1 inventory, test-only oracle misclassified as release leak, release oracle reachability, unpoisoned product, mock fixture, semantic/telemetry mixing, fake thread label, missing G0 decision, NoGo-as-amendment, and root mismatch; all fail for the intended reason. The real Reference-vs-Reference E2E uses exact pinned binaries/fixtures, produces byte-identical semantic roots across repeated runs, detects all planted divergences, exercises typed non-authoritative outcomes, restores, and validates the final bundle independently. Bootstrap self-tests later register in TD9’s kill ledger rather than creating a second campaign engine. No mutable lab, incomplete official-test inventory, source-regex census substitute, mock-only oracle, error-erasing normalizer, skipped platform reported passed, oracle leak, aggregate G0 green, or unclassified divergence satisfies closure.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — the Tribunal remains the first substantive subsystem, but it starts only after the authoritative SUITE.lock/closure audit and shared verification/logging contract exist. This avoids an unpinned oracle lab and a parallel ad-hoc test runner while still allowing contract extraction, fln-core, fln-hash, and platform work to proceed in parallel after workspace scaffolding.
acceptance_criteria: A complete pin/platform-bound primary epoch lab, C0-C9 schemas and full C1 inventory, typed bounded normalizers/comparators, authoritative Parity Ledger, G0SpikeDecisionV1, planted-divergence self-tests, poison boundary, and shippable-release reachability scan are integrated and independently validated. Oracle failures remain non-semantic outcomes; claim type/state/L-level stay distinct; epoch publication is append-only and atomic; semantic records exclude telemetry; parallel lanes are productive. Windows/later host population remains explicitly BLOCKED on its owner until real evidence. All named model/mutation/no-mock/recovery/logging tests pass. No incomplete inventory, mutable/partial lab, mock oracle, normalized-away error, overclaimed row, hidden exclusion, oracle leak, or missing/invalid G0 decision satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Bootstrap implementation started by session agent (claim blocked by rur/xwf held elsewhere). Surface: crates/fln-conformance, scripts/tribunal/, tribunal/epochs/, ci/PARITY_LEDGER.txt.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Early read-only acceptance review of the active bootstrap: (1) ledger::validate_fixtures does root.join(fixture).exists() without rejecting absolute paths, .. traversal, symlink escape, or non-regular targets, so a purported repo-relative evidence row can cite arbitrary host files. Enforce lexical and canonical confinement to the workspace and retain source line numbers for exact diagnostics. (2) Normalizer::paths_v1 accepts arbitrary strings and globally replaces them. A caller can pass error, x, or an entire diagnostic body; the three substring markers only catch some deletions and one surviving marker can mask another discarded diagnostic. Validate absolute path prefixes and normalize structured path fields/boundaries, with multiple-error/warning/unsolved-goal mutation tests. (3) OracleFallback::use_poisoned_diagnosis_value_gate_inert returns raw T, structurally laundering away the poison tag; a method name cannot enforce gate/cache inertness. Keep the taint in the return type or expose diagnostic-only operations that cannot yield an unpoisoned product. Also, the current L0-no-fixture test comment expects acceptance but asserts parse error; cargo test should catch that transient inconsistency.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Second acceptance pass on the newly added ledger/tests: the real ledger currently promotes p8a observables to L2 OBSERVED even though p8a is open and its cited core_observables lane has known unbounded-Nat/Int, corruption-validation, and corpus-totality gaps (p8a comment 9). Plan 4.2 defines L2 as a gated corpus pass with exclusions explicit; either keep these rows at honest L0/L1/TARGETED or record explicit exclusions and only advance them when the owning evidence closes. The String.hash row also cites BLAKE3 vectors, which are fln-hash identity evidence rather than evidence for Lean String.hash. Separately, poison_compile_out::the_scanner_detects_a_planted_leak only calls str.contains on a literal; it does not exercise collect_rs_files or the real violation scanner, so a broken traversal survives the planted test. Factor the scanner and seed a real isolated source tree. The cfg!(feature) default test proves only the test build omitted the feature, not yet the promised release-artifact absence check.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Epoch-lab/E2E acceptance review: gen_epoch_manifest.sh generate mode mkdir -p plus redirects directly into an existing published epoch, so it can overwrite an allegedly immutable lab and leave stale transcript files when the slice changes. Generate in a new scratch snapshot, validate completeness, then require an explicit reviewed first publication; existing epochs should be check-only. Set LC_ALL/TZ for the whole generator so glob/tool inventory ordering is not inherited-locale dependent. The comment claims a self-contained slice, but select_slice only chooses the ten smallest files and performs no self-containment classification. More importantly, ref_vs_ref.sh invents a minimal fln-e2e/1 logger instead of extending the shared fln.e2e/2 evidence family: early failures have no terminal record or committed manifest/bundle; JSON strings are printf-unescaped; there are no canonical input roots, epoch/mode/profile/thread/seed/cache facts, per-step supervisor/resource/exit artifacts, bounded output, cancellation/recovery, or failure-atomic final-state proof. Under rur and this bead mandatory DoD, a passing diff alone cannot close the E2E claim; route the oracle commands through the shared supervisor/evidence contract and validate/commit the bundle.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Bootstrap implemented + verified by session agent (claim still blocked by rur/xwf). Delivered: Parity Ledger schema + seeded ledger + gate test; comparison-class normalizers with never-discard-an-error law + planted-divergence tests; oracle-precedence ladder as data; ORACLE_FALLBACK poison machinery behind oracle-fallback-dev with compile-out test + workspace leak grep; epoch lab tribunal/epochs/v4.32.0 (verified binary identity, executable inventory, env-normalization recipe, C1 slice of 10 upstream elab tests with recorded baselines, drift mode); ref_vs_ref.sh smoke differential green end-to-end (twice-run byte-identity, baseline match, planted divergence detected, recovery). Commit 06ba84b. Scope notes (typed): C1 import uses plain-invocation transcripts as observed-behavior baseline (upstream-harness semantics + full C0-C9 scaffolding + instrumented oracle are follow-on beads); dashboard aggregation exists as the ledger aggregate API + gate test, visual dashboard pending. Will close when claimable.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Post-commit acceptance audit of 06ba84b (bead correctly remains open): (1) check.sh only adds gen_epoch_manifest.sh/ref_vs_ref.sh to governed inputs and shellcheck; neither `gen_epoch_manifest.sh --check` nor `ref_vs_ref.sh` is executed by scripts/check.sh or .github/workflows/ci.yml, so the required regeneration/regression CI job and permanent Reference-vs-Reference obligation do not exist. (2) Ledger rows store oracle_kind and comparison_class as unchecked strings, disconnected from OracleAuthority/ComparisonClass/NormalizerId; typos or invented authorities/normalizers pass the gate. Make these parsed typed/versioned values and reject unknown entries. (3) The committed epoch lab has executable hashes and 10 plain-invocation transcripts, but the bead also mandates symbol/header inventories, CLI and LSP transcripts, artifact fixtures, explicit quirk/defect ledgers, per-platform labs, C0-C9 scaffolding, and upstream expected-outcome mapping. The comment classifying those as follow-on cannot close this bead because its acceptance explicitly forbids deferring or narrowing listed scope. Earlier comments 27-29/33 also remain unresolved (fixture escape, arbitrary normalizer prefixes, poison laundering, overclaimed L2 rows, fake scanner mutation, mutable published epoch, and ad-hoc unbundled E2E).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Review sweep (Claude Fable session): two reporting defects fixed in committed fln-conformance code. (1) ledger.rs validate_fixtures rendered the row INDEX as 'PARITY_LEDGER.txt:{line}' — a missing-fixture error pointed at the wrong line for any ledger with comments/blank lines. Row now carries its 1-based source line and validate_fixtures reports it (regression: ghost-fixture error asserts line 2). (2) normalize.rs compare(): when transcripts differed only below line granularity (e.g. trailing newline), the finding read 'length divergence: ours N lines, oracle N lines' with equal N — self-contradictory triage output. Now reports 'sub-line divergence (line endings or trailing bytes): ours X bytes, oracle Y bytes' (regression added). Detection behavior unchanged in both cases — divergences were already caught; only the rendered findings were wrong. Gates green (fmt/clippy -D warnings/tests/UBS 0 critical, isolated target).
