title: W1: Evidence-runner bootstrap — causal supervision, durable bundles, sealed toolchain
description:
Extract the trustworthy process/evidence substrate that fln-8mj needs in order to close without making the entire permanent quality-system bead an operational prerequisite. This bead is a strict prerequisite slice of franken_lean-rur, not a replacement: RUR retains the machine-checked verification registry for every implementation bead, the growing scenario family, permanent Tribunal/CI obligations, archive policy, and all future subsystem integration.

Implement the Linux evidence runner and the three bootstrap wrappers as a coherent trusted result boundary: target-before-exec causal containment; exact process identity; PID-reuse-safe signalling; bounded setup, execution, cancellation, cleanup, and finalization; one typed terminal outcome; durable write-once bundle publication and read-only independent validation; an evidence envelope beginning immediately after fresh artifact-directory creation; per-attempt isolated build state; and exact locked-toolchain execution sealed against ambient Cargo/rustc configuration.

The initial consumers are scripts/check.sh, scripts/e2e/structure_gate.sh, and scripts/e2e/closure_audit.sh. They must emit concise human logs plus bounded schema-versioned NDJSON and independently validatable bundles for success, semantic failure, unexpected failure, cancellation, resource exhaustion, setup fault, concurrent source drift, and internal fault. Release-level claims use real processes, filesystems, compiler, and structure-guard binaries; mocks may support unit tests but never close this bead.

## Acceptance Criteria

All behaviors and boundaries in this description, Design, and the structured Acceptance Criteria field are mandatory. The bead closes only with the named unit, fault, stress, hostile-environment, full-gate, and no-mock E2E evidence; no case may be silently deferred, weakened to a mock, or promoted from an inconclusive/internal result.
design:
The supervisor launches a tiny stopped-exec gate as the actual session leader and future target PID. The child arms parent-death containment, publishes or exposes an identity-bound inert T/t state, and cannot execute user argv until the parent has verified direct parentage, session/process-group identity, start ticks, pidfd, and readiness. Only then does the parent resume the same PID into exec. Setup/readiness has a distinct budget and execution timing begins after successful release. Fast leader exit, detached descendants, PID churn/reuse, pidfd exhaustion, lost-wakeup ordering, never-stops, stop-then-dies, and guardian failure all fail closed without signalling unrelated processes.

Shell-to-guardian and finalizer launches use the same stopped-child admission law. HUP/INT/TERM transition atomically into one finalization handler. Finalization is bounded and mode-aware, records any cleanup uncertainty, and never promotes Inconclusive or InternalFault. Bundle decisions are durable and write-once; validate-bundle is side-effect-free, while a separate explicitly named adoption operation may recover a valid pre-marker decision using exclusive marker publication, fsync through the artifact-root ancestor chain, and full revalidation. Concurrent adoption is idempotent and cancellation cannot be adopted as pass.

The evidence envelope starts once a fresh artifact directory is atomically accepted. Inventory, vendor binding, governed-root hashing, host/argv capture, source snapshotting, compiler-environment sealing, and initial event creation all run under the terminal/finalizer state machine. The compiler lane resolves SUITE.lock before repository-controlled compilation, invokes the exact rustup channel, isolates Cargo home and target directories without deleting user caches, and excludes or rejects rustflags, encoded rustflags, rustc/wrapper overrides, alternate toolchain selection, target-specific rustflags, and repository/ancestor/Cargo-home configuration. Evidence records the effective compiler path/version/commit and admitted environment.
acceptance_criteria:
All cases have deterministic unit tests and real CLI E2E scripts with detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON logging. The built-in self-test and a separate source-stable external matrix run at least 100 iterations each for immediate exit 0, registered semantic nonzero, unexpected nonzero, and signal termination with zero false internal faults, zero hangs, exact classifications, and zero survivors. Named tests cover delayed-stop/early-CONT ordering, never-stops, stop-then-dies, detached descendants, PID reuse/churn, pidfd exhaustion, guardian failure, setup timeout versus execution timeout, output/resource budgets, and cancellation storms.

Deterministic HUP/INT/TERM injection at spawn, readiness, running, terminal publication, finalizer entry, decision write, marker link, and post-fsync yields exactly one schema-valid terminal event and durable bundle matching the linearization point. Relative and absolute publication paths terminate identically; escaping/reused paths fail before mutation; validate never creates files; adoption is crash-recoverable and concurrently idempotent. Faults at every step from artifact-directory creation through first stage readiness, including mutation during initial hashing, still finalize a typed durable partial bundle.

A hostile compiler-environment matrix injects cap-lints allow via ordinary/encoded/target rustflags, fake rustc and workspace wrappers, alternate toolchain, and repository/ancestor/Cargo-home config. Each is rejected before repository compilation or proven absent from the effective compiler argv, no hostile marker executes, and a clean recovery follows. Finally, one stable-source scripts/check.sh run plus both real no-mock structural E2Es pass, their committed bundles validate independently, logs are bounded and secret-redacted, stderr is retained, exact argv/cwd/hashes/timings/resources/exit/signal/cleanup/final state are present, and all named artifacts are recorded in bead close evidence.
notes:
This bead exists to remove the operational dependency cycle between fln-8mj and franken_lean-rur. It may close only the reusable evidence-runner/bootstrap substrate. It does not close or narrow RUR, which remains responsible for the verification-manifest schema, coverage registration across every implementation bead, archive/retention policy, permanent CI integration, and the expanding Tribunal/E2E scenario system.

MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead own Tests and Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane with real compiler, processes, filesystems, artifacts, and wrappers. A mock may support a unit test but may not close a gate or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, and gate ids; exact argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected versus actual; monotonic start/end/duration; setup/execution/cancellation/cleanup/finalization budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; first-divergence data; publication linearization point; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI and closeout: archive logs, fixtures, stress results, hostile-environment results, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, the bead-specific unit/property/mutation/fault/stress suites, both shared no-mock structural E2E scenarios, and independent validation of every named closing bundle. Close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

comments.text: REGRESSION FOUND AND FIXED (pre-existing, post-24caee7, previously undetected because no one ran the full gate after the pause): the evidence self-test PASSED standalone but FAILED under the gate's own supervised stage — 'exact process-group cleanup left its descendant unreaped' (bound_group_stale_identity), then 'finalizer probe left its bound lifetime unreaped' (shell_finalizer_* probes). Root cause: those cases relied on init auto-reaping orphaned probe descendants; under nesting, the runner's supervisor above the self-test is a child subreaper, adopts the zombies, and never promptly reaps them — the cases' reap_adopted_children polls can never observe the exit. Fix: cmd_self_test now enables child-subreaper on ITSELF at entry (it owns every probe tree it spawns), making orphan adoption + reaping deterministic on every topology — standalone, nested stage, and CI. Verified: nested run (evidence.py run supervising evidence.py self-test — the exact gate stage-1 shape) now passes 40/40 with wrapper exit 0; standalone still 40/40. Full sealed check.sh gate attempt 2 now running end-to-end.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: MILESTONE (commit a3baa4a): the full sealed check.sh gate is GREEN end-to-end — the first green full gate since the 24caee7 stopped-exec rework. Committed bundle: target/check/check-20260724T074913Z-3627827 (evidence-self-test 40 cases nested, shellcheck, sealed fmt/check/clippy/test/structure-guard on a cold isolated build with per-stage pinned-identity proof, vendor-tree, ubs --ci clean). Work items now COMPLETE: #1 stress campaign (407 envelopes), #2 sealed-toolchain lane, #3 hostile-env matrix, #9 post-fix full-gate close evidence, plus the nested-supervision determinism regression fix. REMAINING before close (per comment 254 + the gap matrix): #4 full HUP/INT/TERM boundary injection campaign (hooks exist; spawn_bind + several probes committed; complete matrix pending), #5 early-envelope fault matrix incl. mutation-during-initial-hashing, #6 named stress gaps (pidfd exhaustion, PID reuse/churn, cancellation storms), #7 deliberate consumer scenarios for unexpected-failure/source-drift/internal-fault, #8 validate/adoption naming decision. NOTE for fln-8mj: the sealed compiler-environment lane you block on is landed and gate-proven as of a3baa4a — your Design REVIEW AMENDMENT requirements (exact locked cargo/rustc, all ambient config channels rejected, isolated homes/targets, effective compiler identity + admitted env as evidence facts) are all live in check.sh stages.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: WORK ITEM #4 IN PROGRESS (signal-boundary injection campaign) — implementation landed, validation in flight. The committed matrix now covers every acceptance boundary with all three watched signals: runner-side self-test cases boundary_spawn_{sighup,sigint,sigterm} (signal queued against the blocked guardian launch gate, admission window widened via --test-before-stop-delay-ms; cancellation wins before admission, readiness typed setup_cancelled, target never executes), boundary_readiness_* (signal in the widened readiness-to-release window; child admitted but never released), boundary_running_* (signal after the released target published its PID; tree proven dead), plus the pre-existing terminal_commit_* 3/3. Shell-side: run_shell_finalizer_probe generalized and the matrix extended to spawn_bind x3, active_wait x3, NEW decision_write x3 (checkpoint in check.sh on_exit immediately before complete-bundle: signal claims the empty bundle.decision, typed CANCELLED exit, no marker), NEW marker_link x3 (new --test-marker-pause-ready/-release hooks on complete-bundle hold the decision-to-marker window open inside write_signal_committed_atomic_new; the signal must LOSE against the linked decision and the committed bundle survives + validates), post_decision x3, helper_failure (cleanup-uncertainty dimension) retained. FOUND ALONG THE WAY: a launcher-disposition determinism hole — a detached launcher (nohup/shell bg) hands the self-test SIG_IGN for HUP/INT; probe shells inherit it and POSIX forbids non-interactive bash from trapping ignored-at-entry signals, so HUP/INT finalizer probes hang (proven live via /proc SigIgn=0x7 on the spinning probe shell; the prior gate lane was immune because the supervised-stage exec chain resets dispositions). Fix: cmd_self_test now pins HUP/INT/TERM to SIG_DFL at entry — same domain-ownership rationale as its child-subreaper line. Full standalone self-test rerunning now (launched deliberately under nohup as the regression shape).

title: W3: fln check-olean — the Independent Judge CLI and the G1 evidence bundle
description:
Ship fln check-olean — the Independent Judge (§8.6, G1): the standalone auditor that re-checks an entire olean set (all of mathlib in minutes) on a kernel that shares no code, no compiler, and no bignum with the thing it is checking — the program FIRST public artifact, shipped before FrankenLean has elaborated a single file.

Composition: Grimoire read (full-recheck validation level) + Crucible K1/K2 + fln-checker per consensus policy + receipts + optional certificate emission. CLI contract: stable exit codes, --json output (versioned schema), per-declaration verdicts with stable error classes, a summary receipt binding the input set hash, engine build hashes, policy, and totals; --receipts writes the receipt set + transparency-log append. Performance: PG-1 — full mathlib olean set <= 5 min on the 32-core profile, <= 25 min single-thread.

This bead includes the G1 gate evidence assembly: zero acceptance divergence against the Reference toolchain across the full mathlib olean set at the pin, every divergence (if any) triaged under the §8.2 oracle-or-spec-investigation-first law. Acceptance: G1 declared with the evidence bundle (run logs, receipts, Parity Ledger rows, PG-1 numbers on the baseline machine). Tests: CLI transcript fixtures (exit codes, --json schema); interrupted-run drills (partial output never masquerades as complete); determinism — two runs produce identical receipts.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
fln check-olean is a standalone air-gap-capable auditor composed of full-recheck Grimoire decode, K1/K2, fln-checker consensus, receipts, optional certificates, and transparency append; it never invokes the Reference or another Lean implementation at runtime. Human and versioned robot interfaces have stable documented exit codes and per-declaration records with input/declaration/root hashes, verdict/error/resource class, engines/builds/fuel/policy, trace/evidence links, and explicit complete/incomplete run status. The summary receipt binds the exact manifest-complete input-set hash, epoch, modes, policy, engine builds, totals and result; --receipts atomically writes a complete set and log entry. Interrupted, cancelled, resource-exhausted, disk-full, corrupted, or disagreement runs cannot masquerade as complete, publish partial authority, or collapse Inconclusive. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover CLI parsing/rendering, schema/exit mapping, manifest ordering, receipt/certificate tamper, consensus outcomes, partial output, retry/idempotence, budgets and recovery; named skipped-file, false-summary, forged-receipt, disagreement-outvote, and interrupted-success mutants are killed. Real no-mock G1 E2E rechecks every pinned mathlib olean with zero acceptance divergence, runs 1/8/32-thread determinism, plants corruption/disagreement/interruption, restores, and retains strict per-row/summary/receipt/transparency/final-state bundles. PG-1 is no more than 5 minutes on the 32-core profile and 25 minutes single-thread with raw host/cache/sample evidence. All dependencies, G1 assembly, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, kernel/codec/differential/mutation/fault/platform/performance lanes pass. No sampled set presented as all mathlib, aggregate-only verdict, partial run presented complete, runtime Reference fallback, mock-only corpus, unstable robot schema, waived divergence, or deferred listed CLI/evidence feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — robot stdout is versioned, line-oriented, data-only, and terminates every invocation with an explicit complete or incomplete terminal record. Human progress, diagnostics, and decoration go to stderr without corrupting robot output. Cancellation, resource refusal, crash, malformed artifact, and partial corpus traversal emit bounded typed terminal records and nonzero documented exits; parser/model/E2E tests prove pipe safety and recovery.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative output is strict schema-versioned canonical semantic NDJSON containing normalized inputs, roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, wall time, temporary paths, cache/performance facts, and scheduler details live only in a separately linked bounded telemetry stream and cannot enter authoritative digests. Validators reject merged streams, unknown versions, extra or missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, or telemetry-root drift. Productive 1/8/32 runs prove nonempty completed work and deterministic reduction rather than thread-count labels.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W7: Anvil & Verdict — simp core, rewrite indexes, owned SAT, grind
description:
Workstream W7 "Anvil & Verdict" (§12): rewrite and decision engines under the single constitutional rule FL-INV-06 — every engine is untrusted; every output enters the environment only through a kernel-checked artifact. That rule buys unlimited freedom below it.

Scope: simp faithful core (simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree retrieval, congruence interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors) implemented natively end-to-end behind obs-class Mirror facade rows, fidelity-tested against Corpus simp-heavy files + instrumented-oracle rewrite traces; compiled per-library rewrite indexes shipped as content-addressed Ledger artifacts (staleness impossible by construction); norm_num/omega native cores (fln-bignum-backed, certificate-producing, kernel-replayed); Verdict — owned CDCL SAT (watched literals, VSIDS/CHB, modest honest inprocessing) with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker so bv_decide sheds the external CaDiCaL TCB (PG-9: 100% agreement, wall <= 2x Reference at G5, ratcheting); grind reimplemented natively (congruence closure, e-matching, theory hooks) entering at L1/L2 and ratcheting to L3 on the grind corpus by G4 — its semantics still move upstream, the census pins the coupling per epoch; e-graph saturation lane (frontier; honest scoping — conditional rewrites and binder-crossing rules restrict the eligible subset; completeness fallback to the faithful core; PG-A measured, not assumed); speculative tactic portfolios (frontier). New lanes promote only by shadow-run matrix protocol (§12.6/§18.9) with auto-demotion on breach.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — full W7 closure includes franken_lean-engine-backed-tactic-integration-c90. Engine implementations are not user-compatible merely because direct APIs pass; they execute through the production Mirror facade and Golem-hosted tactic path, preserve TacticM state and InfoTree behavior, and kernel-check every result. Ledger publication of compiled rewrite indexes remains the W8 integration owned by fln-publish-rewrite-indexes-gca.

Stage topology: G4 consumes the production engine-backed integration needed by full mathlib, including faithful simp, certificate-backed arithmetic/Verdict, and grind at its required L-level. The full W7 epic additionally retains frontier e-graph and tactic-portfolio shadow promotion and therefore blocks G6 rather than delaying G4. No frontier feature is dropped, and no G4 core engine claim is deferred to G6.
acceptance_criteria:
W7 closes only when every Anvil/Verdict engine remains untrusted and no result enters an environment without a complete kernel-checked artifact under FL-INV-06. Faithful simp matches the pinned simp-set, dsimp/simp-only, discrimination retrieval, congruence, firing order, proof term, diagnostic, and trace behavior on the full registered corpus. Versioned compiled rewrite indexes are deterministic, content-addressed, root-bound, stale-proof, published through the owning W8 integration, and fall back to faithful recomputation. norm_num and omega emit compact kernel-replayable certificates over owned arithmetic. Verdict provides owned CDCL, proof logging, and an independently implemented checker; bv_decide has 100 percent corpus agreement and PG-9 evidence. Native grind reaches the required L3 rows by G4 with epoch-pinned semantics. Frontier e-graph saturation and tactic portfolios implement the full scoped feature set, including conditional/binder eligibility and faithful completeness fallback, and promote only through versioned shadow-run matrices with automatic demotion on breach. Unit/property/model/differential/trace suites cover simp rules, indexes, certificate versions/checkers, SAT state, grind theory hooks, e-graph extraction, portfolio arbitration, cancellation and budgets; named mutants skip proof checking, accept stale indexes, alter firing/tie-break order, corrupt SAT proof, overclaim grind rows, cross an ineligible binder, or bypass demotion, and each is killed. Real no-mock Golem/Mirror E2E executes production tactics on mathlib files, plants stale/corrupt/wrong-proof/engine divergences, restores exact inputs, and proves recovery with strict bundles and per-row evidence levels. G4 core integration plus full frontier obligations, all W7 children, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, Tribunal/Mirror/mutation/fuzz/fault/thread/performance lanes are green before W7 closes and feeds G6. No external solver, unchecked certificate, direct-API-only tactic proof, stale cache acceptance, frontier leakage into faithful/sound artifacts, timing-only claim, mock-only Golem, or deferred listed W7 feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — terminal G4 closure names exact engine, façade, certificate, local-index publication, child, Parity, mutation, benchmark, and evidence roots. Shipped local rewrite indexes require the W8 local publication bead; otherwise W7 closure must state builder/reader-only. G4 evidence depends on cgt and cannot be inferred from component prose or unpromoted rows.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Unknown schema, missing/duplicate ids, broken order/linkage, unbound artifacts, truncation, merged streams, or cleanup/final-state mismatch fail. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W1: UPSTREAM_LEDGER + suite upgrade protocol — deliberate, Tribunal-diffed lock bumps
description:
## Description

Own UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md and the deliberate Tribunal-diffed suite upgrade protocol (§2.11, R16). Seed and preserve every planned foundation capability: asupersync deterministic ordered merge if its reviewed primitives are insufficient; franken_networkx incremental dominator maintenance; frankensearch transaction-aligned incremental commit hooks; franken_markdown Lean lexer profile; frankensqlite CAS blob access patterns; and in-repo fln-bignum with the second-suite-consumer revisit trigger. Clean-room design-level reuse records provenance/license facts without copying an unapproved implementation into the runtime closure.

Every ledger row follows a closed state machine: proposed, investigated, upstream-requested, accepted-upstream, released, pinned-in-SUITE.lock, acceptance-evidenced, load-bearing, rejected, or superseded. A prose row, open request, merged-but-unreleased change, or available-but-ungated version cannot satisfy a consumer. Cross-repository work names the exact external issue/commit/release and remains non-authoritative until the pinned closure, canonical contract/census diff, and local Tribunal acceptance evidence agree.

The upgrade protocol applies to additions, removals, upgrades, downgrades, retargeting, nightly/target-feature/profile changes, Reference/Corpus advances, and path/commit/tree/checksum changes. Validation occurs against an isolated candidate. The current lock remains authoritative until closure/allowlist delta, contract and census regeneration/diff, Tribunal comparison, component migration, rollback proof, and unchanged-root revalidation complete. Cancellation, external unavailability, source drift, or incomplete evidence leaves the old lock untouched and the candidate non-authoritative.

A waiver may only defer or block a candidate with owner, exact scope, rationale, expiry, and review date. It may never authorize D1/D3/D8 violation, unknown dependency, load-bearing unknown, incomplete evidence, or a release claim. No prose-only intent, emergency label, or manual bypass promotes a component.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites upstream_ledger_state_machine, suite_upgrade_candidate_model, lock_delta_contract_join, waiver_authority_model, and suite_upgrade_no_mock_e2e cover every state/transition, component kind, lock change class, candidate stage, closure/contract/Tribunal evidence join, rollback, cancellation/fault/retry, external-evidence identity, and waiver boundary. UPSTREAM_LEDGER includes every seed entry, owner/repository/link, rationale, exact status, acceptance gate, consumer, fallback, and revisit trigger. Mutations for premature state promotion, current-lock modification before validation, hidden transitive dependency, stale/mixed census or Tribunal roots, unchanged-label skipping validation, missing rollback, expired/nonexpiring waiver acceptance, constitutional-violation waiver, and prose-only load-bearing promotion are killed. The real no-mock E2E creates an isolated candidate change, demonstrates its complete failure list, attaches valid closure/contract/Tribunal/migration/rollback evidence and passes, plants a hidden dependency and stale root and fails, restores, and proves the original authoritative lock root unchanged. Strict NDJSON records run/change/component/ledger-transition ids, exact old/new lock and closure roots, pins and deltas, external evidence identity, contract/census/Tribunal roots, expected/actual stage, rollback outcome, publication authority, cleanup, and final current-lock root. No candidate or ledger row closes on mocks, prose, partial evidence, or a waiver of constitutional/load-bearing obligations.
acceptance_criteria: Every required foundation capability is represented by a closed-state UPSTREAM_LEDGER row with exact external provenance, owner, gate, consumer, fallback, and revisit trigger. Every class of SUITE.lock change validates in an isolated candidate against complete closure, canonical contract/census diff, Tribunal evidence, migration, rollback, and unchanged-root checks before atomic publication. All named model/mutation/no-mock E2E and strict logging/recovery requirements pass. Waivers may defer/block only; they never authorize constitutional violations, unknowns, incomplete load-bearing components, or claims.

Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W10: native_decide Tier 2 — trace-guided kernel replay discharges ofReduceBool
description:
Implement native_decide Tier 2 — trace-guided kernel replay (§11.6): on certified paths, ofReduceBool is DISCHARGED, not trusted — the Tier 1 replay receipt (function-entry digests over the registered reflection set, branch decisions, literal-arithmetic transcripts) is replayed through Crucible as the DEFAULT for certified profiles, so the compiler and VM leave the trusted base for every evaluation the tier covers.

Scope and honesty rules: the tier holds only where the receipt covers the full evaluation (the registered reflection set; evaluations escaping it remain Tier 1 and are marked so); the trust ledger records per build which tier was achieved, because "we no longer trust the compiler" is a claim with a scope; the cost multiple of replay vs native evaluation is measured and published (an OBSERVED number, per §20.4 — never asserted); Tier 3 (a verified evaluator proven against the Judgment Specification) remains explicitly research and is NOT this bead. Certified --reproducible builds enable Tier 2 by default; standard builds keep Tier 1 sampling policy.

Acceptance: the reflection corpus discharges through kernel replay on certified profiles with zero trust extension to Golem/Iron for covered evaluations; the trust ledger and fln audit --tcb reflect the tier per declaration; the published cost-multiple table lands in the evidence bundle for G6. Tests: e2e certified-build test where a native_decide theorem verifies with the compiler deliberately perturbed (a wrong compiled result MUST be caught by replay — the whole point, proven by mutation); receipt-coverage boundary fixtures (an evaluation outside the registered set must degrade to Tier 1 with the degradation logged and marked, never silently claimed as Tier 2); replay determinism across the certified matrix.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. In certified profiles, Tier 2 is the default for every native_decide evaluation wholly covered by the registered reflection set: the complete Tier 1 receipt is deterministically replayed through Crucible and ofReduceBool is discharged by a kernel-checked artifact, removing Golem/Iron from the trusted base for that evaluation.
2. Coverage is computed per evaluation and provenance-bound. Any escape, unknown operation, incomplete/tampered receipt, replay budget exhaustion, or unsupported version degrades explicitly to Tier 1 with a typed reason and trust-ledger mark; it can never silently inherit Tier 2. Standard builds retain the Tier 1 sampling policy and Tier 3 remains out of scope.
3. fln audit --tcb and the build/declaration trust ledger report achieved tier, covered operations, receipt/proof roots, fallback reason, and evidence. Covered compiler perturbations that change native results are caught by replay before theorem admission.
4. Named unit/property/differential/mutation suites cover full/partial reflection coverage, replay semantics, receipt binding, certified/default policy, explicit degradation, audit aggregation, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and bit-deterministic replay across the certified matrix. Wrong-compiled-result, missing-coverage-edge, silent-Tier1, skip-certified-replay, and false-audit-tier mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E certified build runs real native_decide corpus theorems, deliberately perturbs a compiled result, proves replay rejection, verifies covered theorems with no compiler trust extension, exercises an out-of-set evaluation and its logged Tier 1 degradation, then rebuilds cleanly across certified platforms. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/build/declaration/evaluation/receipt/replay/proof/tier ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical term/environment/reflection/receipt/proof roots, coverage and expected/actual verdicts, cost/timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. Reproducible evidence publishes replay-to-native cost multiples as observed distributions, not assertions, and archives cross-platform matrices, perturbation mutants, trust ledgers, receipts/proofs, and E2E bundles. Close wording is scoped per covered evaluation and never claims Tier 3 or universal compiler independence.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — unsupported operations may be preclassified Tier 1. Tampered receipts, cancellation, replay-budget exhaustion, malformed artifacts, or incomplete verification are failures or Inconclusive, not automatic successful Tier-1 downgrades. Any G6 cell requiring Tier 2 remains blocked until real Tier-2 evidence passes. Tier transitions are atomic, root-bound, independently validated, and never inferred from absence of a crash.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.

title: W7: Frontier lanes — e-graph saturation + tactic portfolios, shadow-run promotion
description:
Implement the frontier rewrite lanes (§12.3, §12.7): the e-graph equality-saturation lane over simp-set subsets with proof extraction (rewrites recorded as justifications, extracted proofs replayed through the ordinary lemma-application path, kernel-checked like everything else — honestly scoped: conditional rewrites and binder-crossing rules restrict the eligible subset; the lane is an accelerator with a completeness fallback to the faithful core, never a replacement; its win condition is MEASURED via the PG-A profile, not assumed), and speculative tactic portfolios (first-with-work-stealing, deterministic winner selection by registered CGSE policy, losers cancelled through their asupersync regions; snapshot-cheap try trees — the search-side dividend of O(1) environment snapshots, exposed as tactics and through Envoy).

Both lanes promote ONLY by shadow-run (§12.6/§18.9): dual-run on the same demand nodes, products diffed, divergence and latency distributions recorded, promotion per workload/epoch/platform cell with deterministic sampling continuing afterward and auto-demotion on any soundness, fidelity, or tail-latency breach — promotion is a matrix, never a global flag. Frontier features never leak into faithful/sound artifacts (D18).

Acceptance: e-graph lane produces kernel-checked results agreeing with the faithful core on its eligible subset, with the measured win condition published; portfolio winner selection deterministic under the lab at any thread count; shadow-run promotion machinery generic enough for the other promotion consumers (K2 optimizations, Iron tiers, epoch bridges). Tests: extraction-proof replay fixtures; eligibility-boundary fixtures (ineligible rewrites must route to the faithful core); cancellation-correctness under portfolio racing (obligation-leak oracle clean); auto-demotion drill (planted breach must demote and file the incident bundle).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this bead owns OQ-5. Before promotion, record the exact eligible e-graph rewrite subset, exclusion semantics for conditional and binder-crossing rules, the pinned PG-A workload/profile, minimum win and tail-regression thresholds, proof-extraction cost limits, fallback behavior, and the decision-log amendment. Shadow-run data is evidence, not a substitute for the kernel-checked extraction law.
acceptance_criteria:
1. OQ-5 is resolved by a versioned reviewed row-per-rewrite-class decision table before e-graph promotion: it enumerates the eligible simp subset, conditional and binder-crossing boundaries, proof-construction rule, fallback, resource envelope, corpus, and measured win condition with latency/tail and proof-cost thresholds. Unknown or unmeasured classes remain ineligible. The frontier e-graph lane accepts only that explicitly evidenced subset; every equality-saturation step records a justification, extracted proof replays through ordinary lemma application, and the kernel checks the result before admission. Failure or budget exhaustion falls back without changing semantics. 2. Speculative tactic portfolios fork O(1) snapshots, execute in separate asupersync regions, cancel losers without obligation leaks, and select a winner through a registered deterministic CGSE policy whose result is schedule-independent at threads 1, 8, and 32. 3. Both lanes use a generic shadow-run promotion matrix keyed by workload, epoch, platform, mode/profile, and evidence version. Promotion requires semantic equality plus the OQ-5 measured latency/tail/proof-cost criteria, continues deterministic sampling, auto-demotes and files an incident bundle on any soundness/fidelity/tail breach, and cannot leak frontier products into faithful/sound artifacts. 4. Named unit/property/model/DPOR/differential/mutation suites cover e-graph congruence/rebuild/extraction, every OQ-5 eligibility boundary, proof replay/fallback, saturation limits, portfolio cancellation/winner ties, schedule perturbations, matrix transitions, stale evidence, and auto-demotion. Unchecked-extraction, ineligible-rewrite, nondeterministic-winner, leaked-obligation, false-win, and failed-demotion mutants must be killed. 5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs real eligible/ineligible Lean goals through faithful and frontier lanes, kernel-checks extracted proofs, races a real tactic portfolio under schedule perturbations, gathers shadow distributions, promotes one qualified cell, proves a below-threshold cell stays unpromoted, plants a semantic/tail breach, verifies immediate demotion and incident creation, and confirms faithful artifacts remain unchanged. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/goal/egraph/rewrite/proof/portfolio/candidate/promotion-cell/incident ids, bead/claim/invariant/Parity/OQ-5 rows, redacted argv/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical input/result/proof/artifact roots, winner policy and shadow diffs, latency/resources/status, cleanup, and final state. 6. CI archives the reviewed OQ-5 decision, proof traces, schedule/crashpacks, shadow/promotion matrices, incident bundles, mutant outcomes, PG-A measurements, and evidence manifests. Any speed win is reported with corpus/machine/distribution; no global promotion or frontier-to-faithful leakage is permitted.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — consume a generic W1 shadow-run promotion protocol rather than owning one inside W7. E-graph and tactic-portfolio lanes remain distinct before this terminal integration. E-graph Inconclusive may run the faithful fallback but remains distinct evidence; if every portfolio candidate is non-authoritative there is no winner. Promotion/demotion is atomic and evidence-rooted. Named suites: egraph_proof_extraction_model, portfolio_deterministic_winner_dpor, frontier_leak_guard, shadow_demotion_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Unknown schema, missing/duplicate ids, broken order/linkage, unbound artifacts, truncation, merged streams, or cleanup/final-state mismatch fail. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W11 integration: Terminal InfoView + WASM Judge completion join
description:
Completion and release-integration join for the two plan §16.4 products. franken_lean-terminal-infoview-client-9q6 owns fln goals, Lantern RPC parity, terminal rendering, OQ-7 widget behavior, and the real PTY/tmux failure-recovery lane. fln-wasm-judge-browser-kvj owns the integrity-bound offline stock-browser Judge, sealed-capsule verification, native/WASM vectors, accessibility, adversarial browser matrix, and OQ-12 package implementation. Their different code, dependencies, and evidence remain separate.

This join owns only cross-product release coherence: both products are installed and discoverable under the same epoch/release manifest; fln identity/help/docs report their independent versions, support tiers, evidence links, and capability requirements honestly; shared receipt/capsule/snapshot vocabulary does not drift; robot schemas use the common evidence envelope without conflating browser verdicts with live proof-state grades; packaging or failure of either product cannot silently masquerade as success for the other. The front-door bead may integrate fln goals after the terminal child closes and does not wait for browser implementation, while W11 and G6 require this join and therefore both products.

Verification cross-checks release manifests, CLI discovery, schema/version links, support-tier reporting, offline assets, and independent failure isolation. A combined no-mock release smoke runs the installed terminal client against a real daemon and the installed browser package against a real sealed capsule, then exercises one product failing while the other remains correctly reported. It archives concise human logs and bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with both child evidence ids, release/epoch/build hashes, expected versus actual support state, commands/assets, first divergence, cleanup, and final state.

## Acceptance Criteria

Close only when both child beads are closed without narrowed scope and the integrated release exposes both products with correct independent identities, support tiers, schemas, evidence, failure isolation, and combined no-mock release smoke artifacts. The terminal client cannot stand in for the browser Judge; a core-only WASM artifact cannot stand in for the stock-browser package; browser delay cannot block unrelated front-door work; and no child test, negative/recovery lane, logging field, or user-visible feature may be dropped at this join.
design: Decomposition law: terminal RPC/TUI and offline WASM/browser implementation are parallel children because they share neither runtime nor primary user journey. This bead is a join only. franken_lean-eb3 consumes the terminal child directly; the W11 epic consumes this join and therefore retains the complete browser requirement. OQ-7 remains owned by franken_lean-v2p and consumed by the terminal child; OQ-12 remains owned by the packaging contract and consumed by the browser child.
acceptance_criteria:
Both dedicated children are closed with their full unit/property/model/mutation, real no-mock E2E, negative/recovery, resource, determinism, accessibility, and detailed logging contracts. The release manifest and installed surfaces expose terminal and browser products independently with correct epoch/version/support/evidence links and failure isolation. The combined release smoke proves both real paths and one-product-fails behavior. No child substitution, hidden coupling, narrowed scope, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — own pre-release manifest fragments, schema vocabulary, product metadata, and coherence validation only. Final signed/installed release-manifest integration is W12 authority. Fragments bind reproducible semantic/payload roots while signatures, notarization, timestamps, transparency and operational telemetry remain separate envelopes. Consumer absence cannot block fragment production and fragments cannot claim an installed release.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.

1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W5/W10: Iron target/profile contract spike — resolve OQ-4 before FIR freezes
description:
Resolve plan OQ-4 before the FIR pipeline or Iron implementation freezes an expensive backend boundary. This is a contract and feasibility spike, not a substitute for fln-y55 or fln-o0j. It must determine: the supported-target tier beyond x86-64 and aarch64; whether static emission and Iron-JIT share one backend with certified/frontier profiles or require separated implementations; the target-independent FIR/FLBC-to-machine contract; relocation, calling-convention, register-allocation, executable-memory, unwind, and object/executable ownership boundaries; how target features enter cache keys and receipts; and how faithful, sound, frontier, and certified artifacts prevent JIT or host-specific behavior from leaking across modes.

The decision must preserve the closed dependency universe and sovereign-backend goal: no LLVM, Cranelift, system assembler/linker, or hidden C compiler on the Iron path. The optional C backend remains only the D2 parity/exotic-target lane. Use a thin real vertical prototype and measurements to price the alternatives before selecting one; do not ratify from prose alone.

## Acceptance Criteria

A versioned backend contract and decision-log row resolve every OQ-4 branch with evidence, rejected alternatives, migration consequences, safe fallback, and explicit target support tiers. Representative real lowering probes cover closures, calls/tail calls, allocation/RC boundaries, exceptions/effects, literals/bignum calls, relocations, debug/source mapping, and target-feature selection. The selected boundary is consumed by both fln-y55 and fln-o0j; neither may invent a second incompatible machine interface.

Named deterministic unit/property/mutation tests cover IR validation, calling-convention/relocation rules, unsupported features, malformed machine inputs, resource limits, and byte-stability. A real no-mock E2E probe emits and executes supported-host code and validates a non-host target artifact structurally; negative/recovery scenarios prove unsupported targets, unavailable executable memory, invalid relocations, and interrupted publication fail typed and leave no trusted artifact. Human logs and schema-versioned bounded NDJSON record exact inputs, target/profile/features, toolchain/host facts, byte hashes, timings/resources, expected versus actual, first divergence, exit/signal, and artifact references. All prototype claims are labeled at their honest evidence level; no benchmark or target-support claim is promoted from a mock or unmeasured design.
acceptance_criteria:
OQ-4 is resolved before FIR or Iron interfaces freeze by a versioned reviewed backend contract and decision-log row covering support tiers beyond x86-64/aarch64, shared-versus-separated static/JIT architecture, the target-independent FIR/FLBC machine contract, calling conventions, register allocation, relocations, object/executable ownership, executable memory, unwind/debug mapping, target-feature cache/receipt identity, and faithful/sound/frontier/certified isolation. Rejected alternatives, migration consequences, safe fallback, and owner are explicit. A thin real vertical prototype lowers and executes closures, direct/tail calls, allocation/RC, exceptions/effects, literals/bignum calls, feature variants, and relocations on a supported host and structurally validates a non-host artifact without LLVM, Cranelift, system assembler/linker, or hidden cc; the C lane remains D2-only. fln-y55 and fln-o0j consume the identical frozen boundary. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover IR validation, ABI/relocation rules, deterministic bytes, unsupported/malformed features, budgets, cancellation, executable-memory denial, and publication atomicity; named host-leak, profile-leak, wrong-relocation, nondeterministic-layout, and hidden-tool mutants are killed. The real no-mock E2E includes positive host execution, non-host structural proof, unsupported target, invalid relocation, unavailable executable memory, interruption, exact restoration, and recovery with strict evidence containing target/profile/features/toolchain/host/input/output hashes and resources. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, prototype differential/mutation/fault lanes pass. No prose-only decision, mock benchmark, second machine interface, hidden external backend/tool, unmeasured support claim, JIT leakage into certified artifacts, unresolved branch, or deferred listed contract item satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — canonical FLBC remains target-independent. Any target-specific machine IR or legalization belongs after FLBC in W10 and binds an explicit target/profile/features/runtime contract; it cannot contaminate W5 semantic identity. The contract inventories ELF, Mach-O, and PE constraints, platform-runtime allowlists, target-feature identity, W^X lifecycle, and unsupported-target nonpublication. Cross-target tests compare target-neutral roots, never machine bytes.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical authoritative NDJSON contains normalized semantic inputs, roots, decisions, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, time, paths, cache/performance, and scheduler facts are linked bounded telemetry only. Independent validators reject unknown schema, extra/missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, telemetry in semantic hashes, and final-state mismatch. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves nonempty completed work and canonical reduction.
notes: MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead own acceptance): unit/property/mutation coverage must exercise every contract rule, malformed input, boundary, resource, determinism, cancellation, and failure-atomicity case. At least one real no-mock host execution plus one structurally validated non-host artifact and a negative/recovery scenario are required. Emit concise human logs and bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step, bead/claim/invariant/gate, exact redacted argv and cwd, epoch/mode/profile/target/features/toolchain/host, input and artifact hashes, expected versus actual, monotonic timings, resources/budgets, exit/signal, stdout/stderr references, first divergence, publication and cleanup final state. Archive the decision, rejected alternatives, fixtures, raw measurements, logs, and reproducible commands; skips are typed limitations and cannot satisfy the skipped claim.

comments.text: Created during plan-space audit because OQ-4 was previously resolved only inside fln-o0j, after fln-y55 could freeze a backend-hostile FIR boundary. fln-y55 now depends on this evidence-backed decision; fln-o0j consumes the same contract transitively.

Target tiers, shared-versus-separated profiles, cache/receipt inputs, mode isolation, real host/non-host probes, negative recovery, and detailed logs are explicit. No wording change needed.

Rejected bv suggestion that this decision depend on the full Golem runtime integration: that would resolve the interface only after its consumer implementation and recreate the freeze-too-early problem.

title: G0: The Laws of the Machine — pre-construction spike gate (§22.1)
description:
## Description

Gate G0, The Laws of the Machine (§22.1), is the pre-construction decision gate: ten compile-tested spikes retire the load-bearing unknowns before W2-W12 interfaces freeze. The ten exact questions cover ABI/OLEAN resurrection, K1 verdict methodology, Golem ABI-valued execution, Vellum syntax/hygiene fidelity, exact olean re-emission, fuel parity, the defeq tail/K2 design, Native Mirror partition viability, instrumented-oracle traces, and authoritative dependency closure/SUITE.lock.

Each child emits exactly one independently validated G0SpikeDecisionV1. G0DecisionSetV1 joins exactly those ten rows; it is not a green-count dashboard. Outcome is Ratified, Amended, or NoGo, while evidence authority separately records Complete, Inconclusive, or InternalFault. Only a Complete Ratified row, or a Complete Amended row after the exact §25 plan/contracts/dependencies/downstream acceptance changes and full rerun, can satisfy a spike. NoGo, missing/stale evidence, unresolved divergence, resource/cancellation refusal, failed run, or InternalFault blocks.

OQ-1 and OQ-2 must be resolved at G0 by G0-5 and G0-6 respectively; assigning either downstream does not retire its load-bearing unknown. G0-8 must resolve OQ-3’s feasibility boundary, exact Native-Mirror partition law, unsupported-row treatment, and ordered closure plan. W6 may own implementation/promotion of already classified rows but cannot inherit an unpriced partition unknown.

The gate requires a single shared SUITE.lock/Reference/Corpus epoch, canonical ABI/OLEAN/census roots, symbol/rule namespaces, oracle classifications, comparison classes, claim scopes, fixture manifests, resource-accounting ownership, and interface commitments across all ten rows. It rejects aggregate green, hidden exclusions, mock-only evidence, comparison downgrade, double-counted work, semantic/telemetry mixing, fake thread labels, surviving named mutations, and any interface-freeze receipt issued before valid decisions.

## Success Criteria

All ten child beads complete their full unit/property/model/mutation, real no-mock positive/failure/restoration/recovery, resource-boundary, productive thread where applicable, strict semantic log, linked telemetry, and independent bundle-validation obligations. Each decision row binds exact question/outcome/evidence authority, D7 claim type and evidence state, epoch/corpus/platform/mode scope, fixture/contract/implementation/mutation/E2E roots, oracle/comparison, budgets/usage, limitations, interface effects, and reviewed amendments. Cross-spike model and mutation suites plant missing/duplicate/stale rows, root/namespace/oracle/comparison/claim/resource conflicts, mock substitution, NoGo or Inconclusive promotion, incomplete amendment propagation, hidden OQ deferral, fake parallelism, semantic/telemetry merge, and premature freeze; every case fails with exact first divergence and recovery. The final real gate E2E validates child bundles read-only and atomically publishes only a complete consistent decision set. No owner assignment, benchmark, prototype compile, percentage, unpriced unknown, or deferred OQ satisfies G0.
design:
G0DecisionSetV1 is the sole gate join and contains exactly one G0SpikeDecisionV1 for each §22.1 child. Child evidence stays child-owned. Decision outcome is Ratified, Amended, or NoGo; evidence authority is an orthogonal Complete, Inconclusive, or InternalFault field. Complete Ratified and fully propagated/rerun Complete Amended are the only satisfying states. NoGo and non-authoritative evidence remain useful findings but block.

Amended rows bind exact §25 wording, rationale, blast radius, affected contracts/interfaces, stable downstream owners, dependency edits, migration/rollback where relevant, and all amended acceptance results. The validator reads the actual plan and graph identities; prose assertion cannot satisfy propagation.

OQ-1 is closed only by G0-5’s serialization decision, OQ-2 only by G0-6’s fuel decision, and OQ-3 only by G0-8’s feasibility/partition/unsupported-row/closure-plan decision. None can be deferred as an unresolved unknown. Later workstreams may implement a resolved contract only.

The join validates common epoch and canonical contract/census roots, symbol/rule ids, oracle and comparison classes, claim/evidence scopes, platform/mode/profile, fixture manifests, resource ownership, productive-thread evidence, and interface-freeze guards. Canonical semantic records and nondeterministic telemetry are separate. G0 introduces no runtime prototype authority.
acceptance_criteria: G0 closes on exactly ten unique current independently validated G0SpikeDecisionV1 rows and one consistent G0DecisionSetV1. Only Complete Ratified or fully propagated and rerun Complete Amended rows satisfy; NoGo, Inconclusive, InternalFault, stale/missing/mock/root-mismatched/overclaimed evidence blocks. OQ-1, OQ-2, and OQ-3 are resolved at their assigned G0 spikes, not handed downstream unresolved. The join enforces identical authoritative epochs/contracts/namespaces/oracle/comparison/claim/resource identities, complete child test/mutation/no-mock/logging/recovery evidence, and no premature interface freeze. Named gate model/mutation/E2E tests cover every missing/duplicate/conflicting/overpromoted/amendment/OQ/thread/telemetry fault and exact recovery. No aggregate green, owner assignment, benchmark, compile-only prototype, hidden exclusion, or deferred unknown satisfies G0.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

This is terminology-only; crate names, public Lean-compatible surfaces, scope, tests, dependencies, and acceptance functionality are unchanged. Immutable earlier comments may retain the old word but are not current authority; fln-7gr6 owns the repository-wide migration and collision guard.

title: W9: Lantern & Palimpsest — daemon server, snapshots, causal proof graph
description:
Workstream W9 "Lantern & Palimpsest" (§14, §15): the single-daemon server behind the unchanged wire dialect, and the provenance substrate that makes the system explain itself.

Lantern: LSP + $/lean/* byte-compatible (plainGoal, plainTermGoal, rpc/* sessions, waitForDiagnostics, semantic tokens, widget delivery) — vscode-lean4 connects unmodified; one daemon, one shared immutable import heap (kills the ~60s x N-workers x GBs multiplication; PG-4/PG-6); snapshot machinery: immutable DocumentSnapshots with publication generations (stale-diagnostics race structurally impossible), sub-command incrementality from the finest enclosing checkpoint, cancel-correct under typing bursts, stable-identity diagnostics dedup, idempotent precondition-checked code actions, progressive proof-state negotiation, per-root epoch capsules, session replay bundles (server bugs become deterministic objects); the machine session API — fork a proof state, apply a tactic, read goals, O(1) per branch, thousands of branches per daemon (§14.4, the number that embarrasses the 18-735s per-branch literature).

Palimpsest: always-on ring-buffer traces + opt-in archives (frankensqlite); the causal proof graph (Bet B7) with typed edge families and completeness classes — ONLY Complete/Conservative edges may drive invalidation or trust (the welding law); unification forensics (minimal disagreement), instance why-not trees, semantic blame slices; semantic diff levels + semantic merge (blocks silent semantic conflicts Git cannot see; nothing merges around the kernel); fragility signals (advisory, D3); time travel, fln replay, repro bundles; build forensics as SQL.

Exit: gate G5 — vscode-lean4 smoke matrix green, PG-4/5/6/L met, session API live.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design: Stage topology: G5 consumes the concrete Lantern, snapshot/session, and replay/protocol stack needed for the daily-driver editor claim. Full W9 additionally closes Palimpsest, causal proof graph completeness, forensics, semantic diff/merge, why-trusts, time travel, repro bundles, and SQL observability, and therefore blocks G6. W9 consumes the G4-complete toolchain and Ledger core through its component graph, not W8 federation/distribution bookkeeping. Every W9 feature remains mandatory before the final sovereign release.
acceptance_criteria:
W9 closes only when Lantern and Palimpsest share one immutable snapshot/provenance truth. Lantern is byte-compatible with the pinned LSP and $/lean dialect for initialization, document lifecycle, diagnostics, goals, terms, RPC sessions, semantic tokens, completions, code actions, progress, and widgets; unmodified vscode-lean4 passes. One daemon shares one import heap while DocumentSnapshots, publication generations, regions, stable diagnostic identities, preconditioned idempotent actions, progressive goals, epoch capsules, and cancellation prevent stale or cross-document publication. Sub-command incremental processing equals clean processing. ProofStateSnapshot and the machine session API fork O(1), enforce capability/budget/epoch ownership, support thousands of branches, release deterministically, and never treat provisional state as verified. Replay bundles reproduce protocol failures exactly. Palimpsest records typed causal nodes and edges for declarations, instances, simp, macros, elaboration, effects, and kernel verdicts with explicit completeness classes; only Complete or Conservative evidence drives invalidation or trust. It provides minimal unification disagreement, why-not instance trees, blame slices, semantic diff/merge that cannot bypass the kernel, fragility signals, why-trusts, time travel, repro bundles, and bounded SQL forensics. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation/lab tests cover protocol ordering, generation races, cancellation storms, snapshot/session lifecycle, capability denial, replay, causal completeness, welding law, graph queries, merge conflicts, storage crash/corruption, budgets, and recovery; named stale-publication, missing-edge, overclaimed-completeness, and merge-around-kernel mutants are killed. Real no-mock editor, terminal/session, replay, causal-query, semantic-merge, crash/fault, and long-soak E2E lanes emit strict bundles. G5 daily-driver evidence plus full Palimpsest scope, all W9 children, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, protocol/editor/lab/thread/platform/performance gates are green before G6. No patched editor, per-file duplicated import heap, mock-only RPC, stale diagnostic, incomplete edge used as complete, advisory signal used as authority, silent semantic merge, unbounded trace/query, or deferred listed W9 feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — publish an explicit late-producer handoff matrix. W9 freezes provenance edge schemas, completeness grades, admission rules, and welding laws and proves current producers; a G6-facing downstream join proves real adoption by W11/W12 suggestion, capability-session, branch/merge, migration, and repair producers. Synthetic future edges cannot satisfy production integration, and W9 does not wait on future workstreams.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W5: Intrinsic table — the extern census implemented natively, row-per-symbol evidence
description:
Implement the intrinsic table from the extern census (§11.4, Appendix C): every @[extern] symbol in Init/Std/Lean at the pin — Nat/Int/Array/ByteArray/String/Float ops, IO, Task, ST, environment access, compiler-API entry points — as native Rust intrinsics beneath the broader Mirror facade. The table is GENERATED from the census (mechanical extraction, W1), and the census diff is the first artifact of every epoch advance.

Semantics: Float arithmetic is IEEE-754 binary64 with the Reference printing/parsing conventions; transcendentals route through fln-libm (§6.8) — no platform libm on any path that can reach an artifact or receipt (D21); UInt*/USize follow the ABI width rules; @[implemented_by] swaps honored at the boundary the ecosystem respects. Every row carries fixtures and a Parity-Ledger L-level; unclassified rows do not exist (census totality).

Acceptance: the census fully populated with an implementation status per row (G2 requirement: fully populated with fixtures); differential parity on the intrinsic fixture corpus vs the Reference (bit class where applicable, obs class where documented); missing-intrinsic calls produce typed unsupported errors naming the row, never UB or a guess. Tests: per-row fixture families generated alongside the table; boundary-value sweeps for numeric ops; String UTF-8 position-convention fixtures; Float printing round-trips.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

## Success Criteria

All five exact children close with a bijective pinned W5 extern census, every known row implemented and evidence-bound, effects/ownership/mode/profile typed, Unsupported limited to negative unknown/invalid rows, and real Golem differential/recovery evidence.
design: TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are franken_lean-pw6t generated extern schema/dispatch, franken_lean-m7vm numeric/data families, fln-65t5 IO/Task/ST/effect families, franken_lean-zm78 environment/compiler service families, and fln-52h0 census-totality/differential join. This epic closes only on a machine-validated join of those exact child implementation, fixture, mutation, E2E, and evidence roots; prose-only completion or future-consumer evidence cannot substitute.
acceptance_criteria:
The generated intrinsic table is row-complete over every pinned extern in Init/Std/Lean, including Nat/Int/UInt/USize, arrays/byte arrays, strings, floats, IO, Task, ST, environment and compiler APIs. Each qualified row binds exact universes/telescope/signature, ABI widths, ownership/RC, effects/capabilities, implemented_by behavior, mode/profile support, implementation, fixtures, comparison class, L-level and evidence; missing, duplicate, stale, unknown or unclassified rows fail generation/build. Native implementations match Reference bit behavior where required and explicit observational classes elsewhere. Floats are IEEE binary64 with exact parse/print; transcendentals use fln-libm and no platform libm can affect artifacts/receipts. Unsupported calls return typed row-naming errors, never guess, panic or UB. Unit/property/model/differential/mutation suites are generated per row and include numeric boundaries, UTF-8 conventions, object/ownership, effects, cancellation/resources, and epoch diffs; named missing-row, wrong-width, platform-libm, implemented_by, ownership, effect and placeholder-success mutants are killed. Real no-mock Golem E2E executes the complete fixture families through production dispatch, plants unsupported/mismatch/fault cases, restores, and retains strict row/input/output/root/resource bundles. Census totality and epoch diff are permanent CI; dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, ABI/Mirror/differential/platform lanes pass. No hand-authored parallel table, census gap, platform libm, hidden oracle, stub success, direct-call-only evidence, overclaimed L-level, or deferred listed extern satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — distinguish W5 runtime extern rows from the broader W6 Native Mirror façade. Every pinned W5 extern row must have an implemented, typed, differential-validated disposition at closure. Unsupported is only the negative result for unknown, invalid, wrong-epoch, or out-of-scope calls; it is not a waiver for a known row. Generated dispatch, mode/profile support, effects, ownership, cancellation, and evidence state are bijective with the census.

Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable).
