title: W10: Outbound ABI door — our plugins in the Reference host, per-platform matrix
description:
Implement the outbound ABI door (§17.4, §6.6, risk R5): FrankenLean-built plugins loading into the REFERENCE host — the harder direction of the bidirectional plugin promise, staged deliberately to G5 with its own Tribunal matrix per platform (inbound landed with W2).

Why harder: the Reference host has internal assumptions (allocator behavior, initialization ordering, symbol resolution details) we do not control; our emitted artifacts must satisfy them without any cooperation from upstream. Method: build against the generated ABI contract; validate through the C4 cross-load matrix outbound lanes; every discovered host assumption becomes a contract row (or an honest per-platform tier in the Parity Ledger rather than blocking G5 — the R5 fallback).

Acceptance: FrankenLean-built precompileModules artifacts and @[extern] libraries load and run in the Reference toolchain on the covered platforms; the outbound matrix green per platform or tiered honestly; mixed pipelines (Reference host importing our plugin importing our oleans) exercised. Tests: the outbound C4 lanes per platform; symbol-resolution and initializer-ordering fixtures in the Reference host; a soak with the Reference test suite driving our plugin surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Emit FrankenLean precompileModules artifacts and @[extern] libraries that satisfy the mechanically generated pinned ABI contract and load into the real pinned Reference host without upstream cooperation. Cover symbol names/visibility, object layout/ownership, allocator conventions, initialization ordering, dynamic-loader semantics, exceptions, and supported calling conventions per platform.
2. The C4 outbound matrix runs on every declared platform and records earned level and explicit exclusions per row; an uncovered host assumption creates a generated contract row or honest platform tier, never a silent workaround. Mixed pipelines cover Reference host to FrankenLean plugin to FrankenLean/Reference-produced oleans and back as specified.

Named unit/property/golden/differential/mutation suites cover ABI contract generation/consumption, symbol resolution, initializer order, retain/release and exception edges, platform loaders, missing/incompatible symbols, unload/reload where supported, cancellation, and failure isolation. Layout-drift, missing-export, reordered-init, ownership, and false-tier mutants must be killed.
4. A shared no-mock E2E Tribunal lane builds real plugins with FrankenLean and loads/runs them in the real pinned Reference toolchain on each covered platform, exercises precompileModules and extern libraries plus mixed producers, runs the applicable Reference runtime/test-suite soak, rejects an intentionally incompatible plugin, and verifies subsequent clean loading. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/platform/host/plugin/module/symbol/initializer ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate/Parity rows, redacted argv/env/cwd, pin/build/ABI/loader/profile facts, canonical contract/binary/module/output hashes, expected/actual load/call/exit, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
5. CI archives generated contracts, symbol/layout reports, per-platform matrices, Reference transcripts, soak/failure artifacts, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. No platform or outbound direction is called compatible from an inbound-only or mocked-host test.

Consume the certified-platform decision and classify every excluded host/target/plugin row. Real per-platform tests prove ownership, unwind/panic, symbol, calling convention, lifecycle, cancellation, and recovery behavior; one platform cannot proxy another.

title: W6: Native tactic framework + bootstrap-safe primitive tactics behind facade rows
description:
Implement the native tactic framework + core tactic set (§10.5): goal management, metavariable assignment, evalTactic dispatch, elab_rules/macro_rules registration and matching, focusing/structuring combinators, evalExpr-class services, and the InfoTree hooks as Athanor code; the core tactic set (rfl-class closing, intro/apply/cases/induction machinery, simp via Anvil, decide via Crucible, omega/bv_decide via Verdict, norm_num-class evaluation) native behind Mirror facade rows — each with equivalence class, golden-trace fixtures, and its own Parity-Ledger L-level.

USER tactics — mathlib, aesop, project elab blocks — run on Golem and call this framework through the facade, receiving GoalState views and capability contexts. No tactic, native or user, can construct a trusted declaration except through Crucible (FL-INV-02). The framework observable semantics (state threading, backtracking, error propagation, InfoTree shape) are conformance surface, proven by the metaprogram-corpus rig running the ecosystem REAL tactic code.

Acceptance: the curated mathlib-tactic slice (G0-8) executes through the production framework with environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, and generated names diffing clean against the oracle at declared L-levels; core tactics pass their per-row fixture families. Tests: tactic-framework state-threading property tests; backtracking/error-propagation fixtures; facade-row differential families per core tactic; FL-INV-02 mutation test (a tactic attempting direct environment admission must be structurally impossible — compile-time — or caught).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — W6 owns goal management, state threading, backtracking, evalTactic dispatch, InfoTree hooks, facade contracts, direct kernel-backed decide/rfl-class behavior, and the bootstrap-safe primitive tactic subset needed to reach G3. It defines stable typed dispatch seams for simp, norm_num, omega, bv_decide, and grind but must not fake those engines before W7. Engine-backed rows remain honest L0/L1 with precise typed Unsupported results until franken_lean-engine-backed-tactic-integration-c90 promotes them. G3's curated metaprogram slice reports those levels explicitly; full engine-backed tactic functionality is preserved and completed before G4.
acceptance_criteria:
The native framework implements pinned goal management, mvar assignment, evalTactic dispatch, registration/matching, focus/structure combinators, evalExpr-class services, error propagation, backtracking and InfoTree hooks through stable facade contracts. User mathlib/project tactics run on Golem with exact GoalState/capability views. Bootstrap-safe primitives including rfl/intro/apply/cases/induction and direct kernel-backed decide behavior preserve Reference environments, goals, messages, InfoTrees and generated names. Engine-backed simp/norm_num/omega/bv_decide/grind seams are typed and stable but remain explicit L0/L1 Unsupported until the W7 integration promotes them; no fake success. Every native/user result reaches declarations only through Crucible and configured consensus; direct admission is structurally impossible. Unit/property/model/differential/mutation tests cover state threading, focus/backtrack, exceptions, registration order, goal closure, InfoTrees, capabilities, budgets/cancellation, unsupported rows and recovery; named state-leak, swallowed-error, wrong-dispatch, fake-engine, direct-admission and InfoTree-drop mutants are killed. Real no-mock curated tactic E2E executes on Golem, compares Reference observables per row level, plants/restores failure/cancellation, and retains strict row/state/trace/root/final bundles. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, compile-fail/Mirror/trace/mutation lanes pass. No hosted tactic framework, placeholder engine result, direct trusted mutation, direct-API-only proof, hidden oracle, overclaimed row, mock-only tactic, or deferred listed primitive/framework behavior satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — the framework owns tactic state, dispatch, focus, backtracking, InfoTree, primitives, cases/induction, and evalExpr integration, not the W7 engines. Unsupported W7 rows remain precise L0/L1 outcomes until the engine-integration bead atomically promotes them; compile success or generated stubs cannot. Named suites: tactic_state_machine_model, tactic_admission_compile_fail, golem_tactic_facade_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative evidence is canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON validated independently; telemetry is a separate non-authoritative stream and cannot affect hashes, comparison, admission, cache, publication, or promotion. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/duplicate ids, broken step order, unbound artifacts, silent truncation, merged streams, and cleanup/final-state mismatch. Cancellation, exhaustion, and InternalFault never enter cache, negative storage, proof admission, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 records prove useful overlap, completed work, retries, and schedule identity. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: G0-8: Mirror-facade viability spike — census + stubs vs real mathlib tactics
description:
G0-8 Mirror-facade viability spike (§22.1-8, feeds §4.3/§18.2): generate the builtin census and facade stubs for a metaprogram-heavy slice (Lean.Meta core + the tactic-framework entry points); compile a curated set of REAL mathlib tactic files against the facade with native services stubbed at prototype depth; enumerate every API whose semantics resist native backing and assign each an honest L-level.

This is the plan CENTRAL bet (B2) priced before interfaces freeze, and the direct mitigation of Critical risk R1 (Meta-API dependence effectively unbounded — packages compile against Lean.* internals that may expose object identity or allocation order the native facade cannot reproduce). The census partition (toolchain-api native / library-code source-elaborated / user-facing-data) must prove TOTAL on the slice: no unclassified symbol, ever — a symbol the census cannot classify blocks the L-level, never gets guessed.

Acceptance: (a) census extraction runs mechanically over the pinned Init/Std/Lean sources for the slice and emits symbol, signature, telescope, effect class, partition class; (b) generated facade stubs compile against real mathlib tactic code; (c) a written resistance list: every symbol whose observable semantics leak implementation detail, each with equivalence class (bit/obs/perf), honest L-level, and Behavior Note candidate; (d) the ordered closure plan for the facade L-level ratchet (resolves OQ-3). Tests: the slice + tactic files seed the metaprogram-corpus rig (§18.2 Mirror conformance).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this G0-8 spike owns the feasibility half of OQ-3. It produces the initial symbol-by-symbol census partition, dependency-closure ordering, generated facade schema, and evidence for real tactic/metaprogram probes. It must identify load-bearing unknown rows and hand a versioned decision artifact to franken_lean-epx; it does not claim the production facade L-level ratchet complete.

MirrorSliceDecisionV1 binds the exact transitive symbol closure rooted at the curated Lean.Meta and tactic entry points, the SUITE.lock epoch, generated-census schema, source/environment extraction inputs, Golem-spike ABI contract, fixture set and decision status. The slice may be smaller than the eventual Mirror, but it is total within that frozen closure: every dependency, generated declaration, instance/attribute registration, intrinsic, opaque constant and user-visible data row is present exactly once or the spike blocks.

Each row distinguishes toolchain-API-native, library-source-elaborated, user-data/opaque and RequiresHandler/unsupported prototype behavior, with exact signature/universes, module/owner, effect/capability/determinism class, ABI conversion, object-identity observables, ordering, resource/cancellation behavior, evidence level and W6 promotion owner. Compile compatibility, load/link compatibility and executed semantic compatibility are separate grades. A stub or typed Unsupported can prove boundary honesty, never semantic success.

The real execution path consumes franken_lean-7xe: upstream-authored tactic source is input data, prototype native services and Golem execute it, and the Reference runs only as Tribunal oracle/fixture producer. No Reference elaborator/runtime is linked, hosted or invoked as a product fallback. If a load-bearing row resists native backing, G0 cannot close by assigning it away; it must amend the boundary/interface. Only a proved non-load-bearing row may be handed to W6 with an explicit typed boundary and freeze guard.

The decision artifact includes an ordered dependency-closure plan, cost/row counts, hard-row blast radii and exact downstream API/schema commitments. W6 consumes the same stable row IDs and cannot silently repartition them.

G0 DECISION INTEGRATION — emit exactly one G0SpikeDecisionV1 whose domain payload is MirrorSliceDecisionV1. Outcome is RatifyNativeMirrorPartition, AmendPartitionOrClosurePlan, or NoGo mapped to Ratified/Amended/NoGo. This decision resolves OQ-3 at G0 by freezing the feasibility boundary, exact toolchain-API/library-code/user-data partition law, treatment and evidence state of every unsupported row, dependency/closure order, and downstream W6 owner plan. W6 may implement and promote classified rows but may not receive an unpriced partition unknown. A stub-linked success, omitted load-bearing row, hidden oracle, unsupported row normalized to success, mock tactic, Inconclusive/InternalFault, or incomplete real tactic closure blocks.
acceptance_criteria:
Mechanical extraction over the pinned Reference environment produces a row-complete Lean.Meta/tactic slice census with qualified symbol, universes/telescope/signature, attributes, effect/ownership class, partition class, dependencies, comparison class, support status, evidence and fixture; missing, duplicate, stale, generated, or unclassified symbols block the spike. Generated facade stubs compile real curated mathlib tactic/metaprogram files and route calls through prototype native services or precise typed Unsupported outcomes with no hidden oracle. A reviewed resistance table records every implementation-detail leak or hard semantic row, its bit/observational/performance equivalence, current honest L-level, fixture, Behavior Note candidate, load-bearing unknown, owner, and promotion prerequisite. OQ-3 feasibility closes with a versioned dependency-ordered facade closure plan handed to the production census bead, not a false production-completeness claim. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover census joins, stub signatures, dependency ordering, unsupported rendering, effect/capability facts, generated declarations, and row promotion; named missing-row, stale-row, wrong-partition, placeholder-success, and hidden-fallback mutants are killed. The real no-mock Mirror-seed E2E compiles and executes representative tactic paths where prototype depth permits, records exact unsupported rows elsewhere, plants census/facade divergences, restores inputs, and retains strict symbol/trace/root/evidence bundles. Dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and census/Mirror lanes pass. No source-regex substitute where environment extraction is required, compile-only success presented as semantic parity, guessed row, fake implementation, overclaimed L-level, unresolved load-bearing row, or deferred listed spike output satisfies closure.

The mechanically extracted slice closure reconciles source, compiled environment and generated declarations one-to-one and proves no missing, duplicate, stale or extra row. Every row's signature, ownership, effect/capability, partition, identity/order observables, resource/cancellation behavior, evidence grade and promotion prerequisite are validated. Exact row/name/depth/dependency/output/work limits and fixed cancellation points yield typed Inconclusive without a partial authoritative census.

Real curated mathlib tactic/metaprogram sources both compile and execute through the prototype facade on the G0-3 Golem path where their rows claim semantic support. Reference runs are oracle-only comparisons. Unsupported rows produce exact typed row-ID diagnostics and cannot be counted in the executed denominator. Productive 1/8/32 fixed-input runs preserve semantic traces, generated names, diagnostics, roots and decision rows.

Unit/property/model/mutation suites cover closure joins, overloads, generated declarations, instances/attributes, effect and capability facts, ABI converters, row ordering, partition transitions and resistance-table completeness. Mutants omit dependencies, classify library source as native, return placeholder success, hide RequiresHandler, call an oracle fallback, change a stable row ID, promote compile-only evidence, or drop an identity/order observable; each is killed by a named assertion.

Strict bundles separate semantic records from timing/host telemetry and include pin/census/Golem/fixture/row/trace/root IDs, exact executed/unsupported status, evidence and claim class, limits/usage, mutation/first divergence, restoration and final state. Validators reject unclassified or load-bearing deferred rows, denominator games, stale generated stubs, hidden fallbacks, cross-pin evidence, fake 1/8/32 work or an OQ-3 handoff without a frozen boundary.

title: W2: Marrow object model + tri-state RC — the lean_object ABI twin
description:
Implement the Marrow object model + tri-state reference counting (§6.1, §6.1b, §6.2; crates fln-rt + fln-unsafe-abi): the Reference runtime object model, exactly, under the exported lean_* names — object header (RC word, memory-kind/other byte, tag byte), constructor objects with scalar-field packing, arrays/scalar arrays/strings (UTF-8 with byte-length/capacity semantics), closures (arity, fixed args), thunks, tasks, IO.Refs, external objects with class vtables, bignum objects (fln-bignum-backed mpz-shim view), small Nats as odd tagged pointers ((n << 1) | 1). Every layout constant comes from the W1 generated contract tables.

Two heaps, one membrane: CompatHeap (exact ABI, membrane-only allocation, all unsafe confined to fln-unsafe-abi with ledger rows per site) vs NativeHeap (typed asupersync region handles); generated converters with declared ownership and failure semantics; lazy boundary-specific conversion (a metaprogram that never inspects a term never forces its Compat projection — memory-doubling risk R10 is the named gate PG-6, not an accident). Tri-state RC: single-threaded fast path, atomic shared, persistent-never-counted — matching upstream so ABI-crossing borrow/inc/dec behaves identically. Hardened builds: debug ownership shadows (double-release + foreign-pointer misuse detection, quarantine, deterministic replay events).

Structural laws (D3): no fln-unsafe-* crate depends on fln-kernel/fln-checker; no unsafe export launderable into a checked declaration; panic law at the ABI boundary (§6.5). Acceptance: G0-1/G0-3 findings folded; C4 probes pass both directions on the implemented subset. Tests: layout unit tests generated FROM the contract module; RC balance property tests; ownership-shadow mutation tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: Marrow implements every generated lean_object layout and exported ownership contract required by the pinned ABI: headers, tags, scalar packing, constructors, arrays/scalar arrays, UTF-8 strings with byte length/capacity, closures and fixed args, thunks, tasks, IO.Refs, external class/vtable objects, bignum views, and odd-tagged small Nats. All offsets/tags/widths come from generated contract rows and compile-time/layout tests. CompatHeap and NativeHeap are type-separated; generated lazy converters are the only membrane and declare ownership, allocation, failure, capability, and no-claim boundaries. Tri-state RC matches single-threaded, atomic-shared, and persistent-never-counted behavior across borrow/inc/dec and concurrent transitions. Every unsafe site lives only in fln-unsafe-abi, has a ledger row and safe fallback, cannot depend on kernel/checker or export a checked-declaration capability, and catches panics at the ABI boundary. Debug ownership shadows detect/quarantine/replay double release, foreign pointers, wrong heap, stale external class, and RC imbalance. Unit/property/model/mutation suites cover every generated layout, tag/packing boundary, object lifecycle, conversion, RC interleaving, external callback, malformed pointer/class, resource limit, and recovery; named drop/retain/tag/layout/converter/unsafe-laundering mutants are killed. Real no-mock C4 E2E loads Reference-built plugins and upstream-generated objects, calls both directions, exercises faults and cleanup, and retains strict ABI/ownership/root/process evidence across certified platforms. G0 findings, dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, unsafe/dependency guards, stage0/plugin/soak/thread/platform lanes pass. No handwritten layout, raw pointer outside membrane, eager whole-world conversion, unchecked foreign object, leak/double-free waiver, hidden upstream runtime, unsafe authority laundering, or deferred listed object family satisfies closure.

title: W10: Iron backends — owned native codegen + JIT, C backend as parity apparatus
description:
Implement the Iron backends (§17.3, §11.3): the sovereign path — FLBC to native objects/executables via an owned baseline code generator (NO LLVM, no cranelift, no cc), x86-64 + aarch64 first, so lake build of executables needs no external toolchain (the G6 headline; resolves weakness census #12 partially and the mandatory-cc contract entirely). Iron-JIT (frontier, standard-mode only, fln-unsafe-jit): a baseline template JIT from FLBC with inline caches burned in — the tier that makes hot tactic loops approach precompiled speed; certified builds never enable it; results bit-identical to the interpreter by construction, spot-checked by a standing dual-run rig (PG-7: JIT >= 0.5x Reference-precompiled on the corpus).

Also: --backend c — emit C in the Reference idiom and drive the USER toolchain exactly as upstream leanc does (D2 tool #1, optional), for parity studies and exotic targets; never the anchor (D16). Both backends dual-run governed: Iron pinned to interpreter results, interpreter pinned to Reference observable evaluation semantics. OQ-4 (target set beyond x86-64/aarch64; JIT/static emitter sharing) is resolved inside this bead and recorded in the decision log.

Acceptance: real executables (incl. a Lake-built project binary) produced by Iron run correctly with zero cc present; JIT dual-run rig green; C-backend output compiles under the Reference leanc contract on the parity corpus. Tests: differential execution interpreter-vs-Iron-vs-C-backend on the corpus; executable-page management ledger rows audited (fln-unsafe-jit, D3); JIT-vs-interpreter bit-identity spot checks in CI; cross-arch CI lanes.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OQ-4 HANDOFF — consume the versioned contract from fln-iron-target-profile-contract-ba9 transitively through fln-y55. Iron static emission and JIT must use the selected shared/separate profile architecture, supported-target tiers, target-feature cache-key law, and mode/certification boundaries. Evidence that contradicts the contract reopens OQ-4 before interface drift is landed.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The owned Iron baseline compiler lowers validated FLBC into native objects/executables for x86-64 and aarch64 without LLVM, Cranelift, a system compiler, or any unapproved external tool. It implements target ABI, linking/runtime metadata, relocations, unwind/error behavior, deterministic emission, and executable closure required by the resolved OQ-4 target/profile contract. 2. Iron-JIT is frontier/standard-only in fln-unsafe-jit with every executable-memory operation ledgered and narrowly scoped; certified builds structurally cannot enable it. JIT and static outputs are pinned to interpreter semantics and bit-identical observable results for covered programs, with safe fallback on unsupported code or allocation failure. 3. --backend c emits the pinned Reference idiom and invokes only the optional D2 C compiler capsule with full provenance; absence yields typed capability guidance. It remains parity/exotic-target apparatus, never the sovereign anchor. OQ-4 final target and emitter-sharing decisions are recorded before interface freeze. 4. Named unit/property/golden/differential/mutation suites cover instruction selection, registers/spills, calls/returns/closures, GC/RC/FBIP edges, literals/control flow/exceptions, relocations/linking, deterministic objects, executable-page lifecycle, unsupported target/fallback, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and interpreter-Iron-JIT-C equivalence. Miscompile, bad relocation, W+X, JIT-in-certified, hidden-cc, and nondeterministic-object mutants must be killed. 5. A shared no-mock E2E lane builds and runs real standalone and Lake project executables with compiler tools absent from the environment, repeats on real x86-64/aarch64 runners, compares interpreter/Iron/JIT and optional real C-backend results, exercises unsupported/JIT-failure recovery, and scans processes/binaries for forbidden dependencies. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/program/target/backend/function/object/link/page ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/env/cwd, build/epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/tool-availability facts, canonical FLBC/object/executable/output roots, expected/actual, fallback and unsafe-ledger ids, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state. 6. Reproducible PG-7 evidence shows JIT at least 0.5 times pinned Reference-precompiled performance on the declared corpus/machine and publishes distributions. CI archives cross-arch byte/equivalence matrices, C parity outputs, unsafe audits, mutant outcomes, benchmarks, and E2E bundles; no sovereign claim closes with cc present or JIT enabled in certified mode. 7. This bead owns the W10 half of risk R12: it consumes the exact Golem/Mirror profile handoff, demonstrates which measured hot paths are removed by static Iron, JIT, or native-facade fast paths, re-runs fidelity and determinism after each change, and leaves any remaining ecosystem-tolerance miss as an explicit BLOCKED or downgraded performance claim for G6. No optimization may alter a verdict, diagnostic, artifact, effect order, or Mirror row to obtain the target.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — unsupported static Iron inputs fail typed with no artifact and no automatic C or interpreter fallback. Only frontier JIT may explicitly fall back to the interpreter under recorded policy; C requires explicit backend selection and full subprocess evidence. Machine output binds target/profile/features/runtime contract, and every failure path leaves no promoted artifact. Tests cover unsupported instructions/targets, legalization failure, cancellation, W^X, fallback policy, and recovery.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.

title: Gate G1: The Independent Judge — all of mathlib re-checked, zero divergence, PG-1
description:
Convergence gate G1 "The Independent Judge" (§22.3): Marrow + Grimoire + Crucible check EVERY mathlib olean at the pin with zero acceptance divergence against the Reference toolchain; PG-1 met (<= 5 min on the 32-core profile, <= 25 min single-thread); fln check-olean ships as a standalone auditor.

Why this gate is first and why it matters strategically: the program FIRST public artifact is the strongest independent verification the Lean world has ever had — a foreign-blooded kernel (no shared code, no shared compiler, no shared bignum) re-checking the entire corpus in minutes — shipped BEFORE FrankenLean has elaborated a single file. It is simultaneously the deepest de-risking event available: it proves the ABI/olean/kernel spine (the layout extraction methodology, the region codec, the judgment inventory) against the full Corpus at production scale.

Evidence bundle required to declare the gate: full-corpus run logs on the baseline machine; receipts + transparency log entries; the kernel differential rigs green (lean4checker/lean4lean agreement); Parity Ledger rows for the kernel/olean/ABI surfaces at their achieved L-levels; PG-1 and PG-8 numbers with claim states; every divergence (if any occurred) closed through the §8.2 triage law with fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
G1 depends on the concrete Independent Judge product and evidence surfaces, not on completion of umbrella workstreams. fln-fur supplies the full-recheck olean reader, K1/K2/checker consensus, receipts, CLI, and corpus evidence; franken_lean-3j8 supplies the standing foreign-witness differential rigs; fln-a6q and fln-544 supply calibrated PG-1/PG-8 measurement. Unrelated W2 scope such as olean-next, plugin doors, effects, libm, and the full ABI workstream must not delay the first public auditor. The W3 epic depends on this gate as its integration exit, never the reverse.

WITNESS-SCOPE CORRECTION — the independent checker path is full-corpus for K1/K2/fln-checker/Reference/lean4checker. Lean4lean/export is an additional deterministic sampled and high-risk-class witness, not a falsely promised all-Corpus throughput dependency.
acceptance_criteria:
G1 closes only on a manifest-complete no-mock recheck of every mathlib olean at the pinned Corpus commit, with no hidden exclusion and zero acceptance divergence among FrankenLean K1/K2, fln-checker, the Reference kernel, and lean4checker; lean4lean/export witnesses cover a deterministic declared sample plus every high-risk judgment class; every pairwise disagreement halts publication and retains minimized fixtures plus traces. fln check-olean ships as a standalone air-gap-capable auditor with stable human and versioned robot schemas, typed malformed/corrupt/resource/cancellation/InternalFault outcomes, no panic, no external Lean implementation in the runtime path, and clean recovery after every refusal. Canonical judgments, receipts, logical roots, transparency-log entries, and Parity Ledger rows bind exact toolchain, Corpus, input closure, engine/fuel policy, mode, determinism class, and evidence level. Full-corpus artifacts and diagnostics are bit-identical at 1, 8, and 32 threads; the certified platform matrix is explicit. PG-1 is met on valid baseline runs at no more than 5 minutes on the 32-core profile and 25 minutes single-thread, and PG-8 read throughput is reported at its honest claim state with raw samples, host/isolation/cache facts, tails, invalid-run retention, and no timing-only correctness claim. Unit/property/model tests cover every judgment and codec boundary; mutation campaigns kill skipped kernel rules, corrupted relocation/layout data, stale cache acceptance, wrong engine consensus, receipt tampering, and Inconclusive-to-rejection promotion. The real full-corpus E2E includes positive, planted divergence/corruption, exact restoration, and recovery phases under the shared evidence contract with bounded separate logs and independently validated committed bundles. All owning bead gates, scripts/check.sh, fmt, cargo check/test, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, differential rigs, codec rigs, thread matrix, mutation lanes, and fault drills are green. No sampled-corpus substitute, unclassified divergence, missing witness, mock-only oracle, performance waiver presented as success, or deferred listed feature satisfies G1.

WITNESS-SCOPE CORRECTION — K1, K2, fln-checker, the pinned Reference, and lean4checker remain manifest-complete over the full Corpus. Lean4lean/export witnesses run on a deterministic, versioned sample plus every high-risk judgment class as specified by the plan; every exercised pairwise disagreement still blocks and is minimized. Evidence must state coverage separately so sample witnesses can neither masquerade as full-corpus throughput nor weaken the full-corpus authorities.

title: W8: Publish compiled rewrite indexes — Ledger CAS + olean-next attachment integration
description:
Integrate W7's deterministic compiled rewrite-index format with the W8 Ledger and Grimoire artifact planes. Publish each per-library discrimination automaton as a typed demand-node product and olean-next attachment keyed by the exact simp-set semantic hash, implementation/schema version, epoch, mode, and CGSE policy; hydrate it through local/remote CAS validation; project it into daemon sessions without rebuilding; and invalidate or reject it structurally whenever any key input changes.

This bead resolves the stage boundary cleanly: W7 implements and proves the faithful simp driver plus deterministic index builder/reader equivalence; W8 owns transactional publication, retention, hydration, invalidation, quarantine, and cache provenance. No W7 component may claim a Ledger-published artifact before this integration exists.

Acceptance: a mathlib slice builds indexes once, publishes atomically, reloads from a fresh process and from a validated remote-cache fixture, and yields candidate sets and simp results identical to a fresh in-memory index; unchanged simp sets reuse byte-identical artifacts; changed rules/options/epoch/schema cannot load stale artifacts; promotion crashes leave no half-published attachment.

Verification: unit schema/key tests, builder-vs-loaded representation equivalence, stale-key and poisoned-cache mutations, kill-at-every-promotion-step drills, and shared no-mock E2E scripts for build -> publish -> hydrate -> simplify with complete CAS keys, manifests, hashes, receipts, expected/final states, and first-divergence logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
Each per-library compiled discrimination automaton publishes as a typed Ledger demand-node product and olean-next attachment keyed by the exact simp-set semantic hash, builder/schema version, epoch, mode, CGSE policy, platform-independent format, and owning roots. Publication is atomic and crash-recoverable; retention, quarantine, local/remote hydration, provenance, and validation belong to W8. A fresh process and production daemon can load a valid artifact without rebuilding and obtain candidate sets, ordering, proof terms, and simp results identical to W7 in-memory construction. Unchanged inputs reuse byte-identical artifacts; any rule, attribute, option, epoch, schema, mode, policy, checksum, receipt, or root change structurally prevents stale loading. Remote poison becomes a miss plus security evidence, never acceptance; promotion crashes leave no visible half attachment. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover key/schema encoding, builder-loaded equivalence, deterministic serialization, retention, quarantine, stale dimensions, poison/truncation, duplicate publication, budgets, and recovery; named omitted-key, stale-hit, unvalidated-remote, half-promotion, and reordered-candidate mutants are killed. title: W6: Unifier/defeq — the approximation ladder, implemented against golden traces
description:
Implement unification + defeq against golden traces (§10.2): transparency-aware whnf/isDefEq mirroring the Reference approximation ladder — syntactic fast paths, first-order approximation, Miller patterns, delayed assignments, postponement, unfolding by reducibility and definitional height, structure-eta awareness. Mathlib DEPENDS on the precise power of the unifier (proofs elaborate because a particular approximation fires); this is where bug-for-bug earns its keep and where Critical risk R2 lives.

Method (the R2 mitigation): implemented AGAINST the instrumented-oracle golden decision traces (G0-9 -> §18.3) — which approximation fired, what postponed, what unfolded, in what order — at Corpus scale, with trace-replay rigs running continuously. Every T3 divergence is triaged with the strong prior that it is a fidelity defect; a persistent divergence class becomes a faithful-mode blocker and a published erratum with quantified Corpus blast radius, never a quiet Behavior Note (the epoch ships R3, not R4, until closed). Failures are recorded as typed constraint cells (HasType, IsDefEq, SynthesizeInstance, Coerce, ResolveName, ElaboratePattern, ProveTermination) with origins, dependencies, attempts, and outcomes — the substrate Palimpsest blame slices are computed from.

Acceptance: trace-replay agreement on the pilot corpus, expanding toward Corpus scale with the divergence ledger empty or fully classified; the G0-7 tail suite green through the production engine; constraint-cell recording complete per the perturbation validator. Tests: golden-trace replay rigs per approximation stage; postponement-order fixtures; defeq differentials vs the oracle on C0-C2.

No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

title: W6: Term elaboration — coercions, match compilation, recursion elaborators, exact generated names
description:
Implement term elaboration (§10.3): coercion insertion, implicit/strict-implicit/instance-implicit binder handling, auto-bound implicits, structure instances and anonymous constructors, dependent match compilation into auxiliary matchers with generated equation lemmas and splitters MATCHING the Reference generated-name and shape conventions (metaprograms pattern-match on these artifacts — they are API, not internals), deriving handler dispatch, attribute application, variable/section/namespace resolution with the Reference ambiguity rules, and the recursion elaborators (structural, well-founded with the same measure-synthesis surface, partial, unsafe) lowering to EXACTLY the kernel-visible constructions of §1.2 (recursors, WellFounded.fix, unfold equations — the kernel sees only these).

Termination elaboration records a machine-readable TerminationCard (call graph, candidate measures, generated obligations, chosen theorem dependencies) — the artifact the Bloodhound repair lane consumes when a termination proof breaks under migration (§16.1).

Acceptance: generated-name conventions byte-agree with the oracle on the match/equation-lemma corpus (C2 metaprogramming-weight packages); recursion lowerings kernel-check identically; TerminationCards emitted and schema-validated. title: W1: Typed outcomes, diagnostic causes, and faithful projections — FL-INV-07, R9
description:
## Description

Integrate the W1 operation-outcome, diagnostic-cause, and renderer contracts without conflating legitimate domain rejection, resource/cancellation refusal, or internal faults. fln-171x owns the versioned fln-core values, pure compatibility/serialization rules, authority semantics, and exhaustive diagnostic-cause vocabulary. franken_lean-wlan owns per-epoch faithful/sound projections, transcript comparison, frontend/channel/exit behavior, normalizers, and real diagnostic conformance. This epic is their strict terminal evidence join.

Every authoritative operation yields Complete with its domain result, Inconclusive with explicit cancellation/resource/dependency/incomplete-authority facts and authority=false, or InternalFault with invariant/evidence facts. Inconclusive/InternalFault can never become acceptance, rejection, cacheable failure, successful absence, or ordinary user error. Diagnostic causes remain structured orthogonal values: syntax, macro, elaboration, kernel rejection cause, corrupt/epoch-mismatched artifact, ABI/capability/plugin/build/protocol/replay failures, and internal invariant violation. Unknown versions fail typed; authoritative matches are exhaustive; panics never become user diagnostics.

Per-epoch renderers bind mode, frontend, format, channel, path/color policy, comparison class, exit class, and deterministic ordering. Faithful rendering matches the pinned Reference at each honest exact/semantic row. Sound mode may add registered richer context without changing authority, source position, severity, or faithful output. Receipts always retain the typed cause even when displayed text is equivalent.

The title no longer mislabels D8: D8 is the Oracle-Only Law, while this epic enforces FL-INV-07 and renderer risk R9. Future decoders/subsystems retain their own panic-freedom and adapter campaigns through the verification registry; W1 proves the reusable vocabulary and representative real projections without claiming absent integrations complete.

## Success Criteria

Both child tasks close with compatible schema/epoch/mode identities and independently validated bundles. The parent join proves every named outcome and cause, authority-preserving serialization, exhaustive matching, typed unknown handling, bounded detail/truncation, faithful/sound isolation, exact/semantic comparison honesty, deterministic order, positions/severity/channels/exits, and cause/evidence retention. Named unit/property/model/golden/fuzz/mutation suites and a real no-mock C-family transcript E2E cover malformed/corrupt/aged/resource/cancellation/internal-fault cases, retry/recovery, and independent evidence validation. Mutants that promote Inconclusive, hide InternalFault, trust unknown versions, swallow variants, lose cause/evidence, drift channels/order, leak sound wording, erase bodies through normalization, panic on malformed input, or substitute mocks must all fail for the intended reason. Strict semantic NDJSON and human output derive from typed facts; linked telemetry cannot perturb semantic roots. No stringly cross-crate authority, panic-as-diagnostic, exact-text overclaim, unmarked truncation, mode leak, deferred listed variant, or downstream-adapter waiver satisfies this epic.
acceptance_criteria: The epic closes only after fln-171x and franken_lean-wlan have current independently validated evidence under compatible outcome/cause schema, Reference epoch, mode, frontend, and comparison identities. Every original taxonomy, variant, serialization, panic-freedom, faithful/sound rendering, transcript, ordering, position, severity, exit, fuzz, mutation, no-mock E2E, logging, and recovery requirement is retained by a named child and proven in the terminal join. Inconclusive and InternalFault remain non-authoritative everywhere; future subsystem adapters stay mandatory on their owning beads.

title: W1: Mode system + certified axis — structural faithful/sound/frontier, D18 leak prevention
description:
## Description

Implement the faithful/sound/frontier mode contract and the orthogonal determinism, reproducibility-eligibility, provenance, and evidence axes (§4.2, D17, D18). fln-core owns only closed mode types, determinism-class vocabulary D0-D4, reproducibility profile, provenance/claim-row identifiers, and pure compatibility rules. fln-conformance owns leak scanning and evidence-ledger projection. Core/runtime crates never read Tribunal or Parity-Ledger state.

Mode is a semantic input; D0-D4 classifies an operation; --reproducible requests a certified-eligible artifact set; L/R evidence describes proof and is neither a semantic input nor a self-certification bit. Every artifact/cache key/receipt binds producing mode, epoch, CGSE policy, determinism class, target/profile, and complete content-hashed input closure. Artifacts may bind a stable claim-row identity; receipts/evidence bundles bind the evaluated ledger snapshot. Missing, unknown, malformed, forged, or stripped mode/provenance never defaults.

Certified eligibility is recomputed independently over the authoritative SUITE.lock/closure contract. The closure includes sources, toolchain, options, plugin hashes, target/profile/features, policy epochs, and every semantic input. A marker alone is not authority. D2 products and unadmitted nondeterministic inputs are structurally excluded from certified artifacts. Mode-neutral sharing is allowed only for a registered neutral schema with identical semantic roots; mixed-mode cache hits are forbidden. Cancellation/resource exhaustion before atomic publication leaves no cacheable artifact.

D18 is structural: frontier-only olean-next, e-graph/portfolio, Iron-JIT, MCP write, and epoch-bridge code/products are compile/capability gated and independently scanned from faithful/sound closures. This task delivers the reusable contract, scanner, sidecar manifest, and representative real integrations. Every later producer owns adoption through the verification registry, and W12 proves release-wide closure; no feature is deferred, but W1 does not falsely claim integration with absent subsystems.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites mode_product_lattice_model, artifact_compatibility_matrix, certified_closure_model, mode_propagation_property, and mode_isolation_e2e cover every mode/determinism/reproducibility/evidence combination, serialization/cache/receipt keys, neutral sharing, closure completeness, D2 standard versus certified behavior, feature/capability gates, cancellation/nonpublication, and 1/8/32 schedule matrices. Every intentional sound/faithful divergence names a registered Behavior Note; evidence is read from the ledger and cannot alter semantic identity. Mutations for missing mode defaulting to sound, stripped/forged frontier or sound markers, trusted marker without recomputed closure, evidence-level change altering identity, mixed-mode cache hit, omitted closure input, external product marked certified, runtime-if-only isolation, cancellation publication, and missing Behavior Note are killed. A real no-mock E2E builds representative products in all three modes, plants and repairs frontier contamination and closure omissions, proves cache isolation and recovery, and performs two isolated certified builds with byte-identical outputs/sidecars. Strict semantic logs record mode/class/epoch/closure/product roots and expected/actual decisions; host/time/PID/path/performance facts remain linked telemetry. Release-wide adoption remains a mandatory W12 gate.

Mode, D0-D4 class, certified eligibility, and L/R evidence remain distinct. Unknown/forged/stripped provenance never defaults; neutral sharing requires registered identical semantics; cancellation and resource refusal publish nothing; frontier products cannot enter faithful/sound closures. All named model/property/mutation/no-mock E2E and productive 1/8/32 tests, strict semantic logs, two-build identity proof, recovery, and independent evidence validation pass. Downstream owners retain mandatory adoption and W12 owns release-wide closure.

title: W1: Tribunal campaign machinery — mutation, fuzzing, drills, delta-debugger, thread matrix
description:
## Description

Own the six reusable Tribunal campaign frameworks and their machine-checked downstream owner matrix (§18.2–18.3): named mutation/kill accounting; budgeted fuzz generation/replay; fault-and-recovery drills; failure-signature-preserving shrink; no-mock evidence attestation; and deterministic productive thread matrices. W1 proves each framework on a real controlled fln-conformance target. Production adapters remain mandatory on their subsystem owners: grammar/source on Vellum; kernel terms on the kernel differential rig; region/OLEAN on W2 codecs; VM opcodes on Golem/FLBC; CAS/manifest faults on W8 Ledger; server/editor faults on replay/protocol rigs. A registered-but-inactive production adapter cannot satisfy its downstream gate.

Campaign evidence has honest D7 classes. Fuzzing and mutation are statistical; bounded schedule/model exploration is bounded_model; they detect counterexamples but do not prove an FL-INV invariant. Each invariant owner names its constructive/static/formal enforcement separately. A mutant counts as killed only when the named discriminating test fails for the intended reason. Compile failure, unrelated gate failure, timeout, another mutant, or harness InternalFault is not a kill. Equivalent and unbuildable mutants are typed, evidenced classifications and cannot silently shrink the denominator.

Every mutation binary binds source root, patch digest, build identity, mutant id, target/production-path reachability, expected discriminator, and release-exclusion proof. Mutation controls are absent from release artifacts. Shrinking preserves original failure signature, oracle authority/comparison, resource class, and reproduction; it emits a candidate artifact. Checking a fixture into the repository is a separate reviewed action, never an automatic CI mutation.

Fault campaigns freeze a complete boundary/faultpoint census before claiming every promotion step. Expected final state is a product-by-product vector; restart alone never passes. Abrupt termination uses a platform-neutral semantic capability with platform-specific rows rather than projecting Unix signals onto Windows. Thread matrices use identical content-hashed closures and logical budgets, require actual nonempty parallel partitions at 1/8/32 and a qualified 96+ host weekly, and compare canonical semantic outputs; wall time/scheduler/host telemetry is separate.

## Success Criteria

Named suites campaign_owner_matrix, mutation_kill_ledger_model, fuzz_seed_replay, fault_boundary_registry, shrink_signature_preservation, no_mock_attestation, and productive_thread_matrix prove all six frameworks on real controlled targets, exact downstream ownership/activation, D7 classifications, intended-reason kills, denominator accounting, source/build/release binding, platform capabilities, final-state vectors, reproducible replay, productive parallelism, and independent bundle validation. Required mutant families preserve kernel-rule, transaction/effect, codec bounds, retain/release, capability, stale publication/cache/epoch, forged evidence, and outcome-promotion cases; framework self-mutants include false kill, denominator drop, mock substitution, failure-signature drift, incomplete fault census, restart-as-pass, fake thread label, semantic/telemetry mixing, and release mutation hook. Fuzz/drill/shrink/thread E2Es use real filesystems/binaries/targets, enforce budgets, plant and recover named failures, leave no automatic workspace edits, and retain generator/target/build hashes, patch/seed/PRNG, schedule/replay receipt, coverage, faultpoint, expected/actual state vector, shrink lineage, first divergence, cleanup, and final state. All downstream adapters remain gate-blocking on their owners; W1 closure proves the framework and registry, not imaginary mature subsystems.
design: INVARIANT CAMPAIGN OWNER — bind campaign ids directly to FL-INV-01 through FL-INV-07 and their owning beads. In particular, the thread matrix is the standing FL-INV-01 enforcement lane; facade perturbations feed FL-INV-03; codec corruption/round-trip campaigns feed FL-INV-04; stale/forged/cache-input and epoch-bridge mutants feed FL-INV-05; engine-admission bypasses feed FL-INV-02/06; cancellation/resource/error promotion feeds FL-INV-07. A campaign run that merely executes without killing its named mutant is a failure.
acceptance_criteria: All six campaign frameworks, their strict schemas/validators, and a machine-checked production-adapter owner matrix are complete. W1 demonstrates each framework on a real controlled target with intended-reason mutation kills, budgeted replay, exact fault final states, signature-preserving shrink, no-mock attestation, and productive semantic 1/8/32 comparison. D7 claim classes remain honest and release artifacts contain no mutation controls. Every production adapter remains mandatory on its downstream owner and cannot close there until active and green. All model/mutation/E2E/logging/recovery requirements pass; no compile-only kill, denominator manipulation, restart-only drill, automatic fixture edit, fake thread lane, mock release evidence, or deferred campaign family satisfies closure.

title: W7: grind reimplementation — congruence + e-matching, honestly staged L-levels
description:
Reimplement grind natively (§12.6): grind is core-toolchain machinery at the pin, so under the Oracle-Only Law it is reimplemented — congruence closure, e-matching, and its theory hooks as Anvil engines behind facade rows — developed against instrumented-oracle decision traces and Reference-as-oracle differentials.

Staging is deliberate and honest: its Parity-Ledger rows ENTER at L1/L2 and ratchet to L3 on the grind corpus by G4 rather than pretending day-one exactness, because grind semantics are still moving upstream and the census pins our coupling per epoch. Mathlib-layer automation (aesop, polyrith, ...) is user code on Golem and needs NO reimplementation. All outputs kernel-checked per FL-INV-06.

Acceptance: the grind corpus at L3 by G4 (goal-closure agreement with classified exclusions); decision-trace replay green on the covered fragment; epoch-diff tooling flags upstream grind changes as census deltas requiring row review. Tests: congruence-closure property tests (equivalence-class correctness vs a simple oracle implementation); e-matching trigger fixtures from the traces; differential goal families with the exclusion ledger explicit.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Reimplement the pinned grind core natively: congruence closure, e-matching, theory hooks, and Native Mirror facade rows are project code developed from instrumented-oracle traces and differential observations. Upstream grind implementation never executes as a component; mathlib-layer automation remains user code on Golem.
2. All grind-produced proof artifacts are replayed and kernel-checked before environment admission. Unsupported, exhausted, cancelled, malformed, or divergent cases yield classified refusal/Inconclusive and cannot be cached or rendered as rejection/acceptance.
3. Parity rows begin honestly at L1/L2 and reach L3 on the declared grind corpus by G4 with goal-closure agreement and an explicit, reproducible exclusion ledger. Covered decision traces replay; epoch census-diff tooling flags every upstream grind/facade change for row review and prevents stale evidence promotion.
4. Named unit/property/model/differential/fuzz/mutation suites cover equivalence-class correctness against a simple independent oracle, congruence explanations, e-matching triggers/substitutions, theory-hook boundaries, trace replay, facade dispatch, budgets, deterministic ordering, and exclusion classification. Unsound merge, missed trigger, unchecked output, hidden exclusion, and stale-epoch mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs real grind corpus goals and user automation through Golem, compares closure/refusal and covered traces with the pinned Reference, replays proofs through the kernel, exercises an excluded and over-budget case, applies a synthetic epoch census delta, and verifies clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/goal/trace/eclass/ematch/proof/Parity-row ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/epoch/mode/profile/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical goal/result/proof roots, expected/actual closure/refusal and first divergence, exclusion reason, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives golden traces, corpus/exclusion manifests, proof receipts, epoch diffs, mutant outcomes, and E2E bundles. The bead cannot claim L3 beyond covered rows or treat a classified exclusion as agreement.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — GrindTheoryHookManifestV1 assigns every pinned hook to grind or a named owned W7 engine. A row reaches L3 only with no unclassified divergence; exclusions and unsupported rows retain lower or excluded states. An independent simple model detects errors, but kernel replay of emitted proof artifacts is authority. Named suites: grind_congruence_model, grind_ematch_substitution, grind_epoch_row_guard, grind_no_mock_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Unknown schema, missing/duplicate ids, broken order/linkage, unbound artifacts, truncation, merged streams, or cleanup/final-state mismatch fail. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: W11: WASM Judge browser — offline sealed-capsule verification
description:
Implement the WASM Judge half of plan §16.4b according to the versioned OQ-12 packaging contract. Ship the certified K1 and structurally independent fln-checker seats, bounded sealed-capsule decoder, certificate and receipt verification, transparency-log inclusion/consistency checks, and an integrity-bound stock-browser shell. A user can open the package offline, select or drag a real .flnpack, re-check declarations and dependency closure against the same canonical vectors as native execution, inspect verdicts and graph slices, and export a verifiable result without trusting a network service.

Preserve the trust boundary exactly. Unknown package/capsule/certificate versions fail closed; UI, worker, core WASM, and fixture assets are content-bound by the selected packaging contract; no JavaScript result can manufacture kernel acceptance; disagreement halts; certificate failure falls back to bounded recomputation where the contract permits; resource exhaustion/cancellation is Inconclusive; no filesystem, network, platform libm, native plugin, or hidden oracle participates. Full browser elaboration remains explicitly outside Tier 1 and cannot inflate the Judge claim.

The browser surface is accessible by keyboard and screen reader, gives progressive but honestly graded progress, renders stable typed errors for corrupt/epoch-mismatched/unsupported/oversized inputs, and survives worker restart, refresh, retry, and interrupted capsule streaming without remembering false success. Declaration and dependency browsing is bounded and paginated. Browser-local state is versioned and never treated as attestation without fresh integrity and receipt verification.

Verification includes unit/property/model tests for manifest binding, version negotiation, streaming decode, budgets, certificate fallback, receipt/log consistency, result export/import, worker isolation, accessibility state, and native/WASM vector identity. Named mutants bypass an asset hash, accept an unknown version, trust a UI verdict, skip one engine seat, promote Inconclusive, drop a dependency, ignore a byte flip, reuse stale browser state, or report success before terminal verification. The no-mock E2E runs offline in every declared stock-browser engine against a real sealed mathlib-slice capsule, verifies native-vector identity, rejects tampered capsule and mismatched UI/core assets, exercises budget exhaustion and worker crash, refreshes/retries, and proves clean recovery and final state.

Emit concise accessible human status and retained bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate, browser/platform/epoch/mode/profile/build and asset hashes, package/capsule/receipt/certificate/input/output ids, policy and engine seats, expected versus actual verdict/error/consumption, monotonic timings, memory and decode budgets, worker/exit status, console and artifact references, first divergence, recovery, cleanup, and asserted final state.

## Acceptance Criteria

The Judge closes only when the OQ-12 package is shipped as an integrity-bound offline stock-browser experience; real sealed-capsule verification, native/WASM vector identity, two-seat disagreement behavior, bounded decoding/recomputation, accessible browsing, every named negative and mutation, worker/refresh recovery, and the declared browser matrix pass with archived reproducible human and NDJSON evidence. A core-only WASM file, mock browser, network service, UI-trusted result, skipped browser, hidden oracle, or deferred listed Tier-1 feature fails closure.
acceptance_criteria:
The Judge closes only when the OQ-12 package is shipped as an integrity-bound offline stock-browser experience; real sealed-capsule verification, native/WASM vector identity, two-seat disagreement behavior, bounded decoding/recomputation, accessible browsing, every named negative and mutation, worker/refresh recovery, and the declared browser matrix pass with archived reproducible human and NDJSON evidence. For every declared browser engine, two cold-state runs of the same package, capsule, epoch, mode, policy, and budgets must produce byte-identical exported verification payloads and identical canonical semantic records: declaration order, dependency closure, seat verdicts, errors, consumption, receipt/certificate ids, and final state. Timings, browser-generated run ids, and artifact paths are explicitly excluded from equality; worker scheduling, refresh, and retry may not change semantics. Package assembly also repeats from the same locked inputs and must yield identical asset order, hashes, manifest, and distributable bytes. A core-only WASM file, mock browser, network service, UI-trusted result, skipped browser, hidden oracle, nondeterministic semantic/export output, or deferred listed Tier-1 feature fails closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — lock and verify every build input, bundle root, service-worker asset, and offline cache generation. Stale service-worker/cache states are detected, quarantined, cleared or migrated transactionally, and recovered without mixing generations. Unsigned local reports are explicitly non-release evidence; embedded transparency proofs are separately verified and cannot be inferred from local success. Real offline browser tests cover first load, stale upgrade, corruption, interruption, and recovery.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.

title: W2: Compacted regions — construction, mmap load, relocation, page sharing
description:
Implement compacted regions (§6.4): region construction (object-graph serialization with pointer relocation tables), region loading (mmap, relocate-or-copy fallback, page-sharing across consumers — the enabler of the Lantern shared import heap §7.5), and region hygiene (immutability enforcement, persistent-RC marking).

Design law: this is deliberately THE SAME code path the Grimoire olean codec uses — olean fidelity and runtime-region fidelity are one test surface, so every codec round-trip test also exercises the runtime region machinery and vice versa. mmap and arena primitives beyond what asupersync region heaps provide live in fln-unsafe-region with ledger rows (D3).

Acceptance: a real mathlib olean region loads via mmap with relocation and is traversable as live objects (G0-1 promotion to production code); page-sharing verified across two consumers of one loaded region (RSS accounting shows shared pages, the PG-4/PG-6 mechanism); immutability violations trap in hardened builds. Tests: relocate-or-copy fallback forced and verified; corrupted-relocation-table fuzzing under resource budgets (hostile olean is a DoS surface even in safe Rust — R18); crash-during-construction drills leaving no half-published region (atomic staging).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: One compacted-region implementation owns object-graph construction, relocation-table emission/validation, immutable persistent marking, mmap loading, relocate-or-copy fallback, page sharing and atomic publication; the olean codec uses this exact path. Every pointer/offset/tag/length/reachability/allocation/expanded-weight boundary is validated under budgets before live exposure. A real pinned mathlib olean region loads through mmap and is traversable as Marrow objects; two consumers share immutable pages with retained RSS/page evidence. Fallback yields identical objects/roots, and hardened writes to immutable regions fail typed. Corrupt/truncated/future relocation data, unavailable mmap, cancellation, resource exhaustion and crash during construction leave no visible half region and recover cleanly. All project-authored memory-map primitives remain isolated and ledgered in fln-unsafe-region with structural no-authority laws. Unit/property/model/fuzz/mutation tests cover layout/relocation permutations, aliasing, fallback, sharing, immutability, budgets and every promotion boundary; named wrong-relocation, bounds-skip, private-copy-disguised-as-share, partial-publish and unsafe-launder mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E performs load/share/fallback/corruption/fault/restoration and retains strict region/page/root/process/final-state bundles. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, unsafe/dependency/codec/fuzz/fault/platform lanes pass. No second codec region path, unchecked relocation, copy presented as sharing, mock-only mmap, mutable published region, partial publication, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.

title: W1: Windows functional CI — early platform honesty, certification deferred
description:
Stand up windows-x86-64 functional CI from W1 (§18.3, D17): windows is FUNCTIONAL from the start (build, unit tests, the growing conformance subset), while bit-certification under --reproducible remains a separate declared decision made later (the manim precedent).

Why early: platform assumptions fossilize fast — path handling, UTF-16 position conversions at the LSP boundary, dlopen vs LoadLibrary in the plugin door, mmap semantics in region loading, process spawning in the D2 subprocess protocol. Discovering these at W12 would mean re-opening frozen interfaces. The certified matrix (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64) runs the determinism closure; windows runs the functional lanes and reports honestly in the Parity Ledger platform column.

Acceptance: CI matrix includes windows building the workspace and running the unit + conformance-smoke lanes; platform-conditional code carries explicit capability errors (never silent substitution); the Parity Ledger platform column reports windows rows at their honest L-levels. Tests: the CI lane itself, plus a fixture asserting that platform-gated features fail closed with typed CapabilityDenied errors rather than degrading silently.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — Windows functional evidence consumes the Tribunal epoch, comparison-class, Parity-Ledger, outcome, and shared semantic-log contracts; it does not invent platform-specific evidence dialects. A real native windows-x86-64 runner is mandatory for closure. Unavailable infrastructure, unsupported capability, resource refusal, cancellation, or runner fault is a typed non-passing row; it cannot be reported green or converted into a semantic divergence.

Freeze a platform-capability matrix covering paths/separators and case behavior, UTF-8/UTF-16 position conversion, dynamic-library discovery/loading, mapped-region and atomic replace semantics, D2 subprocess support, cancellation/termination equivalents, file locking, symlink/reparse behavior, and every supported CLI/server/plugin lane. Portable semantic comparisons bind identical logical inputs and declared normalization; Windows-specific results retain their own comparison class. Functional L-level and --reproducible certification remain separate axes.

Canonical evidence contains exact input/output roots, capability and semantic decisions, comparison classes, outcomes, and cleanup/final state. Host identity, timestamps, duration, job/PID, absolute paths, scheduler details, and performance counters remain linked telemetry and do not perturb semantic roots. Windows population belongs here; the Tribunal bootstrap may register a BLOCKED platform row but cannot mark it passed without this real-host bundle.
acceptance_criteria: A required real native windows-x86-64 CI lane uses the exact locked toolchain/closure and consumes Tribunal/Parity/shared evidence schemas. It builds every supported workspace/target and runs unit plus growing conformance lanes.
