title: W5: FIR pipeline — validated IR stages, pass witnesses, RC/FBIP disciplines
description:
Implement the FIR pipeline (fln-comp, §11.1): elaborated terms -> FIR (ANF/LCNF-class IR keeping the Reference-established compilation disciplines: specialization of instance/closed arguments, simplification, closure conversion, explicit boxing, reference-count insertion with borrow inference and constructor-reuse/FBIP analysis — kept because FBIP-style reuse is the performance model the ecosystem code is written against and is observable through performance cliffs) -> FLBC register bytecode.

Engineering law: every pipeline stage has a versioned IR schema, a deterministic printer, and a validator; every optimization pass is a pure ValidatedIr -> (ValidatedIr, PassWitness) transformation with declared preconditions, a preservation argument or executable validator, a resource-growth bound, deterministic ordering, and a fallback to its input. The RC-insertion pass runs an ownership validator (no use-after-consume, balanced joins and exception edges, capture correctness, ABI ownership contracts) deliberately SIMPLER than the optimizer it audits. Executable-closure selection records WHY each declaration is retained (direct call, closure capture, initializer, reflection, exported symbol, conservative opaque edge) so dead-code questions have answers.

Acceptance: the G0-3 micro-corpus compiles through the full pipeline with every pass witness validating; compiled results observably identical to interpretation of the unoptimized form (pass-preservation differential). Tests: IR printer/parser round-trips; ownership-validator mutation tests (a planted dropped-retain must be caught); pass-ordering determinism at {1,8,32} threads.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

## Success Criteria

All five exact children close with target-neutral canonical stages, pass witnesses, independent ownership validation, only same-stage optional fallback, typed mandatory-lowering refusal, complete differential/mutation/no-mock evidence, and exact recovery.
design:
OQ-4 HANDOFF — this FIR implementation depends on fln-iron-target-profile-contract-ba9 and must implement its selected target-independent lowering and machine-boundary contract. FIR validation and pass witnesses must reject backend-specific leakage not authorized by that contract; any incompatible change reopens the decision bead rather than creating a second implicit backend interface.

TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are fln-8m1z FIR schema/ingress, franken_lean-tiw9 specialization/simplification, fln-41jd closure conversion/boxing, fln-6n1k RC/borrow/FBIP plus independent validator, and franken_lean-o6bl mandatory FLBC lowering. This epic closes only on a machine-validated join of those exact child implementation, fixture, mutation, E2E, and evidence roots; prose-only completion or future-consumer evidence cannot substitute.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement the complete deterministic elaborated-term to FIR to FLBC pipeline with versioned schemas, parsers/printers, and validators for every stage, preserving specialization, simplification, closure conversion, boxing, RC insertion with borrow inference, constructor reuse/FBIP analysis, and executable-closure selection with explicit retention reasons.
2. Every optimization is a pure ValidatedIr to ValidatedIr plus PassWitness transformation declaring preconditions, preservation evidence or executable validation, resource-growth bound, deterministic ordering, and failure fallback to its input. The independent ownership validator checks consumes, joins, exception edges, captures, and ABI ownership contracts and remains structurally simpler than the optimizer it audits.
3. Named unit/property/metamorphic/differential/mutation suites cover schema compatibility, printer/parser round trips, validator rejection, pass witnesses and fallback, closure-retention reasons, RC/borrow/FBIP edges, exception paths, resource bounds, cancellation, and compiled optimized behavior equal to unoptimized interpretation. Dropped-retain, use-after-consume, bad join, missing capture, false-retention, and pass-order mutants must be killed.
4. G0-3 micro-corpus programs compile through every stage and validate every witness. Pipeline outputs, diagnostics, witnesses, and FLBC are bit-identical at thread counts 1, 8, and 32 for the same inputs and profile.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane compiles and executes representative user-class programs and a real plugin, compares optimized FLBC results/effects with unoptimized interpretation, injects a planted ownership defect and rejected pass, verifies input fallback, and then proves clean recovery. Human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON include run/program/stage/pass/witness ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/cwd, schema/build/profile/thread/seed facts, canonical IR/program/output hashes, retention/ownership decisions, expected/actual, growth/resource/timing/status facts, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives stage IRs, witnesses, validator reports, deterministic thread matrices, mutant results, and evidence manifests; close evidence names every suite and may not claim semantic preservation, ownership safety, or performance from mocked execution.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — an optional same-stage optimization may fall back only to independently validated same-stage input. Failure of mandatory lowering returns typed Inconclusive or InternalFault and publishes nothing; no fallback may cross incompatible IR stages or fabricate output. Executable-validator evidence is classified separately from proof claims. Boundary/model/mutation tests cover each pass, stage identity, validator independence, optional rollback, mandatory refusal, cancellation, and exact recovery.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical authoritative NDJSON contains normalized semantic inputs, roots, decisions, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, time, paths, cache/performance, and scheduler facts are linked bounded telemetry only. Independent validators reject unknown schema, extra/missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, telemetry in semantic hashes, and final-state mismatch. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves nonempty completed work and canonical reduction.

title: W9: Forensics, semantic diff/merge, why-trusts, time travel, SQL
description:
Implement the forensics + semantic diff/merge surfaces (§15.2, §15.3, §15.3b, §15.3c, §15.4, §15.5): unification forensics (sound-mode failures report the MINIMAL DISAGREEMENT — smallest subterm pair, transparency in force, approximation stage that gave up, assignment trail — rendered as a diff, not a wall; faithful renders upstream text verbatim); instance forensics (the Synod why-not tree with a one-line head above the expandable tree); both projections of one mechanism — the semantic blame slice (minimal or near-minimal-honestly-labeled causal subgraph, stable node ids: robots get the typed slice, humans get the rendering).

Semantic diff (fln diff): levels source -> syntax -> interface -> proof -> reduction -> extension -> executable -> evidence, so "proof-only change" is a CHECKABLE claim. Semantic merge: branches merge automatically when the graph proves their transactions commute AND the combined declarations re-check through Crucible; text-conflict-but-semantically-independent proposes the patch; text-clean-but-semantics-collide (instance/simp/notation effects) BLOCKS the silent merge and explains — the failure mode Git cannot see; nothing merges around the kernel. Fragility signals (§15.3c): advisory-only (D3), constitutionally barred from Crucible. fln why-trusts: the trust cone walk (axioms, sorryAx, native_decide receipts + tiers, extern/plugin surfaces, --trust extensions). Time travel: goal-state scrubbing, fln replay <decl> from the recorded closure, repro bundles packing a bug input closure. Build forensics: the SQL surface (fln doctor --sql + shipped report views).

Acceptance: forensic outputs validated on curated failure corpora (unification and instance failures with known causes); merge-conflict fixtures demonstrate the semantic-block on Git-clean merges; why-trusts output matches hand-computed cones. Tests: blame-slice minimality checks on seeded contradictions; diff-level property tests (a proof-only edit shows empty interface diff); merge commute-proof mutation tests; replay determinism fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this bead owns OQ-11. Define semantic-merge scope v1 as an explicit allowlist of extension/effect classes proven commutative, the read/write and generated-name conditions, the cases requiring replay in both orders, and the mandatory human-resolution boundary. Record the decision and test both false-positive prevention (text-clean semantic conflict blocks) and false-negative avoidance on proved-independent changes.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Unification and instance failures expose typed semantic blame slices with stable node ids: smallest or honestly labeled near-minimal disagreement, transparency and approximation stage, assignment/candidate trail, and exact causal spans. Sound mode renders concise diff/why-not views; faithful mode preserves pinned diagnostic text while retaining typed evidence out of band. 2. OQ-11 is resolved before semantic-merge/API freeze by a versioned reviewed effect-class table that distinguishes provably commutative, commutative only under explicit preconditions, proposal-only, and human-resolution-mandatory extension effects. Each row names required completeness grade, proof/witness, root and epoch preconditions, conflict behavior, apply authority, rollback, fixtures, and evidence. Unknown, Opaque, Observed-only, or incompletely witnessed effects never auto-merge. fln diff reports source, syntax, interface, proof, reduction, extension, executable, and evidence deltas so proof-only/interface-stable claims are mechanically checked. Semantic merge proceeds only when Complete/Conservative graph evidence and the OQ-11 row prove transaction commutativity and the combined result rechecks through Crucible; semantic collisions in text-clean changes block with an explanation, while safe text conflicts only propose a patch until explicit apply. 3. Fragility signals remain advisory and have no path into Crucible/trust decisions. why-trusts walks complete transitive cones for axioms, sorryAx, native_decide tier receipts, plugins/externs/facade classes, and --trust. Time travel scrubs recorded goals, replay reconstructs the exact declaration closure, repro bundles seal inputs, and doctor --sql exposes versioned read-only report views with bounded queries. 4. Named unit/property/metamorphic/model/mutation suites cover blame minimality/stability, faithful rendering, each diff layer, every OQ-11 effect class and precondition, commute proofs, combined kernel recheck, trust-cone completeness, advisory isolation, replay determinism, bundle closure, SQL schemas/limits, cancellation, and recovery. Hidden-semantic-conflict, unknown-effect-auto-merge, weak-completeness-merge, merge-without-check, incomplete-trust-cone, advisory-to-kernel, and nonminimal-blame mutants must be killed. 5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs curated real unification/instance failures, proof-only and interface-changing diffs, one safe merge per auto-merge-eligible OQ-11 class, proposal-only and human-required refusals, a Git-clean semantic collision, why-trusts against a hand-computed project, goal time travel/replay from a sealed bundle, and SQL reports; planted unknown/incomplete and bad merges are blocked and recovery verified. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/declaration/failure/blame/diff/transaction/effect-class/trust/replay/query ids, OQ-11 decision version, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd/SQL parameters, epoch/mode/profile/platform/seed/budget facts, canonical source/environment/graph/receipt/bundle/rollback roots, expected/actual slice/diff/merge/verdict/query, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state. 6. CI archives the reviewed OQ-11 table, failure corpora, minimality witnesses, diff/merge proofs, trust-cone fixtures, replay bundles, SQL goldens, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. No merge mutates user files without explicit apply and no advisory, unknown effect, weak graph grade, or mocked graph can justify trust or invalidation.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — why-trusts reports completeness grade and conservative potential separately from observed causality; an Opaque or Observed cone is never called complete. Every answer binds amv.11 provenance ids, graph generation, evidence roots, omissions, unknowns, and query budget. Unit/model/property/mutation and real CLI E2E cover cycles, incomplete history, unavailable producers, bounded traversal, deterministic witness order, and honest refusal.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.

title: W2: olean codec write + .ilean — byte-identical faithful emission, atomic promotion
description:
Implement the olean codec, write side + .ilean (§7.3, §7.3b): region construction the Reference loads — identical header discipline, identical object layout — and in faithful mode BYTE-IDENTICAL output for FL-INV-04 (read a Reference olean, rebuild, diff bytes = empty; then the harder fresh-elaboration direction for the deterministic subset, residual freedoms pinned by registered CGSE policies per spike G0-5).

Two identities travel with every stored object: semantic hash (canonical meaning under the schema) and byte hash (stored encoding). Cross-file artifact sets (.olean + .ilean + native siblings) promote through a transaction manifest: staged, verified, then published atomically together or rolled back together — a crash mid-promotion leaves unreachable staging, never a half-published module. The .ilean codec is an exact epoch projection (spans, identifier occurrences, resolution targets with the pin field order, omissions, and position conventions) generated from the same normative schema layer as the olean codec so writer and checker cannot skew; richer server metadata lives in Palimpsest — .ilean stays exactly as boring as the ecosystem expects.

Acceptance: FL-INV-04 round-trip green at 100% of the Corpus set (PG-8); Reference toolchain loads and checks our written oleans (mixed-producer matrix seed); kill -9 drills at every promotion step leave recoverable state. Tests: byte-diff rigs per corpus file; .ilean field-order fixtures; the fault drill family (§18.2) for promotion.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: The writer builds olean regions and ilean artifacts from the same generated normative schema as the reader/checker. For 100 percent of the pinned Reference-produced Corpus set, faithful read then rebuild then write is byte-identical under FL-INV-04; fresh elaboration matches the Reference on the explicitly ratified deterministic subset, with every residual serialization freedom governed by the G0-5 CGSE table and honest Parity Ledger scope. Semantic and byte hashes both travel with each stored object and cannot be conflated. Ilean field order, omissions, byte positions, occurrence kinds and resolution targets match the pin exactly; richer server metadata remains outside ilean. Multi-artifact sets stage, verify and atomically publish through one manifest so kill/crash/disk-full/cancellation at every step leaves either the old complete set or unreachable recoverable staging, never mixed visibility. Unknown versions, malformed internal state, conflicts, and budgets fail typed and atomic. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation tests cover every schema field, freedom policy, byte/semantic hash, deterministic order, ilean record, manifest state, fsync/link/promotion step, retry, and recovery; named reordered-field, omitted-byte, hash-conflation, half-publish, stale-manifest and frontier-leak mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E round-trips the full Corpus, loads our outputs in the Reference, performs mixed-producer/interleaved builds, injects each promotion fault, restores, and retains strict bytediff/manifest/root/process/final-state bundles. PG-8 write identity is complete; dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, codec/differential/fault/reproducibility/platform lanes pass. No canonical-but-different substitute for required faithful bytes, handwritten schema, ilean enrichment, partial artifact publication, silent fallback, mock-only Reference consumer, unclassified residual, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.

title: W9: Palimpsest — event journal, causal proof graph, the welding law
description:
Implement the Palimpsest substrate + causal proof graph (§15.1): structured elaboration events — unifier attempts, instance candidates, macro expansions, scheduler decisions, cache hits, kernel verdicts — recorded per declaration into ring-buffered traces (always-on cheap tier) and full archives (opt-in, frankensqlite-backed), keyed to Syntax ranges. Recording NEVER changes results (observer discipline, enforced by dual-run rigs: traced and untraced runs must produce identical products).

From the journal, the causal proof graph (Bet B7): typed nodes (source ranges, commands, expansions, declarations, instances, searches, tactic steps, kernel/checker verdicts, extension entries, build facets, capability sessions, branches/merges) and typed EDGE FAMILIES (IMPORTS; READS_TYPE vs READS_VALUE vs UNFOLDS; SELECTS_INSTANCE/REJECTS_INSTANCE; USES_SIMP_RULE; EXPANDS_FROM/GENERATED_BY; READS_GRAMMAR/WRITES_GRAMMAR; PROVES/CHECKED_BY/CROSS_CHECKED_BY; COMPILES_TO/EXPORTS_SYMBOL/INITIALIZES_BEFORE; READS_FILE/SPAWNS_TOOL/LOADS_LIBRARY; INVALIDATES/REUSES; SUGGESTED_BY; MIGRATES_TO/REPAIRS), every edge carrying span, phase, policy/epoch, receipt id, and a COMPLETENESS CLASS: Complete (perturbation-tested), Conservative (may over-approximate, must not miss), Observed, Opaque, Inferred.

THE WELDING LAW (stated once, enforced everywhere): only Complete and Conservative edges may drive Ledger invalidation or any trust decision; Observed/Inferred may rank, explain, suggest — never invalidate. Impact cones (interface, definitional, instance/search, syntax, executable, evidence, conservative-potential) computed from deltas — the same cones the Ledger matrix consumes and reviewers read as release risk. Storage: franken_networkx in-process over frankensqlite journals; optional frankengraphdb export is frontier, derived, never load-bearing. Acceptance: observer-discipline dual-run rig green; cone computation validated against the invalidation matrix fixtures; completeness-class enforcement CI check (an Observed edge reaching the invalidation path must be structurally impossible). Tests: journal crash-recovery; graph rebuild-from-journal determinism; export redaction fixtures (§15.3c privacy law).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Journal structured per-declaration events for unifier attempts, instance candidates, macro expansions, scheduler decisions, cache outcomes, kernel/checker verdicts, effects, and publication into an always-on bounded tier plus opt-in full frankensqlite archives keyed to exact Syntax ranges. Traced and untraced dual runs must have identical semantic products, diagnostics, artifacts, and logical roots.
2. Build the causal graph with all plan-specified typed node kinds and edge families. Every edge records source span, phase, policy, epoch, receipt/evidence id, and exactly one completeness class: Complete, Conservative, Observed, Opaque, or Inferred. Graph rebuild from the same journal is deterministic.
3. Enforce the welding law structurally: only Complete and Conservative edges can reach Ledger invalidation or trust decisions; Observed and Inferred may only rank, explain, or suggest. Interface, definitional, instance/search, syntax, executable, evidence, and conservative-potential impact cones match perturbation-validated invalidation fixtures. Optional frankengraphdb export is derived/frontier and never load-bearing.
4. Named unit/property/metamorphic/model/mutation suites cover event schemas, ring overflow, archive transactions, every node/edge/class, cone propagation, welding policy, traced/untraced equivalence, rebuild determinism, redaction/privacy, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and crash recovery. Observer-effect, dropped-edge, class-escalation, inferred-invalidation, and redaction-leak mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane elaborates real declarations with and without tracing, verifies identical products, queries all relevant impact-cone classes, crashes/restarts the real journal store, rebuilds the graph, rejects a planted Observed-to-invalidation path, exports a redacted bundle, and confirms final state. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/declaration/event/node/edge/receipt/transaction ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical source/product/journal/graph/logical roots, completeness and cone decisions, expected/actual, timings/resources/status, artifact/WAL paths, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives dual-run diffs, perturbation/cone witnesses, graph rebuild hashes, fault matrices, privacy fixtures, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. No invalidation, trust, completeness, or observer-discipline claim closes from mocked persistence or an untested edge family.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — ring overflow, truncation, dropped events, unknown producer versions, and observation gaps emit typed incompleteness/drop facts bound to exact producer and interval. They never masquerade as a complete causal graph. Synthetic future-edge fixtures test schema compatibility only and cannot satisfy real producer adoption. Model/fault tests cover wraparound, concurrent readers, interruption, replay, exact loss accounting, and clean restart.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. title: G0-5: Byte-identical olean re-emit spike — enumerate and pin serialization freedoms
description:
G0-5 Byte-identical re-emit spike (§22.1-5, feeds §7.3): read a set of Reference-produced oleans, rebuild the compacted region from the decoded object graph, and byte-diff the output against the original. Then the harder direction: elaborate a module fresh (prototype path) and match the Reference emission for the deterministic subset.

This is the feasibility proof for FL-INV-04 (codec fidelity: read-then-write is byte identity in faithful mode) and directly prices risk R3 (byte-exact write over-constrained by hidden emission nondeterminism). Every serialization freedom found — object traversal order, region layout choices, padding, interning decisions — must be ENUMERATED and pinned with a registered CGSE policy, or (if genuinely nondeterministic upstream) documented as the scoped-down law of R3 (load-compatible + canonical write with the byte law kept for read-then-write).

Acceptance: (a) read->rebuild->bytediff == identical for the fixture set; (b) an exhaustive written enumeration of serialization freedoms with a pinned policy per row; (c) a verdict memo: FL-INV-04 stands as stated, or the R3 fallback scope is invoked with exact wording for the Parity Ledger row. Tests: the round-trip harness becomes the standing codec rig (§18.2 codec rigs) and the FL-INV-04 CI gate at PG-8.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — G0-5 owns OQ-1 and emits a checked OleanSerializationFreedomV1 row for every generated OLEAN-contract field and writer stage, not merely freedoms noticed informally. Each row binds stable id, source/contract anchor, affected byte range/object relation, pinned Reference behavior, semantic and byte effects, deterministic CGSE policy, mode/epoch/platform scope, fixtures/perturbations, claim/evidence class, and immutable roots. Missing, duplicate, stale, unexercised, or unknown rows block OQ-1.

G0-5 is finite feasibility evidence, not formal proof of FL-INV-04. Exact read/rebuild/re-emit is an invariant claim in OBSERVED state over the complete manifest; serializer/state models are bounded_model. FL-INV-04 remains TARGETED outside that evidence until its standing Corpus gate enforces it.

Emit exactly one G0SpikeDecisionV1 outcome: RatifyExactReadReemit; RatifyExactReadReemitAndInvokeR3ForFreshEmission, with the exact deterministic fresh-emission subset and §25 amendment; or NoGo. R3 may narrow only the fresh-emission promise. Failure of faithful read→rebuild→write byte identity cannot be converted into load-compatible success or an amendment.

The fixture manifest binds producer binary/pin/platform, exact bytes, module/import closure, contract root, semantic and byte hashes, environment normalization, freedom rows exercised, and expected Reference/FrankenLean load results. Both implementations load the re-emitted artifact in separate Tribunal contexts. Fresh prototype emission has an explicit deterministic subset and cannot borrow the Reference writer.

Budgets cover input/output bytes, object/edge/relocation counts, depth, expanded weight, interning, allocations/reservations, logical work, cancellation, and log volume with checked arithmetic, atomic output publication, failure recovery, and retry. Productive 1/8/32 rebuilding/validation uses real partitions and canonical merge while respecting format order. Canonical semantic rows exclude host/time/PID/path/performance telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Every manifest-bound Reference artifact satisfies exact read→rebuild→emit byte identity plus graph/semantic/load checks, and fresh prototype emission matches only its explicitly registered deterministic subset. OleanSerializationFreedomV1 is row-complete over every generated contract field/writer stage and records source anchor, byte/object effects, Reference behavior, CGSE policy, scope, fixtures, perturbations, and evidence. Named serialization_freedom_totality, byte_semantic_dual_hash_model, codec_budget_matrix, atomic_output_recovery, mixed_loader_matrix, productive_rebuild_determinism, and g0_5_no_mock_e2e suites cover all freedoms, malformed/future inputs, budgets/cancellation/retry, separate Reference/FrankenLean loads, and productive 1/8/32 work. Mutants include semantic/byte hash swap, relocation base, nondeterministic metadata, equal-semantics/different-bytes normalization, partial output, freedom-row omission, comparison downgrade, stale interning/padding/order, hidden Reference writer, and fake thread. G0SpikeDecisionV1 is exactly RatifyExactReadReemit, RatifyExactReadReemitAndInvokeR3ForFreshEmission with formal §25 amendment and full rerun, or NoGo. Claim/evidence classes and semantic/telemetry logs remain separate. No load-compatible substitution for read-write identity, prose-only inventory, unclassified mismatch, or hidden nondeterminism satisfies closure.

title: W12: Witness claim matrix + docs CI — evidence-gated wording, safe mode, auto-demotion
description:
Implement Witness — the claim-to-proof matrix + documentation CI (§20.4, Bet B8): every public claim (compatibility, soundness, determinism, performance, recovery, security) is a row — claim id, exact text or template, allowed evidence state (OBSERVED with a no-mock reproduction command | TARGETED | HYPOTHESIS | PROVEN with stated scope | BLOCKED | RETIRED), owner workstream, scope (epoch/platform/mode/corpus), required gate + artifact schemas, freshness predicate, forbidden stronger wording, fallback text.

Documentation CI parses the docs against the matrix and REJECTS wording stronger than the evidence permits; coverage counts are generated from the ledgers, never hand-maintained; a benchmark is OBSERVED, never PROVEN; a Crucible-checked theorem is PROVEN relative to the assumptions its receipt names. Companion machinery: SAFE MODE — on InternalFault, checker disagreement, or corruption detection, the affected process fails closed, quarantines its caches, and requires independent revalidation before its outputs are trusted again; AUTO-DEMOTION — a promoted lane breaching thresholds demotes itself and files the incident bundle. The system explains its own behavior with the same rigor it demands of proofs.

Also here: the public Parity Ledger dashboard and the Behavior Notes register (BN-01..BN-08 seeded from §18.8, grown by evidence) as published, generated artifacts. Acceptance: docs CI catches a seeded overclaim (planted wording stronger than the matrix state must fail the build); safe-mode drills quarantine and revalidate; the dashboard regenerates from ledger data alone. Tests: matrix-schema validation; freshness-expiry fixtures (a stale OBSERVED claim must demote to its fallback text); auto-demotion incident-bundle fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

## Success Criteria

Claim schema/docs, durable quarantine/revalidation, and promotion/demotion/incident children close on exact roots. D7 claim type stays orthogonal to evidence state; demotion precedes serving; quarantine survives restart; re-promotion is explicit; docs never overstate evidence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — seed and validate Behavior Notes BN-01 through BN-09, including BN-09 for --trust journaling (the plan references BN-09 at §17.1 even though §18.8's seed paragraph stops at BN-08). Witness schema/docs CI can be built early, but this bead closes only after its runtime companions exist: consensus faults, Ledger quarantine/revalidation, and shadow-run auto-demotion. Its new dependencies make that closure honest.

TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are franken_lean-n8hw claim schema/docs/dashboard/Behavior Notes, fln-wxr6 durable safe-mode quarantine/revalidation, and fln-zfyt promotion/demotion/incident bundles. This epic closes only on a machine-validated join proving D7 claim types remain orthogonal, quarantine precedes serving, and no automatic re-promotion occurs.

1. Every public compatibility, soundness, determinism, performance, recovery, and security statement maps to a versioned Witness row containing stable claim id, exact text/template, state OBSERVED/TARGETED/HYPOTHESIS/PROVEN/BLOCKED/RETIRED, owner, epoch/platform/mode/corpus scope, required gates/artifact schemas, freshness predicate, evidence roots, forbidden stronger wording, and safe fallback text. State semantics preserve the plan distinction: benchmark observations are never proofs and proven statements name assumptions.
2. Documentation CI parses all designated public surfaces, rejects missing/unknown claims and wording stronger or broader than the live row permits, generates counts from ledgers rather than hand-maintained totals, expires stale evidence to fallback text, and prevents skipped/waived tests from satisfying their claims. The public dashboard and seeded/growing Behavior Notes registry are reproducibly generated from authoritative data alone.
3. Safe mode activates on InternalFault, checker disagreement, corruption, or specified evidence failure; it fails closed, quarantines affected outputs/caches, demotes dependent claims, and requires independent revalidation before trust restoration. Promoted lanes auto-demote on semantic/fidelity/tail breaches and file complete incident bundles without waiting for human action.
4. Named unit/property/golden/model/mutation suites cover matrix schema/state transitions/scopes, doc parsing/templates, freshness/expiry, fallback wording, generated counts/dashboard/BN registry, quarantine/revalidation, promotion/demotion, concurrent evidence updates, and failure atomicity. Overclaim, stale-pass, manual-count, skipped-gate, failed-quarantine, and failed-demotion mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane builds real project documentation/dashboard from live ledgers, plants overstrong and unregistered wording and observes CI failure, expires evidence and verifies fallback, triggers a real safe-mode corruption/checker-disagreement drill, revalidates independently, promotes a qualified cell, plants a breach, and verifies demotion plus incident. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/claim/evidence/doc/gate/cache/promotion/incident ids, bead/invariant/Parity rows, redacted argv/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/corpus/freshness facts, canonical matrix/doc/ledger/artifact roots, expected/actual state/text/action, timings/resources/status, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives matrix snapshots, doc findings, dashboards/Behavior Notes, quarantine/revalidation receipts, incident bundles, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. No public wording survives outside the matrix or at a stronger scope/state than its current no-mock evidence.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — D7 claim_type is orthogonal to evidence state and L/R level; weaker evidence can never justify a stronger claim. Safe-mode quarantine is durable, dependency-scoped, failure-atomic, and independently revalidated. Demotion precedes further publication, produces an incident bundle, and never auto-repromotes; explicit reviewed evidence is required. title: W6: Mirror conformance rig — real tactic code differentials, T2/T3 tiers, PG-M
description:
Stand up the Mirror conformance rig — the metaprogram-corpus differential (§18.2): the ecosystem real tactic and metaprogram code (corpus family C2: packages chosen for metaprogramming/FFI/Lake/syntax weight) executed on Golem against the native facade, with environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, generated names, and authority outcomes diffed against oracle runs. The rig is nightly and includes randomized workloads. This is where census totality and every facade-row L-level are earned continuously.

Also in scope: the T2/T3 differential elaboration tiers (T2: acceptance, diagnostic positions/severities, and statement-level environment identity; T3: term identity under a versioned registered normalizer limited to declared alpha/mdata freedoms); declaration outputs cross-checked through Crucible and fln-checker; trace-replay integration with golden decision traces; deterministic seed and thread matrices; PG-M measurement; and self-tests using seeded facade defects, comparison-class drift, and detection-power mutations.

Harness closure for this bead is finite and precedes G3: the complete production parser/elaborator/scheduler/kernel/Golem/facade path is wired, the rig runs in CI and nightly, G3 Init/Std plus the curated metaprogram slice pass at their declared L-levels, seeded defects are detected, normalizers and artifacts are versioned, and failures produce retained reproducers and first-divergence evidence. Component or mock-only execution cannot close it.

Campaign promotion remains mandatory after harness closure. G4 runs this same registered rig over full mathlib and owns T2 = 100%, the published T3 ratchet, and PG-M <= 1.25x; G6 owns PG-M <= 0.9x and the final L4 matrix. Those later gates cannot close without their campaign evidence, but later corpus thresholds do not create a backward dependency that prevents the harness from existing for G3. The rig remains a permanent continuous obligation; no gate retires or replaces it.

## Acceptance Criteria

Close only when the production integration graph and real no-mock G3 campaigns above are green, every registered comparison class and facade row exercised by the gated slice has deterministic row-linked evidence, seeded dispatch/effect/object-identity/order/error/generated-name defects are detected, T2/T3 normalizers reject undeclared erasure, and human plus schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts make failures reproducible. Full-mathlib T2/T3 and PG-M promotion remain explicitly owned and blocking in G4/G6. Neither mocks, stubs, unclassified rows, a future-gate result reported early, nor deferral of the continuing campaign satisfies closure.
design:
FL-INV-03 ENFORCEMENT AND W6 INTEGRATION RIG — every builtin-census row is exercised at its registered equivalence class through the real parser, elaborator, scheduler, kernel, Golem, and native-facade path, with oracle observations, environment/InfoTree/diagnostic/generated-name comparisons, deterministic seeds, and row-linked evidence. Unknown, skipped, mock-only, or unclassified rows block the claimed L-level. Mutation families perturb dispatch, effect projection, object identity, allocation/order observables, errors, and generated names and prove the rig detects each defect.

The graph must require, not merely mention, the complete production path: Vellum; G2-proven Golem; W3 kernel/checker; monadic transactions; unifier/defeq; term elaboration; Synod; native tactic framework; deterministic scheduler; census facade; and Tribunal campaign machinery. This rig is the concrete integration artifact consumed by G3. Component unit success without this joined no-mock path cannot satisfy W6 or G3.
acceptance_criteria:
The complete parser/elaborator/scheduler/kernel/Golem/facade integration runs the G3 Init/Std and curated real-metaprogram campaigns with deterministic row-linked T2/T3 evidence, versioned normalizers, golden-trace replay, seeded defect detection, negative/recovery coverage, and retained human plus schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts. Every unknown, skipped, mock-only, or unclassified row blocks its claimed L-level. The rig is registered as a permanent CI/nightly obligation. G4 and G6 remain separately blocked on full-mathlib T2 = 100%, the T3 ratchet, and their PG-M thresholds using this same rig; those later campaigns are preserved without creating a backward gate dependency.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — Reference timeout, crash, cancellation, and resource refusal map through the typed oracle adapter and never become semantic rejection. Nightly random workloads use a frozen manifest and seed. Normalizers are row-specific, versioned, bounded, and cannot erase errors, authority, diagnostics, ordering, or unsupported rows. PG-M at G3 is a baseline only; later gates own thresholds. Named suites: comparison_class_guard, normalizer_non_erasure, mirror_campaign_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative evidence is canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON validated independently; telemetry is a separate non-authoritative stream and cannot affect hashes, comparison, admission, cache, publication, or promotion. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/duplicate ids, broken step order, unbound artifacts, silent truncation, merged streams, and cleanup/final-state mismatch. Cancellation, exhaustion, and InternalFault never enter cache, negative storage, proof admission, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 records prove useful overlap, completed work, retries, and schedule identity. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: W3: Kernel differential rigs — foreign witnesses, term storms, delta-debugging
description:
Stand up the standing kernel differential rigs (§8.7, §18.2): Crucible vs the Reference kernel (via lean4checker on shared oleans); Crucible vs lean4lean verified rules (fixture translation through the export format); K1 vs K2 continuous; fuzzed-term storms (well-typed generators + mutation) where ANY pairwise disagreement is a finding — the kernel is the one subsystem where we volunteer to be outnumbered.

Triage law (§8.2, R7): a Reference-accepted / Crucible-rejected finding opens an oracle-or-spec INVESTIGATION first — never auto-classified as our defect or theirs; if the Reference accepts a term the type theory rejects (upstream soundness bug — it has happened), FrankenLean matches the THEORY, files the reproducer upstream, and records the Behavior Note (soundness beats bug-parity, D23). Any soundness-side divergence in our direction is a stop-ship publish-the-finding event. Every finding auto-shrinks via the Tribunal delta-debugger into a minimal fixture.

Acceptance: all four rig families running in CI on the fixture corpus + nightly on larger storms; external-witness sampling (lean4checker/lean4lean seats in the §8.3c council on sampled classes) wired; the delta-debugger produces minimal reproducers automatically. Tests: seeded-divergence mutants per rule family must be detected by the rigs (mutation-kill evidence per §18.2); rig determinism (same seed, same storm, same verdicts).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-02 ENFORCEMENT RIG — add a named admission-bypass mutation family: direct raw environment insertion, forged/deserialized checked tokens, cache-hit promotion without live kernel validation, receipt/checker success treated as authority, rejection-to-success mapping, and Inconclusive/InternalFault promotion. Each mutant must be killed by compile-time structural checks or a real publication-path E2E; pairwise verdict agreement alone is insufficient.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All four standing rig families run: Crucible versus Reference through shared olean/export fixtures, Crucible versus translated lean4lean rules, K1 versus K2, and seeded well-typed plus mutation term storms; every pairwise disagreement is a persisted typed finding and no majority vote can suppress one.
2. External-witness sampling records exact tool/build/pin, fixture translation, judgment class, fuel/profile, and provenance. Reference-accepted/Crucible-rejected cases open an INVESTIGATION rather than an automatic blame assignment; D23 soundness divergences create a Behavior Note and stop-ship evidence.
3. The Tribunal delta-debugger deterministically reduces every supported finding to a replayable minimal fixture while preserving the same disagreement class, seed, oracle inputs, and resource envelope; cancellation or exhaustion is Inconclusive and never rejection.
4. Named unit/property tests cover fixture translation, verdict normalization, witness disagreement, deterministic seed replay, malformed witness output, resource budgets, and shrink preservation. Mutation suites plant at least one divergence in each kernel-rule family and prove every planted defect is killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane invokes the real pinned Reference/oracle and available independent checker witnesses on a positive corpus case, a planted divergence, automatic shrinking, and clean post-failure recovery. It emits concise human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON containing run/scenario/step, bead/gate/claim/invariant and Parity-Ledger row ids, exact redacted argv/cwd, pin/build hashes, seed/thread/fuel/profile/platform, canonical input and verdict hashes, pairwise matrix, first divergence, shrink trace, budgets/timings/exit status, artifact paths, cleanup, and asserted final state.
6. CI runs fixture-corpus rigs on every applicable change and archived larger deterministic storms nightly; close evidence identifies every test, mutant, minimal reproducer, manifest, and artifact bundle and may claim no parity level for skipped witnesses.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — generated well-typed storms, term families, and shrinker outputs are independently type-validated outside K1 before they count as differential evidence. Shrinking preserves well-typedness, exact disagreement class, roots, comparison class, and witness authority. Mutants for self-blessed invalid fixtures, changed disagreement class, lost dependencies, and nondeterministic minimality must be killed; minimized repros are re-run against every named witness.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative output is strict schema-versioned canonical semantic NDJSON containing normalized inputs, roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, wall time, temporary paths, cache/performance facts, and scheduler details live only in a separately linked bounded telemetry stream and cannot enter authoritative digests. Validators reject merged streams, unknown versions, extra or missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, or telemetry-root drift. Productive 1/8/32 runs prove nonempty completed work and deterministic reduction rather than thread-count labels.

title: W4: Hygiene + macro transactions — stable generated names, memoizable expansion
description:
Implement hygiene + macro expansion transactions (§9.2): macro scopes exactly as the Reference implements them (scope-decorated names, Syntax quotation/antiquotation semantics, macro_rules matching), with generated identifiers derived from STABLE expansion paths (origin command -> macro invocation -> quotation path -> local ordinal) — never thread timing or allocator addresses, because metaprograms and diagnostics observe generated names (determinism of names is a compatibility requirement).

Macro expansion is a TRANSACTION: an invocation receives syntax + hygiene seed + grammar/extension snapshot + declared capabilities + a deterministic budget, and returns expanded syntax, generated names, messages, a source map, extension reads, and capability events. Memoizable exactly when all reads are known: a file-reading macro caches against the capability-recorded canonical path AND content hash; a clock-reading macro is opaque in faithful mode and denied by default in hardened sound profiles.

Acceptance: the G0-4 hygiene rig green on production code; expansion-path name derivation verified stable across thread counts and runs; transaction memoization correctness (cache hit == fresh expansion) under capability-read tracking. Tests: hygiene corner-case corpus (shadowing, splices, cross-macro capture); quotation/antiquotation round-trips; a capability-lying mutant (macro reads a file without declaring it) must be caught by the perturbation validator.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

## Success Criteria

Both exact children close with capture-free faithful names/source maps, complete read/effect accounting, nested failure-atomic rollback, uninstrumented-read uncacheability, deterministic replay, and complete adversarial/no-mock evidence.

Every expansion runs as an explicit atomic transaction over syntax, hygiene seed, grammar/extension snapshot, capabilities and deterministic budget, returning expansion, names, messages, source map, declared reads and capability events; failure/cancellation/resource outcomes leak nothing. Memoization is allowed only when all reads are known and key-bound: file reads include canonical path plus content hash; clock/opaque effects follow faithful and hardened policies exactly. Cache hits equal fresh expansion in syntax, names, messages, maps, events and roots. title: Gate G2: The Golem Wakes — user code runs, plugins load, PG-7 floor
description:
Convergence gate G2 "The Golem Wakes" (§22.3): user-class compiled Lean code executes on Golem with #eval/IO/Task parity on the fixture corpus; Reference-built plugins load and run (ABI door, inbound); initialization ordering proven; PG-7 interpreter floor met on early corpora; the intrinsic census fully populated with fixtures.

This gate proves the execution half of the Native Mirror bet: the VM value-identity claim (FLBC values ARE ABI objects — one calling convention across interpreted code, native intrinsics, and foreign plugins) holds for the code class Golem will actually run in production (mathlib tactics, lakefiles, #eval bodies). It is the prerequisite for W6 tactic-framework work at scale and for the Lake surface (lakefile.lean execution).

Evidence bundle: the parity corpus green under both the production effect plane and the lab runtime; real plugin load/run transcripts with initialization-DAG receipts; C4 inbound matrix results per platform; PG-7 measurements against the W1 baseline with claim states; RC-balance and ownership-shadow soak results.

title: W1: Canonical contract extraction — ABI, OLEAN/ILEAN, extern and builtin inventories
description:
## Description

Integrate the complete mechanically extracted contract surface required by D5/D9 and Appendices B/C. This is the terminal W1 contract epic, not a second extractor. Its decomposed implementation tasks are: fln-k5rr for the canonical pin/target inventory schema and atomic publication protocol; franken_lean-b7n8 for target-indexed ABI layout, exported lean_* signatures, ownership/effect policy, tags, packing, and generated Rust constants; franken_lean-io7w for exact OLEAN/ILEAN header, epoch, relocation, sharing/back-reference, ModuleData, extension, and validation contracts; fln-3tye for pin-verified extern/builtin Reference-environment censuses and Mirror partition inputs; and franken_lean-w75y for the canonical render/join, drift CI, generated compilation, and real W1 handoff E2E.

One schema-versioned inventory rooted in authoritative SUITE.lock inputs feeds every Markdown contract, Rust constant, census/façade table, fixture manifest, and drift report. Raw observed facts and reviewed policy are distinct, bijectively joined tables. Every row binds extractor/version, exact Reference commit/tree, source locator, target/ABI class where applicable, raw evidence, policy, and stable identity. No layout value is hand-transcribed; no source regex substitutes for a required environment walk; no Reference code links, runs, or falls back inside a FrankenLean release/runtime component.

Generation is offline and deterministic. A fresh candidate is published atomically only after all domain inventories, row/policy totality, cross-output agreement, target and pin identity, generated-code compilation, resource bounds, and unchanged governed-input roots validate. Partial traversal/output, load-bearing ambiguity, unavailable authority, cancellation, source drift, or resource exhaustion is non-authoritative and preserves the prior generation.

The earlier semantic cycle is removed without dropping proof. This epic closes on deterministic extraction, canonical joins, generated compilation, and real local handoff consumption. G0-1 owns real-mathlib ABI/OLEAN resurrection, G0-3 owns ABI-valued execution, and later codec/intrinsic/Mirror beads own production conformance. G0/W1 aggregation joins those downstream bundles; this producer does not circularly require its own consumers to close first.

## Success Criteria

All five implementation tasks close with independently validated evidence rooted in the same SUITE.lock/Reference epoch and compatible schema/target namespaces. The terminal join proves one canonical inventory root, complete ABI/OLEAN/ILEAN/extern/builtin row and policy coverage, deterministic cold regeneration, generated Rust compilation, atomic drift publication, and real no-mock repository handoff consumption. Named unit/model/property suites and the cross-domain E2E cover target matrices, format fields and sharing, environment-walk completeness, policy bijection, deterministic identities, candidate lifecycle, and failure recovery. Named mutations perturb every domain fact/policy/output, mix pins/targets/schemas, omit or duplicate rows, substitute source heuristics, hand-edit generated output, leak a Reference path, suppress drift, publish partially, or promote Inconclusive; every mutation is killed for the intended reason and followed by exact recovery. Human logs and strict versioned NDJSON retain causal run/scenario/step ids, domain/extractor/schema/target/epoch facts, raw/policy/canonical/output roots, row totals and unresolved rows, expected/actual first divergence, resource/process outcome, commands and bounded separate stdout/stderr artifacts, publication authority, cleanup, and final state. No mock-only consumer, handwritten constant, parallel extractor, unclassified row, cross-target projection, incomplete result, or circular downstream waiver satisfies closure.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — one canonical extracted inventory feeds every generated surface. ABI layout/tags/exports come from the pinned lean.h and build metadata; olean/ilean layout comes from module.cpp, compact.{h,cpp}, Lean.Environment.ModuleData, and Lean.Server.Ilean; extern and builtin inventories come from a pin-verified Reference environment walk rather than source regex, preserving qualified names, universes, binders, signatures, generated declarations, and attributes. ABI status, Mirror partition, ownership/effect class, and support level are explicit keyed policy inputs joined against the extracted inventory; extraction fails on missing, duplicate, stale, or unknown policy rows. Markdown contracts, Rust constants, façade/census tables, fixtures, and drift reports render from the same canonical intermediate so they cannot disagree by construction. Extraction is offline, read-only over vendor/lean4-src, deterministic under locale/path/time variation, and never executes upstream as a FrankenLean component.
acceptance_criteria: The epic closes only after fln-k5rr, franken_lean-b7n8, franken_lean-io7w, fln-3tye, and franken_lean-w75y have current independently validated bundles under one authoritative lock/pin, compatible schema/target namespaces, and a successful terminal cross-surface join. Every original ABI, OLEAN, ILEAN, extern, builtin, policy, generated-output, determinism, drift, compilation, no-mock handoff, failure/recovery, mutation, logging, and evidence requirement is owned by a named child and present in the join; none is deferred or replaced by prose. Full downstream real-mathlib/runtime parity remains mandatory on G0-1, G0-3, codecs, intrinsics, and Mirror owners, and those gates consume this immutable handoff root without becoming circular prerequisites of the producer.

Exact extraction map from the pin: ABI header/tags/layout/calling-convention typedefs and exported LEAN_EXPORT declarations are in vendor/lean4-src/src/include/lean/lean.h; olean v2/v3 header, data section, closure-offset/lib relocation trailer, alignment/base-address rules, and loader checks are in src/library/module.cpp plus src/runtime/compact.{h,cpp}; ModuleData is in src/Lean/Environment.lean; .ilean schema is Lean.Server.Ilean in src/Lean/Server/References.lean and its writer is src/Lean/Elab/Frontend.lean; extern semantics are exposed through Lean.getExternAttrData? in src/Lean/Compiler/ExternAttr.lean. - Function census ownership classes faithful (b_lean_obj_arg->BorrowedArg, unsigned->Value on the lean_ctor_get_* family).
- Integrity chain intact: INVENTORY_DIGEST == sha256(contracts/abi_inventory.json); ABI_CONTRACT.md names the same digest; LEAN_H_SHA256 == sha256(the actual pinned lean.h) — so drift detection is anchored to the real pin, and md<->inventory<->code share one root.

title: W8: Incremental law — true-cone invalidation, early cutoff, perturbation-validated matrix
description:
Implement the incremental law + invalidation matrix (§13.2): an edit invalidates exactly the records whose hash inputs changed — the TRUE dependency cone computed on recorded edges (franken_networkx dominator/reachability machinery), not the import-file cone. Whitespace, comments, docstrings (non-doc facets), reordering of independent declarations, and edits to unrelated declarations in an imported file invalidate NOTHING downstream. FL-INV-05 (a Ledger hit is semantically identical to recomputation) is enforced by the conservative rule set — anything not proven irrelevant is relevant — plus a standing Tribunal rig that randomly recomputes hits and diffs.

Two refinements make the law sharp: EARLY CUTOFF (a re-run node whose output digest is unchanged stops the cascade — the salsa/Adapton discipline, with statement-hash vs value-hash separation doing the work: an opaque proof-body change invalidates the proof receipt and codegen closure, not its typed dependents); and the PUBLISHED invalidation matrix per change class (trivia-only -> source maps; proof-body edit -> that decl check + receipt + executable closure; type edit -> typed dependents; instance add/remove -> affected class-head search keys via the §10.4 negative-cache dependency keys; notation change -> GrammarEpoch descendants after the activation point; option change -> declared readers; plugin change -> classified reads + ABI load products) — each with its conservative fallback and each PERTURBATION-VALIDATED (mutate an allegedly-irrelevant input, require bit-identical products, demote to opaque on failure). Answer identity vs trace identity is explicit.

Risk R13 (stale hit) law: any confirmed stale hit disables the implicated rule class globally (suffix-barrier fallback) until proven, with a public incident bundle. Acceptance: PG-3 (leaf-edit p50 <= 15 s, p95 <= 90 s warm) trajectory; metamorphic invalidate-nothing suite green; random-recompute rig running. Tests: the C8 edit-sequence corpus with expected cones; perturbation campaigns; clean-rebuild-equivalence.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-05 ADMISSION OWNER — define one cache-hit promotion API that validates node kind/schema/version, full semantic and capability input closure, epoch/mode/profile/platform/determinism class, artifact hashes, required kernel/checker evidence, publication generation, and any epoch-bridge proof before returning a reusable result. Miss, corrupt, stale, unsupported, cancelled, exhausted, or inconclusive candidates recompute or remain typed inconclusive; none becomes reject or accept. Random recomputation, perturbation, clean-build equivalence, and a stale-hit mutation family continuously enforce the invariant.
acceptance_criteria:
One typed cache-hit promotion API validates node kind/schema/version, complete semantic and capability input closure, epoch/mode/profile/platform/determinism class, artifact hashes, publication generation, kernel/checker evidence, and any epoch-bridge proof before returning reusable results. Miss/corrupt/stale/unsupported/cancelled/exhausted/inconclusive candidates recompute or remain typed; none becomes accept or reject. The published row-per-change invalidation matrix covers trivia, comments/doc facets, independent declaration reorder, proof body, statement/type, instance/class-head search keys, grammar activation, option readers, plugin/effect reads, source maps, codegen and every future class, with conservative fallback and answer-versus-trace identity. True-cone reachability uses recorded edges; unchanged output digest stops cascades with statement/value separation. Perturbation validates every claimed irrelevant input. A confirmed stale hit globally disables the rule class to the suffix-barrier fallback and emits a public incident bundle. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation tests cover C8 edit sequences, cones, early cutoff, closure completeness, reordered inputs, random recomputation, clean-build equality, corruption/cancellation/resources and recovery; named dropped-edge, stale-hit, wrong-cutoff, omitted-capability, overbroad relevance and Inconclusive-promotion mutants are killed. Real no-mock mathlib/Lake E2E performs each change class, compares warm to clean builds, plants stale entries, proves fallback and recovery, and retains strict keys/cones/roots/artifacts/final-state bundles across 1/8/32 threads. PG-3 meets warm leaf-edit p50 15 seconds and p95 90 seconds with raw host/cache/tail evidence. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, perturbation/recompute/mutation/fault/thread/performance lanes pass. No file-cone substitute, heuristic unrecorded dependency, stale acceptance, silent rule re-enable, timing-only equivalence, mock-only cache, or deferred matrix row satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — maintain a complete current versioned invalidation registry with total unknown-to-Opaque and suffix-barrier fallback, not an impossible promise over every future change class. Random recompute and perturbation are statistical detection, while FL-INV-05 enforcement comes from complete semantic/capability/read closure plus conservative opacity. A confirmed stale rule is persistently generation-quarantined before further hits or in-flight publication and requires explicit re-enable evidence. This bead owns correctness and G4 baseline; final PG-3 production performance moves downstream. Named suites: cache_hit_authority_model, invalidation_registry_totality, stale_rule_quarantine_recovery, invalidation_c8_no_mock_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Unknown schema, missing/duplicate ids, broken order/linkage, unbound artifacts, truncation, merged streams, or cleanup/final-state mismatch fail. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: fln-kernel K1: recursor-reduction slice — KR-205 dispatch, KR-316 iota, KR-317 K-conversion, KR-955 Quot computation (zht follow-up, closes z6c false-reject families)
description: The z6c kernel replay (tribunal/fixtures/c3/KERNEL_REPLAY_FINDINGS.md) triaged all 522 Init.Prelude false-rejects to named reduction-gap families; ~489 (eliminators, custom eliminators, equation-lemma helpers, decidability instances, brecOn helpers, match auxiliaries) require iota, plus proj-family cases that need iota/structure-eta to expose constructors. Scope, implemented exactly per the vendored pin (KERNEL_CONTRACT.md anchors into vendor/lean4-src/src/kernel/): (1) KR-205 recursor dispatch in whnf_core — quot computation first, then inductive iota, fired when the application head is a stable Const; (2) KR-316 iota: major premise at nparams+nmotives+nminors+nindices, whnf'd, with K conversion (KR-317), Nat-literal-to-constructor conversion, and structure-eta coercion applied before rule matching; matching RecursorRule rhs instantiated with the recursor's levels, applied to params+motives+minors, then the constructor's last nfields args, then trailing args; (3) KR-317 to_cnstr_when_K: k-flagged recursors replace any major whose whnf'd inferred type has the right inductive head with the nullary constructor, gated on type defeq; (4) KR-955 quot_reduce_rec: Quot.lift (mk at 5, f at 3) and Quot.ind (mk at 4, p at 3), mk must whnf to a 3-arg Quot.mk application, trailing args preserved, gated on env quot initialization (QuotVal present). OUT of scope (documented follow-ups, absence only under-accepts, never over-accepts): KR-313 Nat literal acceleration (needs fln-bignum), KR-314 String literal expansion, KR-315 unit-like eta, KR-903 structure eta in defeq, iota RHS admission checks (KR-803 lands with inductive admission). Verification: mutation-killed unit tests through the public surface for every new rule branch (wrong-rule-selection, missing-K, wrong quot positions, dropped trailing args, under-applied recursor no-fire), budget/Inconclusive behavior on reduction loops, covenant guards stay green (12 KLOC, forbid unsafe, dep set unchanged), and the z6c kernel replay re-run as the no-mock E2E with before/after rejection counts recorded on this bead and z6c.
acceptance_criteria: KR-205 dispatch, KR-316 iota, KR-317 K-conversion, and KR-955 Quot computation match the pinned contract, preserve trailing arguments, honor under-application and initialization guards, and return typed budget/cancellation outcomes. Named public-surface tests kill wrong-rule, missing-K, wrong-position, dropped-trailing-argument, and no-fire mutants without broadening authority. The real z6c olean replay records exact before/after rejection-family counts, foreign-witness evidence where applicable, a negative lane, and a clean recovery replay in retained human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts with the fields specified in Notes. At each declared thread profile, two runs of the same fixture with identical epoch, budget, seed, and engine build must produce byte-identical reduced terms, verdicts, diagnostic identities, consumption facts, and canonicalized semantic NDJSON fields; monotonic timings and artifact paths are explicitly excluded from equality. Any repeat divergence is a finding, never normalized away. Covenant, fln-kernel, replay, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, and UBS gates are green. Deferred KR-313/KR-314/KR-315/KR-903/KR-803 work remains explicit and is not claimed by this closure.
notes: Verification evidence is retained in both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON. The no-mock z6c replay records run/scenario/step and KR rule ids, bead/invariant/epoch, exact fixture and module hashes, reference and engine build identity, per-family before/after accepted/rejected/Inconclusive/InternalFault counts, selected-rule and major-premise facts, expected versus actual reduced term/verdict/error/consumption, budgets, thread/seed, mutant id, first divergence, command/exit status, stdout/stderr artifact references, cleanup, and asserted final recovery state. A deliberately invalid, under-applied, wrong-rule, or wrong-Quot-position input is the negative lane; a clean replay of the valid fixture after every failure proves recovery and nonpollution. Log volume is bounded and failures remain diagnosable from retained artifacts alone.
close_reason: Landed 75203bf: KR-205/316/317/955 implemented per pin; 44/44 tests, 6/6 mutants killed (verified by application), no-mock replay E2E green — Prelude census 1233→1412 accepted, 522→343 rejected, 0 Inconclusive, no new rejection class. Residual families decomposed into fln-d4x (KR-903/315 defeq eta) and franken_lean-irm (KR-313/314 literals). Gates: fmt/clippy/UBS/structure-guard green, covenant 1903/12000 LOC.
