title: W3: KERNEL_CONTRACT.md — the rule-by-rule Judgment Specification, written first
description:
Write KERNEL_CONTRACT.md — the Judgment Specification (§8.1b, Appendix A) — as the FIRST W3 deliverable, before any engine code: a rule-by-rule account of the implemented type theory, each rule anchored to (a) the Reference source lines that define it and (b) the literature that justifies it. Both kernel engines, the independent checker, and the Tribunal term generators are built against this document.

Inventory (Appendix A): typing rules for every expression constructor; universe-level normalization incl. imax _ 0 = 0; defeq — beta, eta (functions AND structures), delta ordered by definitional height with reducibility-independence, zeta, iota incl. K-like subsingleton elimination, definitional proof irrelevance in Prop, Quot.lift/Quot.mk computation, Nat/String literal rules with the exact accelerated Nat operation set (add, sub, mul, div, mod, pow, gcd, beq, ble, blt, land/lor/lxor/shifts as the pin defines); inductive validation (strict positivity, universe constraints, large elimination, mutual, nested-to-mutual, reflexive); structure eta/projection coherence; declaration admission rules; partial/unsafe quarantine; environment uniqueness rules; resource accounting hooks (counted, never semantic — exhaustion is a verdict about a run, never about a term, FL-INV-07).

The inversion that is itself a product: the Reference specification is its C++; FrankenLean specification is a document the C++ is CHECKED AGAINST, rule by rule, by the differential harness. Acceptance: every rule row has a Reference source anchor, fixtures listed or stubbed, and an unambiguous statement a second implementer could code from; review pass against G0-2 findings; versioned per epoch. Tests (the document is CI-checked like code): an anchor-resolution check verifies every cited Reference source location exists at the pin and fails on drift; a coverage check cross-references the rule inventory against the C0 fixture families (every rule names at least one fixture or an explicit stub with an owner); the Parity Ledger kernel rows link back to rule ids and CI fails on a dangling link; spec-change discipline — any edit to a rule statement requires touching its linked fixtures in the same change or CI flags the divergence, with the diff logged rule-by-rule.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-02 CONTRACT OWNER — KERNEL_CONTRACT.md must specify the admission authority as a typed state machine, not merely the typing judgments. Define untrusted declaration/environment inputs, the exact fln-kernel check transition, the opaque non-serializable checked capability/result, publication semantics, and every allowed consumer. Prove at the crate/API level that receipts, certificates, fln-checker verdicts, deserialization, cache hits, plugins, frontier engines, and unsafe boundary crates cannot construct or launder kernel acceptance. The rule inventory includes compile-fail and mutation fixtures for every forbidden bypass and FL-INV-07 outcome non-promotion.
acceptance_criteria:
KERNEL_CONTRACT.md is a versioned epoch-bound implementable specification written before engine completion. Every expression typing, universe/imax normalization, beta/function-and-structure eta/delta/zeta/iota/K-like elimination, proof irrelevance, Quot, literal/accelerated Nat operation, inductive positivity/universe/large-elimination/mutual/nested/reflexive, projection/coherence, declaration admission, partial/unsafe quarantine, environment uniqueness, fuel/resource, and FL-INV-07 rule has a stable id, unambiguous premises/conclusion, Reference source anchor, literature rationale, error/verdict class, and positive/negative/boundary fixture or owned explicit stub. It also specifies FL-INV-02 as a typed authority state machine: untrusted inputs, sole kernel check transition, opaque nonserializable checked capability, publication consumers, and why receipts/certificates/checker/cache/plugin/frontier/unsafe results cannot mint acceptance. CI resolves anchors against the cryptographic pin, checks inventory and C0/Parity-Ledger links, rejects dangling/duplicate/stale rules, and requires linked fixture changes with rule edits. Compile-fail/API/dependency fixtures and named mutations cover direct construction, deserialization, replay, raw insertion, unsafe laundering, skipped checks, and Inconclusive promotion. A second implementer review demonstrates the document is sufficient to implement each rule without consulting engine code; G0-2 findings are reconciled row by row. Real no-mock contract E2E validates anchors/inventories on a clean pin, plants source/rule/fixture/link drift, restores exact bytes, and retains strict rule-level evidence. Dependencies, docs checks, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and spec mutation lanes pass. No prose-only authority claim, missing rule, unresolved stub, source line without pin binding, fixture-free normative row, engine behavior overriding the spec silently, or deferred listed rule satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — KERNEL_CONTRACT is normatively prior even though prototype rules already exist. Before any further authority interface freezes, reconcile every implemented judgment, side condition, error, fuel rule, and artifact outcome bidirectionally to a versioned contract row with exact source/pin anchors. Closure permits no unresolved fixture stub, provisional rule, missing negative case, or prose-only mapping. A planted implementation-without-row and row-without-implementation mutant must fail the terminal validator.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative output is strict schema-versioned canonical semantic NDJSON containing normalized inputs, roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, wall time, temporary paths, cache/performance facts, and scheduler details live only in a separately linked bounded telemetry stream and cannot enter authoritative digests. Validators reject merged streams, unknown versions, extra or missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, or telemetry-root drift. Productive 1/8/32 runs prove nonempty completed work and deterministic reduction rather than thread-count labels.

title: fln-conformance: fail closed when tracked Beads ownership evidence is unavailable
description:
## Problem

The production KERNEL_CONTRACT validator can validate declaration-stub ownership only when canonical Beads ownership evidence is present. RCH remote execution currently omits `.beads/issues.jsonl` from the synchronized checkout. The validator correctly fails closed, but this means the bead’s prior claim that local, clean-clone, and strict-remote validation all agree is false. The ownership contract must remain fail-closed while its canonical evidence becomes explicitly and reproducibly available in every required execution environment.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. From a checkout whose local `.beads/issues.jsonl` exists, run `cargo test --locked -p fln-conformance --test kernel_contract`; observe the real ownership cases can load the canonical export.
2. Run the same test with `RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- cargo test --locked -p fln-conformance --test kernel_contract`.
3. On a worker whose transfer policy excludes `.beads/`, observe `the_contract_parses_resolves_and_covers` and the clean case of `the_checker_detects_planted_drift_and_gaps` fail with typed missing-evidence diagnostics for `.beads/issues.jsonl`.
4. Confirm that deliberately missing, malformed, empty, duplicate, noncanonical, stale, and phantom-owner evidence still fails closed; the repair must not turn any of these cases into acceptance.

## Constraints

No hard-coded owner allowlist, test-only bypass, blanket acceptance when `.beads` is absent, weakening of the planted mutation, or dependency on mutable user-global RCH configuration. The checked evidence must be canonical, tracked or mechanically bound to the canonical export, reproducible in clean clones, and transferred or derived under an explicit repository-owned contract.

## Acceptance Criteria

All machine acceptance criteria on this bead are satisfied: typed fail-closed classification for every invalid evidence class; equivalent canonical evidence in local, clean-clone, CI, and strict RCH execution; named unit/integration tests; a no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E matrix; schema-versioned detailed NDJSON evidence; and all formatting, checking, Clippy, test, UBS, KERNEL_CONTRACT, and strict-remote gates green.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The kernel-contract validator loads canonical bead ownership evidence and distinguishes a successful nonempty export from missing, unreadable, malformed, empty, duplicate-ID, noncanonical, stale, phantom-owner, and resource-exhausted inputs; every invalid class fails closed with a typed diagnostic and nonzero exit. The loader preflights explicit maximum file bytes, line bytes, record count, ID bytes, and parse nesting depth, with zero, exact-limit, and one-over boundary tests, bounded diagnostics, no panic, no partial owner set, and successful recovery after restoring an in-budget canonical export. 2. The exact canonical ownership evidence used by validation is available and equivalent in local worktrees, clean CI clones, and strict RCH remote workers. The solution must use an explicit tracked/synchronized evidence contract or explicit transfer inclusion; it must not hardcode current owner IDs, bypass validation in tests, or silently accept absent evidence. If a derived CI ownership manifest is used, it is mechanically bound by content hash and provenance to .beads/issues.jsonl, and missing or stale bindings fail. 3. Named unit and integration tests cover valid real ownership plus missing, unreadable, malformed, empty, duplicate, noncanonical, stale, phantom, every exact resource boundary, and recovery cases, including exact diagnostic classification and unchanged state after refusal. 4. A no-mock E2E/CI matrix runs the real validator locally, in a clean checkout, and under RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1. It covers the positive path, every failure class, resource refusal, exact restoration, and recovery without replacing the real evidence loader. 5. Every E2E scenario emits schema-versioned line-oriented NDJSON recording scenario, evidence path and content hash, provenance/binding, configured and consumed resource limits, command, worker/build identity, expected and actual classification, exit code, separate bounded stdout/stderr artifact paths, cleanup result, and final recovery state. A strict validator rejects missing/extra/stale/mismatched fields and the retained manifest-backed bundle validates independently. 6. Acceptance requires cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, focused kernel-contract tests, the strict remote no-mock matrix, UBS on changed files, and the existing KERNEL_CONTRACT validation gate, all green.
close_reason: Fail-closed ownership evidence is source-bound locally and explicitly manifest-bound on archive-only RCH, with clean-clone full gates and a 26-case strict-remote immutable bundle passing.

Production validate now always records an evidence-load failure and checks stub owners only against successfully loaded IDs; there is no absent-evidence bypass. Added production-path cases for NotFound, PermissionDenied, malformed JSON, noncanonical ID, empty export, duplicate ID, phantom owner, and a valid real owner. Verification: rustfmt --check and git diff --check green; local cargo test -p fln-conformance --test kernel_contract 4/4 green; strict RCH remote injected fail-closed test 1/1 green; cargo clippy --no-deps -p fln-conformance --test kernel_contract -- -D warnings green; UBS exit 0 with 0 critical (31 warning, 26 info inventory findings). title: Reconcile plan §15 '(fln-trace, fln-graph)' vs §21 crate map (no fln-graph crate)
description:
Plan §15's section title names two crates — fln-trace, fln-graph — but the authoritative cycle-free crate map in §21 (and AGENTS.md's layering chain) lists only fln-trace; the workspace scaffold (bead fln-8mj) follows §21. Either §15's title is stale (Palimpsest's causal-graph store lives inside fln-trace on franken_networkx per §15.3c) and the plan text should drop fln-graph, or a real fln-graph crate is intended and must be added to §21's map + WORKSPACE_GRAPH.txt with a rank and charter. Per the spec-first workflow, amend the plan first, then the workspace. One-line decision; blocks nothing today.

## Acceptance Criteria

The plan is amended before any workspace change. §15 names only the selected owner crate (`fln-trace`); §15.3c still states that Palimpsest stores the causal graph in process using franken_networkx over frankensqlite journals and preserves the optional frankengraphdb export lane; §21, AGENTS.md, ci/WORKSPACE_GRAPH.txt, and the scaffold all agree that no `fln-graph` crate exists. A repository-wide literal search finds no stale `fln-graph` reference. This is a documentation-only architecture decision, so code unit/E2E tests are not applicable; verification is the exact-text search plus cross-document crate-map consistency review recorded in the close reason.

A repository-wide literal search finds no stale `fln-graph` reference. This is a documentation-only architecture decision, so code unit/E2E tests are not applicable; the verification is the exact-text search plus cross-document crate-map consistency review recorded in the close reason.
close_reason: Decision: no fln-graph crate. §15.3c already states the causal graph lives in-process on franken_networkx over frankensqlite-backed journals — i.e., inside Palimpsest (fln-trace); §21's authoritative crate map and the fln-8mj scaffold agree. Amended §15's section title to '(fln-trace)' — the single fln-graph reference in the repo (verified by grep). Docs-only decision bead; no code, no workspace change needed. The optional frankengraphdb export remains the frontier lane per §15.3c.

title: W11: Leapfrog surfaces — Bloodhound, Folio, Envoy, terminal InfoView, WASM Judge
description:
Workstream W11 "Leapfrog surfaces" (§16): the products that make FrankenLean more than parity — each strictly downstream of the trust core, each degradable without touching the trust story.

Bloodhound (fln-hound, frankensearch): in-toolchain hybrid lexical+semantic search over the live environment, indexed incrementally at Ledger commit; exact?/apply?-class premise retrieval as a candidate generator whose candidates are VERIFIED by actual elaboration (ranking is UX, proofs are proofs); CandidateCards in three grades (initial/refined/verified); proof repair consuming the causal graph (diff environments, find earliest failed constraint cell, hole the blame slice, search replacements, validate through the full pipeline, emit patch transaction with rollback — the migration autopilot engine); counterfactual analysis and minimization along declared objective vectors. Folio (fln-doc, franken_markdown+fmd-math): doc-gen4-surface-compatible docs as an incremental Ledger facet — native HTML/PDF, native math, receipt-bound pages that go stale honestly rather than lying. Envoy (fln-mcp, fastmcp_rust): the agent door — ProofStateSnapshot as the round-trippable session primitive; budgeted, macaroon-scoped tools (goal_state, apply_tactic, search_lemmas, check_term, eval, get_certificate, ledger_query, trace_query); transactional multi-agent orchestration (patch proposals, never direct trusted mutations; mission receipts with rollback roots). Terminal InfoView (fln goals over frankentui). The WASM Judge (§16.4b): K1 + fln-checker compiled to WASM — sealed-capsule verification in a browser tab, the public face of the trust story.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
acceptance_criteria:
W11 closes only when all five leapfrog products ship as independently usable, evidence-linked surfaces downstream of the trust core. Bloodhound incrementally indexes the live Ledger with deterministic hybrid retrieval; every CandidateCard is explicitly initial, refined, or verified, and apply/exact/proof-repair candidates become trusted only after full elaboration and kernel checking. Proof repair uses causal blame, validates replacements end to end, emits rollback-capable patch transactions, and never edits trusted state directly; counterfactual/minimization objectives are declared. Folio generates doc-gen-compatible incremental HTML/PDF with native math, receipt-bound freshness, accessibility, and no external LaTeX/browser service. Envoy exposes versioned macaroon-scoped MCP tools over immutable ProofStateSnapshots, typed budgets, transactional multi-agent proposals, mission receipts, revocation, and rollback roots; search/ranking and eval never widen authority. fln goals uses the same Lantern snapshot/RPC truth as editors, supports SSH/tmux/narrow/no-color/assistive text, and keeps ANSI out of robot schemas. The WASM Judge verifies sealed capsules offline in stock supported browsers with K1 plus the independent checker, integrity-bound assets, native/WASM vector identity, explicit TCB/resource/version facts, accessible failures, and no network service. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation suites cover ranking verification, repair rollback, document freshness, MCP auth/capabilities, terminal rendering/generation, capsule decoding/integrity/browser limits, cancellation/resources, and independent product failure; named provisional-to-verified, stale-page, authority-escalation, ANSI-leak, and browser-asset-mismatch mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E runs search/repair, docs, MCP sessions, PTY terminal, and offline browser capsule verification, then injects one failure at a time and proves exact recovery: index rebuild without false verification, stale-page regeneration, revoked/expired capability refusal followed by a fresh authorized session, terminal disconnect/reconnect from the same snapshot, browser worker/refresh restart from the sealed capsule, and one product failure never masquerading as another success. Strict combined release bundles retain each product identity/evidence tier, failure and recovery transition, separate bounded streams, hashes, resources, cleanup, and final state in human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts that validate independently. All W11 children and terminal/WASM join, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, accessibility/security/browser/editor/platform/fault/performance lanes are green before G6. No candidate ranking presented as proof, direct trusted mutation, stale receipt hidden, mock-only MCP/browser/editor, terminal substitute for WASM, core-only WASM substitute for browser packaging, network-required verification, or deferred listed W11 feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — hybrid retrieval follows the D3-aware contract: deterministic only within a declared inference profile, with candidate membership/validity independent of ranking. W11 outputs are release-ready unsigned artifacts with manifests and evidence; W12 performs final installation, signing, notarization, and release envelope. The terminal join names exact child/product/evidence roots and cannot close on sampled docs, mock clients, or unclassified ranking drift.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W6: Athanor, Synod & the Mirror — elaborator, instances, scheduler, facade
description:
Workstream W6 "Athanor, Synod & the Native Mirror" (§10, §4.3): the elaborator — the program-defining workstream carrying the two Critical risks (R1 Meta-API dependence, R2 unifier fidelity tail).

Scope: the monadic tower semantics (CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM state shapes) as NativeHeap structures with explicit ElabTxn transactions (five outcomes, leak-audited rollback) and an explicit metavariable dependency graph with targeted wake-up; unification/defeq mirroring the Reference approximation ladder EXACTLY, implemented against the instrumented-oracle golden decision traces (§18.3) — the fidelity burden is entirely ours (no hosted fallback exists); term elaboration (coercions, binders, structure instances, match compilation matching generated-name conventions — metaprograms pattern-match on them, they are API; deriving; recursion elaborators lowering to exactly the kernel-visible constructions); Synod instance resolution (Reference semantics — priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, defaults, tabling — behind an indexed relational engine emitting SearchCards + TerminationCards); the NATIVE tactic framework (goal management, evalTactic dispatch, core tactic set behind Mirror facade rows); the deterministic dataflow scheduler (Bet B4: speculative parallel elaboration, canonical-order commit, effect summaries validated by perturbation — FL-INV-01 by construction); the builtin census + generated Mirror facade v1 (census totality: no unclassified symbol, ever — R1 mitigation).

Exit: gate G3 "The Mirror Holds" — Init/Std elaborate end-to-end from source with T2 identity, stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint bit-stable, metaprogram-corpus rig green at declared L-levels, ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out check passes.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — interpret core tactic set behind facade rows by stage: W6 completes the native framework and bootstrap-safe primitive rows; W7 supplies simp/norm_num/omega/bv_decide/grind and their full facade integration. The W6 epic may close with those future engine-backed rows at an explicit L0/L1 only if their generated shapes, typed Unsupported behavior, owners, fixtures, and promotion prerequisites are present. No placeholder success result or hidden oracle is permitted.

Closure topology: all W6 components feed the strengthened franken_lean-26y integration rig; that rig feeds Gate G3; this epic depends on G3 as its declared exit while retaining every component obligation. W6 consumes the proven G2 runtime rather than the broader W5 aggregation epic. This removes empty umbrella hops without weakening parser, kernel, Golem, elaborator, scheduler, Mirror, T2/T3, fixpoint, or Oracle-Only evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
W6 closes only when Athanor, Synod, deterministic scheduling, and the Native Mirror elaborate pinned Init/Std from source and drive real metaprograms through Golem with no hosted upstream implementation. CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM states are exact typed native structures; ElabTxn has five explicit outcomes and leak-audited rollback; metavariable dependencies wake only affected work. Unification and definitional equality reproduce the complete Reference approximation ladder against versioned golden decision traces, including fuel and transparency behavior. Term elaboration covers coercions, binders, structures, matches, deriving, recursion, diagnostics, InfoTrees, and exact generated names. Synod preserves priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, defaults, tabling, termination and negative-cache keys while emitting replayable SearchCards and TerminationCards. The native tactic framework and bootstrap-safe primitives preserve goal/TacticM state; engine-backed W7 rows remain explicit L0/L1 Unsupported until their owning integration promotes them, never fake success. The builtin census and generated facade are total, typed, evidence-leveled, and reject missing/stale/unknown rows. Speculative declaration work commits only in canonical order under validated effect summaries and registered CGSE policies, with identical outputs at 1, 8, and 32 threads. Unit/property/model/trace-replay/mutation suites cover every monad transaction, mvar graph edge, unifier approximation, elaborator form, instance rule, tactic transition, facade row, schedule/effect class, cancellation, and resource boundary; named mutants kill rollback leaks, stale wakeups/caches, search-order drift, generated-name drift, hidden oracle paths, and schedule-dependent publication. The real no-mock Mirror rig executes Init/Std and curated mathlib tactics, compares environments/InfoTrees/diagnostics/names/effects, proves the double-elaboration fixpoint and ORACLE_FALLBACK absence, and retains strict positive/divergence/fault/recovery bundles. G3, all W6 children and integration rig, scripts/check.sh, full cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, differential/trace/Mirror/thread/mutation/fault/performance lanes are green. No Lean.Elab source standing in for native implementation, unclassified facade symbol, placeholder tactic success, mock-only tactic corpus, schedule-sensitive output, normalized-away divergence, or deferred listed W6 feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — the terminal join records Synod SearchCard ownership and term-elaboration TerminationCard ownership, then names every exact child id, evidence root, Parity row, implementation root, mutation result, and G3 receipt. Prose such as all children complete cannot substitute for a machine-validated join manifest.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative evidence is canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON validated independently; telemetry is a separate non-authoritative stream and cannot affect hashes, comparison, admission, cache, publication, or promotion. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/duplicate ids, broken step order, unbound artifacts, silent truncation, merged streams, and cleanup/final-state mismatch. Cancellation, exhaustion, and InternalFault never enter cache, negative storage, proof admission, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 records prove useful overlap, completed work, retries, and schedule identity. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W12: fln library crate — the embeddable prover, capability-first API
description:
Implement the fln embeddable library crate (§17.2, the library door): embed the prover — parse, elaborate, check, query — in Rust programs with the SAME engine and the same guarantees as the toolchain binaries; verification inside build tools, CI services, and agents becomes a Cargo.toml line, the posture the Reference architecture makes awkward (process-spawning, stage0, C toolchain) and this one makes trivial.

API design laws: capability-first — an embedder hands the engine a Cx and receives determinism, cancellation, and budget enforcement STRUCTURALLY (no global state, no ambient authority; the embedder controls filesystem/network/clock exposure); typed errors per the D8 taxonomy crossing the API boundary as versioned values, never panics (a panic across the embedding boundary is an invariant failure); the toolchain binaries (fln-cli) are thin shells over this crate so the library IS the tested product, not a parallel implementation; epoch selection explicit (Engine::builder().toolchain_epoch(...)); results carry receipts and logical roots so embedders can persist and re-verify.

Acceptance: an example embedder (checked into examples/) parses, elaborates, and kernel-checks a small project and retrieves a receipt, under a restricted Cx that denies network — demonstrated in CI; fln-cli refactored to consume the library surface; API documented with the semver-per-epoch policy. Tests: embedding e2e test with detailed logging (each API call logged with inputs/outcomes); a panic-containment test (an induced internal fault surfaces as InternalFault, never unwinds across the boundary); Cx-denial tests (denied capabilities produce typed CapabilityDenied); thread-safety smoke under the lab runtime.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — this bead owns the complete capability-first library API and conformance tests over the real parser, Golem, elaborator/kernel, and Ledger engines. It exposes the API that toolchain front doors must consume, but does not claim to refactor nonexistent binaries. franken_lean-eb3 depends on this bead and proves that every CLI personality is a thin shell over the library with a structural no-bypass check.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Expose a documented Rust API for parse, elaborate, check, environment/query, receipts, and logical roots using the same engine/configuration as toolchain binaries; fln-cli is a thin consumer rather than a parallel implementation. Epoch, mode, profile, consensus policy, and resource budgets are explicit builder inputs with semver-per-epoch policy.
2. Every entry requires an embedder-supplied Cx/capability set; no global mutable engine state or ambient filesystem/network/clock/process authority is consulted. Cancellation, deadlines, memory/weight/heartbeat limits, and deterministic scheduling are structural and propagate typed outcomes.
3. All user/malformed/resource/capability failures cross the public boundary as versioned typed D8 taxonomy values with source/evidence context. Internal invariant faults are contained and reported as InternalFault; no unwind/panic escapes the embedding boundary and no partial declaration publishes. Results bind receipts and environment/logical roots suitable for persistence and independent revalidation.
4. Named unit/property/model/DPOR/fuzz/mutation suites cover every builder/API operation, configuration/epoch mismatch, capability allow/deny, cancellation/resource limits, concurrent isolated engines, deterministic outputs, receipt/root binding, malformed inputs, panic containment, and transaction rollback. Ambient-authority, CLI/library-drift, escaped-panic, partial-publish, and hidden-default-epoch mutants must be killed.
5. A checked-in real example embedder uses the published crate API to parse, elaborate, kernel-check, query, persist/reload, and retrieve/reverify a receipt for a small project under restricted Cx with network denied. A shared no-mock E2E compares its outputs/diagnostics/roots with the real CLI, triggers denial and internal-fault containment, runs concurrent sessions under the lab, and proves clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/embedder/API-call/engine/transaction/declaration/receipt ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted arguments/cwd, crate/build/epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget/capability facts, canonical input/environment/output/receipt roots, expected/actual typed outcomes, timings/resources/status, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI builds docs/examples and archives API/schema goldens, CLI equivalence matrices, DPOR schedules, denial/fault artifacts, mutant outcomes, and E2E manifests. No mock engine or process-spawn wrapper can satisfy the embeddable-same-engine contract.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — Rust semver tracks the Rust API and is not forced to a major version per Lean epoch; epoch and schema compatibility are explicit data axes. Any caught panic poisons the engine region, cancels/drains obligations, forbids further publication, and requires independent revalidation before reuse. Capability and Cx ownership, cancellation, resource, ABI, panic, and multi-epoch tests exercise the real embeddable API.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: G0-3: Golem execution spike — user-class code + real plugin on ABI-valued VM
description:
G0-3 Golem execution spike (§22.1-3, feeds §11): execute REAL user-class compiled Lean code end-to-end on the prototype VM — an #eval/IO/Task parity micro-corpus compiled from source by the prototype pipeline, plus loading a precompiled Reference-built plugin at the pin — through the prototype intrinsic table operating on ABI values.

The claim being priced: FLBC values ARE Marrow ABI objects, so interpreted code, native intrinsics, and Reference-built plugins share one calling convention with zero conversion. If that identity does not hold in practice (hidden boxing mismatches, RC discipline drift at the call boundary, initializer ordering surprises), the whole membrane design must be amended BEFORE W5/W6 freeze interfaces.

Acceptance: (a) the parity micro-corpus (pure computation, Nat/String/Array ops, closures, IO println/file ops, Task spawn/bind/get ordering, panics, partial non-termination guard) produces observably identical results to the Reference running the same corpus; (b) a real precompiled plugin dlopens, resolves lean_* imports against prototype Marrow exports, runs its initializers in Reference order, and its functions are callable from interpreted code; (c) RC balance verified (no leak, no double-free) under the debug ownership shadow. Tests: the corpus + plugin harness become the seed of the C4 native-ABI probe family and the PG-7 interpreter benchmark baseline.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — the executed artifact under test must be produced from source by the FrankenLean prototype FIR/FLBC pipeline. The Reference may compile/run the same source only as an isolated Tribunal oracle. Reference-generated FIR/FLBC, an upstream runtime embedded in the host, or host-language execution is not the prototype path. The precompiled plugin is user-class fixture code, not upstream toolchain implementation.

The corpus manifest binds exact source/import bytes, options, prototype compiler/schema/contract roots, Reference transcript and comparison class, intrinsic row ids, ABI inventory root, platform, and expected effects. The plugin manifest additionally binds bytes/hash, producer pin/toolchain, target/format, required/provided symbols, initializer DAG, ownership/effect signatures, trust class, and expected outputs. Missing/extra imports, ambiguous symbol versions, or root mismatch fail before publication.

Emit one G0SpikeDecisionV1 row: RatifySingleAbiValueDomain, AmendMembrane, or NoGo. Row-level findings cover boxing/tags, retain/release ownership, calls/returns/exceptions, closures, tasks, IO, initializer order, effects, and plugin boundaries. Amendment identifies every converter or interface delta and proves it does not violate §11.1’s ABI register-value contract; an accidental hidden conversion is not success.

Distinguish complete user value/exception, user panic, plugin ABI violation, plugin failure, resource/cancellation Inconclusive, and harness InternalFault. Budgets cover VM steps, recursion/stack, heartbeat, ABI allocations/bytes, tasks/depth, IO bytes/handles, initializer work, output, and logs with checked ownership and retry. Do not claim recovery from arbitrary process/allocator abort beyond the explicitly supported fault model.

The ABI-valued execution identity is an invariant claim with OBSERVED manifest evidence. PG-7 seed measurements are benchmark rows only. Canonical semantic evidence contains logical inputs/outputs/effects/ownership/decision facts and roots; host/time/PID/path/performance facts are linked telemetry. The 1/8/32 lane performs real nonempty parallel corpus/task work where semantics permit, preserves within-program partial order, and reduces canonically.
acceptance_criteria: A manifest-complete real source corpus is compiled by the prototype FIR/FLBC pipeline and executes with actual Marrow ABI register values; pure Nat/String/Array, closures, IO, Tasks, panics/exceptions, guards, cancellation, and budgets match the isolated pinned Reference at declared comparison classes. A manifest-bound real precompiled user plugin resolves prototype exports, validates required/provided symbols and ownership, runs its initializer DAG in Reference order, and is callable with no hidden conversion. Named abi_value_transition_model, rc_exception_join_model, task_partial_order_model, initializer_dag_model, plugin_refusal_atomicity, execution_budget_matrix, and g0_3_no_mock_e2e suites cover all transitions/effects/failures/recovery plus productive 1/8/32 work. Mutants include host/Reference execution substitution, boxing/tag/ownership drift, hidden converter, initializer reorder, task order loss, fallback, partial initialization/publication, outcome promotion, manifest/root mismatch, and fake parallelism. G0SpikeDecisionV1 is exactly RatifySingleAbiValueDomain, fully specified AmendMembrane with rerun, or NoGo; correctness and PG-7 benchmark evidence remain distinct. Strict semantic logs retain corpus/plugin/contracts/intrinsics/resources/ownership/expected/actual/mutations/cleanup/final state separately from telemetry. No mock plugin, upstream runtime fallback, leak/ownership debt, host-language substitute, or unresolved membrane finding satisfies closure.

title: W4: Error recovery + speculative boundaries — repair without changing acceptance
description:
Implement error recovery + the speculative boundary map (§9.3): specified recovery — recovery productions per syntax category, resynchronization tokens, and the RULE that recovery may add diagnostics but never change acceptance. Two boundary products serve the editor: the exact command stream (Reference parser semantics, sole authority over meaning) and a speculative boundary map (a conservative scanner recovering enough structure after malformed edits to schedule work and show syntax diagnostics); speculative boundaries can never commit declarations, and disagreement between the two is journaled as a recovery event.

One law rules everything: no recovered node reaches Athanor without an explicit RecoveredSyntax marker, and no declaration dependent on an unacknowledged repair is ever published (Behavior Note BN-05: extra diagnostics after a syntax error are possible in sound mode; acceptance is unchanged; faithful mode reproduces upstream recovery behavior).

Plus sub-command incremental relex/reparse feeding Lantern snapshots (§14.3): edits inside a command re-lex and re-parse below command granularity where the GrammarEpoch permits. Acceptance: malformed-input corpus keeps serving diagnostics/completions downstream of errors; acceptance-invariance verified (recovery on/off changes no accept/reject verdict on the corpus); incremental reparse products identical to full reparse on the C8 edit sequences. Tests: fuzzed malformed inputs (grammar-mutation fuzzing) never panic and never change acceptance; recovery-event journaling fixtures; RecoveredSyntax-marker propagation tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Preserve two distinct products: the exact command stream is the sole semantic authority, while the conservative speculative boundary map may schedule/editor-highlight only and can never commit a declaration. Every disagreement is journaled with both products and source spans.
2. Recovery productions and resynchronization are specified per syntax category. Every recovered node carries an explicit RecoveredSyntax marker through downstream handling, and no declaration depending on an unacknowledged repair is published. Recovery on versus off changes no accept/reject result; faithful mode reproduces Reference recovery behavior and sound mode may add only documented BN-05 diagnostics.
3. Sub-command relex/reparse respects GrammarEpoch and produces byte-/structure-equivalent authoritative results to a full reparse for all certified C8 edit sequences; stale or epoch-mismatched memo entries cannot publish.
4. Named unit/property/fuzz/mutation suites cover every recovery category, marker propagation, speculative/authoritative disagreement, malformed and adversarial input without panic, acceptance invariance, incremental/full equivalence, cancellation/resource exhaustion as Inconclusive, and failure-atomic snapshot publication. Mutants that publish speculative or unmarked repaired nodes must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock editor/daemon E2E scenario applies real malformed edit sequences, verifies downstream diagnostics/completions, compares recovery on/off acceptance and incremental/full products, exercises a planted invalid repair and restart/recovery, and confirms no repaired declaration escaped publication. Concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON include run/request/snapshot/edit/epoch ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, source/input/product hashes, expected/actual acceptance and diagnostics, disagreement/repair markers, threads/seed/budgets/timings/status, artifacts, cleanup, and asserted final generation.
6. CI archives deterministic fixtures, edit sequences, journals, mutation results, and evidence manifests; close evidence names each suite and no editor compatibility or acceptance claim is earned from mocked boundaries.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — universal acceptance preservation is enforced by separating authoritative recovered grammar publication from candidate publication and refusing any unclassified delta; corpus tests detect but do not prove universality. Component closure uses the real Vellum session harness. The full daemon/editor scenario remains owned by W9 and consumes this result, preventing a backward dependency. Recovery tests assert exact prior grammar/session roots after injected publication faults.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical authoritative NDJSON contains normalized semantic inputs, roots, decisions, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, time, paths, cache/performance, and scheduler facts are linked bounded telemetry only. Independent validators reject unknown schema, extra/missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, telemetry in semantic hashes, and final-state mismatch. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves nonempty completed work and canonical reduction.

title: G0-6: Fuel-parity spike — calibrate the heartbeat law tick-for-tick
description:
G0-6 Fuel-parity spike (§22.1-6, feeds §4.2/§6.3/§8.5): calibrate the heartbeat-counting law — upstream counts heartbeats in allocation-linked units (maxHeartbeats default 200000, units of thousands) — on an instrumented corpus until faithful-mode timeout behavior matches the Reference tick-for-tick, and document any irreducible divergence class.

Why it matters: determinism of FAILURE is part of determinism. Mathlib and downstream CI depend on maxHeartbeats/maxRecDepth limits firing on the same inputs; a reimplementation that times out differently silently changes which proofs are accepted in practice. The heartbeat hook lives in the owned allocator (§6.3), which is why the allocator is not an off-the-shelf component. This spike also prices risk R11 (heartbeat parity chaotic at fine grain) and resolves OQ-2 (the achievable tolerance class).

Acceptance: (a) an instrumented corpus where Reference heartbeat consumption is captured per declaration (instrumented-oracle apparatus); (b) prototype counting law reproduces timeout/no-timeout verdicts on every corpus entry, with tick deltas quantified; (c) a declared tolerance class (exact / bounded-drift / law-level parity per BN-08) ratified into the plan; (d) maxRecDepth accounting verified on deep-recursion fixtures. Tests: fuel-parity fixtures join the faithful-mode differential rig; any future allocator change must keep them green.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
FuelParityDecisionV1 freezes the measured law rather than only a corpus score. It binds epoch/pin, Reference patch and binary, prototype revision, charge-event taxonomy and source anchors, option names/defaults/scales mechanically extracted from the pin, mode/phase/target/platform, maxHeartbeats and maxRecDepth semantics, counter width/overflow law, cancellation checkpoints, and the chosen claim class. Semantic units are logical events and checked integer counters; wall time, allocator RSS, CPU instructions, host pointers, and scheduler timing never substitute for heartbeat facts.

The instrumented oracle must provide a non-perturbing prefix trace of each charge site and recursion-depth transition. Final totals alone are insufficient: prototype and Reference compare cumulative counts and timeout eligibility at deterministic event checkpoints so compensating dropped/double charges cannot pass. maxRecDepth has its own enter/leave/unwind model and is never inferred from allocation counts. The trace consumes the noninterference guarantee owned by G0-9.

Tolerance is a versioned matrix over relevant operation family, mode, target and platform, joined conservatively into the public faithful claim. Exact, bounded-drift and law-level cells are never averaged. A nonzero tolerance must state exactly which limit intervals can diverge and cannot support an unconditional tick-for-tick or same-limit verdict claim. If the result is not exact, this spike atomically amends D-07, sections 4.2/6.3/8.5, BN-08, the Parity Ledger, diagnostics/cache rules, and every downstream allocator/kernel/VM acceptance contract before G0 can close. Inconclusive or an unpriced cell blocks the interface decision.

The prototype exposes a read-only FuelChargePlan and usage record for production owners; it is a spike artifact, not a hidden runtime component or compatibility shim. Trace events, output bytes, declarations, names/depth, work, recursion, storage and cancellation have explicit limits. Exhaustion produces typed Inconclusive with a deterministic non-authoritative prefix and no tolerance decision.

G0 DECISION INTEGRATION — emit exactly one G0SpikeDecisionV1 whose domain payload is FuelParityDecisionV1. Outcome is RatifyFuelParityLaw, AmendFuelParityLaw, or NoGo mapped to Ratified/Amended/NoGo. This row closes OQ-2 at G0: an amendment carries exact §25 wording and every downstream fuel/heartbeat/default/cache/interface change plus a complete rerun; naming a later owner cannot defer the unknown. Evidence authority, D7 claim type, observed scope, contract/fixture/mutation/E2E roots, resource usage, limitations, and affected interfaces use the Tribunal schema. No averaged tolerance, incomplete event prefix, unresolved maxRecDepth interaction, failed run, Inconclusive/InternalFault, or benchmark-only result can satisfy the decision.
acceptance_criteria:
A manifest-bound instrumented corpus records pinned Reference heartbeat and maxRecDepth consumption per declaration, including exact-bound, one-under/over, allocator-heavy, reduction-heavy, recursion-heavy, cancellation, and fault cases. The prototype allocator/kernel counting seam reproduces timeout versus completion on every row, records exact tick deltas without saturation/wrap, and never turns exhaustion into rejection or acceptance. OQ-2 closes with a reviewed exact, bounded-drift, or law-level tolerance class, quantitative evidence, affected modes, rendering/cache rules, Behavior Note BN-08 implications, and an interface decision before allocator/kernel freeze; faithful claims cannot exceed the class. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation tests cover charging sites, batching, nested allocation, recursion, thread partitions, boundaries, cancellation, and recovery; named dropped/double charge, overflow, schedule-dependent count, timeout-promotion, and hidden-clock mutants are killed. The permanent real no-mock fuel E2E compares Reference and prototype across 1/8/32 threads, plants charge-law mutations, restores exact inputs, and retains strict counters, verdicts, roots, processes, cleanup, and final-state evidence. Future allocator changes run the same lane. Dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and differential/thread/resource lanes pass. No timing proxy, verdict-only aggregate without tick data, silent tolerance widening, sound-mode result presented as faithful, Inconclusive collapse, unresolved OQ-2 branch, or deferred listed requirement satisfies closure.

Mechanical pin extraction proves the option defaults, scaling, counter widths and every Reference charge/depth site; hand-copied constants do not count. The manifest covers zero, one-under, exact and one-over limits; charge-free work; single and nested charges; batching; deep recursion and unwind; allocator-, reduction-, elaborator- and VM-heavy paths; cancellation at every fixed checkpoint; counter overflow models; and recovery. Each row binds exact source/fixture, options, event-prefix trace, totals, completion/timeout/depth outcome and authority.

An independent event/depth model and named mutants drop, double, move or batch a charge; alter scale/default; saturate/wrap; skip unwind; observe cancellation late; use wall time; make counts schedule-dependent; promote exhaustion; or let tracing perturb the counter. Every mutant is killed at the intended checkpoint. Productive 1/8/32 runs process real independent declarations and prove byte-identical per-declaration semantic traces/results under fixed inputs; thread labels without partitioned work fail.

Strict semantic NDJSON records decision-cell ID, pin/patch/prototype/fixture/options, event/checkpoint/source anchor, expected/actual prefix count and depth, totals/delta, tolerance class, verdict/authority/cacheability, limits/usage, mutation/first divergence, restoration and final state. Timing/host telemetry is a separate bounded non-authoritative stream referenced by digest. The retained bundle validates offline and rejects missing cells, aggregate-only totals, mixed claim classes, stale pins, trace perturbation, silent truncation, wrong mutants or unresolved OQ-2.

4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. title: W11: Envoy — MCP tools, ProofStateSnapshot, macaroon authority, multi-agent orchestration
description:
Implement Envoy (fln-mcp, §16.3): the agent door — MCP tools over the Lantern session API: goal_state, apply_tactic (against forked snapshots, budgeted), search_lemmas (Bloodhound), check_term, eval (capability-scoped), get_certificate, ledger_query, trace_query (Palimpsest). Budgets (heartbeats, wall, memory, expanded weight) are first-class parameters; every tool result carries provenance; read tools are sound-mode; write-to-file tools are explicit and default-off.

The session primitive: ProofStateSnapshot — a versioned, schema-registered, canonical serialization of an elaboration point (goals with local contexts, metavariable assignments, environment logical root, journal position, option set) — ROUND-TRIPPABLE (a snapshot IS a resumable state), stable across releases, structurally shared with the live store. Authority is macaroon-scoped (asupersync tokens): a credential names readable environment roots, the budget envelope, the consensus policy it may request, and whether write-tools exist — attenuable delegation, every check receipted. Robot responses carry schema/epoch/profile versions, request + snapshot ids, resource facts, data grade (provisional/verified), evidence links; mutating tools carry idempotency keys + preconditions.

On top: transactional multi-agent orchestration — fork a branch per agent, assign disjoint impact regions from Palimpsest cones, stream scoped context packs, accept PATCH PROPOSALS (never direct trusted mutations), re-elaborate + kernel-check each, detect semantic conflicts via §15.3b, merge deterministically, emit a mission receipt with a rollback root — agent success measured by accepted kernel-checked commits. Plus the remote check service (sealed capsule delta in, full-policy consensus verdict + signed transparency-logged receipt out; workers may lie, results validate locally). Acceptance: the tool surface validated against a frozen JSON schema contract; snapshot round-trips bit-stable; a two-agent orchestration scenario end-to-end with a semantic-conflict block demonstrated. Tests: budget-exhaustion typed results (FL-INV-07 over the wire); macaroon attenuation fixtures (a narrowed token cannot widen); idempotency/precondition replay tests; schema-freeze contract tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

## Success Criteria

Portable snapshot codec, MCP/macaroon authority, multi-agent merge/orchestration, and remote consensus service close on exact roots. O(1) live handles remain distinct from portable serialization; authorization never grants proof authority; real local and separate-host E2E and recovery pass.
design:
OQ-11 HANDOFF — Envoy depends explicitly on franken_lean-05g and consumes its semantic-merge v1 allowlist, effect/read-write/generated-name proof rules, replay requirements, and mandatory human-resolution boundary. Multi-agent orchestration may propose patches, but it must re-elaborate and kernel-check them and must block any semantic conflict outside the proven commutative subset; no agent vote or textual cleanliness can bypass that dependency.

TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are fln-zddn portable snapshot codec, franken_lean-87av MCP/macaroon authority, fln-bz4h orchestration/semantic merge, and fln-hq6i remote consensus service. This epic distinguishes O(1) live Lantern handles from size-dependent portable serialization and closes only on exact child/evidence roots plus real local and separate-host E2E.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Expose versioned MCP contracts for goal_state, apply_tactic, search_lemmas, check_term, capability-scoped eval, get_certificate, ledger_query, and trace_query over the real Lantern/session engines. Heartbeat, wall, memory, expanded-weight, cancellation, and output bounds are first-class; read tools are sound-mode and file-mutating tools are explicit, separately authorized, preconditioned, and default-off. 2. ProofStateSnapshot canonically and round-trippably serializes goals/local contexts, metavariable assignments, environment logical root, journal position, options, epoch/profile/schema, and evidence links. Resume preserves semantics and stable identity across supported schema releases while sharing immutable live storage; malformed/stale/foreign snapshots fail typed without publication. 3. Macaroons scope readable roots, tools, budget envelope, consensus policy, expiry, and write authority; attenuation can only narrow and every authorization decision is receipted. Robot results include schema/epoch/profile, request/snapshot ids, resource facts, provisional/verified grade, roots, and evidence; mutations require idempotency keys and atomic preconditions. 4. Orchestration consumes the exact versioned OQ-11 effect-class decision from franken_lean-05g, forks isolated branches, assigns impact regions, streams scoped context, accepts patch proposals only, re-elaborates and kernel-checks each, and auto-merges only rows proven commutative under satisfied Complete/Conservative preconditions. Proposal-only, human-required, unknown, Opaque, and weak-evidence effects block automatic application with a typed explanation. Successful deterministic merges emit a mission receipt plus rollback root. Remote check workers consume sealed deltas and return signed full-policy receipts that are locally validated; workers are never trusted. 5. Named unit/property/model/DPOR/fuzz/mutation suites cover every schema/tool, snapshot round-trip/upgrade/resume, budget exhaustion as Inconclusive, macaroon attenuation/expiry/root scope, idempotency/preconditions, branch isolation, every OQ-11 merge/refusal class, deterministic merge/conflict, rollback, and remote-result validation. Authority-widening, direct-mutation, unchecked-patch, unknown-effect-auto-merge, weak-grade-merge, stale-precondition, and trust-worker mutants must be killed. 6. A shared no-mock E2E lane uses real MCP clients against Envoy for every tool, round-trips and resumes snapshots, proves narrowed credentials cannot widen, runs real concurrent agent branches through one eligible safe merge and proposal-only/human-required/unknown-effect blocks, validates a remote-check receipt, injects failure, rolls back, and confirms recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/client/request/tool/snapshot/token/branch/patch/effect-class/mission/receipt ids, OQ-11 version, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted args/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical input/environment/output/rollback roots, authorization/grade/kernel/merge decisions, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state. 7. CI archives frozen schemas, conformance transcripts, snapshot corpora, DPOR schedules, the consumed OQ-11 table/hash, auth/remote fixtures, mission receipts, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. Mocked clients/workers may support units but cannot close MCP, authority, orchestration, semantic-merge, or remote-trust claims.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — distinguish O(1) live ProofStateSnapshot handle forks from potentially size-dependent portable encoding. Separate portable codec/schema evolution, MCP tools and macaroon authorization, multi-agent orchestration/semantic merge, and remote consensus-check service before this terminal join. Orchestration consumes suggestions and merge-engine authority; remote consensus consumes watch/distribution, certificate verification, and sealed capsules. Named tests cover codec fuzzing, auth denial, capability revocation, merge conflicts, cancellation, remote Byzantine/partial failure, cleanup, and real no-mock service E2E.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.

TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are fln-zddn portable snapshot codec, franken_lean-87av MCP/macaroon authority, fln-bz4h orchestration/semantic merge, and fln-hq6i remote consensus service. This epic distinguishes O(1) live Lantern handles from size-dependent portable serialization and closes only on exact child/evidence roots plus real local and separate-host E2E.

title: W2: stage0 ABI gauntlet — upstream generated C runs the runtime suite against Marrow
description:
Stand up the stage0 ABI gauntlet as a standing Tribunal lane (§6.6, §18.2): compile the Reference own stage0-generated C (test apparatus only, via the optional D2 cc) against Marrow exported lean_* surface, and run the upstream runtime test suite through the membrane.

Why this is the strongest ABI rig available: stage0 is the exact code the ecosystem ships — millions of lines of generated C exercising every corner of the object model, RC discipline, boxing rules, and exported function census. If the membrane is wrong ANYWHERE, upstream own code says so, loudly and early, with a concrete failing call site. This displaces guesswork about ABI corner cases with an executable oracle. Note the D8 boundary: stage0 here is TEST APPARATUS (fixture corpus), never a runtime component; the lane runs in the Tribunal sandbox and its products are conformance evidence only.

Acceptance: the gauntlet builds and links against Marrow on linux-x86-64 first (other platforms staged); the upstream runtime suite passes on the implemented ABI subset with every failure triaged to a contract row (fix or honest Unsupported status); the lane runs in CI on membrane-touching changes. Tests: this IS the test; additionally a seeded-defect mutation (perturb one exported function ownership convention — the gauntlet must catch it, proving the lane kills mutants per §18.2).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria: The Tribunal compiles the pinned Reference stage0-generated C through the sanctioned D2 compiler capsule against Marrow exports and runs the upstream runtime suite as test apparatus only. The exact source/archive/compiler hashes, generated contract root, platform, commands and ABI symbol/ownership coverage are retained; no stage0 product enters release binaries or runtime dependencies. Linux x86-64 is complete first and every additional platform has an explicit matrix status. Every implemented-subset failure maps to one contract/census row and is fixed or remains precise Unsupported; no pass aggregate hides rows. Unit/harness/mutation tests validate build/link/run classification, symbol coverage, output bounds, interruption/failure cleanup and evidence parsing; named ownership, tag/layout, retain/release and exported-signature defects are each caught by the gauntlet. The real no-mock E2E includes successful suite, planted ABI defect, exact restoration and recovery with strict compiler/source/binary/symbol/process/final-state bundles. CI runs the lane on membrane changes; dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS and platform/artifact D8 scans pass. No handwritten ABI substitute, mock generated C, stage0 runtime component, compiler outside D2, unsupported failure counted as pass, unresolved implemented-row failure, or deferred listed platform status satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: fln-kernel K1: nested-inductive translation — the _nested.* auxiliary block machinery (Lean.Syntax to full ruleset)
description:
Follow-up to franken_lean-ap6, which admits every Init.Prelude declaration kind but applies a documented PARTIAL ruleset to the one nested block (Lean.Syntax, num_nested=2): well-typedness of types/ctors/recursor types/rule RHSes and arity cross-checks run, but strict positivity (KR-606) and recursor regeneration (KR-800..803) are skipped, because both are defined on the pin's auxiliary translation: elim_nested_inductive_fn (inductive.cpp:800-1100) replaces nested occurrences (e.g. Array Syntax) with _nested.* auxiliary types, validates the resulting mutual block, and translates results back (KR-608). Implement that translation so nested blocks get the FULL ruleset: reconstruct the auxiliary mutual block from the decoded post-elaboration rows (the olean does not carry the pre-elaboration declaration — the reconstruction inverts the translation, instantiating the nested type's own spec from the environment), run positivity + regeneration on it, and map the regenerated recursors back for the byte-exact cross-check (Syntax.rec/rec_1/rec_2 with 3 motives). Priority note: Init.Prelude has exactly ONE nested block and it already admits under the partial ruleset with its recursors typechecked — this slice matters at G-scale (mathlib is full of nested inductives), not for the current census. Verification: Syntax block moves from nested_partial_blocks=1 to full admission in the replay census (ratchet the e2e assertion when it lands); unit fixtures for a nested block (e.g. Tree with List Tree field) with byte-exact regenerated recursors; mutation kills on the translation's occurrence-replacement and the translate-back mapping; census + gates per the standard DoD.

## Acceptance Criteria

Complete the pin-anchored nested-inductive auxiliary translation so every nested block, beginning with Lean.Syntax, receives the same full positivity, constructor, universe, recursor-generation, and decoded-rule cross-check as non-nested blocks; no partial-ruleset admission satisfies closure. Unit tests must cover successful single and mutually nested families, nested occurrences under multiple type constructors, parameters and indices, recursive and reflexive shapes, universe and Prop boundaries, malformed translations, exact resource limits, cancellation, InternalFault, failure atomicity, and recovery. Independent model and property tests must verify occurrence replacement, auxiliary naming and hygiene, translate-back identity, canonical ordering, and byte-exact regenerated recursors across insertion and declaration permutations. Named mutants must remove or misroute nested occurrences, alter auxiliary names, skip positivity, trust decoded rules, corrupt motive or minor mapping, and break translate-back; each must be killed by a discriminating test. The real pinned no-mock replay must move nested_partial_blocks from 1 to 0, exercise positive, deliberately invalid, resource, cancellation, and recovery phases at threads 1, 8, and 32, and preserve byte-identical authoritative verdicts, roots, diagnostics, and counters. Retain concise human logs and strict schema-versioned NDJSON with exact input and fixture hashes, expected and actual rule results, first divergence, budgets and consumption, separate bounded streams, authority and cacheability, cleanup, and final state; validator self-tests must reject malformed, extra-field, stale-input, wrong-mutant, merged-stream, and surviving-mutant evidence. No oracle runtime, unchecked or partial fallback, acceptance or rejection of Inconclusive, unsafe code, or covenant overrun satisfies closure. All focused tests, replay mutation and recovery lanes, scripts/check.sh, fmt, cargo test, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, structure and line-count guards, the bc7 matrix must contain zero uncovered nested-inductive rows and all currently runnable zht integration checks that do not require unfinished sibling slices must be green with retained artifacts recorded. Final zht and G1 aggregation remains downstream and is not a circular prerequisite for this child.

PIN-PROBE CORRECTION — Lean.Syntax has two nested channels at the pin: direct Array Syntax and worklist-cascaded List Syntax. The translation must discover both transitively, deduplicate auxiliary work deterministically, and preserve Reference auxiliary/recursor ordering. Closure requires regressions for direct plus cascaded discovery, duplicate reachability, stable auxiliary order, and a mutant that stops after the direct Array edge.

comments.author: OliveAnchor
comments.text: PROBE COMPLETE (census deep-dump + pin recon). Decoded reality of the one Prelude nested block: Lean.Syntax (Type, 0 params/indices, singleton all, num_nested=2 — the field counts AUX TYPES, matching the pin's m_aux2nested.size(), question resolved). Ctors: missing; node(SourceInfo, SyntaxNodeKind, Array Syntax); atom(SourceInfo, String); ident(SourceInfo, Substring.Raw, Name, List Syntax.Preresolved). Only ONE direct nested occurrence: node's 'Array Syntax'. The second aux comes from the pin's WORKLIST cascade: copying Array (params instantiated alpha:=Syntax) yields Array.mk's field 'List Syntax', itself nested, so List is copied too — aux order [_nested.Array, _nested.List]. ident's 'List Preresolved' is NOT nested (Preresolved precedes the block). Decoded recursors: rec/rec_1/rec_2, each motives=3 minors=7 lparams=[u], SHARED telescope (motive order: Syntax, Array Syntax, List Syntax = main-then-aux creation order; minors: 4 Syntax ctors [node's minor takes IH (motive_Array field), ident's minor has NO IH] + Array.mk [IH motive_List] + List.nil/cons), differing only in the major (Syntax vs Array Syntax vs List Syntax) and rules (4 / 1 / 2). Pin algorithm mapped in full (inductive.cpp: elim_nested_inductive_fn 882-1077, restore 795-873, rec-name map 1088-1114, driver 1116-1182): forward pass detects nested apps in ctor fields (head=declared inductive, first nparams args mention block types), copies the head's WHOLE mutual block with instantiate_lparams(I_lvls)+instantiate_pi_params(nested args)+re-abstraction over d's params, dedups by param-normalized structural eq, worklist-iterates; ordinary checker (incl. positivity) runs on the TRANSLATED block; recursors renamed main.rec/.rec_1/.rec_2 and restore_nested maps aux constants back. KEY DESIGN FACTS: (a) decoded rows are the RESTORED forms, so the forward pass applies directly to decoded ctor types; (b) aux names never survive restore, so our aux naming need not replicate mk_unique_name — deterministic names fine; (c) type order in the translated block must be main-types-then-aux-in-creation-order so motive indices line up; (d) after full-ruleset checking of the translated block, regenerate recursors, apply rec-name map + restore, compare byte-exact (ap6 pin-expr-eq) vs decoded rec/rec_1/rec_2; recompute num_nested=aux count and re-enable the is_rec/is_reflexive cross-check for nested blocks. admit.rs insertion points: nested gates at 1377/1393/1423(check_constructors full flag)/1438-1443(check_nested_partial 1451-1488 to be replaced); Engine struct 407-428, entry 1670.
