title: W11: Folio — native docs facet, receipt-bound pages, incremental rendering
description:
Implement Folio (fln-doc, §16.2): doc-gen4-surface-compatible documentation as a Ledger facet — docstring markdown through franken_markdown, mathematics through fmd-math rendered natively to HTML and PDF (zero browser-side math stack), syntax highlighting via a Vellum-fed lexer, cross-reference and instance graphs via franken_networkx, INCREMENTAL (only changed declarations re-render — mathlib docs become minutes, local, offline).

Documents are receipt-bound: a rendered page embeds the logical root and receipt links for every checked excerpt, and a later source change marks the artifact STALE rather than leaving a verified badge lying (the Witness discipline of §20.4 applied to documentation). The frontier lane: franken_manim consumes Palimpsest event sequences to render animated proof explanations (goal transformations under a tactic, unification constraints resolving, induction structure, before/after of a repair) — explanatory projections that link to checked declarations and are never themselves certificates.

Acceptance: doc output for a mathlib slice matches the doc-gen4 surface conventions (URLs, anchors, structure) so existing links and tooling survive; incremental re-render touches only the changed-declaration cone; receipt-staleness demonstrated (edit a source, the page marks stale). Tests: rendering golden tests (HTML structure + PDF layout smoke); math-rendering fixtures vs known TeX corpus; incremental-facet metamorphic tests (doc-irrelevant edits re-render nothing); link-integrity checks across the generated set.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Generate doc-gen4-surface-compatible URLs, anchors, page structure, cross references, instance graphs, and searchable metadata as a Ledger facet. Render docstring markdown with franken_markdown, TeX mathematics with native fmd-math to HTML and PDF, and syntax highlighting from Vellum; no browser-side math, LaTeX process, network, or external renderer is required.
2. Every checked excerpt/page embeds its declaration/environment logical roots and receipt/certificate links. Source, proof, dependency, epoch, or evidence changes invalidate the exact facet cone and mark previously published pages stale before replacement; stale content cannot display a verified badge. Doc-irrelevant edits render nothing.
3. Optional animated explanations consume Palimpsest events and link to checked declarations while being labeled explanatory projections, never certificates or trust evidence. Missing/partial events degrade honestly without changing page validity.
4. Named unit/property/golden/metamorphic/mutation suites cover markdown/HTML structure, native math and PDF layout, URLs/anchors/links, lexer highlighting, graph data, deterministic rendering, receipt binding/staleness, exact incremental cones, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and atomic publication. Stale-badge, excess-rerender, broken-anchor, external-renderer, and projection-as-proof mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane builds real docs for a mathlib slice fully offline, compares surface conventions with doc-gen4 fixtures, validates HTML/PDF and all links, applies doc-irrelevant and declaration-changing edits, proves exact rerender/staleness behavior, renders an available event animation, injects a broken receipt, and verifies safe recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/facet/page/declaration/render/link/receipt/generation ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget/network facts, canonical source/environment/page/PDF/receipt roots, invalidation/render decisions, expected/actual, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives HTML/PDF goldens, math corpus, link reports, cone witnesses, stale/recovery fixtures, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. No link compatibility, offline, incremental, or verified-page claim closes from mocked rendering or unbound receipts.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — require a complete pinned-Corpus offline documentation build with accessibility checks and a full URL, anchor, link, asset, declaration, and receipt census. Target-neutral content has deterministic rules; target-specific operational envelopes remain separate. Slice builds are development evidence only. Mutation/fuzz/E2E cases cover broken links/anchors, duplicate ids, math/layout edge cases, accessibility failures, stale receipts, interrupted incremental publication, offline loading, and exact recovery.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W8: Ledger & Lake — content-addressed build fabric, invalidation law, cache
description:
Workstream W8 "Ledger & Lake" (§13): the build fabric (Bet B1) — the environment as a Merkle DAG of content-addressed declarations; builds as memoized queries; the ecosystem quality-of-life complaint #1 (file-granular invalidation) deleted.

Scope: Ledger records (decl_hash over name, elaborated statement, value class, direct dep hashes, options-that-matter, epoch, mode) generalized to demand nodes across five planes (source/language/trust/build/service) with retention tiers T0-T4; the incremental law (§13.2): true-dependency-cone invalidation on recorded edges, early cutoff (unchanged output digest stops the cascade; statement-hash vs value-hash separation), and the published, fixture-tested, perturbation-validated invalidation matrix per change class with conservative fallbacks (FL-INV-05: anything not proven irrelevant is relevant); the Lake surface (lakefile.lean executes on Golem — the config DSL is user code, so Golem IS the compatibility mechanism; lakefile.toml fast path; require via D2 git; exit codes and --json pinned; fln build explain shows Reference-decision vs native-decision); cache federation (CAS sync over atp multi-donor fountain-coded with HTTPS fallback; remote entries are untrusted until validated — malformed entry = miss + security event; fln cache shim preserves community muscle memory); epoch-stable caching + bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) + the migration autopilot (§13.6); watch mode + distributed elaboration (results re-verified by local Crucible — distribution without distributed trust).

Exit feeds gates G4 (mixed-build matrix) and G6 (PG-3 cone, PG-10 cache).

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — W8 closure includes fln-publish-rewrite-indexes-gca so the plan claim that per-library indexes ship through CAS and olean-next is proven at the subsystem boundary. The migration autopilot itself is downstream integration in franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w; W8 provides sound cache/bridge/invalidation primitives without a placeholder repair engine.

Stage topology: G4 consumes the concrete Ledger core needed for full-Corpus builds: records/CAS, FL-INV-05 invalidation and early cutoff, Lake, and their real evidence. Full W8 additionally includes rewrite-index publication, watch/distributed elaboration, cache federation, and epoch bridges, so the epic blocks G6 Ledger GA rather than delaying G4. W8 consumes the G2-proven runtime through its concrete Lake dependency and does not wait on W5 aggregation bookkeeping. Every W8 feature remains mandatory before G6.
acceptance_criteria:
W8 closes only when Ledger and Lake implement one deterministic, crash-safe build fabric over a typed Merkle DAG. Declaration and demand-node records bind name, statement/value class, direct dependency hashes, relevant options, epoch, mode, plane, determinism class, evidence, and retention tier across source/language/trust/build/service planes. FL-INV-05 is enforced: the perturbation-validated change-class matrix proves every irrelevant change and conservatively invalidates anything not proven irrelevant; true-cone invalidation, statement-versus-value separation, dominators/SCCs, and digest early cutoff never accept a stale result. CAS promotion and frankensqlite journals are atomic under kill-9, corruption, disk-full, partial download, duplicate work, and restart. Lake runs lakefile.lean on Golem, supports the compatible TOML path and D2 git capsule, preserves CLI/exit/robot schemas, and explains Reference versus native rebuild decisions. Rewrite indexes publish through CAS/olean-next with exact roots. atp multi-donor federation and fallback validate all remote bytes before use; malformed data is a miss plus security event. Watch and distributed elaboration preserve schedule independence, cancellation, local Crucible re-verification, and no distributed trust. Epoch bridges are allowlisted, versioned, conservative, reversible, and never silently repair. Unit/property/model/metamorphic/mutation suites cover record/key identity, retention, invalidation classes, early cutoff, Lake resolution, CAS transactions, remote validation, watch events, distribution, bridge refusal, budgets, and recovery; named stale-hit, dropped-edge, partial-promotion, unverified-remote, and cross-epoch mutants are killed. Real no-mock E2E builds mathlib/Lake projects cold and warm, performs leaf and interface edits, publishes/loads indexes, hydrates across clean/lossy paths, injects crashes/corruption/network loss/disk full, and proves exact recovery in strict bundles. G4-required core plus full W8 federation/distribution/bridge scope, all children, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, fault/metamorphic/thread/platform/performance lanes including PG-3/PG-10 are green before G6. No file-cone substitute, heuristic stale acceptance, mock-only cache/Lake, external trust, hidden repair, unvalidated distributed result, unclassified bridge, or deferred listed W8 feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — terminal W8 closure names every exact child and evidence root for Ledger-production handoff, cross-module speculation, PG-3 production performance, local and remote rewrite publication, federation and epoch bridge, watch and distributed build, FL-INV-05, G4, PG-3, and PG-10. Epoch migration remains a typed W12 handoff rather than duplicated implementation. Prose-only completion, same-host-only distributed proof, or missing production adapter cannot close.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Unknown schema, missing/duplicate ids, broken order/linkage, unbound artifacts, truncation, merged streams, or cleanup/final-state mismatch fail. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W4: GrammarEpoch + memoization — grammar as versioned data, typed parser effects
description:
Implement GrammarEpoch versioning + parse memoization (§9.1): the grammar state as versioned DATA, not hidden globals — a GrammarEpoch content-hashes the active syntax declarations, parser descriptors, precedence tables, scoped notation, token parsers, and relevant options; every parse node binds the epoch it was recognized under; memoization keys on (position, category, precedence, GrammarEpoch); a syntax-changing command invalidates exactly the parse products that read the changed components.

Parser registrations emit typed effect summaries (AddsSyntax, AddsToken, ChangesPrecedence, OpensScope, RegistersMacro, OpaqueParserEffect) — an opaque effect is legal for fidelity but forces the conservative suffix barrier and disables distributed parsing past that point (precision is earned by evidence, never assumed; the system starts conservative and ratchets, §10.6).

Why load-bearing: this is what makes sub-command incremental reparse (Lantern), the Ledger notation/syntax invalidation-matrix row, and distributed parsing sound rather than hopeful. Acceptance: epoch hashing deterministic and stable across hosts; memoization hit/miss behavior verified against the invalidation matrix fixtures (a notation change invalidates exactly the affected epoch descendants after the activation point); opaque-effect barrier demonstrated. Tests: property tests — memoized parse == fresh parse under any epoch-respecting edit sequence; C8 incremental edit-sequence corpus with expected invalidation cones.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. GrammarEpoch is deterministic versioned data that content-hashes all active syntax declarations, parser descriptors, precedence tables, scoped notation, token parsers, and relevant options; every parse product records the epoch under which it was recognized. Cross-host/profile fixtures yield the same epoch for the same canonical grammar state.
2. Memoization keys include position, category, precedence, and GrammarEpoch. Syntax changes invalidate exactly products that read changed components after activation, matching the Ledger invalidation matrix; stale or foreign-epoch products cannot publish.
3. Registrations emit typed summaries for AddsSyntax, AddsToken, ChangesPrecedence, OpensScope, RegistersMacro, or OpaqueParserEffect. Opaque effects are fidelity-legal but impose a conservative suffix barrier and disable distributed parsing beyond it until stronger evidence exists.
4. Named unit/property/metamorphic/mutation suites cover canonical epoch hashing, each effect summary and dependency edge, notation/precedence/scope/token invalidation, activation boundaries, cache hit/miss facts, opaque barriers, malformed registrations, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and memoized-parse equals fresh-parse over generated epoch-respecting edit sequences. Over-invalidation, under-invalidation, stale-hit, and omitted-barrier mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane applies the C8 incremental edit corpus to a real parser session, demonstrates exact invalidation cones and cache behavior for a syntax change, demonstrates the opaque suffix barrier, compares incremental with cold full parses, and verifies clean recovery after a rejected registration. Human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON record run/session/edit/epoch/component ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, canonical grammar/input/product hashes, cache and invalidation decisions with reasons, expected/actual outputs, thread/platform/seed/budgets/timings/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives cross-host determinism, edit fixtures, invalidation witnesses, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests; close evidence names every suite and may not claim distributed-parse precision beyond the measured typed-effect coverage.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — canonical grammar identity compares complete canonical rows after digest lookup so collisions cannot cross-hit. Unknown options, registrations, or effects conservatively invalidate. The effect model covers add, scope open/close/restore, shadowing, option changes, imported grammar state, removals, and opaque unknown fallback. Property/model tests cover collision injection, interleaving, restoration, imported state, unknown effects, and deterministic recovery.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical authoritative NDJSON contains normalized semantic inputs, roots, decisions, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, time, paths, cache/performance, and scheduler facts are linked bounded telemetry only. Independent validators reject unknown schema, extra/missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, telemetry in semantic hashes, and final-state mismatch. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves nonempty completed work and canonical reduction.

title: W3: Crucible & Bignum — dual-engine kernel, independent checker, receipts
description:
Workstream W3 "Crucible & Bignum" (§8): the trusted kernel — the one subsystem where FrankenLean volunteers to be outnumbered — plus the owned arithmetic beneath it.

Deliverable order is constitutional: KERNEL_CONTRACT.md (the Judgment Specification, §8.1b) comes FIRST — a rule-by-rule account of the implemented type theory, each rule anchored to Reference source lines, against which both engines and the Tribunal generators are built. Then: K1 certified small-step evaluator (auditability-first, the code a skeptical logician reads in an afternoon); K2 NbE accelerator (memoized whnf keyed on term-hash x transparency, lazy delta by height) cross-checked against K1 continuously; fln-checker, a deliberately DIFFERENT independent implementation (eager normalization, own decoder over canonical wire objects, no unsafe-membrane dependency, WASM-clean, non-sharing proven structurally); fln-bignum (limb-based, ABI-layout-matching, thresholds tuned on the kernel-reduction corpus, PG-K gate vs GMP); typestate admission (UnvalidatedExpr -> ScopedExpr -> CheckedExpr, DeclarationEnvelope, four-way verdict incl. Inconclusive per FL-INV-07); typed resource budgets (§8.2c); fuel parity (§8.5); certificates + receipts + transparency log (§8.6); consensus policies where disagreement halts and never outvotes (§8.3c).

Covenant: fln-kernel <= 12 KLOC, forbid(unsafe), deps = fln-bignum + read-only term views + core/alloc only, one exported authority (check), CI-counted. Divergence law: kernel disagreement with the Reference at the pin is release-blocking; soundness beats bug-parity (D23). Exit: gate G1 — every mathlib olean re-checked with zero acceptance divergence, PG-1 met.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design:
EPIC CLOSEOUT FOR FL-INV-02 — the W3 evidence map must show the single authority API, opaque checked-state construction boundary, every environment publication consumer, prohibited dependency edges, compile-fail proofs, bypass mutation kills, and real no-mock admission E2E. K1/K2/checker consensus strengthens evidence but never changes the constitutional rule that fln-kernel rejection or inconclusive makes publication impossible.

Closure topology: component beads build the kernel/checker/receipt/CLI surfaces; fln-ob6 is the concrete G1 integration and performance gate; this W3 epic depends on that gate as its declared exit. It does not depend on the entire W2 epic, because the exact olean reader and environment/term-view prerequisites are already carried by fln-fur and the component graph. This preserves every W3 and G1 requirement while allowing the Independent Judge to ship before unrelated W2 frontier/runtime/plugin work.
acceptance_criteria: W3 closes only when the reviewed KERNEL_CONTRACT judgment inventory precedes and governs two complete kernel engines plus the independent checker. K1 is the audit-first small-step authority; K2 is an NbE accelerator cross-checked continuously; fln-checker uses a structurally independent decoder and normalization strategy, is WASM-clean, and shares no unsafe membrane. fln-kernel remains forbid-unsafe, no more than 12 KLOC, depends only on the allowed minimal closure, exposes exactly check over Environment and Declaration, and has no constructible checked-state or publication bypass. Typestate admission, every declaration family and nested/private-reference rule, universes, reduction, definitional equality, recursors, quotient/inductive checks, fuel parity, typed expanded-weight/resource budgets, and Accepted/Rejected/Inconclusive/InternalFault separation match the contract and FL-INV-02/07. Owned bignum preserves ABI layout and exact arithmetic while meeting PG-K. Canonical judgments, certificates, consensus policies, receipts, and transparency entries bind inputs, roots, engine, fuel, mode, and evidence; disagreement always halts, and unknown certificate versions fall back to recomputation rather than widening trust. Compile-fail/API-visibility and dependency-graph tests prove authority confinement. Exhaustive unit/property/model/foreign-witness/differential/fuzz/mutation tests cover every rule and malformed/boundary case; named mutants skip each critical check, forge checked state, alter fuel, bypass consensus, corrupt certificates, and promote Inconclusive, and each is killed. Real no-mock E2E rechecks all pinned mathlib oleans, exercises malformed/corrupt/resource/cancellation/fault/recovery paths, validates independent witnesses and receipts, and retains minimized divergences in strict bundles. G1, all W3 children, scripts/check.sh, full cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, kernel line/dependency/unsafe guards, corpus/thread/platform/performance rigs are green. No partial judgment inventory, shared implementation masquerading as independence, quorum override, unchecked engine result, unsafe laundering, sampled-corpus closure, bug-parity soundness regression, or deferred listed W3 feature satisfies closure.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: Gate G3: The Mirror Holds — Init/Std from source, T2 identity, fixpoint bit-stable
description:
Convergence gate G3 "The Mirror Holds" (§22.3): the native toolchain elaborates Init and Std end-to-end FROM SOURCE — every builtin either native behind its facade row or library-code source-elaborated, zero upstream implementation executing anywhere — with T2 identity against the Reference, the stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint bit-stable, and the metaprogram-corpus rig (curated mathlib-tactic slice through the facade) green at its declared L-levels. The ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out check passes on the gate build.

This is the gate that proves the central bet (B2): the parser, elaborator, instance engine, tactic framework, and scheduler reproduce the Reference at stdlib scale, and real ecosystem metaprogram code cannot distinguish the native facade from the machinery it replaced. It is the last gate before the full Corpus becomes the target.

Evidence bundle: T2 differential results over Init/Std (acceptance, diagnostics, statement-level environment identity); the fixpoint runs (bit-identical oleans + logical roots across double elaboration and a freshly rebuilt toolchain); metaprogram-rig results with the facade-row L-level table; census-totality check output; the D8 compile-out proof on the gate binaries; PG-5 thread-matrix results at stdlib scale.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: G3 consumes the concrete Mirror conformance integration rig, the proven G2 runtime, and the completed Vellum contract. franken_lean-26y is now blocked on every W6 production component plus parser, kernel/checker, Golem, and Tribunal machinery, so its T2/T3, real-tactic, fixpoint, thread-matrix, and facade-row evidence cannot run against stubs or an incomplete elaborator. The W6 epic depends on G3 as its declared exit; G3 never waits on W6 aggregation bookkeeping.
acceptance_criteria: G3 closes only when the native parser, macro expander, elaborator, unifier, instance engine, tactic framework, scheduler, VM, and Native Mirror elaborate all pinned Init and Std source end to end without executing upstream implementation code. Every builtin census row is either a native typed facade implementation or upstream-authored library source elaborated by FrankenLean; census totality rejects missing, duplicate, stale, unknown, fallback, or mispartitioned rows. T2 is complete over Init/Std: acceptance, diagnostic positions/severity and faithful rendering, and statement-level environment identity match the Reference with every residual classified under the oracle ladder. The stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint is bit-identical for oleans, diagnostics, logical/provenance roots, receipts, and generated names both on the same toolchain and after a fresh rebuild. Real ecosystem metaprograms and curated mathlib tactics execute on Golem against the native facade, with environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, generated names, effects, and per-row L-levels diffed honestly; no row advances beyond its evidence. PG-5 proves bit identity at 1, 8, and 32 threads per commit and 96 or more weekly under registered CGSE policies. ORACLE_FALLBACK and upstream implementation code are proven absent from gate source and release artifacts across all workspace members, targets, and feature combinations. Named mutants kill schedule-dependent merge, wrong instance order, generated-name drift, normalizer overreach, facade misrouting, hidden oracle paths, fixpoint instability, and Inconclusive promotion. The real no-mock E2E covers fresh Init/Std builds, metaprogram execution, planted divergence/leak/fault, exact restoration, and recovery through the shared evidence contract with bounded schema-versioned logs and independently validated bundles. G3 consumes the complete R8 mitigation ledger from G0-4 and the instrumented-oracle expansion traces: every hygiene/macro divergence and Behavior Note has a fixture, comparison class, native owner, and replay result, and no undocumented behavior is reclassified or dropped merely because the broader pipeline passes. All owning bead gates, G2, Vellum, scripts/check.sh, fmt, cargo check/test, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, differential/trace-replay/Mirror rigs, mutation campaigns, thread matrix, and fault drills are green. No prebuilt-stdlib substitute, hosted Lean.Elab implementation, mock-only tactic, partial census, hidden exclusion, unresolved R8 row, overclaimed L-level, or deferred listed feature satisfies G3.

title: fln-kernel K1: admission slice — inductive families (KR-6xx), quotient init (KR-95x), opaque/mutual (KR-977)
description:

PROBE-FIRST MANDATE (the d4x lesson, twice paid): before implementing, extend the replay harness so the 449 currently-unchecked declarations run through the new admission path behind a flag, and triage the ACTUAL failure families with FLN_REPLAY_PROBE + first_divergence — do not implement from the pin source narrative alone. Target census after: unchecked={} for Init.Prelude (everything the pin kernel checks, we check). Verification: per-rule mutation campaign (dropped positivity check and inverted universe condition are NAMED mandatory mutants per AGENTS §Testing), boundary tests in k1_judgments per bc7 discipline, census floor in scripts/e2e/kernel_replay.sh ratchets to assert the unchecked map shrinks to {} (or the honest typed subset), covenant LOC budget (currently 2,758/12,000 — this is the biggest remaining slice; if it threatens the budget the plan must be amended FIRST per D6). title: fln-kernel and olean: govern declarations whose private auxiliaries are absent from artifacts
description:
The ap6 replay census found six non-safe helper definitions whose bodies reference private auxiliary declarations that are absent from the pinned serialized module. The current evidence can therefore report every declaration as checked and accepted while also reporting nonsafe_with_unserialized_refs=6, which conflates successful kernel validation with an artifact that cannot supply the complete dependency closure. This bead owns the cross-layer contract between the olean reader, Grimoire environment, K1 admission, Parity Ledger, and G1 reporting for that case. Mechanically census the exact affected declarations and missing references from the pin; anchor the Reference behavior for unsafe and non-safe admission; determine whether the missing closure can be reconstructed deterministically from governed source or extracted fixture data without violating the Oracle-Only Law. If it can, materialize and kernel-check it through the ordinary authority path. If it cannot, return a typed ArtifactIncomplete Inconclusive outcome with a canonical witness, never Accepted, Rejected, checked, or cacheable, and ensure headline and per-kind counts keep validated, unsafe-not-kernel-checked, and artifact-incomplete rows separate. No hidden allowlist, name-only exception, runtime Reference fallback, or count that presents incomplete rows as checked.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run the pinned Init.Prelude admission census and compare the headline checked and accepted totals with the nonsafe_with_unserialized_refs counter.
2. Inspect the six affected declaration bodies and resolve every referenced constant against the decoded module environment; the named private auxiliaries are absent from the serialized dependency closure.
3. Route those declarations through strict ordinary admission without a special case; validation cannot establish the complete dependency closure from the artifact alone.
4. Observe that a summary which folds those rows into checked or accepted overstates the evidence and can incorrectly satisfy a G1 count.

## Acceptance Criteria

The exact pin census and Reference semantics are source-anchored and versioned. Each affected declaration has a row naming the declaration, missing private references, safety class, artifact and source hashes, attempted resolution, final typed outcome, authority and cacheability, and evidence grade. Ordinary complete definitions still check through the sole public kernel authority; reconstructable closures are deterministic and content-bound; irreconstructable closures yield Inconclusive ArtifactIncomplete with canonical witnesses and cannot enter an environment or satisfy G1 acceptance. Unit tests cover all six known rows, a complete control, zero, one-under, exact, and one-over resource boundaries, missing and extra auxiliaries, digest collisions, stale fixtures, cancellation, InternalFault, nonpublication, and clean recovery. Independent model and property tests cover census partitioning, canonical witness order, source or artifact permutation, and count conservation. Named mutants that mark incomplete rows checked, collapse unsafe with validated, omit a missing reference, accept a stale reconstruction, or cache Inconclusive must be killed. A real pinned no-mock positive, incomplete, mutation, and recovery E2E lane runs at threads 1, 8, and 32 with byte-identical authoritative counts, roots, diagnostics, and witnesses, plus strict human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts with exact commands, inputs, budgets, streams, first divergence, cleanup, and final state. Validator self-tests reject malformed, extra-field, stale-input, wrong-count, wrong-mutant, merged-stream, and surviving-mutant evidence. scripts/check.sh, focused and workspace tests, kernel replay, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, covenant guards, the bc7 matrix must contain zero uncovered rows for this artifact-completeness contract and all currently runnable zht and G1 integration checks that do not require unfinished sibling slices must be green with retained artifact paths. Final zht and G1 aggregation remains downstream and is not a circular prerequisite for this child. No typed limitation may disappear into a success total.

title: fln-kernel K1: soundness-boundary + inference test coverage (zht follow-up)
description:
Verification child for franken_lean-zht. Maintain the finite coverage audit for every K1 judgment, reduction, definitional-equality, resource, and admission rule that is actually implemented in fln-kernel. This bead owns evidence, not production semantics: it must never add a test-only acceptance path, weaken a rule, or stand in for an unimplemented kernel slice.

Maintain a rule matrix keyed by KERNEL_CONTRACT identifier with implementation location, public-surface fixture, positive case, discriminating rejection or near-miss, boundary/resource case where applicable, determinism status, mutation or defense-in-depth evidence, and no-mock replay coverage. Every implemented branch must have a named test through the public kernel authority. Rules not yet implemented remain explicitly assigned to their production slice, including franken_lean-5p2 for KR-205/KR-316/KR-317/KR-955, and become mandatory rows here as soon as that slice lands. The matrix must distinguish implemented-and-covered, implemented-but-uncovered, not-yet-implemented, structurally enforced, and externally replayed; an unimplemented row can never be reported as passing.

For each rule family, cover the successful judgment and the closest unsound or false-accepting alternative, not just malformed syntax. Required families include typing and inference, beta/zeta/projection/delta and recursor reductions, universe equivalence, proof irrelevance boundaries, application and binder congruence, eta laws, declaration safety and authority transitions, fuel/cancellation/FL-INV-07 outcomes, and schedule-independent verdict plus consumption. Use single-defect mutation kills where one site owns the property. Where redundant checks deliberately provide defense in depth, document and test the minimal multi-site mutant needed to remove the property rather than overclaiming a single-site kill.

The shared real-path no-mock E2E is the pinned real-olean kernel replay seeded by franken_lean-z6c and extended as K1 grows. It must record before/after accepted, rejected by stable family, inconclusive, and internal-fault counts; cross-check selected accepted modules with the pinned foreign witness; exercise a deliberately perturbed or invalid declaration that must not be accepted; and finish with a clean recovery replay proving later valid work is unaffected. Future full differential rigs in franken_lean-3j8 are additive and do not replace this closure evidence.

Emit concise human output and retained schema-versioned NDJSON. Each record includes run/scenario/step and rule ids, bead and invariant, epoch/reference/fixture identity, canonical module/declaration/input hashes, expected and actual verdict/error/consumption, budget/cancellation facts, engine/build and thread/seed facts, mutant or defense-in-depth id when applicable, witness result, exit status, first divergence, artifact references, authority/cacheability, cleanup, and asserted final state. Bound diagnostic volume and preserve stderr artifacts.

Do not close until a mechanical comparison of the implemented K1 rule inventory against this matrix reports zero implemented-but-uncovered rows, every production follow-up has contributed its boundary tests, all named mutations are killed or honestly classified as structural/defense-in-depth checks, the no-mock negative/recovery replay is green, and fln-kernel plus applicable Tribunal quality gates pass.
acceptance_criteria:
The implemented K1 inventory has zero uncovered rows and every row is backed by named public-authority unit/boundary evidence, discriminating negative coverage, and mutation or structural enforcement evidence. Production slices such as franken_lean-5p2 contribute their rule rows when they land. Deterministic verdict/consumption and FL-INV-07 resource/cancellation behavior are covered. The pinned real-olean no-mock replay includes foreign-witness sampling, an invalid or perturbed negative case, clean recovery, retained human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs, and exact before/after counts. No test-only authority path, unimplemented-as-passing row, silent coverage deferral, or undocumented surviving critical mutant satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — this terminal gate mechanically joins the final KERNEL_CONTRACT inventory, every production K1/K2/checker slice, private-reference and nested-inductive rows, witness coverage, mutation-kill records, and exact evidence roots. Zero uncovered rows is asserted here, not on upstream producers. The join rejects duplicate, stale, provisional, unsupported-without-policy, mock-only, root-mismatched, or unbound rows and emits a manifest-complete first-divergence report.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative output is strict schema-versioned canonical semantic NDJSON containing normalized inputs, roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, wall time, temporary paths, cache/performance facts, and scheduler details live only in a separately linked bounded telemetry stream and cannot enter authoritative digests. Validators reject merged streams, unknown versions, extra or missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, or telemetry-root drift. Productive 1/8/32 runs prove nonempty completed work and deterministic reduction rather than thread-count labels.

title: W1 tooling: make Beads workspace durability and doctor results truthful
description:
## Problem

`br doctor --json` reports a healthy SQLite/JSONL store with matching record counts and no reliability anomalies, but still exits nonzero for three distinct classes: `.beads/.gitignore` does not ignore `.write.lock`; `.beads/.write.lock` has a stale mtime and appears orphaned; and `beads.base.jsonl` is absent after a recorded successful flush. It also labels fulfilled closed blocking prerequisites as dead dependencies even though those edges are intentional implementation/evidence provenance and do not block readiness. Leaving real durability warnings mixed with a false-positive graph heuristic makes operators either ignore doctor or remove valuable history.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `br doctor --json` from the repository root and retain the complete machine output and exit code.
2. Confirm the reliability audit, SQLite integrity, JSONL parse, count parity, and sync metadata are healthy while the gitignore, stale-lock, missing-post-flush-anchor, and closed-prerequisite findings remain.
3. Run `br sync --flush-only`, then rerun `br doctor --json`; observe that the export timestamp advances while the merge-anchor finding can remain.
4. Compare `br ready --json` and `br dep cycles --json` with the doctor edge list; fulfilled closed prerequisites do not block readiness and the graph remains acyclic, while a genuinely missing blocker must still be diagnosed.

## Scope

Reproduce each finding against the exact pinned `br` version and determine whether it is repository configuration/state, a supported repair workflow, or an upstream beads_rust defect. Repair the real lock/ignore/merge-anchor conditions through documented supported operations. Preserve every valid closed-prerequisite edge. If the doctor heuristic or base-anchor behavior is upstream, record the exact version/finding id/reproducer, obtain or pin a corrected release when available, and document a narrow expiring classification until then; never blanket-ignore doctor failure.

## Constraints

No direct SQLite editing, JSONL rewriting, deletion, lock removal, or moving any file without the user’s explicit written permission required by AGENTS.md. First prove no live writer owns a lock. Do not remove closed dependency edges merely to silence `dep.dead_closed_blocking_edges`; they encode sequencing and closure evidence. Do not weaken `br doctor`, bypass nonzero status globally, or hide unknown/new findings. All Beads issue/status/dependency changes use `br` only.

## Acceptance Criteria

The machine acceptance field is authoritative and additive to this scope.
acceptance_criteria: 1. On the exact installed/pinned br version, deterministic repro fixtures independently cover incomplete inner gitignore, a genuinely live lock, an orphan/stale lock, successful flush with merge anchor, missing/stale anchor, healthy SQLite/JSONL parity, intentional open-to-closed prerequisite edges, a truly missing blocker, and a genuinely stale/unready issue. Every case produces the correct stable finding_id, severity, message, and exit class; the fix must distinguish real durability failures from graph-history false positives. 2. Before any lock action, a read-only ownership check proves whether a live writer exists. No file is deleted, moved, overwritten, or manually rewritten without the exact user approval required by AGENTS.md; no SQLite or JSONL surgery is allowed. Supported br operations or a reviewed upstream fix create/refresh the merge anchor and leave DB/JSONL hashes and counts synchronized. 3. Intentional closed-prerequisite edges remain present and continue to prove implementation/evidence ordering. Doctor must stop calling fulfilled blockers dead, or a version-bound, finding-id-specific, exact-edge-set classification with owner and expiry must fail closed on any changed/new edge or finding; no blanket exit-code suppression. 4. Named unit/model tests in the owning repair surface cover lock ownership/age boundaries, atomic anchor creation and interruption points, flush idempotence, concurrent writers, closed/missing blocker classification, malformed metadata, permissions, and recovery. Named mutants that ignore a live lock, accept a missing anchor, misclassify a missing blocker as fulfilled, remove closed provenance, or swallow an unknown warning are each killed. 5. A no-mock E2E runs real br commands in isolated retained workspaces for healthy create/update/dependency/flush/doctor, live-lock refusal, orphan-lock diagnosis, interrupted/missing-anchor failure, exact approved repair, concurrent flushes, intentional closed-edge acceptance, genuine missing-edge failure, and clean recovery. It never mutates or cleans the production .beads tree during the test. 6. Human logs and strict schema-versioned NDJSON record br version/build, workspace identity, command/argv/cwd, DB/JSONL/anchor/lock paths and hashes without private contents, writer facts, finding ids/severities, exact edge ids and states, expected/actual exit, separate bounded stdout/stderr artifacts, approved action if any, artifact manifest, cleanup/retention, and final state. Validator self-tests reject missing/extra/stale/swapped/merged-stream/wrong-finding/unknown-warning evidence, and retained bundles validate independently. 7. Final production verification shows SQLite integrity and reliability healthy, DB/JSONL counts and hashes synchronized, lock/ignore/anchor findings truthful, all intentional dependencies preserved, br lint and cycles clean, bv alerts clean, and any remaining upstream limitation explicitly version-bound and non-silent. Applicable format/test/ShellCheck/UBS gates for any repository-owned conformance surface and the upstream br test suite for any upstream fix are green.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Live 2026-07-23 doctor snapshot after a successful br sync --flush-only: workspace_health remains healthy and lint/cycle checks are zero, but doctor exits nonzero for three durability findings: .beads/.gitignore omits .write.lock; .beads/.write.lock has an orphan-like stale mtime; and .beads/beads.base.jsonl is missing despite metadata.last_export_time. Doctor also mislabels 12 intentional fulfilled-prerequisite edges as dead and reports three fully-unblocked open issues that br ready already surfaces. No lock, ignore, anchor, dependency, or database file was moved, deleted, or edited during diagnosis. Implementation must preserve closed prerequisite provenance, separate heuristic advisories from reliability failures, and require explicit user approval before any lock or state-file cleanup.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: PinkCat claimed after graph triage identified this as the only unclaimed actionable track outside the user-excluded work. Starting snapshot-first, read-only classification. No lock, database, or JSONL cleanup/mutation will be performed during diagnosis.

title: fln-hash canon: deep Expr/Level encoding and destruction must be stack-safe
description:
The iterative decoder fix in franken_lean-fnj removes recursive descent, but a successfully decoded or programmatically built very deep valid Expr or Level still reaches two recursive lifecycle paths in crates/fln-hash/src/canon.rs: Canonical::write_body re-encoding/hash materialization and automatic destruction of nested Arc-backed nodes. Either can overflow a normal worker stack and abort the process. Make both paths depth-independent in safe Rust while preserving exact canonical bytes/hashes, structural sharing and reference counts, writer/error behavior, and ordinary shallow-path performance. Do not impose a semantic nesting cap, increase thread stacks, leak trees, or hide fatal probes.

## Steps to Reproduce

After franken_lean-fnj, construct or decode valid right/left-heavy Expr App/binder and Level Succ/Max/IMax chains at increasing depths in sacrificial child processes or threads with explicit 1 MiB and 2 MiB stacks. First canonical-encode/hash the value, then retain cloned/shared sub-DAG roots and release them in varied orders. The current write_body or automatic Drop should overflow at a platform/build-dependent threshold; record the smallest observed encoder and destructor thresholds and exact artifact/build/stack facts. Fatal probes never execute in the main test-runner process.

## Acceptance Criteria

Canonical encoding and destruction perform no input-depth recursion. Encoding is iterative, forward deterministic, byte-for-byte and hash-identical to the prior implementation for all valid shallow/corpus fixtures, and correctly handles writer failure/partial output according to the existing API. Destruction is iterative or otherwise proven stack-bounded with exact Arc ownership for unique nodes, multiply shared DAG nodes, cloned roots, partial construction/decode state, cancellation, error unwinding, and encoder failure cleanup. No unsafe code, intentional leak, global deferred garbage, refcount corruption, double drop, semantic/wire/hash change, or unbounded runtime-worker monopolization is allowed.

Named deterministic unit/property/mutation tests cover every Expr and Level constructor; million-node left/right/alternating chains under a 1 MiB stack; balanced trees; maximally shared DAGs; random clone/drop orders; encoder writer failures at every boundary; partial construction; cancellation/error cleanup; allocation pressure; exact live-object/refcount accounting; exhaustive shallow old-versus-new byte/hash equivalence; and corpus canonical vectors. A real no-mock child-process E2E decodes, canonical-encodes, hashes, clones, and drops a valid at-least-100k-deep artifact with zero signal/hang/leak/survivor, then performs a clean shallow recovery. Negative/recovery scenarios cover truncated input, forced writer failure, and cancellation mid-lifecycle. Emit bounded human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs with seed/depth/shape/share factor, stack/build/toolchain/host facts, input/output/hash, object/refcount counts, timings/resources, expected versus actual, exit/signal, stdout/stderr artifacts, first divergence, cleanup and final state. Archive reproducible bundles and available leak/sanitizer evidence; a fatal-probe-only, mock-only, or leaked-tree result cannot close the bead.
design: Use explicit work stacks for both canonical emission and destruction. The encoder stack carries visit/emit actions that reproduce the existing preorder tag/scalar/child byte grammar exactly, including binder metadata ordering and writer-error precedence, without recursively calling child write_body. Stack-safe Drop takes ownership of the root Arc, repeatedly unwraps uniquely owned nodes, pushes owned children, and otherwise releases only the shared Arc reference; prove that shared descendants are neither traversed prematurely nor leaked and that a last-reference cascade remains iterative. Apply the same law to Expr and Level, keep all logic safe Rust, and bound cleanup batches or yield through the owning higher-level region where necessary without making object liveness nondeterministic. Mutation tests reintroduce recursive encode/drop and must be killed by bounded-stack subprocess scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: All description and design requirements are mandatory. In 100 consecutive bounded-stack child runs per Expr and Level shape, valid deep decode, encode/hash, clone/share, and destruction complete with exact bytes/hashes and zero SIGABRT, panic, hang, leak, refcount error, or survivor; writer/cancellation failures produce the specified outcome and clean state; a shallow recovery follows every failure. Unit/property/mutation/corpus differentials and real E2E bundles validate independently with detailed logs.
notes: MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bug): unit/property/mutation coverage must exercise canonical-byte equivalence, writer failures, unique/shared/cloned/partial/cancelled/resource-constrained values, refcount accounting, and shallow/deep lifecycle behavior. The real no-mock bounded-stack child-process scenario plus negative/recovery coverage is required. Human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts record exact redacted command/cwd, epoch/profile/toolchain/host/stack, seed/input/output hashes/depth/shape/share factor, expected versus actual, monotonic timing, resources/budgets, exit/signal, stdout/stderr references, live-object/refcount facts, first divergence, cleanup and final state. Archive fatal-reproduction metadata without running fatal probes in the main harness; close evidence names all tests, artifacts, leak checks, and reproducible commands. A skipped, leaked, or mock-only case cannot satisfy the lifecycle-safety claim.

Please fold write_body into this bead's iterative-destruction/traversal scope (or track separately) so deep-tree stack-safety is complete across decode (fnj, done), encode (write_body), and drop.

comments.text: Adjacent lifecycle dependency filed as franken_lean-p8a.1 after reviewing the live implementation. Name is itself an unbounded Arc-backed parent chain; automatic Drop, derived structural Eq/Hash/Debug, Ord, quick_cmp_aux, and display recurse through parent depth. Expr and Level leaf payloads include Name, so a deeply nested Name can still make an otherwise shallow value violate this bead lifecycle claim. Canonical Name writing is already being converted to an iterative walk in the live canon.rs diff, which is good, but core Name lifecycle remains. Added p8a.1 as a blocker so 6gy cannot close over an unhandled embedded payload. This is review-only; no active owner file was edited and no fatal probe was run.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Critical review finding in the live uncommitted Drop implementation: both Expr and Level currently use Arc::try_unwrap inside their iterative drains. That does not preserve stack safety when final cloned roots are released concurrently. Two owners may both fail try_unwrap while the strong count exceeds one, then both drop the returned Arc; the last ordinary Arc drop can recursively destroy the deep chain. The Rust standard-library Arc::into_inner contract explicitly guarantees that if every clone calls it, exactly one call receives the inner value, and its linked-list example says the same manual Drop can still overflow if try_unwrap(...).ok() is used instead. The implementation should use Arc::into_inner for the ownership-election step and include a deterministic simultaneous cloned-root drop test, not only sequential retained_root drops. Static review only; I did not edit the active files or run a fatal probe.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Safe independent checkpoint verification on the live uncommitted Rust diff, without opening or executing the process-oriented E2E: rch exec -- cargo test -p fln-core exited 0 with 43 passed, including the new sequential Arc-sharing Drop tests. The targeted constructor/ordering test rch exec -- cargo test -p fln-hash canon::tests::iterative_encoders_cover_every_level_and_expr_constructor -- --exact exited 0 with 1 passed. Static review confirms iterative encoder task order matches the prior wire grammar. These checks do not close the known concurrent try_unwrap defect, and I deliberately did not run the deep lifecycle subprocess test per the user safety constraint.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Integrated RubyForest's concurrent-final-owner review into the live 6gy implementation: Expr/Level drains now use Arc::into_inner instead of try_unwrap, and the sacrificial-process path simultaneously releases two final cloned roots from separate 1 MiB threads. This targets the standard-library ownership-election guarantee that exactly one concurrent caller receives the inner node. Sequential refcount unit tests remain additive; 6gy is still blocked on p8a.1 and the full acceptance matrix.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Follow-up review after f18b2f4: the current working diff replaces try_unwrap with Arc::into_inner in Name, Expr, and Level and adds concurrent cloned-root drops, directly addressing the standard-library ownership-election flaw reported earlier. Safe local cargo test -p fln-core on this newer diff exited 0 with 43 passed. UBS over the four Rust files exited 1 only because it classifies the four deliberate test-only recursive mutation panic sites as critical; formatting, clippy, cargo check, and test build were clean. I did not execute the lifecycle subprocess. This remains a live uncommitted follow-up and still depends on p8a.1 for deep Name structural operations.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Independent fresh-eyes review PASSED (Claude Fable session) — the whole term-plane stack-safety story is correct end to end:
- ENCODERS (canon.rs, 265f260): Expr::write_body and Level::write_body are iterative LIFO work-stacks; I traced the push order for every constructor (App/Lam/ForallE/LetE/MData/Proj; Succ/Max/IMax) and the emission is byte-identical to the recursive form (children pushed in reverse so a subtree fully emits before its next sibling; post-order scalar fields BinderInfo/NonDep emitted at the correct point). Name/KVMap encoding is iterative too, so no path recurses on depth.
- DROP (expr.rs/level.rs/name.rs, f18b2f4): all three use node: Option<Arc<...>> + take_node_for_drop()=take() as a sentinel, Arc::into_inner to drain ONLY unique nodes (shared nodes merely decremented — exact Arc sharing preserved), and moved-out children drop as no-ops. Memory-safe (no double-free, no leak), stack-safe (heap worklist), and each type drains its own chain (a Param's Name drops via Name::drop). node_arc()'s expect is unreachable on a live Expr (None is transient to Drop only).
- DECODERS (mine, fnj): iterative read_expr_iter/read_level_iter, matching grammar.
Coverage is thorough: iterative_drop_preserves_shared_{expr,level}_arcs, recursive_{expr,level}_encoder_mutant on a 1 MiB stack, iterative_encoders_cover_every_constructor, deep-input decode-as-typed-error. No defect or gap found — this bead's implementation is sound.

title: Gate G4: The Corpus Whole — full mathlib, T2=100%, PG-2, mixed builds
description:
Convergence gate G4 "The Corpus Whole" (§22.3): full mathlib elaboration — T2 = 100%, T3 measured with a published ratchet, PG-2(G4) met (wall <= 0.5x Reference, same machine/threads), olean outputs consumed by the Reference toolchain in the mixed-build matrix. This is the gate that makes "an implementation of Lean" a fact rather than a claim: the Corpus is the de-facto behavioral specification (§1.9), and elaborating all of it with statement-identical environments is the definition of having implemented the language.

Also folded in per §22.2/§12.6: grind rows at L3 on the grind corpus; simp fidelity at scale; the Ledger incremental law live under the full Corpus (the PG-3 measurement baseline); native_decide Tier 1 as the sound-mode default; PG-M (metaprogram corpus <= 1.25x Reference).

Evidence bundle: the full T2/T3 differential ledger with every T3 residual classified (fidelity defect closed, or documented free choice with pinned CGSE policy); PG-2/PG-M numbers with claim states; the mixed-producer matrix transcripts (our oleans under the Reference, theirs under ours, interleaved builds); the R2 divergence ledger empty or published as errata with quantified blast radius (the epoch ships R3, not R4, until closed).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: G4 is the full-Corpus promotion over already-proven G3 infrastructure. Its exact production inputs are the engine-backed tactic facade integration; Ledger records/CAS and FL-INV-05 incremental law; real Lake build surface; faithful olean writer/mixed-producer path; and native_decide Tier 1, plus the inherited G3 and performance harness. It deliberately does not wait for W7 e-graph/portfolio frontier work or W8 distributed/cache-federation/epoch-bridge GA work, which are additive full-workstream obligations attached to G6. Full mathlib T2 = 100%, the T3 ratchet, simp/grind rows, PG-2/PG-M, mixed builds, negative/recovery, and detailed evidence remain mandatory here.
acceptance_criteria: G4 closes only when FrankenLean elaborates the manifest-complete pinned mathlib Corpus from source with T2 equal to 100 percent: acceptance, diagnostics, and statement-level environments match the Reference for every declaration with no hidden exclusion. Full-Corpus outputs are deterministic at 1, 8, and 32 threads, mixed-producer builds pass in both directions and interleaved form, and Reference consumers accept FrankenLean faithful oleans byte-for-byte wherever FL-INV-04 requires it. Simp fidelity holds at Corpus scale, grind rows reach L3 on the declared grind corpus, native_decide Tier 1 is the sound-mode default with kernel-checked receipts, and Ledger invalidation performs true-cone-only rebuilds without stale-hit acceptance. Real no-mock E2E includes full clean build, warm rebuild, representative leaf edit, mixed producers, planted divergence/corruption/stale-cache failure, exact restoration, and recovery under strictly validated committed evidence bundles. title: W1: Convergence governance — R15 WIP caps and earliest-failing-gate routing
description:
Implement the program-control mitigation required by risk R15 so a large multi-agent effort converges on R4 instead of maximizing simultaneous subsystem starts. Define a versioned, reviewable policy over the live br dependency graph and evidence registry: distinguish workstreams, gate/contract prerequisites, active incidents/security fixes, verification lanes, and additive leapfrog work; cap active implementation workstreams; route capacity first to the earliest failing convergence gate and its transitive blockers; reserve bounded capacity for independent critical bugs and verification; and freeze additive work when convergence evidence regresses. Freeze means retain every bead and feature in the backlog with reasons and resume conditions, never delete or silently descope it.

Expose a robot-safe command/report suitable for CI and agents. It reads stable br JSON plus evidence/gate state, never launches a TUI, never mutates bead status implicitly, and emits both a concise recommendation and schema-versioned NDJSON containing graph/evidence hashes, active-workstream classification, WIP budget, critical path, earliest failing gate, selected/held work, reason codes, exception owner/expiry, starvation age, and exact next unblocking actions. The policy must handle stale/missing/cyclic/inconsistent graph or evidence inputs as typed inconclusive/internal outcomes, not as permission to start more work.

## Acceptance Criteria

The decision log freezes the initial numeric WIP budget, reserved verification/incident capacity, workstream classification law, priority order, exception authority, expiry/review cadence, fairness/starvation rule, and resume criteria. Configuration changes are reviewed, versioned, and evidenced; agents cannot bypass the cap by splitting or relabeling equivalent work. CI rejects unauthorized over-cap in-progress states and expired exceptions while providing actionable reasons; emergency/security work can proceed under a logged bounded exception without erasing the main critical path. The policy treats bv metrics as advisory evidence and validates semantic dependencies, so keyword similarity or a transient PageRank score cannot rewrite the plan automatically.

Named deterministic unit/property/mutation tests cover empty/single/many-track graphs, ties, multiple failing gates, incidents, verification reservation, starvation, blocked and deferred beads, closed/reopened work, stale evidence, missing owners, cycles, duplicate labels, malicious metadata, expired exceptions, and stable output under reordered JSONL. A real no-mock E2E uses a temporary br project plus real br and bv robot output to exercise claim, over-cap rejection, gate regression freeze, incident exception, expiry, close/unblock, and clean recovery without mutating the production project. Detailed bounded human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs record tool/schema versions, exact redacted commands/cwd, graph/evidence/config hashes, selected/held ids and reasons, monotonic timings/resources, exit status, stderr/stdout artifacts, expected versus actual, and final bead states. A mock-only planner, hidden auto-mutation, feature deletion, or unlogged exception cannot close this bead.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — classification authority is a canonical versioned workstream/gate registry keyed by stable bead ids. Labels, titles, keyword similarity, PageRank, and bv recommendations are advisory only and cannot assign WIP class, rewrite dependencies, or start/hold work. Unknown or unclassified beads consume no free implementation capacity and cannot move to in_progress until reviewed.

The policy distinguishes passed, failed, dependency_blocked, not_yet_runnable, stale, Inconclusive, and InternalFault. It derives dependency blocking from the authoritative br graph rather than the bead status field and routes to the nearest ready transitive blockers of the earliest non-passed convergence gate. Read graph, evidence, config, exception, and adoption snapshots under one consistency protocol; pre/post hash drift yields Inconclusive and no admission recommendation. Missing bv metrics alone does not: bv is optional advisory input.

Do not overclaim race-free atomic admission unless the tracker exposes an atomic policy claim. Concurrent agents may transiently oversubscribe after identical reports; the honest contract is deterministic detection, an ordered newcomer hold rule, and no further admission until the cap drains. A one-time adoption snapshot classifies already-active work and issues a bounded drain plan—no silent perpetual grandfathering and no destructive cancellation.

Priority is explicit: bounded incident/security exception; evidence-integrity repair; nearest ready blockers of the earliest gate; reserved independent verification; fairness within the same eligible class. Starvation never ages additive work above a frozen earlier gate. Exceptions have exact scope/owner/expiry/review and cannot erase features or evidence obligations.

D7 claims remain separate: deterministic conformance to the frozen policy is an invariant; WIP-cap compliance at a specific snapshot is observed; the assertion that the policy improves convergence is HYPOTHESIS/statistical and never proof. Archive the complete normalized graph/evidence/config/exception/adoption inputs, not only their hashes, with each recommendation.
acceptance_criteria: A frozen id-keyed registry and reviewed policy define numeric caps/reservations, state vocabulary, graph-derived blocking, priority, consistency protocol, anti-evasion, adoption/drain, exception, fairness, freeze/resume, and honest claim classes. Robot output is deterministic, read-only, and stable under JSONL reordering; bv remains advisory. Named governance_policy_model, earliest_gate_routing, snapshot_consistency, concurrent_admission_detection, adoption_drain_model, exception_state_machine, and governance_no_mock_e2e suites cover empty/many-track graphs, ties, dependency blocking, all outcome states, graph/evidence drift, bv timeout/absence, simultaneous claims, unknown beads, initial over-cap state, incidents, verification reservation, starvation, reopened work, and exception expiry/renewal. Mutants for status-only blocking, keyword/PageRank authority, auto-mutation, stale-snapshot admission, missing-bv Inconclusive, race-free overclaim, silent grandfathering, unknown-bead capacity, feature deletion, evergreen exception, and hypothesis-as-proof are killed. The real temporary-project E2E consumes real br and robot bv, preserves production state, exercises admission/over-cap/freeze/exception/expiry/close/unblock/recovery, and archives normalized inputs plus strict semantic decisions/final states. No hidden mutation, permissive failure, destructive drain, or feature loss satisfies closure.
notes: MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this governance bead): unit/property/mutation coverage must exercise policy classification, ordering, WIP arithmetic, failure states, exceptions, fairness, determinism, and non-mutation. Real no-mock temporary-project E2E coverage must use br changes and bv robot diagnostics for normal, over-cap, regression, incident, expiry, and recovery scenarios without touching production statuses. Emit concise human and bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step, policy/schema/tool versions, exact redacted argv/cwd, graph/evidence/config hashes, active/selected/held beads and reason codes, budgets/exceptions/starvation, expected versus actual, monotonic timing/resources, exit status, stderr/stdout artifacts, and final states. Archive configs, fixtures, logs, and reproduction commands; a skip, mock-only result, hidden mutation, or lost feature cannot satisfy the claim.

title: W12: Front doors — lean/leanc/lake personalities + the fln multiplexer verbs
description:
Implement the toolchain front doors (§17.1, fln-cli): the binaries lean, leanc (the C-driver personality: flag-compatible, driving the D2 cc when present, explaining its absence via capability errors when not), lake, and the fln multiplexer exposing the new verbs — check-olean, audit (--tcb), replay, doctor (--sql), cache, olean, goals, serve-mcp, why-trusts, diff, build explain, server replay, verify-capsule, identity.

Contract: flags, exit codes, env vars (LEAN_PATH, LEAN_SYSROOT), and --json shapes pinned to the Reference at the pin (CLI transcript corpus, C-family fixtures); --trust levels ACCEPTED with upstream semantics but journaled into receipts and surfaced by fln audit rather than silently honored (BN-09); new flags live under --fln-* only; the version line stays parse-compatible (Reference version/commit fields preserved) with a separate machine-readable implementation field — compatibility never becomes deception, and an exact-reference transcript mode exists for conformance harnesses only. Robot-mode discipline per AGENTS.md: stable versioned schemas, deterministic where possible, explicit exit codes, line-oriented output, no human decoration mixed into machine streams.

Acceptance: the CLI transcript corpus diffs clean against the Reference for the shared surface; each fln verb has a --json schema contract test against a frozen fixture; elan invokes the binaries transparently via the release layout (verified in W12). Tests: golden transcripts per verb (human and JSON modes) with detailed logging of any drift; exit-code matrix fixtures; --trust journaling fixtures; capability-error fixtures for missing cc/git naming the alternative.
