comments.text: Scope sharpened by the fln-d4x probe (decoded evidence, not name-guessing): ALL 11 remaining Init.Prelude false-rejects are yours. 6 match auxiliaries (Nat.mul/pow/mod.match_1, List.set/get.match_1, Array.appendCore.loop.match_1) fail on -shaped pairs — they need the lit⇄constructor defeq normalization (Nat.zero ⇄ lit 0, and proj/whnf exposing OfNat instances down to literals). 5 (UInt8/16/32/64.ofNatLT, Char.ofNatAux) need KR-313 literal arithmetic. After this bead, Init.Prelude replay should reach 1755/1755. Repro: FLN_REPLAY_PROBE="Nat.mul.match_1" env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_local cargo test -p fln-conformance --test kernel_replay -- --nocapture. Baseline at fln-d4x close: 1744/1755 accepted, 11 rejected, 0 Inconclusive.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Correction to the previous comment (shell quoting ate the expression): the 6 match auxiliaries fail on pairs of the shape  OfNat.ofNat.[levels 0] Nat <literal> (instOfNatNat <literal>)  =?=  Nat.zero  — i.e. the expected minor type beta-reduces to motive applied to Nat.zero while the actual type has motive applied to the OfNat.ofNat form; closing it needs whnf/proj to compute the OfNat instance down to the literal AND the literal-vs-constructor defeq normalization (Nat.zero <-> literal 0).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Claimed by Claude Fable session (same agent as the 5p2/d4x arc). Dep edge irm->npl downgraded blocks->related deliberately: irm needs the npl ARITHMETIC CORE, which is landed and verified on main (f86393e + 216b2a3 — KR-313-complete op set, 5725 CPython golden vectors, tests+clippy green). The outstanding npl half (ABI-limb layout view, PG-K gate) pends 53v and is not touched by kernel literal reduction — the kernel consumes BigNat values, not lean_object bignums. irm scope: wire fln-bignum into fln-kernel for KR-313 literal ops, lit⇄constructor defeq normalization (Nat.zero ⇄ lit 0 + OfNat instance computation), KR-314 String literal expansion. Target: 11 decoded Init.Prelude residuals -> 1755/1755.

title: W2: olean-next + fln olean tool — content-addressed modules, sealed capsules, diffs
description:
Implement olean-next + the fln olean tool (§7.4, frontier): the second module lane — a content-addressed, position-independent format: per-declaration records keyed by Ledger hashes, structure-shared term DAGs with dictionary compression, mergeable and diffable, mmap-friendly WITHOUT relocation, carrying certificate attachments (§8.6) inline. olean-next never replaces olean in faithful/sound artifacts (D-08); it is the Ledger native interchange and the format proof-cache federation speaks.

Capsule shapes: THIN (references into the shared CAS) and SEALED (a complete transitive object pack, verifiable on an air-gapped machine — the archival/audit form, and the .flnpack the WASM Judge consumes). Attested evidence objects may carry RaptorQ repair sidecars via the asupersync coder; repair reconstructs to staging, re-verifies the canonical hash and a full schema decode, publishes without changing identity, and journals the corruption/repair event — repair is a durability feature, NEVER a trust shortcut. The fln olean CLI tool converts both directions (olean <-> olean-next) and prints human-readable diffs of module contents — the un-opaquing of weakness census #8 (pointer-dump oleans are unmergeable, un-diffable, un-addressable).

Acceptance: round-trip olean -> olean-next -> olean preserves semantic hash and (faithful path) bytes; sealed capsules verify air-gapped (no CAS access); repair-sidecar loss and corruption drills pass with journaled events; diff output validated on known module pairs. Tests: format fuzzing under budgets; thin/sealed equivalence property tests; a tamper test (any flipped byte fails verification with a typed error naming the object); dictionary-compression determinism (same input, same bytes).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement a versioned, position-independent olean-next format with content-addressed per-declaration Ledger records, shared/dictionary-compressed term DAGs, certificate attachments, deterministic canonical encoding, mmap-friendly reads without relocation, and merge/diff operations. It remains a frontier/native interchange and never replaces faithful/sound olean artifacts.
2. THIN capsules reference validated shared-CAS objects; SEALED capsules contain the complete transitive object pack and verify on an air-gapped machine with no CAS/network access. Both forms resolve to identical semantic/logical roots and enforce version, bounds, dependency, certificate, and canonical-hash checks before publication.
3. Optional RaptorQ sidecars reconstruct only into staging; repaired bytes undergo full canonical hash and schema decode before atomic promotion with unchanged identity and a journaled corruption/repair event. Missing/insufficient/corrupt sidecars never confer trust or overwrite a valid object.
4. fln olean converts olean to olean-next and back, verifies capsules, and emits deterministic human and versioned-JSON semantic diffs. A faithful round trip preserves semantic hash and required original bytes. Named unit/property/fuzz/mutation suites cover codec bounds, DAG sharing/cycles, dictionary determinism, thin/sealed equivalence, merge/diff, every-byte tampering, repair thresholds, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and failure atomicity. Hash-bypass, trust-repair, missing-object, and partial-promotion mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane converts real pinned Reference/corpus oleans, verifies faithful byte/semantic round trips, packs thin and sealed forms, moves sealed output to a network/CAS-isolated process, checks it, compares known module diffs, corrupts/losses shards through the repair threshold, rejects over-threshold tampering, and verifies recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/module/object/capsule/shard/transaction ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/format/mode/profile/platform/cache/network facts, canonical source/object/certificate/capsule/logical roots, expected/actual verify/repair/diff, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives corpus fixtures, round-trip matrices, air-gap manifests, fuzz seeds, repair/fault matrices, mutant outcomes, and evidence bundles. No sealed, faithful-byte, repair, or trust claim closes from mocks or non-isolated verification.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W12: Epoch ratchet — mechanical pin advances, census diffs, migration beads
description:
Implement the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (§22.5, §13.6): the mechanical 8 steps — (1) fetch + hash the official release into the epoch lab; (2) regenerate every census on every platform (ABI, olean, extern, builtin, options, CLI, LSP schemas); (3) run the existing corpus differentially and mine new behavior into fixtures + fresh instrumented-oracle traces; (4) machine-diff the public/Meta API, artifact, ABI, CLI, Lake, LSP surfaces; (5) regenerate facade stubs and OPEN MIGRATION BEADS for every moved symbol; (6) close or classify every new divergence (unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim); (7) run the unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs; (8) publish the capsule, evidence bundle, and migration report — then move the pin.

Cadence target: a new stable upstream release reaches R3 within four weeks and R4 by the following cycle, lag publicly visible on the dashboard (risk R14: the newest closed epoch stays R4 while later epochs ship R2/R3 previews — latest-parity never implied). Supported-epoch count: initial posture current + previous stable (OQ-10). Epoch bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) are the pressure-release valve keeping the treadmill from owning the roadmap.

Acceptance: the ratchet runs end-to-end against a SIMULATED epoch advance (a synthetic delta over the current pin) producing the capsule, the census diffs, the auto-opened migration beads, and the block-on-unclassified behavior; the lag dashboard renders from ledger data. Tests: census-diff correctness fixtures (planted deltas per surface must be caught and classified); bead-opening automation tests; block-on-unclassified enforcement test.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

## Success Criteria

Acquisition/census, differential mining, migration manifest, external real-br bridge, and dual-epoch validation close on exact roots. The release tool never invokes br; one historical adjacent-epoch rehearsal passes; EOL and cache-shim policies resolve; publication is immutable and recoverable.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — resolve the D2 interaction explicitly. Release tooling must not spawn br. The ratchet produces a deterministic schema-versioned migration-issue manifest for every moved symbol and divergence, with exact operator/repository-harness br import instructions; applying it is an external maintenance action, not a FrankenLean runtime subprocess. This bead owns epoch acquisition, regeneration, differentials, surface diffs, facade regeneration, classification gates, downstream dual-epoch runs, capsule/report production, and the manifest. franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w consumes those outputs for project repair.

TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are franken_lean-ckus acquisition/census diffs, franken_lean-zhei differential corpus/fixture/trace mining, franken_lean-20xp deterministic migration manifest, fln-gjup external real-br maintenance bridge, and franken_lean-1w0l dual-epoch validation/report. The release tool never invokes br; this epic closes only on exact child roots and one historical adjacent-epoch rehearsal.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The ratchet implements all eight ordered steps atomically: fetch/hash official release into the isolated epoch lab; regenerate ABI/olean/extern/builtin/options/CLI/Lake/LSP censuses on every platform; run corpus differentials and mine fixtures/traces; machine-diff all public/Meta/artifact/ABI/CLI/Lake/LSP surfaces; regenerate facade stubs and open migration beads per moved symbol; classify every divergence; run unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs; publish capsule/evidence/migration report before moving the authoritative pin.
2. Every generated artifact binds old/new release hashes, platform, extractor/schema versions, source inputs, and toolchain closure. Unclassified divergence, missing census/platform, stale fixture, failed downstream row, absent migration bead, or incomplete evidence blocks pin movement with no partial authoritative state. The Reference remains confined to the epoch lab/Tribunal capacities.
3. Migration bead creation is deterministic/idempotent with ownership, surface, old/new identities, dependency/gate links, acceptance tests, and evidence requirements. Supported-epoch policy starts at current plus previous stable per OQ-10; R3/R4 cadence and lag are dashboard facts, not implied latest parity. Epoch bridges remain separate, off-by-default, allowlisted evidence.
4. Named unit/property/golden/differential/mutation suites plant changes in every census/surface, verify diff classification and fixture mining, bead idempotency/dependencies, downstream dual-epoch execution, publication transaction/rollback, dashboard derivation, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and D8 confinement. Missed-delta, unclassified-pass, early-pin-move, duplicate/missing-bead, and false-lag mutants must be killed.
5. A shared E2E ratchet uses a sealed synthetic delta over the current pin to exercise every changed surface and blocking path, plus a no-mutation dry run against a real signed official release/candidate in the isolated lab; it produces capsule, census/API diffs, traces, migration beads, downstream results, dashboard data, and rollback proof without moving the live pin until all gates pass. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/epoch/surface/census/divergence/fixture/bead/gate/publication ids, redacted argv/cwd, old/new hashes/platform/extractor/schema/profile facts, canonical artifact/capsule/evidence roots, classification and expected/actual gate state, timings/resources/status, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives all censuses/diffs, corpus/trace deltas, generated facade/bead manifests, dual-epoch results, capsule/migration report, dashboard snapshot, rollback proof, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifest. No epoch claim or pin advance can proceed with an unclassified row, mocked census, or missing platform.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — the release tool emits a deterministic migration-issue manifest and never invokes br. A separate maintenance bridge imports it idempotently with real br in a scratch Beads database and retains receipts. Resolve all OQ-10 policy: supported epochs/EOL plus community-cache shim graduation and retirement. Require one full historical adjacent-epoch rehearsal. Use available pinned hashes as authority rather than requiring a nonexistent upstream signature. Split acquisition/census diffs, differential corpus/fixture/trace mining, manifest plus bridge, and dual-epoch validation/capsule publication before terminal join.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.

TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are franken_lean-ckus acquisition/census diffs, franken_lean-zhei differential corpus/fixture/trace mining, franken_lean-20xp deterministic migration manifest, fln-gjup external real-br maintenance bridge, and franken_lean-1w0l dual-epoch validation/report. The release tool never invokes br; this epic closes only on exact child roots and one historical adjacent-epoch rehearsal.

1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W1: Substrate & Contracts — fln-core, extraction law, Tribunal bootstrap, baselines
description:
## Description

This is the W1 Substrate & Contracts aggregation epic (§22.2), not an implementation authority or universal dependency root. Downstream work may depend on the narrow contract it consumes; workstream/release gates use this epic as the W1 milestone. It closes only after every explicit W1 dependency has current independently valid evidence and the canonical lock/contract/Tribunal identities join.

W1 integrates: fln-core Name/Level/Expr/literal/options/positions; owned fln-hash and canonical schemas; the mechanically extracted target-indexed ABI, OLEAN/ILEAN, extern and builtin inventories; authoritative SUITE.lock and closure policy; quality/evidence schemas and Tribunal bootstrap; mode/determinism/certified provenance; typed outcomes and faithful/sound diagnostics; campaign and performance infrastructure plus the pinned two-host Reference baseline; Windows functional honesty; convergence/WIP governance; and UPSTREAM_LEDGER upgrade control. Each feature remains owned by its dedicated bead; this epic does not duplicate it.

W1 closure is a strict evidence join, never a percentage, title/label heuristic, or mostly-green rollup. Every required child contributes one current row with status, claim type/evidence kind, schema, immutable bundle root, source/input root, SUITE.lock and Reference/Corpus epoch, mode/platform/target scope, freshness, named tests/mutations/E2E scenarios, limitations, and independent validation. Missing, stale, mixed-epoch, mismatched-root, mock-substituted, reopened, Inconclusive, InternalFault, or overclaimed rows block closure.

The terminal no-mock integration starts from the authoritative lock; validates workspace and every closure lane; regenerates and consumes the canonical contracts/censuses; runs Reference-vs-Reference Tribunal plumbing; exercises mode isolation and typed diagnostic projection; verifies campaign/performance registries and both host baseline bundles; reports Windows status honestly; and archives one causally joined bundle. Child success evidence remains intact if the join fails, but no parent pass or partial evidence is published.

W1 proves reusable foundations and enforcement mechanisms. Release-wide adoption by later absent subsystems remains mandatory on their workstream beads and W12; this prevents false completion without losing any functionality. The former statement that W1 depends on nothing and everything depends on it is intentionally retired as graphically and architecturally false.

## Success Criteria

Named suites w1_child_evidence_join_model, w1_contract_identity_join, w1_claim_authority_model, and w1_cross_contract_no_mock_e2e validate exact child membership, root/epoch/mode/platform compatibility, evidence freshness and authority, strict all-of closure, terminal recovery, and independent bundle verification. Mutations for dropped child, stale/reopened/Inconclusive evidence, mixed lock/contract/epoch roots, percentage-based close, mock substitution, claim-class promotion, omitted platform/baseline lane, terminal partial publication, and label-based child discovery are killed. Cold generation is deterministic; generated Rust compiles; policy/census rows are total; seeded pin/layout/symbol/schema/normalizer/dependency/mode/diagnostic/campaign/performance mutations fail at their owning child and remain visible at the join. Human and strict schema-versioned NDJSON retain all child ids/roots/statuses/scopes, first failing join fact, commands and bounded artifacts, resources, cleanup, and final state. No ad-hoc runner, duplicate authority, handwritten contract, mock oracle, unsupported platform reported green, missing host baseline, hidden exclusion, or deferred listed foundation satisfies W1.
acceptance_criteria: W1 closes only through a strict independently validated all-of join over every explicit dependency and its current evidence row. All roots, epochs, schemas, modes, targets/platforms, claim types, evidence kinds, test/mutation/E2E inventories, limitations, and freshness must agree; missing, stale, mixed, mocked, reopened, Inconclusive, InternalFault, or overclaimed evidence blocks. The terminal real no-mock integration validates and consumes the shared foundations and publishes atomically with complete logs/recovery. Later release-wide adoption remains mandatory on downstream workstreams/W12 rather than being falsely claimed here.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W7: simp faithful core + deterministic compiled rewrite-index format
description:
Implement the simp faithful core + compiled rewrite indexes (§12.1, §12.2): the Reference simp semantics — simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree candidate retrieval, congruence closure interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors, attribute-driven extension — implemented natively end to end (driver, congruence interplay, extension dispatch are Anvil code behind obs-class Mirror facade rows), with the Corpus simp-heavy files plus instrumented-oracle rewrite traces as the fidelity corpus. Mathlib simp EXTENSIONS and simprocs are user code on Golem calling the native driver through the facade.

Compiled rewrite indexes (§12.2): per-library discrimination automata compiled at Ledger commit time and shipped as content-addressed CAS artifacts (an olean-next attachment) — mathlib tens of thousands of simp lemmas become a preloaded matcher, not a per-session index build. Staleness is impossible BY CONSTRUCTION (content-addressed against the simp-set own hash).

Acceptance: simp outcomes match the oracle on the simp-heavy fidelity corpus (rewrite firings, order where observable, result terms); index-backed retrieval produces identical candidate sets to freshly-built trees (representation-equivalence tests); index build determinism (same simp-set hash, same artifact bytes). Tests: golden rewrite-trace replay; dsimp/simp-only behavioral fixtures; congruence-interplay differentials; a stale-index mutation test (perturbed simp-set with unperturbed index must be structurally unloadable).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — this bead owns the native faithful simp semantics plus a deterministic, content-keyed rewrite-index builder/reader and proves loaded-index candidate equivalence to a fresh tree. It does not claim Ledger publication before W8 exists. Transactional CAS publication, olean-next attachment, remote hydration, retention, invalidation, and quarantine are owned by fln-publish-rewrite-indexes-gca.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement Reference-compatible simp-set discipline, discrimination retrieval, congruence interaction, dsimp, simp-only, attributes, extension dispatch, and observable rewrite ordering natively. Real mathlib simp extensions and simprocs execute as user code on Golem through the Native Mirror facade; no upstream implementation executes as a component.
2. Compile each library simp set into a deterministic versioned discrimination automaton stored as a content-addressed Ledger CAS artifact and olean-next attachment bound to the exact simp-set hash. A missing, corrupt, foreign-version, or stale index is structurally unloadable and falls back to a fresh safe index without changing candidate sets or results.
3. Golden instrumented-oracle traces and the simp-heavy corpus agree on goal outcome, result term, observable firing sequence, and facade calls. Compiled-index retrieval is representation-equivalent to a freshly built tree and same hash/input produces bit-identical bytes at threads 1, 8, and 32.
4. Named unit/property/metamorphic/differential/mutation suites cover simp-set construction, dsimp/simp-only, congruence, attributes/extensions/simprocs, loop/resource limits, candidate-set equivalence, codec/version/hash checks, cancellation as Inconclusive, and deterministic compilation. Stale-index, omitted-candidate, reordered-firing, bypass-hash, and admit-unchecked mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane elaborates real simp-heavy Lean files with real user extensions on Golem, compares traces/results with the pinned oracle, repeats through cold and precompiled indexes, rejects a perturbed stale attachment, rebuilds safely, and proves clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/file/goal/rewrite/index/artifact ids, bead/claim/invariant/Parity rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/mode/profile/thread/seed/budget/cache facts, canonical source/simp-set/index/result hashes, candidate/firing decisions, expected/actual, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives oracle traces, index artifacts, equivalence matrices, mutant outcomes, manifests, and named tests/E2E evidence. No fidelity or precompiled-index claim closes from mocked extensions or unbound artifacts.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — own faithful simp semantics plus a portable deterministic builder/reader format. Real Golem/facade row proof belongs to c90; local CAS and olean-next publication belongs to W8. Replace staleness impossible with collision-aware validation of a complete semantic manifest and canonical bytes, followed by safe rebuild on mismatch. Keys bind ordered rules, priorities, orientation, theorem roots, attributes, schema/epoch, and only actually read options; dynamic simproc results never become static index truth. Named suites: simp_semantics_model, rewrite_index_equivalence, rewrite_index_codec_fuzz, simp_trace_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Unknown schema, missing/duplicate ids, broken order/linkage, unbound artifacts, truncation, merged streams, or cleanup/final-state mismatch fail. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: W9: Replay bundles + protocol rigs — deterministic server bugs, editor smoke matrix
description:
Implement session replay bundles + protocol rigs (§14.3, §18.2): every session can emit a replay bundle (init transcript, edit and request sequence, content ids or sealed sources, scheduling profile, normalized outbound transcript); fln server replay bundle reproduces the session and reports the FIRST divergence — server bugs become deterministic objects, not race anecdotes.

Protocol rigs: recorded editor sessions replayed against BOTH servers (FrankenLean and the Reference) with wire-level diffing under registered nondeterminism classes (request interleaving) normalized by versioned normalizer code; the vscode-lean4 smoke matrix in CI (open, edit, hover, goals, completion, go-to-def, widgets, waitForDiagnostics across representative projects incl. a mathlib slice); the C5 corpus grown continuously from real usage.

Acceptance: replay determinism — a bundle replays bit-identically under the lab runtime; the smoke matrix green across the editor scenarios; first-divergence reporting demonstrated on a seeded server mutant. Tests: bundle round-trip fixtures; normalizer-honesty tests (a normalizer may never discard an error body to pass — planted-divergence detection); torture under the asupersync lab: cancellation storms, fault injection, crash-recovery of session state with seed replay (§18.2 torture lane).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A versioned replay bundle captures the complete init transcript, ordered edits/requests/cancellations, content ids or sealed sources, project/epoch/profile, scheduling/chaos seed, resource budgets, normalized outbound transcript, and hashes of referenced artifacts sufficient for offline deterministic replay. Secrets and private sources obey explicit redaction/sealing policy.
2. fln server replay validates bundle schema/bounds/closure, recreates the session under the lab runtime, and either produces bit-identical normalized output or reports the first divergence with request/event ids, raw and normalized values, normalizer rule/version, and causal context. Malformed/incomplete bundles fail typed and cannot mutate live state.
3. Protocol rigs replay C5 sessions against real FrankenLean and pinned Reference servers. Versioned normalizers may reorder only registered nondeterministic classes and may never erase error bodies, diagnostics, semantic data, or unregistered differences. The unmodified vscode-lean4 smoke matrix covers open/edit/hover/goals/completion/definition/widgets/waitForDiagnostics on representative projects including mathlib.
4. Named unit/property/golden/DPOR/fuzz/mutation suites cover bundle round trips and upgrades, closure/redaction/bounds, normalization idempotence and honesty, first-divergence location, cancellation/fault schedules, session crash recovery, and seed replay. Error-erasing normalizer, omitted-event, nondeterministic-replay, and stale-artifact mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane records real editor sessions, replays them against both real servers, runs the full smoke matrix, injects a seeded server divergence, cancellation storm, and storage/session fault, verifies first-divergence and crashpack reproduction, then confirms clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/bundle/client/server/session/request/edit/fault/normalizer/divergence ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted wire/argv/cwd, pin/build/epoch/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical content/bundle/transcript/snapshot roots, expected/actual, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives C5 bundles, raw/normalized transcripts, normalizer registry, editor recordings, DPOR schedules/crashpacks, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. Mock servers or lossy normalizers cannot close compatibility or replay-determinism claims.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — replay normalizers and request/message validators bind the extracted wire-census row and version. Bundles seal every external effect needed for replay or explicitly refuse replay with a typed unavailable-effect witness; they never silently simulate time, filesystem, network, editor state, or capability results. Protocol fuzzing, real editor transcripts, cancellation races, partial bundles, and deterministic recovery are mandatory.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes:

title: W8: Cache federation + epoch bridges — validated atp CAS, conservative cross-epoch reuse
description:
Implement cache federation + epoch-stable caching (§13.5, §13.6): the CAS syncs over atp (multi-donor, fountain-coded, resumable) or HTTPS fallback, entries signed and verified; a fln cache shim preserves the lake exe cache muscle memory and hydrates from the community existing archives during transition. Because records are keyed semantically, a cache populated by ANY machine on the epoch serves every project shape — first-mathlib-build becomes: sync frontier, elaborate your delta (PG-10: hydration >= 2x lake exe cache get on the lossy-path fixture, >= 1x clean).

Security law (R18): a remote entry is UNTRUSTED DATA until validated locally — node key and schema, content hashes and closure, epoch/platform compatibility, capability-input binding, kernel/checker receipts where relevant, signature policy, decompression bounds; a malformed entry becomes a miss plus a security event, never trusted-state poison; concurrent fill is lease-suppressed; corruption quarantines are first-class records.

Epoch-stable caching: toolchain bumps re-key the epoch, but the Ledger records WHY — a pin advance ships a machine-diffed census, and records provably untouched by the delta may carry epoch bridges (frontier, off by default, conservative allowlist, gated behind its own soundness rig — OQ-6 owns allowlist v1). The migration autopilot (audit project against the new epoch census, propose rewrites from the repair classes, lockstep builds, apply-transaction with rollback; never edits without explicit apply) is specified here and consumes the Bloodhound repair engine (W11).

Acceptance: PG-10 on the WAN fixtures; validation rejects every corpus of malformed entries; bridge soundness rig green on allowlist v1. Tests: multi-donor pull under injected loss; poisoned-entry fixtures; lease-suppression races under the lab; quarantine-and-revalidate drills.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-05 FEDERATION AND BRIDGE BOUNDARY — remote signatures and atp reconstruction prove transport/integrity only, never semantic reuse. Every hydrated object re-enters the local untrusted-candidate validation path. An epoch bridge is a versioned proof object over an allowlisted census-delta class and exact input roots; missing, unknown, ambiguous, revoked, or over-budget bridge evidence is a miss. Poison, replay, cross-tenant/capability, decompression, equivocation, lease, and bridge-unsoundness mutations must fail without contaminating trusted state.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Federate validated CAS entries through real multi-donor, fountain-coded, resumable atp transport with the specified internal HTTPS fallback and a compatible fln cache shim for existing community archives. Entries are semantically reusable across project shapes only within their validated epoch/platform/capability contract.
2. Treat every remote byte as untrusted until local validation confirms node key/schema/version, canonical content and closure hashes, epoch/platform/profile compatibility, capability-input binding, signatures, decompression/size/time bounds, and required kernel/checker receipts. Invalid data becomes a miss plus a bounded security event and quarantine record; it cannot poison trusted state. Concurrent fills are lease-suppressed and publication is atomic.
3. Epoch bridges are frontier, off by default, and allowed only for OQ-6 allowlist-v1 record classes proven untouched by the machine census delta. Each reuse carries old/new epoch identities, delta witness, bridge rule/version, local revalidation, and rollback path. Migration audit/proposal/lockstep/apply remains explicit and never edits without user apply authority.
4. Named unit/property/model/DPOR/fuzz/mutation suites cover manifest/signature/closure validation, decompression bombs, multi-donor reconstruction/resume, lease races, quarantine/revalidation, capability mismatches, epoch-delta proofs, bridge allow/deny, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and crash atomicity. Trust-before-validate, stale-bridge, lease-bypass, quarantine-reuse, and decompression-bound mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane syncs a real cache over atp across lossy/multi-donor paths and the supported fallback, hydrates a real project, races concurrent fills, supplies poisoned/corrupt entries, quarantines and revalidates, exercises an allowed and denied epoch bridge, and verifies exact clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/cache/node/donor/lease/quarantine/bridge/transaction ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted endpoints/argv/cwd, epoch/platform/profile/network/loss/seed/budget facts, canonical manifest/content/closure/receipt roots, validation/bridge decisions, expected/actual, timings/throughput/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. Reproducible PG-10 evidence on declared clean and lossy WAN fixtures shows hydration at least 2 times lake exe cache get on lossy and at least equal on clean, with distributions and corpus/machine/network facts. CI archives poison corpus, transport traces, bridge witnesses, fault matrices, mutant outcomes, benchmarks, and E2E manifests; mocks cannot close security or performance claims.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — separate federation authority from epoch-bridge authority. Federation validates candidates, leases, quarantine, and PG-10 over atp or native asupersync HTTPS fallback; no external downloader. Epoch bridges are off by default and require canonical census deltas, allowlists, promotion evidence, local revalidation, rollback, and typed W12 migration handoff. Platform/target/profile key dimensions apply only to target-sensitive products; portable semantic records retain cross-host reuse. Signature and retention never convey authority. Named suites: remote_candidate_authority_model, federation_lease_dpor, epoch_bridge_allowlist_model, cache_federation_no_mock_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Unknown schema, missing/duplicate ids, broken order/linkage, unbound artifacts, truncation, merged streams, or cleanup/final-state mismatch fail. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: fln-hash canon: recursive encoder (write_body) and Expr/Level Drop overflow on deep trees
description:
Duplicate planning report for the deep canonical encoder and Drop stack-overflow lifecycle discovered after franken_lean-fnj. The full encoder plus destruction scope, evidence, and tests have been consolidated into franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy so there is one implementation owner.

## Steps to Reproduce

See the retained discovery comment on this bead and the canonical reproducer matrix in franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy.

## Acceptance Criteria

This duplicate may close only after its encoder and Drop requirements are explicitly preserved in franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy, the parent fln-hash bead depends on that canonical owner, and the close reason states that no implementation or feature was declared complete here.
close_reason: Duplicate of franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy (deep-tree destruction stack-safety), which the owner filed independently; the extra encoder write_body recursion I noted has been folded into 6gy via comment.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Companion to franken_lean-fnj (which fixed the DECODE path). Discovered during the fnj review (Claude Fable session, 2026-07-22). crates/fln-hash/src/canon.rs Canonical::write_body for Expr and Level is recursive over tree depth, and Expr/Level themselves (nested Arc<ExprNode>/Arc<Node>) recurse on Drop. A sufficiently deep tree therefore overflows the stack when re-encoded (e.g. computing a decl-content digest) or when dropped. LOWER REACHABILITY than fnj: this requires a deep tree already in memory, which now (post-fnj) can arise from decoding a valid deep olean — real mathlib terms are nowhere near deep enough to trigger it, but a crafted valid deep artifact could. Fix: make write_body iterative (explicit work-stack, mirroring the fnj decoder) and add manual iterative Drop impls for Expr and Level that disassemble the Arc chain without recursion. Regression: encode + drop a ~1e5-deep valid term on a 1 MiB-stack thread without abort. Not urgent; filed so the fnj decode fix isn't mistaken for closing the whole depth-safety story.

title: W7: Verdict — owned CDCL SAT with owned proof logging and checker
description:
Implement Verdict (fln-verdict, §12.5): an owned CDCL SAT solver — watched literals, VSIDS/CHB-class heuristics, clause management, inprocessing kept modest and honest — with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker: the bv_decide pipeline bit-blasts through Verdict, Verdict emits a checkable unsatisfiability certificate, and a small independent checker (kernel-adjacent in audit rigor, separate in code) validates it before the reflected proof is admitted. The external-binary TCB (CaDiCaL + LRAT toolchain, weakness census #7) dies here; Behavior Note BN-06 records the change (same theorems, owned certificate chain, no external solver).

Parity posture (risk R17 honesty): correctness is absolute from day one; competitiveness is a ratchet — PG-9 tracks Verdict against the Reference solver on the bv_decide corpus with a published gap budget (100% agreement; wall <= 2x Reference-with-CaDiCaL at G5, ratcheting thereafter). If the gap persists, it PUBLISHES honestly; semantics never depended on solver speed.

Acceptance: 100% verdict agreement on the bv_decide corpus; every unsat certificate validated by the independent checker before admission; PG-9 wall within budget at G5. Tests: SAT/UNSAT differential vs known-good results on standard benchmarks + the bv_decide corpus; certificate-checker independence verified structurally (no shared solver modules); fuzzed CNF + fuzzed bit-blasting inputs under budgets; a planted bogus-certificate must be rejected.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

## Success Criteria

The schema, deterministic solver/logger, independent streaming checker, and bitblast/reflection integration close on exact roots; checker code does not depend on solver implementation; supported/refused rows are total; emitted theorems are kernel admitted; PG-9 and all adversarial evidence pass.
design: TERMINAL DECOMPOSITION — exact children are franken_lean-o5rt CNF/proof schema, franken_lean-h5z1 deterministic CDCL solver/logger, franken_lean-apgi independent streaming checker, and fln-zti3 bitblast/bv_decide/reflection/kernel integration. This epic closes only on their exact roots, checker nondependence on solver code, supported/refused manifest totality, mutation evidence, real E2E, and PG-9 evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement the owned Verdict CDCL solver with watched literals, deterministic VSIDS/CHB-class decisions, clause learning/management, bounded honest inprocessing, versioned proof logging, and a separately implemented certificate checker. No external SAT solver or proof tool executes in any release path.
2. Every UNSAT result used by bv_decide carries a complete certificate accepted by the independent checker before a kernel-replayed reflected proof may enter the environment. SAT assignments are validated against the original CNF. Unknown proof versions, malformed or over-budget proofs, checker disagreement, cancellation, and exhaustion are typed failures/Inconclusive and never admission.
3. Structural evidence proves the checker shares no solver modules and is simpler by KLOC/dependency closure. BN-06 records the owned-chain compatibility change while the Parity Ledger shows 100 percent SAT/UNSAT and goal-closure agreement on the bv_decide corpus.
4. Named unit/property/fuzz/differential/mutation suites cover propagation, conflicts, analysis, backjumping, restarts, deletion, assignment validation, proof encode/decode/check, deterministic seeded search, adversarial CNF/bit-blasting inputs, budgets, and failure atomicity. Bogus-certificate, skipped-clause, invalid-model, and admit-before-check mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs real bv_decide goals through bit-blasting, Verdict, the independent checker, and kernel replay; covers SAT, UNSAT, planted bogus proof rejection, budget exhaustion, and a subsequent clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/goal/CNF/clause/conflict/certificate ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, redacted argv/cwd, build/profile/thread/seed/budget/platform facts, canonical goal/CNF/proof/verdict hashes, expected/actual, checker/kernel decisions, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. Reproducible PG-9 evidence on the declared corpus/machine reports agreement and distributions and meets wall time no worse than 2 times the pinned Reference-with-CaDiCaL at G5, with any miss published rather than hidden. CI archives proofs, manifests, mutant outcomes, benchmarks, and all named tests/E2E artifacts.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — keep the versioned CNF/proof schema, deterministic CDCL solver/logger, independent streaming proof checker, and bitblast/bv_decide reflected-proof integration as distinct authorities. The checker does not depend on solver implementation and has separate decoder, state, normalization, dependency closure, and adversarial corpus. SAT models validate against original CNF; UNSAT proof validates before reflected theorem and kernel admission. CaDiCaL appears only as Tribunal benchmark/oracle. Named suites: cdcl_state_model, verdict_proof_codec_fuzz, verdict_checker_independence, bv_decide_no_mock_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: Gate G6: Sovereign & Beyond — no-cc executables, Ledger GA, R4 declared
description:
Convergence gate G6 "Sovereign & Beyond" (§22.3): Iron builds real executables with no C compiler; Ledger GA with cache federation and PG-3 (the cone) + PG-10 (cache hydration); the leapfrog surfaces shipped (Bloodhound, Folio, Envoy, terminal InfoView, WASM Judge); W12 distribution complete; --reproducible certified across the matrix. The toolchain is one static binary set that installs in seconds and owes the outside world nothing.

Also at this horizon per the gate table: PG-2 tightens to <= 0.35x Reference; PG-M to <= 0.9x (the native facade beating hosted dispatch — the design bet, measured); native_decide Tier 2 (trace-guided kernel replay) becomes the certified-path default (§11.6).

Evidence bundle: a cold-machine, air-gapped install building and checking a real project end to end (C9); Iron-built executables from real Lake projects with zero cc present; PG-3/PG-10 measurements on the WAN fixtures; the reproducible dual-build byte-identity results across the certified matrix; the R4 declaration for the pinned epoch — every mandatory Parity Ledger row at L4, no fallback code, the Witness matrix consistent with every published claim.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: G6 is the final join for complete workstreams and release claims. In addition to G5, W10, W11, W12, and native_decide Tier 2, it directly requires full W7, full W8, and full W9 closure. Thus frontier engine lanes, compiled-index publication, watch/distributed elaboration, cache federation, epoch bridges, causal-graph completeness, semantic merge, forensics, time travel, PG-3/PG-10, and all retained subsystem evidence cannot be lost merely because G4 and G5 use narrower concrete production dependencies. G6 remains the first point where the complete sovereign and beyond claim is allowed.
acceptance_criteria:
G6 closes only when the complete W7, W8, W9, W10, W11, and W12 workstreams are closed and their features ship together: Iron emits real executables with no C compiler present; Ledger provides true-cone invalidation, transactional CAS, watch/distributed elaboration with local re-verification, atp cache federation, retention, and conservative epoch bridges; Palimpsest provides complete causal graphs, replay, semantic diff/merge, why-trusts, forensics, time travel, and SQL; every leapfrog client and service ships; distribution, elan layout, epoch ratchet, claims matrix, and docs enforcement are complete. A cold, air-gapped C9 install of the static binary set builds, checks, runs, audits, and reproduces a real Lake project end to end with no undeclared executable, network, oracle, compiler, or fallback dependency. Iron native code, JIT where certified, C parity apparatus, inbound/outbound ABI doors, WASM Judge, Envoy, Bloodhound, Folio, and terminal InfoView retain their full platform/evidence matrices. --reproducible produces byte-identical binaries, oleans, diagnostics, roots, receipts, indexes, bundles, and install manifests in two isolated builds across every certified platform. PG-2 is no more than 0.35 times Reference, PG-M no more than 0.9 times Reference, PG-3 meets true-cone p50 15 seconds and p95 90 seconds, PG-10 beats lake cache hydration by at least 2 times on the lossy WAN fixture and at least matches it on clean paths, and native_decide Tier 2 is the certified-path default; all performance claims retain raw samples, tails, host/isolation/cache facts, and honest claim states. Risk R12 closes only when the Golem/Mirror profile handoff and Iron optimization ledger prove ecosystem-tolerable performance on the declared real corpus without any semantic or fidelity relaxation. If PG-7, PG-M, or associated tolerance evidence misses, the owning fast-path/JIT work remains open or the performance claim is explicitly BLOCKED/downgraded; G6 may not trade compatibility, determinism, trust, or diagnostic fidelity for speed. Every mandatory Parity Ledger row for the pinned epoch is L4 before R4 is declared; the Witness matrix, Behavior Notes, release artifacts, source scans, and documentation agree and prove no fallback code. Unit/property/model, differential, mutation, fuzz, crash/fault, metamorphic, determinism, reproducible-build, long-soak, and real-service no-mock suites cover every owning surface, including corruption, disk full, network loss, cancellation, revocation, partial promotion, rollback, and clean recovery. Strict schema-versioned bundles and signed receipts make every gate independently auditable. G5 plus all direct dependencies, scripts/check.sh, full cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS gates, Tribunal rigs, certified platform matrices, and performance gates are green from clean checkouts. No partial workstream, shell-out substitute, undeclared external dependency, hidden fallback, sampled compatibility claim, skipped platform presented as certified, non-reproducible artifact, unresolved R12 miss, stale R4 wording, or deferred listed feature satisfies G6.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — remove JIT where certified: certified products use static Iron; JIT remains frontier plus standard only. Byte identity compares two isolated builds of the same target; cross-target equality applies only to target-neutral semantic roots/artifacts. Telemetry and signature envelopes are separately bound. Downgrading a performance claim is honest reporting but does not satisfy an unmet mandatory PG gate unless the plan is formally amended with typed decision evidence.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
