2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: fln-env: apply module contributions atomically with bidirectional provenance indexes
description:
Child 2 of 4 for fln-amv.9.3, after franken_lean-module-provenance-schema-cxn. Implement one preflighted immutable transaction that registers a module record, adds every decoded declaration and extra generated declaration, replays each extension contribution in exact order, and updates module-to-contribution plus contribution-to-module indexes together or publishes nothing.

The canonical provenance manifest is intentionally identity-only, so the apply API must take a separate payload-bearing ModuleApplyTransaction envelope. The envelope carries the validated manifest root and module contribution record, actual Arc-backed ConstantInfo values for declarations and extra declarations, and Arc-backed extension payload entries keyed by descriptor and ordinal. Preflight must derive and compare every declaration name, canonical declaration identity, extension payload digest, ordinal, range, descriptor, module, artifact, epoch, and completeness field one-to-one against the manifest. Missing, extra, reordered, or mismatched payloads are typed refusals. Digest equality never substitutes for byte/value equality: an equal-digest unequal-value case is an explicit identity-collision conflict. Provenance indexes retain identities and Arcs, never flattened payload copies.

Publish one immutable committed state containing Environment, module graph/provenance manifest, and both indexes; do not maintain independently mutable truths. Required index keys include ModuleId to exact contribution record, declaration Name to owner ModuleId/class/source ordinal, and stable ExtensionEntryId plus extension Name and source occurrence to contributor ModuleId; target journal ordinal/range is retained separately as a committed-root-scoped placement witness. Applied extension ranges bind descriptor, starting journal position and checked length, ordered entry identities, contributor module, artifact, epoch, completeness, and the committed provenance root; ranges are placement facts and never the stable content identity. Unknown extension registration, descriptor mismatch, noncontiguous or stale range, and base/checkpoint drift are typed before publication. Opaque payloads remain byte-exact and graded, not decoded or normalized.

Return field-specific outcomes for missing direct targets, partial decoded modules, exact idempotence, conflicting module records, duplicate or conflicting declaration ownership, unknown or mismatched extension descriptors, replay range drift, stale graph/provenance/logical/base/checkpoint identity, identity collision, and every entry/byte/name/index resource limit. Missing targets and partial decode produce an explicit AppliedIncomplete grade that blocks complete-cache/fine-invalidation claims; they are never rendered or cached as rejection. Exact idempotent reapplication is a no-op only when every provenance field and every actual declaration/payload value is equal. Any collision or mismatch is a conflict, never last-writer-wins.

Logical-root behavior is explicit: graph, artifact, ownership, and provenance metadata alone cannot alter Environment logical_root; publishing actual declarations and extension deltas changes logical_root exactly through the existing Environment rules. The result returns and verifies before/after logical and provenance roots so neither topology leakage nor a dropped semantic contribution can hide. Preserve O(1) snapshots and bounded path copying; report allocation/path-copy work facts.

Tests must compare forward and reverse indexes and the combined committed state against independent maps over generated batches; prove all-or-nothing behavior for every early and late failure; cover complete and AppliedIncomplete success, exact idempotence, every conflict field, unknown descriptors, digest-collision fixtures, replay bytes/order/ranges, empty payloads, and opaque/understood payloads; record pointer/allocation sharing; run insertion and thread-order metamorphic matrices; and kill mutants that drop a declaration, extra declaration, extension row, reverse row, payload-binding check, root precondition, or preflight check. Unit evidence is schema-versioned and line-oriented with transaction/root ids, counts, ranges, limits/usage, grade, expected/actual outcome, publication flag, and final state. Real decoded-fixture and shared no-mock E2E remains solely in fln-amv.9.4.

Run fln-env and fln-hash tests, domain checks, targeted and parent fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates. No partial publication, name-or-digest-only payload binding, last-writer-wins ownership, flattened payload copy, disconnected provenance store, hidden logical-root change, or falsely complete partial state.

## Acceptance Criteria

The payload-bearing transaction envelope must bind actual Arc-backed declaration and extension values one-to-one to the validated canonical manifest before atomically publishing Environment, graph/provenance state, and both index directions. Every success grade, exact-idempotence, identity-collision, conflict, incomplete, stale-root, replay-range, descriptor, ownership, payload-binding, logical-root transition, and resource case above is mandatory. Generated independent models, early/late all-or-nothing tables, schedule metamorphics, sharing/allocation evidence, named mutants, detailed versioned unit logs, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates must be green. Real no-mock fixtures remain in fln-amv.9.4. Partial publication, identity-only application, or deferred indexes fail closure.
design:
Plan-space precision addendum - immutable admission plans and publication.

Split application into prepare_module_apply and commit_module_apply. Preparation returns a non-authoritative immutable ModuleApplyPlan binding schema/epoch, transaction ID and canonical request digest, the exact manifest and ModuleProvenanceRootV1, source committed-state revision plus logical/provenance roots, actual Arc-backed payload identities, one module-graph transition plan from franken_lean-module-graph-resource-outcomes-46b, one declaration-admission plan per declaration from franken_lean-j8h, exact extension-state/range proofs from fln-extension-history-checkpoint-identity-41s, both index deltas, expected root transitions, and checked aggregate usage. No pointer address enters identity. Provisional digests, roots, or candidate values confer no authority and cannot be exposed or cached as accepted state.

Commit revalidates the request digest, exact base revision and roots, plan integrity, every actual payload value, extension-state identity, cancellation, and all subplan preconditions immediately before one immutable publication. It consumes each graph, declaration, extension, collision/PMap, and index work fact exactly once; it neither recomputes nor double-charges canonical bytes, collision order, path-copy work, or range identity. A stale, substituted, cross-base, replayed-with-different-inputs, or internally inconsistent plan publishes nothing. Any cancellation or resource failure after preparation discards the entire candidate and leaves the base state value-, root-, index-, cache-, and sharing-identical.

Completeness, semantic transparency, and authority are independent. Fully captured opaque bytes may produce an authoritative exact-replay state while forcing conservative invalidation. Missing or partially decoded data retains AppliedIncomplete only as an explicitly quarantined immutable diagnostic state with authority=false and complete-cacheability=false; it cannot replace an authoritative head or be consumed by kernel, elaboration, fine-invalidation, or accepted-cache APIs. Resource exhaustion or cancellation is Inconclusive, not AppliedIncomplete, and produces no authoritative candidate. Types or capability checks prevent accidental authority promotion.

Every successful commit returns a ModuleApplyReceipt binding transaction/request identities, base and result roots, module/artifact/epoch, completeness/transparency/authority, exact contribution/index identities, usage, and publication kind. Retrying the same request against the same base yields the same plan and result; retrying against its exact result returns AlreadyApplied without replaying an extension row; retrying against a later descendant may return AlreadyApplied only with a receipt/ancestry proof and exact unchanged payload, contribution, and both-index equality. Reusing an ID with different inputs is Conflict; an unproved base mismatch is stale. Retry checks are bounded and obey FL-INV-07.

Application is monotone within one Environment snapshot. Re-registering the same module identity with different artifact evidence, manifest, or actual values returns a typed ArtifactReplacementConflict carrying old/new evidence and a deterministic invalidation seed; it never overwrites or retracts in place. The higher integration layer rebuilds by forking the exact last-unaffected snapshot and invalidating/replaying the affected cone. This bead owns the per-module transition primitive. fln-amv.9.4 owns ModuleBatchApplyPlan: order modules by the exact fln-amv.9.2 request/profile and discovery/replay contract, chain per-module candidates privately, and expose only one final combined state. Failure at module N exposes none of modules 1 through N-minus-1.

ModuleApplyLimits contains independent versioned sub-budgets for manifest/name/depth binding, graph registration, declaration canonical/admission work, extension entries/payload/exact-history comparison, forward/reverse index rows and PMap/path-copy/fresh-node work, plan/receipt/witness bytes, and aggregate checked work. Complete reports exact logical usage. Refusal/Inconclusive reports exact work through the frozen checkpoint plus the triggering dimension and witnessed lower bound without scanning unobserved input. One total precedence table covers malformed input, stale plan/root, idempotence/conflict, candidate collision, checked overflow, cancellation, and simultaneous limits. Candidate equal-digest unequal-value input is IdentityCollisionConflict only after bounded exact comparison completes; exhaustion before that decision is Inconclusive. The same inconsistency already present in validated committed state is InternalFault. Typed allocation claims cover configured budgets, checked arithmetic, and demonstrably fallible reservations only, never arbitrary global allocator abort.
acceptance_criteria:
The payload-bearing transaction envelope must bind actual Arc-backed declaration and extension values one-to-one to the validated canonical manifest before atomically publishing Environment, graph/provenance state, and both index directions. Every success grade, exact-idempotence, identity-collision, conflict, incomplete, stale-root, replay-range, descriptor, ownership, payload-binding, logical-root transition, and resource case above is mandatory. Every negative, cancellation, or Inconclusive case must prove zero publication and be followed by a clean successful recovery transaction whose values, indexes, logical root, and provenance root are verified. Generated independent models, early/late all-or-nothing tables, schedule metamorphics, sharing/allocation evidence, named mutants, detailed versioned unit logs, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates must be green. Real no-mock fixtures remain in fln-amv.9.4. Partial publication, identity-only application, deferred indexes, or an unrecoverable failed transaction fails closure.

Named unit/state-machine/property tests exercise prepare/commit; plan substitution and cross-base use; final cancellation; exact same-base, exact-result, and later-descendant retries with and without receipt proof; same transaction ID with different payload; complete/opaque/partial/missing authority combinations; artifact-replacement refusal; and per-module composition into a privately staged multi-module batch. Independent Vec/BTreeMap models compare the complete Environment, graph/manifest, actual Arc payload values, forward/reverse indexes, receipts, roots, authority, and usage after every transition. Early and last-step faults for every subplan and every zero/one-under/exact/one-over budget prove zero authoritative publication, exact value/root/index/cache/sharing identity of the base, and a later adequate-budget recovery. Productive 1/8/32 schedule tests use real concurrent preparation/interleavings with recorded seeds and byte-identical authoritative results; a sequential loop relabeled with thread counts is insufficient.

Additional discriminating mutants accept a stale or substituted plan, double-charge or recompute a subplan, publish before final cancellation, expose a provisional root, append twice on retry, accept the same transaction ID with changed input, promote AppliedIncomplete to authoritative/cacheable, confuse opaque with partial, overwrite a changed artifact, expose a successful prefix of a failed batch, classify an unfinished collision comparison as conflict, or downgrade a validated-state inconsistency from InternalFault. Each mutant fails at its intended semantic assertion, not compilation or unrelated failure.

Strict schema-versioned local evidence records transaction/plan/receipt IDs, exact base/result logical and provenance roots, module/artifact/manifest/request identities, contribution classes/ordinals and stable extension EntryIds, subplan and aggregate limits/usage/checkpoints, completeness/transparency/authority, retry/replacement/batch position, expected/actual outcome, publication kind, cacheability, sharing/work, mutation identity and first divergence, recovery action, and final state. Validation rejects missing, extra, duplicate, stale, cross-transaction, authority-contradictory, wrong-mutant, or surviving-mutant records. This bead closes on exhaustive local evidence plus the stable batch handoff; the real decoded no-mock batch/failure/recovery lane remains downstream in fln-amv.9.4 and is required before the outer fln-amv.9 epic closes, never circularly before this child.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Plan-space hardening complete: the obsolete direct fln-amv.7 edge was replaced by collision-safe 41s; module-graph and declaration admission plans are explicit inputs; prepare/commit, authority quarantine, idempotent retry, artifact replacement, per-module versus batch publication, single-charge budgets, exact collision classification, productive 1-8-32 tests, mutants, and strict evidence are now normative. Stable EntryId is semantic; journal range is a committed-root placement witness.

title: fln-env: compute deterministic module invalidation cones with provenance witnesses
description:
Child 3 of 4 for fln-amv.9.3, after the atomic committed state and bidirectional indexes. Implement bounded deterministic invalidation queries for five distinct change classes: artifact identity, direct-import topology including every import flag and missing-target appearance, declaration contribution identity/content, extension contribution descriptor/range/payload identity, and opaque or partial completeness boundary.

Queries operate only on the single validated committed state from the atomic-apply bead. Each request carries the expected provenance root and, where semantic state matters, expected logical root; stale roots are refused before traversal. Change seeds are resolved through exact reverse indexes rather than name guesses. Artifact and topology changes seed the owning module; declaration changes seed the exact declaration owner; extension changes seed the exact descriptor/ordinal contributor; a missing target becoming available seeds every exact importing row. Effective-import reverse facts may be reused only where their flag/state phase is semantically exact. Until declaration-level causal edges exist, propagation across module imports is conservatively module-granular and must not claim finer precision.

Results distinguish DefinitelyImpacted, ConservativelyImpacted through opaque, partial, or missing state, Unaffected, Inconclusive resource exhaustion or cancellation, and InternalFault invariant failure. Every impacted module carries a canonical shortest witness path with typed edges and terminal reason. Edge kinds include change seed, owns declaration, owns extension range, direct import with all flags, effective visibility/state propagation, missing target, and opaque/partial boundary. Tie breaks use explicit ModuleId and edge-kind order, never hash, allocation, insertion, or worker order. Definitely impacted always outranks conservative classification for the same module, while all equal-length witnesses use the frozen tie policy.

Opaque or incomplete extension state stops fine-grained reasoning at that boundary but continues conservative module-level reverse propagation. Equal-digest unequal-value collision evidence is an InternalFault/conflict input, never silently unchanged. A corrupt cycle or inconsistent forward/reverse row in an allegedly validated state is InternalFault with no authoritative cone. No finding from Inconclusive or InternalFault may be cached as acceptance, rejection, or an authoritative unaffected result.

Define independent limits for modules discovered, import rows examined, index rows examined, witness edges/bytes, names/name depth, output records, and total encoded/logged bytes. Check limits before expansion or output allocation. Exhaustion and cancellation return a typed Inconclusive envelope that may include a deterministic diagnostic prefix plus exact usage facts, but the prefix is explicitly non-authoritative and cannot drive cache reuse. Cancellation observation and budget tie points are fixed so replay with the same seed/budget is deterministic.

Tests compare random edit cones, grades, and witnesses against an independent graph/index model; cover every change class, import flag, completeness grade, missing-target transition, diamond and equal-shortest tie, validated DAG and corrupted-cycle/inconsistent-index refusal, stale logical/provenance roots, cancellation at every fixed observation point, and every exact resource boundary. Insertion and thread-order metamorphics must produce byte-identical authoritative output. Named mutants omit an import edge, declaration/extension reverse edge, opaque stop, conservative propagation, witness reason, collision check, or root check. Emit detailed schema-versioned line-oriented unit evidence with request/root ids, change seed/class, examined module/import/index counts, grade, canonical witnesses, limits/usage, expected/actual outcome, authority flag, cacheability, and final state.

Run fln-env and fln-hash tests, domain checks, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates. No fine invalidation through opaque data, name-only seed resolution, nondeterministic witness, unbounded traversal, authoritative partial result, or rejection cached from inconclusive work.

## Acceptance Criteria

All five change classes must produce bounded deterministic graded module cones from the single committed provenance/index state, with exact reverse-index seed resolution, stale logical/provenance-root validation, canonical typed shortest witnesses, fixed cancellation points, and FL-INV-07 authority/cache rules. Independent random models, all flag/completeness/missing/collision/corruption/boundary/cancellation tables, schedule metamorphics, named mutants, detailed versioned evidence, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates must be green. Advisory-only, name-guessed, unbounded, or authoritatively partial queries fail closure.
design:
Plan-space precision addendum - versioned old/new changes and exact effective context.

Use ChangeDescriptorV1, whose variants retain the five change classes and bind both expected old state and proposed new state. Artifact changes carry ModuleId plus old and optional new epoch/artifact evidence. Direct-import changes carry exact old/new ordered DirectImport arrays with source ordinals, duplicates, all flags, target appearance/disappearance, and changed isModule state. Declaration changes carry owner, class, source ordinal, exact Name, old canonical identity/actual-value proof, and optional new identity/value proof. Extension changes carry contributor, descriptor, stable ExtensionEntryId, expected snapshot-local ordinal/range, old actual entry identity, and optional new identity. Completeness changes carry old/new capture completeness, semantic transparency, and evidence/authority grade. The old side must match committed state exactly before traversal; mismatch is StaleDescriptor, not InternalFault. Exact no-change yields an authoritative empty cone only after complete validation.

Every request binds EffectiveImportRequestV1: root module, ModuleProvenanceRootV1, pinned global level, explicit root isExported override, every fln-amv.9.2 visibility/data/IR profile field, tie-policy/schema version, and request digest. A supplied effective-import report is reusable only when its graph root, complete request/profile digest, completeness, and canonical report identity match; otherwise recompute through fln-amv.9.2 or return typed stale/incomplete/Inconclusive under the same law. Direct reverse reachability alone justifies only a conservative module cone when exact effective propagation is unavailable. No cache key omits the effective request/profile.

Extension changes seed by stable ExtensionEntryId. Journal ordinal/range is an expected placement witness that may change after a valid branch merge; it is never semantic identity or the sole reverse-index key. Missing-target appearance and disappearance resolve every exact importing row, retaining duplicate ordinals and flags. Topology reorder is observable wherever the pin makes it observable and is never canonicalized as a set.

A proposed candidate with equal digest but unequal actual value is IdentityCollisionConflict, never Unchanged or engine InternalFault. The same inconsistency already present inside supposedly validated committed state or indexes is InternalFault with no authoritative cone. Exhaustion before bounded exact comparison completes is Inconclusive. This candidate-versus-validated-state distinction applies to artifact, declaration, and extension identities.

Invalidation is read-only: it never publishes or mutates Environment, graph, manifest, or index. Evidence publication fields refer only to result/cache publication. A completed DefinitelyImpacted or ConservativelyImpacted cone is authoritative at its exact or conservative grade and cacheable only under ModuleProvenanceRootV1, logical root when semantically required, full ChangeDescriptor digest, full EffectiveImportRequest/profile digest, witness/tie schema, and result grade. Unaffected is authoritative/cacheable only after complete proof with no unresolved opaque, partial, or missing path that could reach that module. Stale/Invalid/Conflict/InternalFault and Inconclusive prefixes never enter authoritative cache; recovery runs against unchanged roots.

InvalidationLimitsV1 separately budgets old/new descriptor rows/bytes, effective-report verification or recomputation, stable-entry/index and collision exact comparison, modules/import/index rows, witness edges/bytes, names/depth, output/serialization, and cache-entry bytes. Underlying facts are charged once. Complete reports exact logical usage; Inconclusive reports deterministic checkpoint, triggering dimension under frozen precedence, and a witnessed lower bound without scanning unseen work. Cancellation is observed at fixed points including immediately before authoritative result/cache insertion. Timing and outer-runner budgets never substitute for in-process limits.
acceptance_criteria:
All five change classes must produce bounded deterministic graded module cones from the single committed provenance/index state, with exact reverse-index seed resolution, stale logical/provenance-root validation, canonical typed shortest witnesses, fixed cancellation points, and FL-INV-07 authority/cache rules. Independent random models, all flag/completeness/missing/collision/corruption/boundary/cancellation tables, schedule metamorphics, named mutants, detailed versioned evidence, fln-env/fln-hash/domain tests, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and predecessor gates must be green. Every negative or Inconclusive result proves nonpublication and is followed by a successful recovery query against the unchanged committed roots. The shared real decoded-fixture no-mock E2E is owned by fln-amv.9.4 and must exercise invalidation, failure, and recovery before the parent closes. Advisory-only, name-guessed, unbounded, authoritatively partial, or independently E2E-complete claims fail closure.

Exhaustive old/new tables and independent graph/index/effective-import models cover add, remove, replace, reorder, duplicate, every import-flag change, target appearance/disappearance, exact no-op, stale old side, every declaration/extension class, stable EntryId with shifted ordinal/range, all completeness/transparency/evidence transitions, and every fln-amv.9.2 global-level/root-export/IR profile. Generated random DAG edits compare exact grades, canonical witnesses, authority, and cache action; an independent conservative oracle proves no false Unaffected when exact propagation is unavailable. Productive 1/8/32 schedules and insertion permutations produce byte-identical authoritative cones/cache keys with replayable seeds.

Zero/one-under/exact/one-over tests cover every descriptor, effective, traversal, collision-comparison, witness, output, and cache budget plus cancellation at every fixed point. Each Inconclusive run proves committed-state and authoritative-cache nonmutation, marks any prefix non-authoritative, and is followed by an adequate-budget query over the same roots yielding the model result. Candidate collision is Conflict; collision or inconsistent rows already present in validated state are InternalFault. Both paths, stale descriptors/reports, and profile mismatch remain distinct in schema and recovery.

Additional mutants trust new state without validating old, collapse ordered duplicates, omit each flag or isModule transition, ignore target disappearance, key extension by ordinal instead of stable EntryId, reuse an effective report under wrong root/profile, omit profile or descriptor from a cache key, cache Inconclusive/InternalFault, mark conservative/opaque path Unaffected, treat candidate collision as unchanged/InternalFault, downgrade corrupt committed state to candidate conflict, mutate an index during query, skip final cancellation, double-charge effective work, or publish a diagnostic prefix. Every mutant is killed by its named semantic assertion.

Strict versioned local evidence records request/change/effective-profile/report/cache-key IDs, exact old/new artifact/row/value/completeness facts, stable EntryId plus placement witness, base roots, examined descriptor/effective/module/import/index/collision/witness counts, limits/usage/checkpoint, canonical witnesses/grade, authority/cacheability/cache action, expected/actual outcome, mutation/first divergence, unchanged-state proof, recovery, and final state. Validation rejects missing, extra, duplicate, stale, swapped-profile, wrong-entry, wrong-mutant, surviving-mutant, or authority-contradictory evidence. This child closes on local proof and stable handoff; fln-amv.9.4 owns real decoded invalidation/failure/recovery required only before the outer fln-amv.9 epic closes.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Plan-space hardening complete: invalidation now consumes versioned old/new ChangeDescriptorV1 and the full fln-amv.9.2 request/profile, seeds extensions by stable EntryId, distinguishes candidate collision Conflict from validated-state InternalFault, proves Unaffected only from complete bounded reasoning, and treats result/cache publication separately from immutable state. Models, every resource/cancellation boundary, productive 1-8-32, cache-key mutants, strict logs, and downstream-only real fixtures remain mandatory.

title: fln-env: preserve module provenance across snapshots checkpoints and semantic merges
description:
Child 4 of 4 and one of two parallel completion blockers for fln-amv.9.3 after the atomic committed-state contract. It is independent of the invalidation-query child: both consume the atomic state, and the parent joins both before integration. Integrate the atomic committed Environment plus graph/provenance/index state with immutable branch snapshots and fln-amv.7 extension checkpoints. Unchanged Environment maps, module graph, manifest records, both index directions, contribution arrays, payload Arcs, and checkpoint bases remain structurally shared; clone is bounded Arc work, not traversal.

A bare ExtensionCheckpoint does not carry contributor ownership, so define a paired, schema-versioned provenance checkpoint envelope rather than guessing on restore. It binds checkpoint mode and descriptor, expected base logical and provenance roots, epoch/artifact identities, base journal identity, exact captured range, ordered entry identities, contributor ModuleIds/classes, completeness grades, and the Arc-backed payload entries already owned by the committed state. Suffix mode captures only the provenance suffix plus an exact base precondition; full-journal mode captures all contributor rows needed to rebuild the journal. Capture verifies payload bytes against entry identities and never flattens or duplicates opaque payload storage.

Restore is one preflighted immutable transaction over the combined committed state. Suffix and full-journal restore preserve contributor module, exact entry identity/order/range, artifact and epoch binding, completeness, both index directions, provenance root, and the Environment logical-root law. Stale base/logical/provenance/checkpoint roots, descriptor or mode mismatch, missing contributor rows, unequal-value identity collision, range drift/overlap/gap, incomplete checkpoint data, and every resource limit are typed before publication. Incomplete or opaque input remains explicitly graded and never becomes rejection or verified completeness. Failure leaves the original combined state byte-, root-, and pointer-identical; a subsequent clean restore must succeed.

Snapshots are O(1) and isolated. Nested and repeated checkpoints bind the exact canonical ExtensionState base and combined-target preconditions. A content-identical reconstructed or sibling base is valid regardless of allocation lineage; an equal-length or equal-digest but canonically different base, or a target changed after plan construction, is stale. Exact repeated restore may be idempotent only when actual values, identities, roots, and contributor indexes all match. Metadata-only provenance changes never alter logical_root, while restored declaration or extension semantic bytes affect logical_root exactly through existing Environment rules.

Implement the provenance-state three-way merge primitive consumed later by fln-amv.11 without duplicating that bead global Environment merge. Given base, ours, and theirs committed roots, it returns an immutable ProvenanceMergePlan plus typed conflicts; it does not publish a globally merged Environment. Identical and one-sided states preserve identity; disjoint module origins combine canonically; shared identical payload Arcs remain shared; compatible extension-origin rows follow their registered merge/checkpoint contract; conflicting module/declaration/extension origins, stale bases, epoch/schema mismatch, identity collisions, range drift/overlap, descriptor conflicts, and completeness disagreement are atomic typed conflicts, never last-writer-wins. The plan carries exact preconditions and before/after provenance expectations so fln-amv.11 can revalidate immediately before global publication.

Recompute and verify the separate provenance root after restore and plan construction. Tests cover snapshot isolation and clone-cost facts; nested/repeated suffix and full restore; sibling/cross-branch stale checkpoints including equal-length different-root cases; every idempotent, one-sided, disjoint, compatible, and conflict merge case; exact actual-payload and contributor identity; root sensitivity and metadata-only logical-root noninterference; independent checkpoint/restore/merge models; pointer/allocation sharing; insertion and thread-order matrices; all exact resource failures, cancellation/inconclusive behavior, and clean recovery. Named mutants lose checkpoint contributor identity, a graph/root precondition, completeness, one reverse row, payload binding, sharing, or conflict detection. Emit detailed schema-versioned line-oriented unit logs with base/checkpoint/restored/plan roots and ids, modes, contributor/range facts, sharing/allocation facts, operations, limits/usage, grades, authority/publication flags, expected/actual outcomes, and clean final state.

Run fln-env and fln-hash tests, domain-registry checks, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and all predecessor and parent checks. Real decoded-fixture and shared no-mock integration remains solely in fln-amv.9.4. No global merge duplication, unpaired checkpoint provenance, advisory-only lifecycle metadata, payload flattening, partial publication, stale-root acceptance, or hidden logical-root change.

## Acceptance Criteria

O(1) snapshots and paired provenance checkpoints for both checkpoint modes must preserve actual payload/contributor identities, ranges, both index directions, completeness, sharing, separate roots, and the logical-root law through preflighted atomic restore. The three-way operation must return the exact revalidated ProvenanceMergePlan consumed by fln-amv.11, not duplicate global Environment merge. Every stale/equal-length-sibling/collision/range/descriptor/origin/completeness/resource/cancellation path, independent lifecycle model, positive/negative/idempotence matrix, schedule metamorphic, sharing/allocation fact, named mutant, detailed versioned log, fln-env/fln-hash/domain test, fmt, clippy, UBS, and predecessor gate is mandatory. Real fixture E2E remains in fln-amv.9.4.
design:
Immutable snapshot and plan law.

A committed snapshot is immutable authoritative primary records plus deterministically derived indexes under exact logical and ModuleProvenanceRootV1 preconditions. Snapshot clone performs a fixed bounded number of shared-root clones independent of cardinality. No authoritative interior mutation or global cache is permitted. Memoized derived data is non-authoritative, root/request keyed, and discardable. Arc is storage only: pointer equality may fast-path one immutable object, but pointer inequality is never conflict, and pointer/refcount/allocation topology never enters canonical identity, roots, plans, diagnostics, or evidence.

A bare extension checkpoint follows fln-extension-history-checkpoint-identity-41s, so content-identical reconstructed bases are valid and allocation lineage is nonsemantic. A paired provenance checkpoint separately binds the combined target. Suffix mode requires the exact ExtensionState base plus expected target logical and ModuleProvenanceRootV1 roots. FullJournal carries complete source journal/contributor rows and has no source ancestry requirement, yet still requires an exact combined target root pair or versioned create-empty sentinel before replacement/addition. Self-contained never means arbitrary last-writer-wins. An unrelated edit does not invalidate the bare FullJournal data, but it stales an already prepared combined-state plan; bounded replanning may succeed if nonconflicting. Content-identical siblings are interchangeable. Exact snapshot means schema/epoch, root pair, and collision-disambiguated canonical state, never branch token or Arc identity.

Capture/restore and provenance merge first return immutable ProvenanceRestorePlanV1 or ProvenanceMergePlanV1. A complete plan binds governed inputs, schema/epoch, target/base/ours/theirs root pairs, checkpoint/manifest identities, ordered primary-record edits, derived index edits, entry/range changes, completeness/evidence refinements, expected final roots, exact resource usage/reservations, and complete conflict facts. Limits cover modules, contributions, ranges, index rows, names/depth, witness/conflict/output bytes, allocations, and total work before expansion/allocation. Inconclusive is non-authoritative, never a truncated complete plan. Apply revalidates every input/root and exact index derivability, consumes reservations once, and after final revalidation performs no unreserved input-sized allocation and has no cancellation point. All semantic planes become visible together. Stale, substituted, partial, cross-root, tampered, or collision-ambiguous plans publish nothing.

For identical governed bytes and artifact/epoch, evidence may refine capture Missing to Partial to Complete, transparency Opaque to Mixed to Understood, and authority by capability inclusion only with exact evidence. Refinement changes ModuleProvenanceRootV1 and not logical_root. Compatible grades/evidence join canonically; downgrade, contradiction, changed governed bytes, or unsupported refinement conflicts. Restore preserves checkpoint evidence and cannot erase stronger target evidence. Inconclusive, missing evidence, or digest equality never upgrades authority.

Replay/restore/merge preserve ExtensionEntryIdV1 while recomputing target journal ordinals/ranges. Duplicate content IDs remain distinct occurrences by contributor/source ordinal. Indexes/witnesses report both stable ID/source occurrence and target position. Lifecycle guarantees in-memory semantics and a W8 handoff of canonical manifest/root, schema/epoch, collision-aware payload requirements, evidence facts, and index-rebuild recipe; W8 owns durable storage, restart, and rehydration authority.
acceptance_criteria:
O(1) snapshots and paired provenance checkpoints for both checkpoint modes must preserve actual payload/contributor identities, ranges, both index directions, completeness, sharing, separate roots, and the logical-root law through preflighted atomic restore. The three-way operation must return the exact revalidated ProvenanceMergePlan consumed by fln-amv.11, not duplicate global Environment merge. Every stale/content-different equal-length sibling/collision/range/descriptor/origin/completeness/resource/cancellation path, independent lifecycle model, positive/negative/idempotence matrix, schedule metamorphic, sharing/allocation fact, named mutant, detailed versioned log, fln-env/fln-hash/domain test, fmt, clippy, UBS, and predecessor gate is mandatory. Real fixture E2E remains in fln-amv.9.4.

Snapshot tests prove fixed-cost clone, old-snapshot stability after arbitrary branch edits, bounded path copying, fresh-reconstruction equality under different Arc topology, and productive schedule-independent 1/8/32 results. Checkpoint tables distinguish suffix with exact extension/combined-target preconditions; FullJournal to empty/exact target without source ancestry; stale target after unrelated edit; successful bounded replanning; content-identical sibling; equal-length/equal-digest canonically unequal target; and duplicate EntryIds at distinct source/journal ordinals.

Independent restore/merge models verify every primary/index edit, final root, exact charge, and complete conflict. Mutants mutate behind snapshots, use Arc identity, accept stale plans, omit/double-charge edits, allocate after final revalidation, publish before indexes, treat FullJournal as arbitrary overwrite authority, or confuse source and target ordinals.

Evidence-refinement tables cover every comparable refinement, localized opaque-to-understood transition, capability addition, target-stronger restore, downgrade, contradiction, changed bytes, and insufficient evidence. Mutants promote partial/opaque/Inconclusive state, erase stronger evidence, change logical_root for evidence-only refinement, or fail to change ModuleProvenanceRootV1.

Collision tests distinguish canonical from digest equality at entry, manifest, root, checkpoint, and plan boundaries. Candidate collision yields conflict/nonpublication; validated-state inconsistency yields InternalFault; claim metadata uses bounded_model/statistical classes accurately. Strict local records include snapshot/plan/checkpoint IDs, root pairs, EntryIds/source/target ordinals, ranges, evidence-axis transitions, nonsemantic sharing facts, reservations/usage, conflict completeness, authority/publication/cacheability, expected/actual, recovery, and final state. This child closes locally and does not wait on fln-amv.9.4 or fln-amv.11.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Plan-space hardening complete: content-identical reconstructed/sibling bases are valid; canonically different or post-plan targets are stale. FullJournal source self-containment is separate from exact combined-target concurrency. Immutable restore/merge plans, evidence refinement, stable EntryId versus rebased positions, no Arc identity, final no-failure publication, exhaustive models/mutants/1-8-32/logs, and the W8 durable handoff boundary are explicit. No dependency on invalidation, .9.4, .11, or W8 was added.

title: fln-env: define the canonical module-provenance schema and graph identity
description:
Child 1 of 4 for fln-amv.9.3. Define the immutable identity-only schema for exact module contributions: imported declaration names, extra generated declaration names, and ordered extension-entry contributions identified by stable ranges and entry identities without copying payloads. Represent understood, opaque, partial, and missing completeness explicitly. Register a dedicated versioned fln-hash domain and canonical encoding whose root covers module and epoch identity, artifact evidence, exact ordered direct-import rows including duplicates and flags, every contribution identity, and completeness grade. The root is an explicit ModuleGraph/Provenance identity for future Ledger demand keys and receipts; prove that it never changes Environment logical_root and is not operational metadata.

Identity-only is deliberate and must be explicit in the public contract. Declaration ConstantInfo values and raw extension payload bytes remain Arc-backed in the Environment/import transaction rather than being duplicated here. The artifact digest plus separate Environment logical root bind semantic content; the schema binds ownership/order/range/completeness. The atomic-apply child owns the one-to-one actual-value transaction preflight, equal-digest unequal-value collision handling, and semantic logical-root transition. This schema must neither imply that names/digests are sufficient to apply a module nor grow a second copy of semantic payload state.

Specify and freeze field and enum tags, positional layout, ordering, duplicate policy, range identity, schema evolution, resource accounting, malformed-input refusals, and canonical set versus ordered-sequence semantics before downstream APIs freeze. Tests include field-by-field golden encodings, domain-registry uniqueness and completeness, canonical insertion-order independence, order-sensitive rows/contribution sequences/ranges, exact logical-root noninterference, exact boundary/resource tables including cumulative decoder accounting before allocation, all enum-tag round trips and unknown refusals, and named mutants that omit an extra declaration, extension contributor, completeness grade, artifact binding, direct-row field, graph identity field, or entry identity. Generated schedule matrices, storage-sharing checks, noncanonical/future/truncated/trailing/unknown inputs, semantic malformed tables, opaque and partial grades, and exact duplicate rows are mandatory.

Run fln-hash and fln-env tests, domain-registry checks, fmt, clippy with warnings denied, UBS, and applicable parent gates. No disconnected provenance truth, host data, payload flattening, name/digest-only application claim, implicit versioning, unbounded decode allocation, or hidden logical-root change.
design:
Normative V1 contribution table and ordinal law.

Before schema freeze, publish one exhaustive generated table with record/field tag, pinned decoder source field, value type, ordered-sequence versus canonical-set law, ordinal namespace and width, duplicate policy, canonical identity inputs, payload-versus-identity ownership, ModuleProvenanceRootV1 participation, completeness-axis effect, malformed outcome, and resource charge. Missing, duplicate, stale, or unclassified rows block closure.

V1 distinguishes: module headers canonically keyed by ModuleId with exact-equality-or-conflict duplicate behavior; direct imports in exact decoder order with zero-based DirectImportOrdinalV1 and duplicates retained; ordinary declaration contributions in exact decoded order under an ordinary ContributionOrdinalV1 namespace; extra generated declarations in a separate tagged ordinal namespace; extension-entry occurrences in exact module-local source order under ExtensionSourceOrdinalV1 with empty/duplicate occurrences retained; applied extension ranges as target-journal start plus checked length scoped to one committed root and extension, never stable content identity; and localized completeness/evidence rows in canonical scope order without lossy aggregation. Canonical ordinals, lengths, and counts are logical unsigned 64-bit values in frozen V1 encoding. Host usize, map iteration, worker assignment, allocation order, or pointer identity never enters canonical bytes; host conversions are checked before allocation.

ExtensionEntryIdV1 derives from the versioned entry domain, epoch, exact descriptor identity, and exact raw payload bytes. Contributor, source/target ordinal, applied range, branch, and Arc address are excluded. Equal descriptor/payload entries share the content ID while distinct occurrences remain identified by ModuleId plus ExtensionSourceOrdinalV1 plus ExtensionEntryIdV1. Target journal positions are scoped to ModuleProvenanceRootV1 and extension and may change after replay/restore/merge.

ModuleProvenanceRootV1 is the sole authoritative aggregate committed identity. It covers schema/epoch, primary module records, ordered import rows, contribution occurrences/ranges, artifact/evidence bindings, and completeness axes. Named subroots are implementation details. Forward/reverse indexes are deterministic derived projections, not competing primary roots; their storage representation does not affect the aggregate root. Atomic publication proves complete derivability and bidirectional coverage. Missing, extra, contradictory, or stale rows in validated state are InternalFault; persisted indexes are rebuildable caches, not truth. ModuleContributionManifestIdV1 identifies one input object, not a second committed-state root.

Completeness has independent capture (Missing, Partial, Complete), transparency (Opaque, Mixed, Understood), and evidence-derived authority capability axes, including inspection, exact replay, complete-inventory, fine-invalidation, and authoritative-cache capabilities. Complete plus Opaque and Partial plus Understood are both valid with limited authority. Localized boundaries remain addressable; decoding recomputes and rejects unjustified authority.

Canonical encoding equality is exact. Digest collision resistance is a cryptographic/statistical assumption, never injectivity proof. IDs and roots are locators and fast inequality checks; equality/idempotence/ownership use collision-disambiguated canonical values. Untrusted equal-digest unequal-value candidates are IdentityCollision; the same inconsistency inside validated state is InternalFault. Collision injection is bounded_model evidence.

This child freezes an in-memory immutable identity contract. It emits a W8 handoff of canonical manifest bytes/root, schema/epoch, collision-aware payload requirements, completeness/evidence facts, and deterministic index-rebuild rules. It does not claim Arc/index/snapshot durability, restart, or crash recovery.
acceptance_criteria:
All identity schema, encoding/tag, domain, resource, noninterference, golden, ordering/metamorphic, sharing, malformed, and named mutation requirements in the description are implemented and green. Named unit/property/model/mutation suites must cover every field/tag/class, golden bytes, registry uniqueness/completeness, canonical and order-sensitive laws, duplicate/range identity, cumulative pre-allocation limits, logical-root noninterference, Arc-backed payload boundary, unknown/future/truncated/trailing input, and all named omission mutants. The shared real decoded-fixture no-mock E2E remains owned by fln-amv.9.4 but is a mandatory downstream integration obligation before the parent closes: it must exercise this exact schema through positive, malformed/opaque/partial/missing, mutant, resource, and recovery paths and emit concise human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step/module/epoch/artifact/entry/range/completeness ids, canonical schema/domain/input/provenance/logical roots, expected/actual decode and authority grade, budgets/timings/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state. This child closes only with its exhaustive local evidence plus an explicit stable handoff contract to that E2E; fln-hash/fln-env tests, domain checks, fmt, clippy, UBS, and predecessor gates are green. No name/digest-only application claim, payload duplication, missing field/resource boundary, or independent E2E-complete claim satisfies closure.

Contribution identity is frozen by an explicit field table, not prose shorthand. Every regular declaration and extra-generated declaration row carries its contribution class, source ordinal within that class, exact Name, canonical declaration identity, contributor ModuleId, artifact/epoch binding, and capture grade. Every direct-import row is an ordered occurrence with source ordinal, exact target identity, all import flags, and duplicates retained. Every extension contribution carries a stable ExtensionEntryId that survives journal rebasing, plus its source journal ordinal/range, descriptor identity, canonical payload identity, contributor, artifact/epoch, and capture grade; EntryId and current journal ordinal are never aliases.

The canonical identity is exactly one aggregate ModuleProvenanceRootV1. Named topology, contribution, completeness, or artifact subdigests may be exposed for diagnosis, but forward/reverse indexes and subdigests are validated projections of the same committed records and never independent roots or parallel truths. A root mismatch between any projection and the aggregate is InternalFault. Canonical identity excludes Arc addresses, allocation order, map shape, host facts, and operational telemetry.

Completeness is represented on orthogonal axes: capture status (Complete, Partial, Missing), payload transparency (Understood, Opaque), and authority/evidence grade. Opaque may still be byte-complete; understood may still be partial. No enum collapse may promote either axis. Cacheability and fine-invalidation authority are derived only from the full axis tuple plus request profile.

Hash collision resistance is a HYPOTHESIS of the selected digest, not an injectivity proof. The schema's bounded collision seam supplies bounded_model evidence that equal digest plus unequal canonical identity remains distinguishable and is handed to atomic application as a collision candidate; it must not claim a practical cryptographic collision was found. Stable IDs and ranges are checked by exact canonical fields whenever equality or ownership is load-bearing.

Local closure requires exhaustive schema/domain/golden/resource/model/mutation proof and a versioned handoff manifest naming every field, axis, stable-ID rule, root projection, and downstream collision obligation. The real decoded-fixture lane is required only before the outer fln-amv.9 epic closes. Additional named mutants swap declaration classes or source ordinals, alias EntryId to mutable journal ordinal, collapse capture with transparency, omit an index projection from root validation, make a subdigest authoritative, or admit Arc/allocation identity; each must be killed by a targeted assertion and recorded in strict versioned evidence.

Golden/property suites cover the complete normative V1 field table; every tag; zero, one, maximum, and overflow ordinal/count; duplicate imports/entries; ordinary-versus-extra namespace separation; range gap/overlap; and insertion/thread permutations preserving ordered source input. Mutants swap ordinal namespaces, derive order from a map, collapse duplicate occurrences, include target journal position in ExtensionEntryIdV1, omit a completeness axis, grant authority, include index layout or Arc address in the aggregate root, or treat digest equality as value equality.

Root tests prove every authoritative fact changes canonical manifest bytes and normally the root, while index/allocation/sharing layout alone does not. Rebuilding both indexes from primary records yields exact query equivalence and bidirectional coverage. Exercise every valid/invalid capture-transparency-authority tuple, localized mixed boundary, monotone evidence fact, malformed escalation, and pre-allocation resource limit.

The stable consumer handoff includes schema/domain versions, canonical/root vectors, ordinal rules, completeness tables, collision outcomes, and deterministic index reconstruction. Local closure earns no real decoded-fixture or durable-restart claim; fln-amv.9.4 and the outer fln-amv.9 own real integration.

Initial scope is limited to the dedicated hash domain, immutable contribution schema, canonical provenance root, resource validation, and exhaustive schema/noninterference tests; downstream atomic application, invalidation, lifecycle, and real fixtures remain in their dependent beads.

comments.text: Schema implementation and acceptance audit are complete within this bead's scope: canonical v1 layout and all enum tags are explicit; cumulative decode budgets report configured limits and total use before allocation; 15 focused schema tests and all 86 fln-env tests pass; 23 independently valid field-family variants round-trip with distinct roots; ordered rows/ranges, canonical sets, seven named omission mutants, logical-root noninterference, resource boundaries, malformed cases, sharing, and the schedule matrix are covered. fln-env all-target clippy and fln-hash library clippy pass with warnings denied; targeted rustfmt/diff checks pass; UBS reports zero critical findings. Closure is intentionally held because the mandatory full fln-hash test target and workspace-wide fmt check currently fail in unrelated concurrently edited files outside this bead; I made no changes in those files.

comments.text: Plan-space handoff for the active owner: acceptance/design were hardened without touching implementation files. Before closure, reconcile the live schema with the generated V1 field/ordinal table, stable content EntryId plus distinct occurrence/target positions, one aggregate ModuleProvenanceRootV1 with derived indexes, independent capture/transparency/authority axes, bounded-model collision claim, local .9.3 handoff, and W8 in-memory/durable boundary. This is additive and preserves every original schema/golden/resource/mutation obligation.

title: W3: fln-bignum — owned kernel-grade bignum, ABI-layout limbs, PG-K gate
description:
Implement fln-bignum (§8.4): kernel-grade arbitrary precision replacing GMP — limb-based magnitude representation matching the ABI bignum-object layout (Marrow mpz-shim is a VIEW, not a conversion), schoolbook/Karatsuba/Toom multiplication with thresholds tuned on the KERNEL-REDUCTION corpus (not synthetic benchmarks), Knuth-D division, binary/Lehmer gcd, exact decimal parsing/printing for literals. Constant-time-ness explicitly NOT claimed (this is a prover, not a KMS).

Why owned: GMP sits under every literal in the Reference and inside kernel reduction itself (§1.10) — an unauditable C dependency in the most trusted position. The kernel accelerated Nat operation set (add/sub/mul/div/mod/pow/gcd/comparisons/bitwise per the pin) routes through fln-bignum so decide-class proofs keep the same asymptotics. The crate stays in-repo rather than the suite (kernel-adjacent code answers to the kernel covenant; revisit trigger recorded if a second suite member needs bigints — §2.11).

Acceptance: PG-K gate — kernel-reduction operation mix <= 1.15x the GMP-backed Reference kernel on the arithmetic-heavy fixture set (parity on the small operands that dominate; bounded, PUBLISHED gap on giants); ABI-layout view verified against C4 probes. Tests: differential vectors vs known-good results across the full operation set incl. boundary limb counts; parsing/printing round-trips vs Reference outputs; threshold-tuning harness checked in with the corpus profile it tuned against.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
fln-bignum is a forbid-unsafe owned limb implementation whose representation supplies a zero-conversion ABI-layout view to Marrow. It implements exact signed/unsigned semantics, normalization, comparisons, add/sub/mul with schoolbook/Karatsuba/Toom thresholds, Knuth-D division, binary/Lehmer gcd, pow, mod, bitwise/shifts, and decimal parse/print for the complete pinned accelerated Nat/literal operation set, including zero, sign, carry/borrow, limb and giant boundaries. Thresholds are selected from a checked-in kernel-reduction corpus profile rather than synthetic-only microbenchmarks and cannot affect results. Unit/property/model/differential/metamorphic/mutation tests compare independent known-good/reference vectors across every operation and boundary, round-trip exact Reference text, and cover alias/view identity, budgets, cancellation, overflow/size refusal, and recovery; named carry, normalization, division, threshold, sign, ABI-view and parsing mutants are killed. Real no-mock kernel/ABI E2E executes arithmetic-heavy declarations and C4 object probes, plants malformed/over-budget cases, restores, and retains strict operands/results/limbs/layout/roots/resource bundles across platforms. PG-K shows the registered kernel-reduction operation mix no more than 1.15 times the GMP-backed Reference, with raw same-machine samples, small-operand parity, published giant gap and honest claim state; no constant-time claim is made. Dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, differential/fuzz/mutation/platform/performance lanes pass. No GMP or other external bignum, conversion disguised as an ABI view, incomplete accelerated operation set, benchmark-only correctness, silent giant gap, synthetic-only threshold tuning, or deferred listed behavior satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — the owned numeric kernel covers division by zero, exclusion of negative zero, signed bitwise and shift semantics, carry/borrow aliasing, canonical normalization, allocation and algorithm threshold boundaries, and equivalence on both sides of every representation crossover. Independent model oracles and fixtures must not be generated through the optimized implementation under test. Mutants at sign, zero, shift, alias, normalization, and threshold boundaries each require discriminating failures.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative output is strict schema-versioned canonical semantic NDJSON containing normalized inputs, roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, wall time, temporary paths, cache/performance facts, and scheduler details live only in a separately linked bounded telemetry stream and cannot enter authoritative digests. Validators reject merged streams, unknown versions, extra or missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, or telemetry-root drift. Productive 1/8/32 runs prove nonempty completed work and deterministic reduction rather than thread-count labels.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Arithmetic-core bootstrap started by session agent (claim blocked; ABI-limb layout half pends 53v contracts). Surface: crates/fln-bignum, scripts/extract/gen_bignum_vectors.py.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Reviewer note on the live uncommitted snapshot, no edits: scripts/e2e/bignum_vectors.sh cannot currently seed its advertised division-by-zero mutant. The seeder replaces the first BigNat::from_u64(0) occurrence, but nat.rs contains no such occurrence; div_rem returns BigNat::zero instead. Because the Python seeder runs while shell errexit is active, its no-op exit also terminates the script before mutation_rc is captured or the seeded_mutant failure event is emitted. The header additionally says truncated-subtraction mutation while the implemented scenario targets division by zero. Use an exact semantically anchored replacement with an asserted single match and capture the seeder status under the same explicit error-handling pattern as other steps. This is an E2E correctness finding, not a claim against the arithmetic implementation.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Second reviewer finding on the live snapshot: the module-level statement that no code path panics on any input and the bead requirement for typed resource exhaustion are not yet true. BigNat::shl accepts an arbitrary u64 count, converts the limb count to usize, then allocates that many zero limbs directly; a huge count can trigger capacity or allocation failure rather than a typed resource outcome, and the usize conversion is not portable to wasm32. Decimal parsing, pow, and other growth paths likewise expose infallible allocation APIs with no budget or cancellation channel. This is expected to remain open during bootstrap, but the public contract must be weakened now or, preferably, the kernel-facing growth operations must use checked sizing plus budget-aware fallible allocation and typed outcomes before the bead can close.

This was run before a stable commit and does not cover PG-K, ABI layout, large-algorithm thresholds, resource/cancellation outcomes, or the shared E2E. The future mutation-seed no-op branch still runs under shell errexit, so source drift can exit before its typed NDJSON failure record unless status capture is made explicit.

title: W12: Distribution & Epochs — releases, elan layout, epoch ratchet, Witness matrix
description:
Workstream W12 "Distribution & Epochs" (§22.5, §13.6, §17.1): shipping the sovereign toolchain and keeping it current against a monthly upstream without ever lying about parity.

Scope: static binaries per platform (lean, leanc, lake, fln) with the elan-compatible release layout so a lean-toolchain line can name a FrankenLean toolchain and everything downstream just works; the install script; version-line parse compatibility with a machine-readable implementation field + fln identity --json (compatibility never becomes deception, §17.1); reproducible releases (built twice in isolated builders and byte-compared; --reproducible certified matrix; windows bit-certification as a separate declared decision, D17); the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (the mechanical 8 steps of §22.5: fetch+hash, regenerate censuses on every platform, run corpus differentials, machine-diff all public surfaces, regenerate facade stubs + open migration beads per moved symbol, close-or-classify every divergence — unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim, run unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs, publish capsule + evidence bundle + migration report); side-by-side epoch installs; the public lag dashboard (target: new stable reaches R3 in four weeks, R4 next cycle); the Witness claim-to-proof matrix + documentation CI (§20.4: docs wording may never exceed evidence state; a benchmark is OBSERVED, never PROVEN).

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
acceptance_criteria:
W12 closes only when FrankenLean ships and can advance epochs without overstating compatibility. Static lean, leanc, lake, and fln binaries install in the exact elan-compatible layout; lean-toolchain selection, version-line parsing, help/exit/robot behavior, and the fln multiplexer match their contracts while machine-readable identity explicitly names FrankenLean. The embeddable fln crate exposes the same engine through capability-first typed APIs with cancellation, budgets, receipts, and no alternate semantics. Installers verify platform, signatures and hashes, perform atomic versioned placement, preserve side-by-side epochs, support rollback/uninstall without deleting user projects, and work air-gapped from a release bundle. Releases are built twice in isolated builders and byte-compared across the certified matrix; Windows functional versus bit-certification status is explicit. The epoch ratchet performs all eight mechanical steps: fetch/hash; regenerate platform censuses/contracts; run Corpus differentials; machine-diff every public surface; regenerate facade rows and open migration beads; close or classify every divergence; run unchanged downstream corpora under both epochs; publish capsule, evidence, migration report, and lag dashboard. Migration autopilot proposes audited reversible repairs with explicit apply and kernel revalidation. The Witness matrix and docs CI bind each sentence/number to typed current evidence, automatically block or demote stale wording, and never convert TARGETED/OBSERVED into PROVEN. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover installers, manifests, layouts, identity/version negotiation, front doors, library API, ratchet states, diff/classification, migrations, claims/docs, cancellation/resources, and rollback; named stale-claim, wrong-epoch, partial-install, nonreproducible, hidden-fallback, and unclassified-divergence mutants are killed. Real no-mock release E2E installs cold on every declared platform, selects via elan, builds/checks/runs a real project through CLI/library/MCP, advances a fixture epoch, exercises failure/rollback/recovery, and validates strict signed bundles. All W12 children plus W10/W11, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, installer/reproducibility/platform/security/migration/corpus lanes are green before G6. No compatibility deception, in-place destructive upgrade, unclassified divergence, mutable published release, hidden network/tool dependency, mock-only installer, stale claim, sampled epoch proof, or deferred listed W12 feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — consume the certified-platform decision, split distribution/claim/epoch joins, and late Palimpsest producer-adoption evidence. Define target-neutral semantic roots, per-target reproducible unsigned payloads, and signed operational envelopes separately. Preserve every W11 release-ready product in the final manifest and installed-product matrix. G6 closes only on exact child ids, platform cells, evidence roots, no-mock hosts, and typed remaining exclusions.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W1: fln-core — names, levels, Expr inventory, options, positions
description:
Implement fln-core (§21, §1.1): the shared vocabulary every crate compiles against. Names (hierarchical, with macro-scope decoration hooks for Vellum), universe levels (zero/succ/max/imax/param/mvar with the normalization laws incl. imax u 0 = 0 — Prop impredicativity depends on it), the kernel expression inventory (bvar de Bruijn, fvar, mvar, sort, const+levels, app, lam, forallE, letE, lit Nat/String, mdata, proj) with the Reference-observable accessories: cached hashes, loose-bvar ranges, has-fvar/has-mvar flags (metaprograms and fast traversal pruning read these — they are API, not internals). Options model with the canonical resource limits (maxHeartbeats 200000 in thousand-units, maxRecDepth 512) and the newer resource-limit surface. Byte positions in UTF-8 exactly as upstream computes them (wire-visible through diagnostics and LSP).

Distinct newtypes per semantic kind from day one (DeclNameId, ExprNodeId, BinderDepth, ReductionFuel, EnvCommitId — §8.2b): no integer is accepted as two semantic kinds anywhere in the workspace.

Acceptance: constructor inventory + flag semantics verified against C0 micro-fixtures derived from the pin; hashing observables match the Reference on the fixture corpus. Tests: property tests for level normalization laws; flag-computation unit tests; fixture diff harness wired into fln-conformance.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: fln-core Name lifecycle and structural operations must be stack-safe
description: Name is an unbounded persistent parent chain backed by Arc nodes. The current automatic destructor and the derived structural PartialEq, Eq, Hash, and Debug implementations recurse through every parent; Ord, quick_cmp_aux, and to_display_string also recurse. A deeply nested Name can therefore exhaust the call stack during ordinary cleanup, comparison, hashing, diagnostics, or rendering. It can also be embedded as a leaf payload in Expr and Level, so those types are not fully depth-independent merely because their own node drains are iterative. Replace every input-depth recursive Name lifecycle and structural traversal with safe iterative logic while preserving the pinned Lean observable hash, prefix-first Ord, quickCmp ordering, rendering, structural sharing, Arc reference counts, and shallow behavior. No semantic depth cap, larger-stack workaround, leak, unsafe code, or hidden fallback is acceptable. Add deterministic constructor-complete tests for deep string, numeric, mixed, shared-prefix, cloned-root, unequal-prefix, overflow-component, diagnostic, and cleanup cases; exact shallow old-versus-new behavior vectors; randomized clone/drop orders; and bounded-stack real-path verification with clean recovery. Coordinate with franken_lean-canon-stack-safe-drop-6gy because Name payloads occur inside Expr and Level and canonical Name writing is already being made iterative there.
