title: W9: Lantern wire + daemon — byte-compatible LSP dialect, one shared import heap
description:
Implement the Lantern wire dialect + daemon topology (§14.1, §14.2): LSP with the $/lean/* extensions — plainGoal, plainTermGoal, the rpc/* session protocol (connect/call/release, reference-holding semantics), waitForDiagnostics, semantic tokens with the Reference legend, diagnostics shapes, widget delivery (client executes JS, wire-compatible pass-through) — BYTE-compatible so vscode-lean4 and every other client connects to lean --server and cannot tell.

Topology: the watchdog/worker PROCESS model is not replicated — only its observable behavior is. One daemon, all open files, all projects on a toolchain epoch, one shared immutable import heap (§7.5): the ~60 s x N-workers x GBs x N multiplication becomes once-per-daemon (PG-4: warm attach <= 2 s p50; PG-6: steady RSS serving >= 8 open mathlib files <= 1.3x ONE Reference worker). Per-file fault isolation becomes per-session region isolation: a crashing elaboration cancels an asupersync region, not a process; the supervisor restarts the session with the same client-visible semantics as a worker restart (BN-04). Transport is asupersync plumbing; zero protocol code written here.

Acceptance: vscode-lean4 connects unmodified and passes the smoke matrix basics; recorded-session replays (C5) diff clean at the wire with registered nondeterminism classes normalized; PG-4/PG-6 trajectory measured. Tests: wire-level golden transcripts; rpc session-lifetime fixtures (reference holding/release); region-crash isolation drills (one session dies, others unaffected, client sees worker-restart semantics).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this W9 wire bead explicitly owns OQ-7. Freeze widget fidelity tiers outside VS Code, the wire-compatible pass-through baseline, the capability and isolation boundary for any franken_node host, supported widget fallback behavior, version negotiation, and the evidence threshold for graduating the exploratory host. fln-in1 consumes this decision and may not silently reinterpret widget semantics.

Dependency topology: Lantern consumes the G4-complete language/toolchain, Ledger records/CAS, FL-INV-05 invalidation, real Lake project state, compacted shared regions, scheduler, and typed diagnostics. It does not require W8 cache federation, epoch bridges, or distributed elaboration to prove the single-daemon user experience. Those full-W8 features remain G6 blockers. This bead must use the same core records and roots that full W8 later federates; no daemon-only cache or project truth is allowed.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement the pinned LSP and Lean extension wire contract for plainGoal/plainTermGoal, rpc connect/call/release with reference lifetimes, waitForDiagnostics, semantic-token legend/data, diagnostic shapes, widget pass-through, and lean --server framing. Registered nondeterminism may normalize only specified fields; all other bytes, error codes, and lifetimes match C5 transcripts.
2. Serve all files/projects for one toolchain epoch from one daemon and shared immutable import heap. Per-session asupersync regions isolate failure; a failed elaboration cancels/restarts only its session while other sessions remain usable and the affected client observes Reference-compatible worker-restart semantics documented by BN-04. No protocol logic is delegated to external/upstream code.
3. Named unit/property/golden/model/mutation suites cover message framing and limits, every extension method, rpc reference retain/release and invalid ids, semantic tokens, diagnostics/widgets, multi-project routing, shared-import identity, cancellation, malformed/adversarial messages without panic, and session crash/restart. Cross-session cancellation, leaked reference, skipped release, stale response, and unbounded-frame mutants must be killed.
4. A shared no-mock E2E lane connects unmodified vscode-lean4 and another real client to lean --server, exercises the full smoke matrix and recorded C5 sessions, opens at least eight mathlib files, crashes one session region, proves others remain live, and verifies reconnection/recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/client/session/project/document/request/rpc-ref/region ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate/Parity rows, redacted transport/argv/cwd, pins/build/epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical import/message/snapshot roots, normalized and raw transcript artifacts, expected/actual wire/status, timings/RSS/resources, cleanup, and final state.
5. Reproducible performance evidence on the declared warm corpus/machine shows attach no more than 2 s p50 and steady RSS for at least eight open mathlib files no more than 1.3 times one pinned Reference worker; distributions, samples, and normalization policy are archived.
6. CI archives golden transcripts, protocol schemas, editor recordings, crash/recovery bundles, mutant outcomes, performance data, and evidence manifests. Mock clients or in-memory transports may support units but cannot close wire compatibility, region isolation, PG-4, or PG-6.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — resolve daemon epoch topology as one immutable heap per epoch capsule with explicit request routing/refusal and no cross-capsule mutable state sharing. Every extracted LSP and $/lean row is implemented or typed unsupported; no selected method list. Restore PG-4 cold attach at no more than 1.2 times the Reference baseline under the declared host/profile. Tests cover concurrent epochs, stale document routing, capsule teardown, cancellation, recovery, and census-total wire behavior.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. title: W5: native_decide Tier 1 — replay receipts, trust-surface provenance
description:
Implement native_decide Tier 1 — replay receipts (§11.6): support native_decide with the Reference semantics and the Reference caveat stated LOUDER (it extends trust to Golem/Iron), plus the first stage of the honest repair: every reflection-class evaluation emits a replay receipt — function-entry digests over the registered reflection set, branch decisions, literal-arithmetic transcripts — recorded against term identities, replayable by Crucible at a stated cost multiple. Certified profiles verify always; standard samples by policy.

Trust bookkeeping shipped with it: provenance marks every native_decide-dependent theorem; fln audit --tcb lists a project trust surface (axioms, native_decide sites, plugins, facade classes); fln why-trusts <decl> walks the transitive trust cone (§15.6 seam — the Palimpsest query lands in W9, but the provenance emission lands here). Tier 2 (trace-guided kernel replay as certified-path default, G6) and Tier 3 (verified evaluator, research) are explicitly OUT of this bead — each tier stands alone and the trust ledger records which tier each build achieved, because "we no longer trust the compiler" is a claim with a scope.

Acceptance: receipts emitted for the reflection corpus; Crucible replay verifies them at the stated cost multiple; sampling policy configurable and journaled; the trust ledger row per build records the tier. Tests: receipt round-trip fixtures; a tampered-receipt fixture must fail verification; provenance-marking completeness on a corpus with known native_decide sites.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Match pinned native_decide semantics while explicitly recording that Tier 1 extends trust to Golem/Iron. Every reflection-class evaluation emits a versioned receipt binding term/environment identities, registered reflection-set entry digests, function-entry digests, branch decisions, literal-arithmetic transcripts, build/epoch/profile, and resulting value/proof identity.
2. Crucible can deterministically replay each receipt at a measured stated cost multiple. Unknown, malformed, tampered, mismatched-term/build, incomplete, or over-budget receipts fail verification without admitting a theorem; certified profiles verify every receipt and standard profiles apply a configurable deterministic sampling policy whose exact decision is journaled.
3. Every theorem depending on Tier 1 is provenance-marked. Build trust ledgers record achieved tier per site/build; fln audit --tcb lists axioms, native_decide sites, plugins, and facade classes, and emitted edges support why-trusts transitive queries. Tier 2/3 claims are explicitly out of scope and cannot be inferred from Tier 1 evidence.
4. Named unit/property/differential/mutation suites cover receipt encode/decode, replay, sampling determinism/configuration, reflection-set changes, branch/arithmetic transcripts, audit aggregation, trust-cone completeness, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and failure atomicity. Tamper-accept, missing-provenance, skipped-certified-replay, biased-sampling, and false-tier mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane runs real reflection/native_decide corpus declarations on Golem, emits and replays receipts through Crucible, compares observable semantics with the pinned Reference, audits the resulting project trust surface, walks a known trust cone, rejects a tampered receipt, and then proves clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/build/declaration/term/receipt/sample/trust-edge ids, bead/claim/invariant/Parity rows, redacted argv/cwd, pin/build/epoch/mode/profile/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical term/environment/reflection/receipt/proof roots, tier and expected/actual replay decisions, measured cost, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives corpus receipts, replay traces, sampling manifests, trust ledgers/cones, mutant outcomes, cost distributions, and E2E evidence. Wording and close evidence must preserve the compiler-trust caveat and may not claim Tier 2 or compiler-independent trust.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — this bead owns receipt and provenance emission plus production Golem replay. Final audit --tcb and why-trusts user presentation belongs to downstream CLI/Palimpsest owners. Receipt rows bind mode/profile, engine versions, input and environment roots, effects, typed outcomes, and replay authority; UI absence cannot block producer closure and UI output cannot grant authority.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical authoritative NDJSON contains normalized semantic inputs, roots, decisions, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, time, paths, cache/performance, and scheduler facts are linked bounded telemetry only. Independent validators reject unknown schema, extra/missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, telemetry in semantic hashes, and final-state mismatch. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves nonempty completed work and canonical reduction.

title: W10/W11: WASM Judge packaging contract — resolve OQ-12 before browser integration
description:
Resolve plan OQ-12 in its named W10 architecture phase before the W11 terminal/browser integration freezes packaging. Define the WASM Judge release boundary: kernel plus independent checker core, capsule decoder and receipt/transparency verification, and the browser capsule experience. The decision may split these into integrity-bound artifacts, but it may not delete the plan browser capability: users must still be able to verify a sealed capsule in a stock browser, inspect declaration/dependency results, and obtain a verifiable receipt without a network service.

Freeze artifact composition and TCB accounting; size, startup, verification-latency, and memory budgets; supported browser/WASM feature matrix; deterministic native-versus-WASM vectors; offline loading and content integrity; CSP and JS-host boundaries; capsule streaming and adversarial resource limits; version negotiation; accessibility and failure rendering; and whether the browser shell is embedded, separately signed, or both. Full browser elaboration remains tier-2-if-ever and must not inflate the Judge tier-1 claim.

## Acceptance Criteria

A versioned packaging contract and decision-log row resolve both OQ-12 alternatives without losing the capsule-browser deliverable. Every shipped artifact has an explicit hash/signature relationship, version/epoch compatibility rule, TCB inclusion statement, resource envelope, update/rollback story, and offline verification procedure. Measured prototypes on the declared browser matrix establish honest size/startup/verification/memory baselines using a real sealed mathlib-slice capsule and the same vectors as native K1 plus fln-checker.

Named deterministic unit/property/mutation tests cover package manifests, version negotiation, content-integrity binding, capsule streaming, unknown versions, truncated/tampered inputs, resource exhaustion, and native/WASM vector identity. A real no-mock browser E2E script runs online-disabled in each supported engine, verifies a good capsule, rejects byte flips and mismatched UI/core assets, survives refresh/retry without false success, and emits detailed human plus bounded schema-versioned NDJSON logs with browser/platform/epoch/build hashes, exact scenario and step ids, input/output hashes, timings/resources, expected versus actual, first divergence, terminal status, cleanup, and artifact references. A skipped browser or mock-only run cannot satisfy support or performance claims.
acceptance_criteria:
OQ-12 is resolved by a versioned reviewed packaging contract and decision-log row that preserves the stock-browser sealed-capsule deliverable while separating full browser elaboration as tier 2 if ever. The contract freezes core/UI artifact composition, hash/signature relationships, epoch/version negotiation, TCB inclusion, CSP and JS-host authority, offline loading, update/rollback, accessibility/failure rendering, streaming, adversarial limits, and explicit size/startup/verification-memory budgets for every supported browser/WASM feature tier. Native K1 plus fln-checker and WASM use the same deterministic vectors and real sealed mathlib-slice capsule; prototypes record honest raw baselines on the declared browser matrix. Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover manifests, integrity binding, unknown/mismatched versions, truncated/tampered capsules, UI/core mismatch, streaming chunk boundaries, resource exhaustion, refresh/retry, native/WASM identity, and rollback; named signature/hash/version/resource/fallback mutants are killed. A real no-mock online-disabled browser E2E in each supported engine verifies a good capsule, rejects byte flips and mismatched assets without false success, retries cleanly, and retains detailed independently validated evidence with browser/platform/epoch/build hashes, timings/resources, expected/actual results, cleanup, and final state. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, browser/security/accessibility/mutation/fault lanes pass. No skipped browser presented as supported, mock-only run, unsigned asset coupling, network service requirement, core-only artifact presented as the browser Judge, tier-2 elaboration inflation of tier-1 verification, unresolved alternative, or deferred listed feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — keep the feasibility and packaging prototype distinct from final browser implementation while retaining a real offline-browser probe, deterministic bundle construction, capability restrictions, integrity roots, failure/recovery evidence, and independent validation. Prototype success cannot promote final C7/browser compatibility rows or substitute for installed-product testing.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes: MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead own acceptance): unit/property/mutation coverage must exercise every package, integrity, version, streaming, resource, determinism, accessibility, and failure-atomicity rule. Real no-mock browser E2E coverage must include good, tampered, mismatched-asset, resource-exhaustion, offline, refresh, and recovery paths on the declared support matrix. Emit concise human logs and bounded schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step, bead/claim/invariant/gate, exact redacted invocation and origin, epoch/mode/profile/browser/platform/build hashes, input and artifact hashes, expected versus actual, monotonic timings, resources/budgets, terminal status, console/stdout/stderr references, first divergence, cleanup and asserted final state. Archive the contract, rejected alternatives, fixtures, raw measurements, logs, and reproducible commands; skips are typed limitations and cannot satisfy the skipped support or performance claim.

title: W11: Bloodhound — verified search, premise retrieval, proof repair, counterfactuals
description:
Implement Bloodhound (fln-hound, §16.1): the candidate-production system under one law — every candidate is untrusted text/terms until Athanor elaborates and Crucible accepts it (ranking is UX, proofs are proofs; determinism class D3, never touching the trust story).

Scope: in-toolchain hybrid search (frankensearch two-tier: lexical + semantic, bundled embedder, no network/Python/service) over statements, names, docstrings, simp/instance membership, indexed INCREMENTALLY at Ledger commit; exact?/apply?-class premise retrieval as a candidate generator verified by actual elaboration; Loogle-style structural patterns on a type-shape fingerprint lane; CandidateCards (source/term, provenance + retrieval channels, required imports/instances, rank components, elaboration outcome, kernel outcome, fragility signals, replay identity) in three grades — initial (low-latency lexical/type-head/local-context), refined (structural unification, graph ranking over Palimpsest cones, prior-use paths, optional learned rerank), verified (kernel-accepted under the exact snapshot); a failed refinement never withdraws a valid initial result.

Proof repair (the migration autopilot engine): diff old/new environment + extension graphs, find the earliest failed constraint cell, re-elaborate with holes around the blame slice, search replacements (renames, binder-order shifts, bridge lemmas, instance-priority shifts, simp-set drift, termination adjustments via TerminationCards, import moves), validate each minimal patch through the FULL pipeline, rank by semantic change size + fragility delta, emit a patch transaction with rollback. Counterfactual analysis (replay under altered policy on branch-local snapshots) and minimization (declared objective vectors, trade-offs shown). Learned components (frankentorch tier): local-first, identity on every card, candidates-only, removal leaves a complete deterministic baseline.

Acceptance: search quality measured on a query corpus; repair success rate measured on a broken-proof corpus derived from real mathlib migrations; every repair kernel-validated. Tests: index incrementality (commit -> queryable within the latency budget); candidate-grade monotonicity fixtures; repair rollback drills; learned-tier removal leaves baseline green.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Incrementally index Ledger commits with local-only frankensearch lexical and bundled-semantic lanes over statements, names, docstrings, simp/instance membership, type-shape fingerprints, contexts, and provenance. No Python, network service, or learned component is required for a complete deterministic baseline.
2. CandidateCards carry source/term, retrieval channels and rank components, required imports/instances, snapshot/epoch/provenance, elaboration and kernel outcomes, fragility signals, replay identity, and initial/refined/verified grade. Refinement may improve/rerank but never withdraw a valid initial result; verified means elaborated and kernel-accepted under the exact snapshot. Rankings remain untrusted UX data.
3. exact?/apply?-class retrieval, structural search, and optional learned reranking only propose candidates. Removing the learned tier leaves baseline results valid. Counterfactuals execute on branch-local snapshots with declared policy changes and minimization reports objective vectors/tradeoffs rather than a hidden scalar.
4. Proof repair finds the earliest failed constraint/blame slice, searches every plan-listed repair class, validates minimal patch transactions through the full elaboration/kernel pipeline, ranks semantic-size and fragility deltas, and carries explicit preconditions plus rollback. It never edits without explicit apply authority.
5. Named unit/property/metamorphic/differential/mutation suites cover commit-to-query incrementality, deletion/epoch invalidation, rank determinism, grade monotonicity, candidate replay, learned-tier removal, counterfactual isolation, each repair class, kernel validation, minimization, rollback, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and failure atomicity. Unverified-label, stale-index, withdrawn-initial, learned-required, and unchecked-repair mutants must be killed.
6. A shared no-mock E2E lane indexes a real mathlib slice, runs the declared query corpus, verifies candidates in exact snapshots, commits an edit and measures query visibility, disables learned ranking, repairs real migration-derived broken proofs with rollback and explicit apply, runs a counterfactual, plants a bogus repair, and confirms rejection/recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/query/index-commit/card/snapshot/repair/patch/objective ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/budget facts, canonical corpus/index/environment/candidate/patch/proof roots, channels/ranks/grades and expected/actual outcomes, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
7. Reproducible evidence publishes retrieval metrics/latency on the named query corpus and repair success/quality on the real broken-proof corpus with distributions and exclusions. CI archives indexes, cards, repairs/rollbacks, learned-removal proof, mutant outcomes, benchmarks, and E2E manifests; rankings can never satisfy proof claims.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — ranking is deterministic only within a declared baseline or inference profile. D3 learned variation cannot alter candidate validity, membership authority, proof admission, or public proof claims. Every candidate is independently validated; unavailable models fall back to a declared deterministic profile with typed evidence. Tests cover rank ties, model drift, profile mismatch, cancellation, stale indexes, invalid candidates, and reproducible baseline output.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W4: Vellum — parser, Syntax/SourceInfo, hygiene, macro transactions
description:
Workstream W4 "Vellum" (§9): the parser and macro engine — the reimplementation graveyard, visited early with flowers (spike G0-4).

Scope: token tables + Pratt-precedence engine with the Reference category/leading/trailing model; Syntax trees with identical node kinds, atom/ident/node structure, SourceInfo discipline (original vs synthetic, whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions — wire-visible, therefore conformance surface); the full extensibility contract (parsing interleaves with elaboration; commands install syntax/macros visible immediately; precedence interactions match). Internals: lossless rope + persistent green tree with red views; origin sets per node projected to the single-primary-span convention at the wire; GrammarEpoch content-hashing of the active grammar state with memoization keyed on (position, category, precedence, epoch); typed parser-registration effect summaries (an OpaqueParserEffect forces the conservative suffix barrier). Hygiene: macro scopes exactly as the Reference implements them; generated identifiers derive from stable expansion paths, never thread timing; macro expansion as a memoizable transaction with declared capability reads (§9.2). Sound-mode improvements (§9.3): specified error recovery (recovery may add diagnostics, never change acceptance; RecoveredSyntax law), sub-command incremental relex/reparse, provenance into Palimpsest.

Exit: hygiene fidelity rig green on the macro-heavy corpus; syntax trees, positions, and hygiene observables byte-agree with the Reference across C0-C2 fixtures.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
acceptance_criteria:
W4 closes only when Vellum parses and expands the full pinned source-language surface with Reference-equivalent tokens, categories, precedence, leading/trailing parser behavior, syntax node kinds, atoms/idents, macro scopes, generated names, and SourceInfo including original/synthetic spans, whitespace attachment, and UTF-8 byte positions. Parsing and elaboration interleave correctly: newly declared syntax, macros, and parsers become visible at the exact command boundary. Lossless ropes/green trees and red views preserve source bytes; origin sets project deterministically to wire-visible spans. GrammarEpoch is a content hash of all active grammar state; memoization keys position/category/precedence/epoch and invalidates exactly; typed parser-effect summaries enforce conservative suffix barriers for opaque effects. Macro expansion is a capability-declared transaction with stable expansion-path identities independent of thread timing. Recovery produces typed RecoveredSyntax that may add diagnostics but never changes acceptance, and sub-command incremental lex/parse remains observationally identical to a clean parse. Unit/property/model/golden suites exhaust precedence, Unicode/invalid UTF-8 boundaries, trivia, synthetic positions, extensibility, hygiene capture/avoidance, epoch invalidation, transaction rollback, cancellation/resource limits, and recovery equivalence; named mutants kill wrong associativity, byte/char confusion, stale grammar reuse, unstable names, leaked macro effects, and acceptance-changing recovery. The real no-mock C0-C2 macro-heavy E2E compares complete syntax trees, positions, diagnostics, generated identifiers, macro results, incremental edits, planted divergence, exact restoration, and recovery against the Reference through strict bundles and 1/8/32-thread schedules. All W4 children, G0-4 evidence, scripts/check.sh, full cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, parser/macro differential, fuzz, mutation, fault, and determinism lanes are green. No parser subset presented as full, source-normalizing tree, hosted Lean parser/macro fallback, debug-text identity, mock-only oracle, silent recovery acceptance drift, or deferred listed W4 feature satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — W4 closes native parser, syntax, source information, hygiene, macro contracts, dynamic registration, and production seams. G3 remains the owner of arbitrary Lean-defined macro/elaborator execution on Golem against the Native Mirror. The terminal join names exact child ids, contract roots, tests, mutants, and evidence rows rather than accepting prose-only completion.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical authoritative NDJSON contains normalized semantic inputs, roots, decisions, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, time, paths, cache/performance, and scheduler facts are linked bounded telemetry only. Independent validators reject unknown schema, extra/missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, telemetry in semantic hashes, and final-state mismatch. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves nonempty completed work and canonical reduction.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: G0-10: Dependency-closure audit + authoritative SUITE.lock
description:
## Description

Own the authoritative SUITE.lock, the complete governed dependency-closure model, reviewed allowlists, lock-only build enforcement, and closure-policy evidence for D1/R16. This bead does not own ABI/OLEAN/ILEAN/extern/builtin extraction or drift generation; franken_lean-53v owns that feature. G0/W1 joins both evidence bundles, so no second extractor or contradictory drift path exists.

Compute separate runtime, build, dev, test, bench, fuzz, proc-macro-host, and tool closures across every certified or functionally supported host/target triple and reviewed feature/profile combination. Cargo.lock membership is necessary but insufficient: resolved features, target predicates, host/target split, build scripts, proc macros, native links, checksums/source/tree, suite package-to-repository mapping, canonical path, exact commit, license, unsafe review, reason, owner, and upgrade policy must all agree bidirectionally with manifests, pinned trees, WORKSPACE_GRAPH, and SUITE.lock.

SUITE.lock binds exact FrankenSuite commits/trees/checksums, the Rust nightly and target-feature sets, Reference epoch/commit/tree, and Corpus commit in one ceremony. --locked alone is not authority. A build is lock-authoritative only after an independent verifier succeeds under the exact toolchain, host/target, feature/profile, suite-path, and closure configuration. Sources are admitted only by the closed D1 allowlist; source syntax alone is not the policy decision.

Malformed or policy-violating complete inputs produce a typed complete violation. Missing checkouts or unverifiable metadata produce Inconclusive. Partial traversal, cancellation, resource exhaustion, graph/root drift, ambiguous identity, or stale evidence can never yield compliant. Candidate lock/allowlist changes validate in isolation and cannot replace the current authority before complete evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites closure_policy_model, target_feature_closure_matrix, suite_path_bijection, cargo_lock_v4_parser, and suite_lock_candidate_e2e cover all closure lanes, host/target splits, feature/profile resolution, manifests and lock grammar, canonical paths/symlinks, exact suite mappings and pins, every policy field, complete/inconclusive outcomes, candidate atomicity, and recovery. The closure inventory and allowlist are bijective over every reachable package/target/source; unlisted or stale rows and policy violations fail with stable exact codes. Mutations for omitted target-only or proc-macro-host edges, suppressed features, stale suite checkout, mismatched package/repo path, path alias/symlink escape, checksum/tree/source/license/policy drift, partial scan promoted to compliant, evidence root detached from candidate lock, malformed/non-v4/duplicate/unterminated lock structures, and reviewless allowlist change are killed. The real no-mock E2E validates a cold authoritative closure, plants each named failure in an isolated candidate, proves typed refusal and unchanged current lock, restores, and independently validates the final bundle. Strict NDJSON records closure lane, host/target, feature/profile set, package source/version/tree/checksum, suite mapping, policy facts, pre/post graph and lock roots, completeness/authority, first divergence, resources, cleanup, and final state. CI runs the audit permanently. No Cargo.lock-only inference, syntax-only source ban, unverifiable pass, hidden transitive dependency, or duplicated extraction path satisfies G0-10.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — eliminate duplicated ownership with franken_lean-53v. This bead owns the complete governed dependency closure, allowlists, authoritative SUITE.lock ceremony, lock-only build enforcement, and seeded closure-policy failures. The mechanical ABI/OLEAN/extern/builtin extraction scripts and their drift CI are owned by franken_lean-53v; G0 closes only with both evidence bundles. Do not implement a second extraction path here.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — every declared closure-policy field is semantic, not decorative. Preserve each SUITE.lock suite path and crate→repo mapping in the parsed model; every manifest suite dependency must canonicalize to that exact pinned repo path, and every pinned crate/repo/path must agree bidirectionally with WORKSPACE_GRAPH and the closure allowlist. Validate vocabulary and observable truth for source, checksum, features, license, build-script, proc-macro, native-link, unsafe-audit, policy, owner, upgrade, and reason; a present-but-ignored field does not satisfy D1. The Cargo.lock parser requires exactly top-level version=4, only the supported package keys/array grammar, terminated dependency arrays, and no duplicate package identity/source/checksum keys. Missing, stale, conflicting, wrong-path, malformed, symlinked, or unverifiable rows fail closed with stable codes and named recovery fixtures.

G0 DECISION INTEGRATION — emit exactly one G0SpikeDecisionV1. Outcome is RatifyClosedDependencyUniverseAndSuiteLock, AmendClosureOrLockContract, or NoGo mapped to Ratified/Amended/NoGo. Ratification requires complete authoritative closure lanes and a published SUITE.lock/allowlist evidence root; an amendment carries exact policy/schema/plan/dependency changes and a complete candidate rerun. Missing checkout/unverifiable metadata/partial traversal/cancellation/resource refusal remains non-authoritative and blocks, while a complete policy violation is a complete NoGo finding until the architecture or candidate is corrected. Contract extraction remains a separate franken_lean-53v handoff and is joined by G0 rather than duplicated here.

DETERMINISM/EVIDENCE AMENDMENT — canonical closure and policy records contain only normalized manifests/lock/suite/package/target/feature/profile facts, expected/actual decisions, logical work/resource facts, roots, mutations, recovery, and final authority. Host, timestamp, duration, PID, absolute-path spelling after canonical identity, scheduler, allocator, and performance data are bounded linked telemetry and cannot enter semantic comparison roots. The fixed closure matrix runs at 1, 8, and 32 threads with actual nonempty package/target-feature partitions where safe and deterministic canonical reduction; intrinsically serial parsing/publication remains ordered and is not relabeled parallel. Missing work partitions, fake labels, schedule-dependent row order, or telemetry-root drift fails G0-10.
acceptance_criteria: The authoritative SUITE.lock and independently computed runtime/build/dev/test/bench/fuzz/proc-macro-host/tool closures are complete, target/feature/profile aware, bijective with manifests/pinned trees/WORKSPACE_GRAPH/reviewed policy, and enforced before any build is called lock-authoritative. All parser/model/mutation/no-mock candidate/recovery suites and strict evidence logs pass. Missing/unverifiable/partial/cancelled/resource-limited/drifting inputs never become compliant. Contract extraction and drift remain wholly owned by franken_lean-53v and are joined later, preserving the feature without duplicate authority.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Fresh structural audit finding before this bead can close: the current SUITE.lock parser validates path= syntax but discards the path, and checks.rs accepts any path-valued dependency whose package name appears as suite-dep. Thus `asupersync = { path = "/tmp/fake" }` can satisfy FLN-STRUCT-010 instead of being bound to SUITE.lock path=/dp/asupersync. Also parse_allowlist presently requires checksum/license/proc-macro/native-link/unsafe-audit/owner/upgrade fields syntactically but discards most values; the audit compares only name/version/source class and build-script absence. Extend the bead acceptance/tests so the exact manifest canonical path must match package -> repo -> SUITE.lock path, suite/workspace source classes are cross-checked both ways, and every declared policy field has validated vocabulary plus mechanically verified semantics where observable. Seed wrong suite path, invalid booleans/audit policy, checksum/provenance drift, proc-macro/native-link drift, and recovery cases. Do not close on field presence alone.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Additional fail-closed parser cases for the xwf work: parse_cargo_lock currently accepts a missing/non-v4 top-level lock version, treats any `key = [` as an ignorable array instead of requiring `dependencies`, and does not error on an unterminated array at EOF. Seed each malformed shape and require FLN-STRUCT-016 rather than relying on a later Cargo invocation. Also reject duplicate package name/version/source/checksum keys rather than last-write-wins.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Fresh preflight review of tools/structure-guard/src/lockfile.rs: parse_cargo_lock is not yet fail-closed for its constrained v4 grammar. It accepts duplicate name/version/source/checksum keys (last value wins), an unquoted source/checksum as None, any top-level version value/duplicates, and EOF while a multiline array remains open; any key with value [ enters the array-skip path. Seed each malformed shape and track seen keys/lock version/array owner plus mandatory close. Also, audit verifies build-script=no against build.rs only when source=workspace; source=suite rows are trusted without inspecting the pinned suite package path. G0-10 must bind every suite package to its SUITE.lock repo/path and verify build-script/proc-macro/native-link claims over the actual pinned closure, or classify the still-unverified rows honestly.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Additional schema-contract gap: CLOSURE_ALLOWLIST.txt says CI fails checksum mismatch and types proc-macro/native-link/unsafe-audit, but AllowRow stores/enforces only name/version/source/build-script/policy. checksum, license, proc-macro, native-link, unsafe-audit, owner, and upgrade merely have to be present and may contain arbitrary or empty values; checksum is never compared to LockPackage. Freeze and validate each documented enum/value law, require checksum=- for workspace/suite path rows, bind unsafe-audit to the declared crate kind/root posture, and verify proc-macro/native-link/build-script facts from actual manifests/targets rather than trusting row text.

title: W8: Ledger records + CAS — demand nodes, five planes, retention tiers
description:
Implement Ledger records + the CAS store (§13.1): every declaration commit produces a record — decl_hash = H(name, elaborated statement, elaborated value/body class, direct dep hashes, options-that-matter, toolchain epoch, mode) — stored content-addressed (frankensqlite metadata + CAS blobs) with dependency edges as first-class rows. The environment at any point is a Merkle frontier keyed by logical roots; a module is a view; a build is a query.

The record generalizes to a demand node — kind, implementation version, epoch, semantic inputs, declared reads/effects (the §10.6 vocabulary), capability inputs, output schema, determinism class D0-D4 — spanning five interlocking planes with separate retention: source, language, trust (kernel/checker receipts), build (facets, codegen, links), service (LSP products, indexes). An LSP hover can be evicted without touching the checked declaration beneath it; retention tiers T0 (trusted roots, never sole-copy-evicted) -> T4 (observational, sampled) with eviction weighing recomputation cost, fan-out, and pressure.

Acceptance: record creation + retrieval on real elaboration output; plane separation demonstrated (service-plane eviction leaves trust plane intact); logical-root stability across hosts. Tests: hash-input completeness property tests (any semantic input change changes the hash; any excluded input does not); retention-tier eviction policy fixtures; frankensqlite crash-recovery drills on the metadata store (WAL semantics honest under kill -9).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-05 STORAGE BOUNDARY — CAS presence, signature, receipt presence, or matching key yields only an untrusted candidate, never a semantically admitted cache hit. Record schemas bind every semantic/capability/determinism input and separate byte identity from logical identity; publication is atomic and generation-bound. Only the validated reuse path owned by franken_lean-5h8 may promote a candidate, and its proof/recompute evidence is linked in the record. Seeded omissions of each hash-input class and stale-generation publication must be killed.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Persist declaration records content-addressed by canonical name, elaborated statement, value/body class, direct dependency hashes, semantic options, toolchain epoch, and mode; dependency edges are first-class rows and the environment is a Merkle frontier addressed by stable logical roots. Real elaboration records retrieve identically across certified hosts.
2. Generalize records to versioned demand nodes carrying kind, implementation version, epoch, semantic inputs, declared reads/effects, capability inputs, output schema, determinism class D0-D4, output roots, and evidence links across separate source, language, trust, build, and service planes.
3. Enforce T0-T4 retention: trusted roots are never sole-copy evicted; lower planes may be evicted by declared recomputation cost/fanout/pressure without invalidating stronger planes. Service-plane eviction demonstrably leaves language/trust records, receipts, roots, and rebuild validity intact.
4. Named unit/property/metamorphic/model/mutation suites prove hash-input completeness and exclusions, canonical serialization, cross-host logical-root stability, dependency traversal, plane isolation, tier policy, concurrent readers/promotions, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and failure atomicity. Omitted-semantic-input, included-observational-input, cross-plane-eviction, stale-root, and premature-promotion mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane elaborates real declarations into frankensqlite metadata plus CAS blobs, retrieves and rebuilds them, evicts service data under pressure, and performs kill-9/crash drills at every metadata/blob promotion boundary before restart and exact state verification. Human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON include run/record/demand/plane/tier/transaction ids, bead/claim/invariant/gate rows, exact redacted argv/cwd, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/seed/cache/disk facts, canonical input/dependency/output/logical/receipt roots, eviction/promotion decisions, expected/actual state, timings/resources/status, artifact/WAL references, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives hash witnesses, host matrix, retention model traces, crash/fault matrix, mutant outcomes, database/CAS manifests, and named test/E2E bundles. No durability, retention, or root-stability claim closes from in-memory or mocked storage.

The language-plane Ledger persists ModuleProvenanceRootV1 with exact canonical manifest bytes, schema/epoch, capture/transparency/authority axes, and collision-aware references to every required declaration and extension payload object. Root equality alone is insufficient on restart: canonical manifest bytes and every actual payload binding are revalidated before authority is restored. Forward/reverse provenance indexes are rebuilt deterministically from primary records or, if cached, treated as discardable derived data and validated for complete bidirectional coverage. Missing, stale, substituted, or equal-digest unequal-byte objects remain untrusted candidates and cannot produce a cache hit.

The W2 handoff is explicitly in-memory and contains the canonical manifest/root, payload requirements, evidence facts, and index-rebuild recipe. This W8 bead owns durable encoding, atomic persistence, restart rehydration, collision-aware retrieval, and exact recovery evidence. Tests and retained records distinguish candidate lookup from validated promotion; include index-cache deletion/rebuild, stale/missing/substituted/equal-digest-unequal-byte objects, partial payload availability, evidence-axis preservation, deterministic reconstruction, typed Inconclusive resource/cancellation, zero-authority recovery candidates, and a later successful rehydration. Mutants trust root equality alone, skip one payload binding, promote a cached index as truth, restore unjustified authority, or let W2 Arc/address identity leak into durable bytes; each is killed discriminatingly.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — declaration identity binds the full canonical payload, truth-bearing metadata, and semantic read/effect manifest, not a vague statement or value class. Retention tier is orthogonal to authority. SQLite metadata and CAS blobs use a journaled visibility generation: stage, validate, publish one marker atomically, replay idempotently, and recover unreachable staging; no cross-store atomicity is implied. Hash, root, signature, or tier alone cannot promote. Preserve ModuleProvenanceRootV1 collision-aware handoff. Named suites: ledger_record_identity_model, plane_authority_separation, cas_visibility_recovery_model, ledger_storage_no_mock_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON is validated independently; telemetry remains separate and non-authoritative. Unknown schema, missing/duplicate ids, broken order/linkage, unbound artifacts, truncation, merged streams, or cleanup/final-state mismatch fail. Cancellation/exhaustion/InternalFault never enters cache, negative storage, admission, publication, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves useful work and deterministic reduction. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: G0-1: ABI resurrection spike — parse a real mathlib .olean via extracted contracts
description:
G0-1 ABI resurrection spike (§22.1-1, feeds §6/§7.2): parse a REAL mathlib .olean at the pin with a prototype Marrow/Grimoire reader — header (magic, version, base address), region relocation, ModuleData traversal — walk every constant and every environment-extension entry, and validate object-graph integrity against the extracted contract tables (not against hand-written constants!).

Why this is first: the entire drop-in promise rests on the object model and the region format being EXACTLY right, and on the extraction methodology (checked-in scripts parsing the pinned lean.h and runtime layout sources -> generated Rust constants module) being viable before anything depends on it. A layout truth discovered late invalidates every downstream interface.

Acceptance: (a) a chosen set of real mathlib oleans (include at least one with heavy environment-extension payloads, e.g. simp-set extensions) fully traversed with zero integrity faults; (b) every field decoded is traceable to a generated contract row with provenance; (c) unknown/opaque extension payloads preserved losslessly and flagged, never guessed; (d) a written findings memo enumerating every layout subtlety discovered (tag values, scalar packing corner cases, relocation quirks) folded into OLEAN_CONTRACT.md / ABI_CONTRACT.md. Tests: fixture corpus checked into the Tribunal (C3 family seed) with a round-trip traversal harness and hostile-input fuzz smoke (bounds, tags, reachability) under resource budgets.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — publish a manifest-complete real-mathlib fixture set. Every row binds exact bytes/size, Reference producer binary/commit, SUITE.lock and Corpus commits, module/import closure, platform, canonical contract root, extension-entry census, selection rationale, clean-clone materialization method, and expected semantic/structural facts. At least one row exercises heavy environment-extension payloads. The closed ci0 stdlib seed proves clean-clone infrastructure only and cannot substitute for the required mathlib rows.

Every decoded field maps to exactly one canonical ContractInventory row id. ABI_CONTRACT and OLEAN_CONTRACT may project/cross-reference that row but cannot become competing authorities. Reference binaries may generate or independently inspect fixtures only inside the Tribunal; no upstream loader/runtime becomes the prototype reader. Unknown opaque payload bytes are preserved with explicit non-authoritative grade and cannot affect trust/invalidation.

Emit one G0SpikeDecisionV1 row. G0-1 outcome is RatifyGeneratedLayoutMethod, AmendGeneratedSchemaOrReader, or NoGo, mapped to the common Ratified/Amended/NoGo vocabulary. Ratification requires every manifested field/constant/extension entry to traverse without unclassified data. Amendment requires exact contract/schema/reader deltas and a complete rerun. A handwritten constant, hidden Reference reader, skipped row, opaque trusted payload, failed run, or unresolved unknown is not an amendment.

The load-bearing claim is invariant: generated-contract-driven decoding; the finite manifest evidence state is OBSERVED and validator/state models are bounded_model. Budgets cover bytes, objects, edges, relocations, aliases, names/depth, expanded weight, allocations/reservations, logical work, output, and logs with zero/exact/one-over cases, checked arithmetic, deterministic cancellation, nonpublication, and retry. Canonical semantic NDJSON excludes host/time/PID/path/performance telemetry. The 1/8/32 lane partitions real nonempty fixture/object validation work where safe and reduces canonically; serial relocation dependencies remain ordered and are not relabeled parallel.

Named abi_resurrection_manifest, relocation_reachability_model, budget_boundary_matrix, contract_row_bijection, and g0_1_no_mock_e2e suites plus hostile-input fuzzing cover all fields/tags/packing/aliases/relocations/reachability/budgets/cancellation/nonpublication/retry and productive 1/8/32 fixture work. title: W2: fln-libm — owned deterministic transcendentals, bit-locked across platforms
description:
Implement fln-libm (§6.8): owned, fixed-implementation Float transcendentals — sin, cos, tan, exp, log, pow, atan2, hypot (and the rest of the family the extern census enumerates) — with documented accuracy (<= 1 ulp target, per-function error tables published) and IDENTICAL BITS on every certified target, locked by cross-platform vectors in CI. No platform libm is linked, period (D21).

Why: Float arithmetic is hardware IEEE-754 binary64 (portable), but upstream defers transcendentals to platform libm, so compiled evaluation, #eval, and native_decide-class computation drift across glibc/macOS/Windows in the last ulps — the Reference cannot promise ITSELF cross-platform bit-stability (§1.6b). Kernel judgments never consume floats (Float ops are opaque to the kernel), so the soundness surface is untouched; the evaluation surface gains bit-stability recorded as a Behavior Note (platforms disagree with each other; we pick one correct answer and keep it everywhere).

Acceptance: cross-platform vector suite green on the certified matrix (identical bits linux-x86-64 / linux-aarch64 / macos-aarch64); error tables generated and published per function; a CI symbol-scan proves no libm import in any release binary. Tests: ulp-error measurement harness against high-precision references; boundary cases (subnormals, infinities, NaN payloads, huge args for range reduction); Float printing/parsing round-trip fixtures matching the Reference conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Implement every Float transcendental enumerated by the pinned extern census with owned fixed algorithms, explicit rounding/range reduction, and no platform-libm call or link in any authoritative path. Define and version NaN payload/sign, infinity, signed-zero, subnormal, overflow/underflow, and exceptional-input behavior.
2. For each function publish reproducible domain-partitioned error tables against an independent high-precision oracle and meet the stated target of at most 1 ulp wherever claimed; any exception is a named bounded Behavior Note, never hidden by aggregate statistics. Kernel judgments remain float-independent.
3. The certified linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, and macos-aarch64 lanes produce identical output bits for the full vector corpus. Windows functional results are reported at their honest level. Release binary/object symbol and dependency scans prove no libm import.
4. Named unit/property/metamorphic/differential/mutation suites cover census completeness, identities where numerically conditioned, monotonic/symmetry/quadrant cases, range-reduction boundaries, subnormals, infinities, all relevant NaN payloads, huge arguments, parsing/printing round trips, cancellation, and resource bounds. Platform-libm-call, last-bit, wrong-quadrant, NaN-canonicalization, and missed-census mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane compiles and executes real Lean Float/#eval programs on every certified platform, captures exact bits and Reference-convention text, compares cross-platform vectors and high-precision error artifacts, scans actual release binaries, injects a known bad vector, and verifies clean recovery. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/function/vector/platform ids, bead/claim/invariant/Parity rows, redacted argv/cwd, toolchain/build/CPU/rounding/profile facts, canonical input/output bits and hashes, oracle value/ulp/error class, expected/actual, timings/resources/status, symbol-scan artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives census coverage, vectors, cross-platform matrices, per-function error tables, binaries/scans, mutant outcomes, and evidence manifests. No determinism or accuracy claim closes from one platform, sampled aggregate alone, or a platform libm oracle in the implementation path.

title: G0-2: Kernel verdict spike — prototype K1 vs provenance-classified checker witnesses
description:
G0-2 Independent-check spike (§22.1-2, feeds §8): a prototype Crucible checks a nontrivial upstream module from its olean — statements AND proofs — with verdicts diffed against lean4checker on the same input.

This is where the kernel judgment inventory (§1.2, Appendix A) meets reality: typing rules for every constructor, universe normalization with the imax collapse, defeq (beta, eta for functions and structures, delta by height, zeta, iota, proof irrelevance, Quot computation, Nat/String literal rules), inductive validation (strict positivity, nested-to-mutual, K-like elimination), structure eta, environment rules. The spike does not need speed (K2 comes later) — it needs to prove the rules are correctly understood and that the differential harness against a foreign checker is practical.

Acceptance: (a) chosen module set (progressively: a small Init module, a Std module, one mathlib file with defeq-heavy content) checks with verdicts identical to lean4checker; (b) any disagreement triaged to root cause — each becomes a fixture and, if upstream-vs-theory, is pre-classified under the §8.2 soundness-beats-bug-parity law; (c) fuel/heartbeat hooks stubbed but counted, proving the accounting seam exists. Tests: the diff harness itself lands in fln-conformance as the seed of the standing kernel differential rig (§8.7) and runs in CI on the fixture set from this spike onward.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — classify every witness by implementation authority, not executable name. Record exact executable/build provenance/hash and oracle_kind. A wrapper or checker using the pinned Reference kernel is ReferenceKernelOracle, never ForeignIndependent; only a separately implemented checker earns ForeignIndependent. Each declaration row binds identical input bytes/hash, prototype and witness verdicts, statement/proof coverage, stable error class, resource outcome, and first divergence. Implicit acceptance because the Reference produced an olean and aggregate module counts do not satisfy the differential.

Publish a row-per-Appendix-A judgment inventory distinguishing real-module coverage, C0 positive/negative fixtures, bounded-model coverage, and explicit unexercised blockers. Unsupported/unobserved rows remain visible and bound the claim. G0-2 emits one G0SpikeDecisionV1 outcome: RatifyKernelInventoryAndVerdictSeam, AmendRuleInventoryOrAdmission, or NoGo. Any unexplained pairwise disagreement is NoGo. A D23 soundness-beats-bug-parity case requires rule/source/literature rationale, minimized fixture, upstream report, exact affected row, Behavior Note, and reviewed admission; it is never inferred merely because the Reference accepted.

Publish a versioned kernel-contract delta consumed by franken_lean-79k; that downstream bead owns final KERNEL_CONTRACT integration and G0-2 does not wait circularly for it. The load-bearing verdict-agreement claim is invariant with OBSERVED manifest evidence; rule/state models are bounded_model.

Unit/property tests use an independent small judgment/universe/substitution model and valid/invalid boundary pairs. Productive 1/8/32 work partitions real declarations/modules while preserving declaration dependency order and canonical result reduction. Canonical semantic logs exclude timing/host/PID/path telemetry.

title: FrankenLean program root: drop-in native-Rust Lean 4 toolchain (plan §0)
description:
Program root for FrankenLean (franken_lean, crate prefix fln-): a ground-up, native-Rust reimplementation of the ENTIRE Lean 4 toolchain — parser, macro engine, elaborator, unifier, instance engine, tactic framework, simp + decision procedures, trusted kernel, compiler, VM, runtime/ABI twin, module codec, build fabric, and language server — that is a drop-in replacement at six binary surfaces (source language, .olean read AND write, lean_object C ABI, .ilean/artifact chain, the Lean.* Meta API via the Native Mirror, LSP + $/lean/* + CLI).

Constitutional laws (never violate): the Oracle-Only Law D8 (upstream implementation code NEVER executes as a component; the Reference lives only inside the Tribunal as differential oracle, fixture/census mine, and Init/Std source input); closed dependency universe (std + pinned nightly + FrankenSuite: asupersync, frankensqlite, franken_networkx, frankensearch, frankentui, franken_markdown, fastmcp_rust, atp); zero mandatory external tools (cc and git optional-only per D2); forbid(unsafe) outside fln-unsafe-abi/-region/-jit; kernel covenant <= 12 KLOC with FL-INV-02; determinism as invariant FL-INV-01; claims have types (D7, L0-L4 / R0-R5 evidence axes).

Success = R4 (drop-in epoch replacement) for the pinned epoch (Reference leanprover/lean4 v4.32.0; Corpus mathlib4 at compatible commit, both pinned in SUITE.lock) on the declared platform matrix, via gates G0..G6 (§22). The single source of truth is COMPREHENSIVE_PLAN_FOR_THE_DESIGN_OF_FRANKEN_LEAN.md; this bead tree is its executable projection. Sequencing bends to dependency; ambition does not bend to sequencing.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
acceptance_criteria:
The program closes only at R4 for the exact SUITE.lock epoch and declared certified platform matrix after G0 through G6 and every W1 through W12 obligation are closed with independently validated evidence. One native Rust type theory and kernel powers all three product shapes: elan-compatible lean/leanc/lake/fln binaries, the capability-first embeddable fln library, and Envoy MCP. Source language, faithful olean read and write, lean_object C ABI, ilean/artifact chain, Lean.*/Init/Std Native Mirror surface, LSP plus $/lean extensions, and human/robot CLI behavior meet their row-level compatibility contracts; every mandatory Parity Ledger row is L4 and the Witness matrix permits every published claim. FL-INV-01 through FL-INV-07, D1 through D23, the Oracle-Only Law, closed dependency universe, optional-only cc/git rule, unsafe-boundary ledger and structural laws, kernel single-authority and 12-KLOC covenant, typed claims, faithful/sound/frontier isolation, and reproducible-build law are continuously enforced by independent mutation-kill tests. A cold air-gapped install on each certified platform builds, checks, runs, audits, edits through unmodified vscode-lean4, exercises the library and MCP session APIs, round-trips mixed artifacts, verifies receipts/capsules, and cleanly recovers from corrupt input, cancellation, resource exhaustion, crash, disk-full, and cache faults without upstream runtime fallback. Full unit/property/model/differential/fuzz/mutation/fault/metamorphic/determinism/soak/platform suites and all PG gates pass with bounded schema-versioned evidence bundles, exact corpus/host/cache/thread facts, raw samples, receipts, roots, cleanup, and final-state proof. Documentation and release metadata cannot exceed current claim evidence and identify FrankenLean without compatibility deception. No prototype, sampled compatibility percentage, hidden exclusion, unclassified divergence, unsafe laundering, undeclared dependency, mock-only release proof, waived gate presented as success, or deferred listed feature satisfies program closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — final G6 evidence consumes the certified-platform contract, late-producer Palimpsest join, corrected per-target versus target-neutral identity layers, split distribution/claim/epoch joins, and complete installed-product matrix. No aggregate green, unsupported platform normalization, sampled client/docs surface, future-producer promise, signature-as-reproducibility, or downgraded mandatory PG claim satisfies release readiness.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — strict canonical semantic NDJSON and separately linked bounded telemetry are mandatory. Semantic records contain normalized ids/inputs/roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state; host, PID, time, paths, cache and performance stay telemetry-only. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/extra/duplicate fields, broken linkage/order, truncation, merged streams, and telemetry in authoritative roots. Productive 1/8/32 applies where safely parallel; named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E close claims.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.
