title: W3: Crucible K1 — the certified small-step evaluator under the covenant
description:
Implement Crucible engine K1, the certified evaluator (§8.3): a direct, boring, small-step-flavored implementation of the full judgment inventory, optimized for AUDITABILITY — the code a skeptical logician reads in an afternoon. K1 is the definitional reference for K2, the engine forced by --kernel k1 for audit builds, and the engine compiled into the WASM Judge.

Constraints (the covenant, §8.1): fln-kernel <= 12 KLOC total across both engines, forbid(unsafe_code), dependencies only fln-bignum + Grimoire read-only NativeHeap term views + core/alloc; zero I/O, zero threads, zero global mutable state, zero plugin hooks; one exported authority: check(Environment, Declaration) -> Verdict. The covenant is CI-enforced: line count + dependency-graph walk + TCB inventory generated from the actual graph every run; growth requires amending the plan first. Typestate admission (§8.2b) and the typed resource algebra (§8.2c) are part of this bead: budgets from the caller Cx (node count, depth, expanded weight, reduction steps, allocation), cooperative cancellation checkpoints, no recursive host traversal over attacker-controlled depth, exhaustion = typed Inconclusive with a consumption profile.

Acceptance: G0-2 module set re-checked by production K1 with verdicts identical to lean4checker; every KERNEL_CONTRACT rule maps to named code + fixtures; DAG-bomb and deep-term fixtures produce Inconclusive under budget. Tests: rule-per-rule C0 micro-fixtures; generated well-typed term storms (agreement with the Reference kernel); mutation campaign — a skipped positivity check or inverted universe condition must be killed by a named test (§18.2).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: FL-INV-02 IMPLEMENTATION OWNER — K1 implements the sole authority transition. Successful checking yields an opaque checked result whose constructors and authority-bearing fields are private to fln-kernel and are never deserializable; all environment publication paths require that live result or an equivalent kernel-owned typestate transition. Rejections and FL-INV-07 Inconclusive/InternalFault values cannot inhabit the accepted state. CI walks public APIs and dependency edges; compile-fail tests reject direct construction, cache/receipt replay as authority, raw environment insertion, and unsafe-crate laundering. A seeded bypass mutation must fail the standing admission E2E before this bead closes.
acceptance_criteria:
K1 implements every closed KERNEL_CONTRACT rule in direct audit-first small-step code and maps each stable rule id to named implementation and fixtures. fln-kernel remains no more than 12 KLOC across engines, forbid-unsafe, minimal allowed dependencies, no IO/threads/globals/plugins, and one exported authority check(Environment, Declaration) to typed Verdict. Successful checks alone produce a private opaque nonserializable capability required by every publication path; Rejected, Inconclusive, and InternalFault cannot inhabit it, and checker/receipt/cache/deserializer/plugin/frontier/unsafe crates cannot forge or launder it. Node/depth/expanded-weight/reduction/allocation budgets and cooperative cancellation come from caller context, preflight work, use nonrecursive attacker-depth traversal, and return exact consumption profiles with failure atomicity. Unit/property/model/generated-term/mutation suites cover every rule, declaration family, deep/DAG bomb, exact budget boundary, cancellation, stale environment, publication API and compile-fail bypass; skipped positivity, inverted universe, nested/private-ref, fuel, raw insertion, and Inconclusive-promotion mutants are killed. Production K1 matches lean4checker/Reference on G0-2 and every currently runnable corpus slice; all divergences halt and shrink. Real no-mock admission E2E includes accepted/rejected/inconclusive/internal-fault, forged capability/receipt/cache attempts, resource/cancellation, exact restoration, recovery, roots and traces in strict bundles. Dependencies including complete nested/private-reference blockers, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, line/dependency/unsafe/API guards, differential/fuzz/mutation/thread/platform lanes pass before closure; final G1 aggregation remains downstream. No subset marketed as full K1, recursive attacker traversal, public checked constructor, second admission path, checker vote overriding kernel, sampled mutation proof, or deferred listed rule satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — the sole kernel authority receives a kernel-owned immutable budget/cancellation request projected from caller Cx; fln-kernel itself does not depend on asupersync and cannot observe scheduler, IO, clock, or ambient capabilities. External effects never enter check authority. Unit/model tests prove projection totality, budget boundaries, cancellation polling, effect absence, and identical semantic results across callers; cancellation/exhaustion remains typed Inconclusive and publishes nothing.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative output is strict schema-versioned canonical semantic NDJSON containing normalized inputs, roots, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, decisions, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, wall time, temporary paths, cache/performance facts, and scheduler details live only in a separately linked bounded telemetry stream and cannot enter authoritative digests. Validators reject merged streams, unknown versions, extra or missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, or telemetry-root drift. Productive 1/8/32 runs prove nonempty completed work and deterministic reduction rather than thread-count labels.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

Review sweep (Claude Fable session, 2026-07-22): did a full soundness read of the K1 kernel (tc.rs/lib.rs/verdict.rs) against KERNEL_CONTRACT.md and the pin — typing (KR-104 Sort u:Sort u+1, KR-108 imax for Pi, KR-106 app), the defeq ladder (structural -> sort-equiv -> binder-congruence via shared fvar -> whnf-core -> proof-irrelevance -> lazy-delta by height -> app-congruence -> eta both ways), de Bruijn instantiate/abstract, projection Prop-leak guard, delta loop (step-bounded, not stack-recursive), unfold restricted to Safe defs — all sound; no over-acceptance found. 2) kr306_proof_irrelevance_requires_defeq_propositions: hp:p, hq:q with p,q distinct Props -> must be NotDefEq. Kills dropping the type-defeq half of proof_irrel_eq (which would equate every proof of every prop). Verified: replacing the final is_def_eq(t_type,s_type) with Ok(true) makes this test FAIL; kr306_in_prop cannot catch it (shared prop).

Both mutation-kills demonstrated live and reverted. Also cleared a UBS critical (a pre-existing panic! in the fl_inv_07 test) by rewriting to assert!(matches!(...)) — behavior-preserving. comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Follow-up (Claude Fable session): added kr310_same_constant_defeq_iff_levels_are_equivalent — KR-310 (same-name constants defeq only with equivalent universe levels) had no test, a universe-soundness boundary. F.u : Sort u; the negative half asserts F at level 0 and F at level 1 are NOT defeq (they inhabit Sort 0 vs Sort 1 — equating them is unsound), and the positive half asserts F at level (max u u) is defeq to F at level u (max u u normalizes to u), so the rule is discriminating not blanket. Mutation-kill verified live: replacing the per-level is_equiv check in KR-310 with true makes the kernel wrongly Accept F.0 == F.1; the test catches it. Restored. title: W6: Monadic tower + ElabTxn — explicit transactions, audited rollback, mvar graph
description:
Implement the monadic tower + ElabTxn (§10.1): the Core/Meta/TermElab/Tactic state SEMANTICS (metavariable contexts with delayed assignments, local contexts, backtracking discipline, option threading, message logs, InfoTree recording) as NativeHeap structures, with Mirror-facade views projected into Marrow objects exactly where user metaprograms touch them — the toolchain pays the ABI shape only at the seam it actually crosses (the Oracle-Only Law dividend, §6.1b).

Elaboration state is EXPLICIT, not ambient: each command runs in an ElabTxn (base logical root, local context, metavariable store, universe store, constraint queue, extension overlay, message buffer, InfoTree builder, capability context, decision ledger, deterministic seed, budget); nested elaboration uses child transactions with exactly five outcomes (commit all; commit diagnostics/info only; roll back completely; expose candidate + unresolved obligations; fork into deterministic alternatives). Rollback is AUDITED: Tribunal leak tests prove no failed alternative leaves assigned metavariables, registered declarations, or messages behind — the bug class upstream ambient monads make chronic is made structural here. Metavariables form an explicit dependency graph (type, context, kind, origin, assignment state + justification, delayed constraints) with targeted wake-up: assigning one reschedules only constraints that actually read it.

Acceptance: tower observable semantics verified against C1/C2 elaboration fixtures; leak tests green across the five outcomes; wake-up precision measured (no spurious rescheduling on the fixture set). Tests: transaction-outcome property tests; a planted leak mutant (child txn leaves an assignment behind) must be caught; InfoTree shape differentials vs the oracle.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
Native CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM states preserve pinned observable metavariable, local-context, option, message, InfoTree and backtracking semantics, projecting Marrow facade views only at user-code seams. Each command owns an explicit ElabTxn binding base root, contexts/stores/constraints, extension overlay, buffers/builders, capabilities, decision ledger, deterministic seed and budgets. Nested transactions implement exactly commit-all, diagnostics/info-only, full rollback, candidate-with-obligations, and deterministic-alternative-fork outcomes; failure/cancellation/resource paths leak no assignment, declaration, extension, message, capability or root. The explicit mvar dependency graph records type/context/kind/origin/assignment justification/delayed constraints and wakes only actual readers. Unit/property/model/differential/mutation tests cover all outcomes/nesting, tower state threading, rollback/idempotence, mvar graph/wakeup, InfoTree shape, budgets and recovery; planted assignment/declaration/message/capability leaks and spurious/missed wakeups are killed. Real no-mock C1/C2 E2E runs elaboration/backtracking/metaprograms, plants failed alternatives, restores, compares Reference states/InfoTrees, and retains strict transaction/root/leak/final-state bundles. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, Mirror/trace/perturbation/lab lanes pass. No ambient hidden state, partial rollback, second mvar truth, eager ABI projection, mock-only tower, untracked effect, or deferred listed outcome/state field satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — model the five nested transaction choices as a versioned TxnDispositionV1 axis inside outer Complete, Inconclusive, or InternalFault outcomes. Cancellation, exhaustion, or internal fault cannot masquerade as a semantic disposition or publish authoritative state. Mvar graphs bind closure identity and assignment revision; unknown or uninstrumented reads conservatively reschedule. No missed reader is an invariant, while no-spurious-wakeup is only a performance target. Child regions, obligations, and tasks drain on every path. Named suites: elab_txn_disposition_model, mvar_wakeup_completeness, elab_txn_fault_recovery_e2e.

W6-W8 EVIDENCE CONTRACT — authoritative evidence is canonical schema-versioned semantic NDJSON validated independently; telemetry is a separate non-authoritative stream and cannot affect hashes, comparison, admission, cache, publication, or promotion. Validators reject unknown versions, missing/duplicate ids, broken step order, unbound artifacts, silent truncation, merged streams, and cleanup/final-state mismatch. Cancellation, exhaustion, and InternalFault never enter cache, negative storage, proof admission, or promotion. Productive 1/8/32 records prove useful overlap, completed work, retries, and schedule identity. Named unit/model/property/mutation/fuzz suites and real no-mock positive/failure/recovery E2E are mandatory.

title: W8: Watch mode + distributed elaboration — local re-verification, no distributed trust
description:
Implement watch mode + distributed elaboration (§13.7): lake build --watch (filesystem events -> cone recompute -> live frankentui progress with dependency-cone visualization) and distributed elaboration — remote workers pulling Ledger tasks over asupersync RPC, results RE-VERIFIED by local Crucible on arrival: distribution without distributed trust (workers may lie; results validate locally; disagreement blocks and never votes — the same trust model Envoy remote check service reuses, §16.3).

Acceptance: watch mode recomputes exactly the invalidation-matrix cone on real edit streams with the frankentui surface live; distributed builds produce bit-identical Ledger states to local builds (FL-INV-01 extends across the wire); a lying-worker fixture (corrupted result) is caught by local re-verification and quarantined. Tests: filesystem-event storm fixtures (rapid saves, atomic-rename editors); worker-loss and partial-result drills; determinism differential local-vs-distributed on the corpus; cancellation of in-flight remote tasks drains cleanly (obligation-leak oracle).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. lake build --watch consumes real filesystem events, coalesces rapid saves/atomic-renames without losing semantic changes, recomputes exactly the perturbation-validated invalidation cone, publishes only complete generations, and renders live dependency-cone progress through frankentui with an equivalent versioned robot stream.
2. Distributed workers pull versioned Ledger demand tasks over asupersync RPC with explicit inputs, budgets, epoch/profile, and determinism policy. Returned artifacts are untrusted until local hash/schema/closure checks and Crucible re-verification; lying, stale, partial, duplicate, or over-budget results are rejected/quarantined and disagreement blocks rather than votes.
3. Local and distributed builds from the same closure yield bit-identical environments, diagnostics, Ledger records, artifacts, receipts, and logical roots at thread/worker perturbations. Worker loss/reschedule and cancellation drain all obligations and never publish partial state.
4. Named unit/property/metamorphic/DPOR/fault/mutation suites cover event coalescing, rename/overflow/rescan, cone equality, UI/robot updates, task leases/idempotency, worker loss/duplication, local verification, cancellation/resource exhaustion, and failure atomicity. Missed-event, excess-cone, trust-worker, first-result-wins, and obligation-leak mutants must be killed.
5. A shared no-mock E2E lane watches a real project through rapid editor-style changes while displaying the TUI, compares computed/actual cones, then runs the same corpus locally and over real worker processes, kills workers mid-task, injects a corrupt result, cancels in flight, restarts, and compares exact final roots/artifacts. Human logs plus schema-versioned NDJSON include run/watch/event/generation/cone/task/worker/lease/result ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, redacted argv/cwd/endpoints, epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread/worker/seed/budget facts, canonical source/input/output/receipt/logical roots, scheduling/verification/quarantine decisions, expected/actual, timings/resources/status, artifacts, cleanup, and final state.
6. CI archives event streams, cone witnesses, local/distributed byte diffs, schedule/crashpacks, quarantine records, mutant outcomes, and E2E manifests. No distributed speed or correctness claim may substitute worker trust for local verification.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — distinguish coherent local watch from distributed build. Watch binds source snapshot and generation, handles rename storms and overflow with full rescan, and never publishes a stale generation; TUI and robot stream derive from one state. Distributed tasks use versioned leases/idempotency, canonical merge, obligation drain, and product-specific admission schemas; Crucible trust alone does not admit diagnostics/build/service products. Release distributed claims require real separate hosts and network. Named suites: watch_generation_model, watch_overflow_rescan, distributed_admission_matrix, distributed_separate_host_e2e.

title: W5: FLBC interpreter — ABI-valued registers, inline caches, PG-7 floor
description:
Implement the FLBC interpreter (fln-vm, §11.2): threaded dispatch, inline caches on constructor tags and closure arities, direct-call fast paths for the intrinsic table, allocation through Marrow (heartbeats count identically to native paths — fuel parity crosses the VM boundary), and the Reference semantics for panics, partial non-termination, stack depth, and checkSystem interruption polls.

The design masterstroke to preserve at all costs: FLBC values ARE Marrow ABI objects, so calls cross between interpreted code, Iron-compiled code, Reference-built plugins, and the Mirror native facade with ZERO conversion — one calling convention for the whole membrane (validated by spike G0-3). Performance floor PG-7: >= 3x the Reference interpreter on the tactic micro-corpus — deliberately conservative because the interpreter is the default vehicle for the long tail of user tactics (the interpreter tax is weakness census #5).

Acceptance: #eval/IO/Task parity corpus green (G2 requirement); PG-7 met on the micro-corpus against the W1 baseline; interruption polling honors the checkSystem contract (latency fixtures). Tests: opcode-level unit tests; VM opcode fuzzing under budgets (§18.2); inline-cache correctness under polymorphic call sites (cache hit == cache miss result); cross-boundary call matrix (interpreted <-> intrinsic <-> plugin) with RC-balance assertions.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — this component bead owns the FLBC interpreter, validated opcode semantics, ABI-valued register/call convention, typed host-call dispatcher contract, inline-cache correctness, budgets/cancellation, and allocator/heartbeat integration. It may test the dispatcher with contract fixtures but may not claim the full generated intrinsic surface or production #eval/IO/plugin parity by itself. Those arrive through fln-k63, franken_lean-hgu, and franken_lean-sno and are proven together by franken_lean-golem-runtime-integration-pbe.
acceptance_criteria:
The versioned FLBC interpreter implements every owned opcode with ABI-valued registers, exact frames/calls/returns/closures/constructors, exceptions, stack/maxRecDepth, panic/partial behavior, allocation/heartbeat charging, checkSystem polling, and typed cancellation/resource outcomes. Inline caches for tags, arities and calls are version/root/shape-bound; a hit is observationally identical to a miss under polymorphism and invalidation. The host-call dispatcher has one typed contract consumed later by the generated intrinsic integration, and interpreted/intrinsic/plugin boundaries preserve the one Marrow calling convention and RC ownership without conversion. Unit/property/model/fuzz/mutation suites cover every opcode, decode/validation, malformed bytecode, stack/frame boundary, cache state, dispatcher outcome, object/RC transition, heartbeat, interruption latency, and recovery; named stale-cache, wrong-opcode, skipped-poll, host-conversion, RC-leak and budget-promotion mutants are killed. Component E2E executes representative validated FLBC and cross-boundary contract fixtures; the downstream production integration supplies complete #eval/IO/plugin parity. Both retain strict bytecode/root/cache/resource/process evidence. PG-7 interpreter performance is at least 3 times the Reference interpreter on the registered tactic micro-corpus with identical results, raw same-machine samples and tails. All dependencies, scripts/check.sh, cargo/fmt/clippy/UBS, VM fuzz/mutation/thread/performance lanes pass. No unvalidated bytecode execution, host-value shadow representation, cache result drift, Reference VM fallback, component-only claim of full intrinsic parity, timing-only correctness, or deferred listed interpreter behavior satisfies closure.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — inline caches bind callsite, bytecode schema, intrinsic-table root, environment/profile/mode, observed shape, and all semantic dependencies. IO/resource failure records the exact externally visible effect prefix and drains or cancels obligations; it never claims irreversible effects were rolled back. Replayability, cacheability, and authority are separate. DPOR/fault tests cover cache collision, stale shape, partial IO, task cancellation, retries, and exact internal final state.

EVIDENCE CONTRACT — canonical authoritative NDJSON contains normalized semantic inputs, roots, decisions, typed outcomes, resource class/consumption, first divergence, cleanup, and exact final state. Host, PID, time, paths, cache/performance, and scheduler facts are linked bounded telemetry only. Independent validators reject unknown schema, extra/missing fields, broken linkage/order, silent truncation, telemetry in semantic hashes, and final-state mismatch. Productive 1/8/32 evidence proves nonempty completed work and canonical reduction.

title: fln-env: bound declaration content hashing and admission with typed atomic resource refusals
description:
Problem: Environment::decl_content_digest currently traverses and buffers input-sized declaration state into an unbounded CanonWriter and returns a bare Digest, while Environment::add_decl performs an unbounded persistent-map insert and can refuse only duplicate names. No current API can truthfully report typed declaration byte/work exhaustion, cancellation, or atomic over-budget refusal. Runner-level time/output limits and PMap collision limits are orthogonal and must not be cited as this contract. Scope: design and implement first-class declaration-identity/admission limits and exact work facts for every ConstantInfo field family, including canonical output bytes, name/level/expression structure and depth, mutual-member rows, constructor rows, recursor rules, and persistent-map admission work. Limit and cancellation observation points must be deterministic; exhaustion/cancellation is typed Inconclusive rather than rejection, acceptance, panic, or partial publication (FL-INV-07). Hashing and admission must be one preflighted atomic operation: failure leaves the original Environment root, contents, and sharing unchanged; a later adequate-budget retry succeeds with the same digest/root as the unlimited mathematical model. Do not preserve an unbounded internal compatibility path merely for convenience; migrate callers to the capability/limit-aware API and keep any explicitly trusted test helper visibly non-authoritative. Avoid double traversal where a bounded canonical sink and checked counters can establish the same facts. Preserve exact Domain::DeclContent bytes for admitted declarations, one-name/one-constant semantics, insertion-order independence, snapshot sharing, and all existing variant identity. Verification must include independent byte/work models; exact-boundary and one-over refusal for every dimension; deep/wide/adversarial declarations; cancellation at every fixed observation point; schedule matrices; failure atomicity and clean recovery; named missing-check, late-check, partial-insert, wrong-actual, cancellation-as-rejection, and digest-drift mutants; comprehensive unit/property/model tests; and a no-mock environment E2E negative/recovery lane with versioned detailed logs and retained independently validated artifacts. This is the explicit home for the resource functionality previously impossible to prove inside fln-amv.12; fln-amv.12 remains responsible only for the finite one-byte enum-tag projection.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every acceptance criterion in the structured field is mandatory, including exact reproducible logical usage; immutable declaration-admission plans; typed Complete/Invalid/Conflict/Inconclusive/InternalFault precedence; no arbitrary allocator-abort recovery claim; exact retry and nonpublication; .13 PMap accounting consumed once; exhaustive boundaries, models, productive 1-8-32 schedules, named mutants, real recovery coverage, and strict independently valid evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
A reviewed declaration-identity/admission contract defines explicit versioned limits and exact usage facts for canonical encoded bytes, name/level/expression nodes and depth, mutual-member rows, constructor rows, recursor rules, declaration count, and persistent-map admission work; unsupported dimensions cannot fall through to an unbounded path. Outcomes keep Complete, typed Inconclusive resource exhaustion/cancellation, duplicate-name refusal, and InternalFault structurally distinct under FL-INV-07. Limit and cancellation observation points and actual-count semantics are frozen and schedule-independent. Declaration hashing plus insertion is one preflighted immutable transaction: no digest/root/environment is published on refusal, the original environment remains value-, root-, and sharing-identical, and a later adequate-budget retry produces the exact existing Domain::DeclContent bytes, digest, and logical root. Every production caller uses the bounded API; no hidden unlimited wrapper, timing-only limit, partial insert, double-counted work, or runner-only substitute is accepted. Unit/property/model tests cover every ConstantInfo variant and field, empty/exact/one-over/large/deep boundaries, checked arithmetic, malformed internal shapes, cancellation at each fixed point, 1/8/32 schedules, snapshot sharing, failure atomicity, and clean retry against independent byte/work models. Named missing-check, late-check, wrong-resource, wrong-actual, overflow, partial-publication, cancellation-as-rejection, and digest-drift mutants are killed. A shared real environment E2E exercises admitted, every resource refusal, cancellation/inconclusive, exact restoration, and later recovery with strict schema-versioned NDJSON containing run/scenario/step ids, bead/claim/invariant rows, governed input hashes, limits/usage, expected/actual outcome, authority/publication flags, exact digest/root values, process/resource facts, separate bounded streams, cleanup, and final state; validators reject missing, duplicate, stale, contradictory, reordered, or extra evidence and retained manifests revalidate independently. cargo fmt --check, all fln-env and relevant fln-hash tests, check/clippy with warnings denied, UBS, scripts/check.sh, the authoritative environment runner, mutation/fault lanes, and parent W2 gates pass. No panic, silent truncation, unbounded bypass, mock-only closure, process budget substituted for in-process refusal, or changed canonical bytes satisfies closure.

Plan-space precision addendum: usage facts are logical, reproducible units: canonical bytes emitted, Name/Level/Expr node occurrences visited, maximum logical depth, mutual/constructor/recursor row occurrences, PMap comparison/path/fresh-node work, and explicitly reserved capacity. They do not claim unique Arc-node counts, CPU instructions, allocator-resident bytes, or globally available memory. Complete reports exact full totals. A refusal reports exact consumption through the deterministic refusal checkpoint, the triggering dimension, and a witnessed lower bound on remaining required work, normally limit plus one; it never scans an unobserved suffix merely to claim an exact total.

A versioned total precedence table covers malformed/internally inconsistent input, duplicate-name observation, checked-arithmetic overflow, cancellation, and simultaneous resource breaches. A duplicate/conflict is authoritative only after its required bounded lookup completes; an unfinished lookup is Inconclusive. For simultaneous resource dimensions at one checkpoint, one frozen dimension order selects the primary reason while all observed facts remain available. Pairwise tie tests prove identical outcome, checkpoint, usage, authority, publication, and cacheability across schedules.

Hashing/preflight produces an immutable non-authoritative DeclarationAdmissionPlan binding schema/epoch, the exact canonical declaration input and provisional digest/bytes, source Environment logical root/revision, duplicate-name observation, usage, and the canonical collision/PMap admission plan. A provisional digest computed before a later refusal is never returned, logged, or cached as authoritative. Commit revalidates declaration, base root, cancellation, and plan integrity, consumes collision/PMap facts exactly once, performs genuinely fallible input-sized reservations where supported, then publishes one immutable Environment. It does not recompute canonical bytes, digest, collision order, or PMap work. Stale, substituted, cross-base, replayed, or internally inconsistent plans are typed under the same precedence table and confer no authority.

Typed memory refusal is limited to configured budgets, checked arithmetic, and demonstrably fallible reservation calls. No in-process claim promises recovery from arbitrary Arc/Box/global allocator abort unless every allocating path is proved fallible. All claimed reservations precede publication; the final phase uses reserved storage, immutable moves, and non-failing bookkeeping. The existing dependency path through fln-amv.14 and fln-amv.13 supplies the bounded PMap contract; no second resource truth or double charge is permitted.

j8h owns the exhaustive per-declaration matrix: every ConstantInfo field and logical usage dimension, zero/one-under/exact/one-over, each cancellation checkpoint, plan substitution/staleness, nonpublication, and retry. Productive 1/8/32 workers process distinct declarations/interleavings with fixed reported seeds and replayable minimized counterexamples; a sequential loop merely labeled with thread counts is insufficient. Additional named mutants double-charge PMap work, omit one usage dimension, expose a provisional digest, accept a stale/cross-base plan, reserve after publication, or treat unfinished duplicate lookup as conflict. fln-amv.11 owns only cross-plane composition; fln-amv.14 owns the authoritative lifecycle/envelope mechanism. Evidence references each independently validated child bundle once.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Dependency-order correction from the final plan audit: j8h now waits for fln-amv.1 and fln-amv.12 as well as fln-amv.14. This freezes mutual-membership and enum-tag identity before the declaration hashing/admission API is redesigned, prevents same-file/API overlap, and preserves the exact existing Domain::DeclContent bytes as a prerequisite rather than a post-hoc migration target. The graph remains acyclic.

title: fln-env: close extension merge validation mutation-proof debt before atomic integration
description:
Closed beads fln-amv.3 and fln-amv.4 established useful baseline behavior and real refusal/recovery records, but their closure evidence does not demonstrate the named mutation matrix required by their acceptance criteria. Preserve those closures and add the missing proof before global Environment integration.

Freeze the extension merge pipeline in this order: bounded descriptor admission and complete descriptor-difference construction; exact branch-ancestry validation; merge-policy dispatch; output construction; publication. Descriptor refusal must inspect zero journal entries, clone zero payloads, select no policy, and expose no product or root. An ancestry refusal must stop at its deterministic exact comparison boundary and expose no product or root. Diagnostics report every simultaneous mismatch in canonical branch-then-field order and both branch-history facts in stable ours-then-theirs order.

The SetUnion raw-order law is exact. After validating the common base, compare the complete ours and theirs suffix sequences by collision-safe canonical ExtensionEntry encoding, then append base_raw plus the lesser suffix block plus the greater suffix block. Never sort or interleave entries inside a suffix block and never remove raw duplicates. Equal suffix blocks are both retained. The semantic view is the stable first-occurrence projection by exact payload bytes over the resulting raw sequence; provenance remains byte-exact. Hashes may accelerate comparison but may not decide equality or ordering without an exact tie-break.

This bead owns the missing semantic mutation bundle and its authoritative child evidence. It does not duplicate the baseline implementation of fln-amv.3/.4 or the global multi-plane publication owned by fln-amv.11.

## Steps to Reproduce

Compare the closed fln-amv.3/.4 evidence and named test inventory against their required mutation matrix. Baseline descriptor and ancestry refusal/recovery passes, but no discriminating compiled kill is identified for each skipped descriptor field, deferred validation, premature policy selection, payload clone, partial product publication, length-only/one-branch/unordered ancestry, or merge-after-refusal mutant. Run the final 41s history seam with those independently selectable mutants; the current proof debt is reproduced whenever a mutant survives, fails only to compile, or is counted by an unrelated failure.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every criterion in this issue's acceptance_criteria field is mandatory, including the final 41s comparison-plan dependency, complete stage/diagnostic/allocation proof, exact SetUnion suffix-block law, named discriminating kills, unit/model/productive 1-8-32 coverage, real Environment refusal/restoration/recovery, and strict independently revalidatable evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
Independently selectable, compiling semantic mutants skip each descriptor field (name, merge, checkpoint, provenance); defer descriptor validation until journal traversal; select a merge policy before validation; clone payloads during refusal; expose a partial product/root; skip ancestry validation; check only length; check only ours; check only theirs; compare entries unordered; or continue merging after refusal. The unmutated baseline passes and a specifically named assertion fails for the intended divergence for every mutant; compile failures, unrelated failures, and generic nonzero results do not count as kills.

Descriptor inputs have explicit name-depth, canonical-byte, diagnostic, work, and cancellation limits. Complete mismatch aggregation is authoritative only after bounded completion; exhaustion or cancellation is typed Inconclusive with authority=false and no product. Exact operation/allocation facts prove descriptor refusal performs zero journal comparisons, payload clones, policy dispatches, or product publications. Ancestry facts identify exact compared entries/bytes, both side lengths and first divergences, and stop before policy/output work. Inputs, roots, sharing, and cache state remain unchanged after every refusal; adequate-budget retry succeeds.

Unit tables and independent generated models cover single and simultaneous descriptor differences on either/both branches, every invalid prefix class on either/both branches, empty/boundary/large journals, all merge policies, repeated refusal, restoration, and recovery. SetUnion cases include one suffix prefixing the other, equal and empty blocks, duplicates in base/each suffix/across suffixes, payload-equal entries with differing provenance, branch permutation, and injected hash collisions. Productive 1/8/32 worker matrices process distinct cases and yield identical canonical diagnostics, roots, work facts, and minimized replayable counterexamples for one fixed input closure.

The real Environment lane runs at least one descriptor-order or premature-publication mutant and one ancestry mutant, restores governed bytes exactly, and then completes a clean recovery. Strict schema-versioned NDJSON records run/scenario/step and bead/claim/invariant IDs; governed source/input hashes; mutation ID and targeted assertion; pipeline stage; descriptor and prefix facts; limits/usage; work/allocation counters; expected/actual outcome; authority/publication/cacheability; before/after roots; first divergence; bounded artifact references; cleanup; and final state. Validation rejects missing, extra, duplicate, stale, reordered, wrong-mutant, unrelated-failure, merged-stream, surviving-mutant, or incomplete evidence; the retained child bundle revalidates independently. All fln-env tests, focused mutation/model suites, the authoritative environment lane, fmt, check, warnings-denied clippy, UBS, scripts/check.sh, and predecessor gates pass. No baseline-only green bar, digest-only equality, raw-entry reordering/deduplication, partial publication, mock-only proof, or deferred named mutant satisfies closure.

The collision-safe history comparison contract is imported from fln-extension-history-checkpoint-identity-41s. This bead consumes that bead's exact comparison plan, checkpoint facts, and logical usage once; it neither redefines digest equality nor rewalks/recharges the history. Its distinct ownership is pipeline stage order, refusal work/allocation, complete diagnostics, raw suffix-block ordering, nonpublication, and the mutation evidence proving those laws. Every ancestry/order mutant must run against the final 41s exact-history API, including injected equal-digest unequal-history inputs, so it cannot accidentally certify the superseded hash-only seam. Evidence references the 41s child bundle by one validated digest and adds only this bead's stage/order/allocation/publication rows.

This P0 bug retains every fln-amv.3/.4 baseline feature, imports the final collision-safe history plan from 41s exactly once, and owns only pipeline order, diagnostics, raw suffix-block law, refusal work/allocation, mutation kills, and authoritative child evidence. Dependencies and the sole fln-amv.11 consumer are exact; unit/model/1-8-32/real-environment/logging/validator obligations are explicit. No further sweep-1 edit required.

P0 remains correct because this is a named mutation-proof blocker for atomic global integration, regardless of generic priority heuristics. No feature, test, E2E, logging, or validator obligation was removed.

title: fln-env: make extension history identity and checkpoint portability exact under collisions
description:
Closed fln-amv.4/.5/.7 provide persistent journals and executable checkpoints, but their cross-bead identity contract is ambiguous under equal digests, independently reconstructed histories, unrelated Environment edits, and retained shared-base memory. Correct the contract additively before provenance lifecycle, attributes, or global merge freeze it.

Cached digests are rejection accelerators only. A mismatch may reject early; equality never establishes ancestry, checkpoint-base equality, or idempotence without shared structural identity or deterministic exact comparison. A suffix checkpoint binds the target extension name and descriptor, exact canonical ExtensionState base, journal length, cumulative payload facts, and suffix. Base root means the target ExtensionState root, not the encompassing Environment logical or module-provenance root. Unrelated Environment edits do not invalidate it. Content-identical independently rebuilt or sibling bases succeed; equal-length or equal-digest unequal histories fail.

Specify the persistence boundary. The W2 checkpoint schema version governs an in-memory semantic structure unless a durable codec is explicitly assigned to its owning codec/CAS bead. Suffix mode may retain a shared base handle for exact proof and must report that retained memory; FullJournal is self-contained. No pointer address enters canonical identity or wire evidence. Any future durable restart encoding remains a separate explicit contract rather than an implied claim here.

## Steps to Reproduce

Construct (a) independently rebuilt content-identical extension bases, (b) equal-length divergent bases, (c) injected equal-digest unequal histories, and (d) an unrelated Environment edit with unchanged target ExtensionState. The pre-correction cross-bead wording permits allocation-lineage rejection, hash-only acceptance, or whole-Environment checkpoint binding, and does not price retained shared-base memory or cancellation. Any such result reproduces this contract bug.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every criterion in this issue's acceptance_criteria field is mandatory: exact collision-disambiguated history proofs; same-process semantic portability; FullJournal target/descriptor/epoch/value binding; logical attributed footprint; nonpublishing plan handoff; fixed limits/cancellation; productive 1-8-32 models; named mutants; real Environment restoration/refusal/recovery; and strict evidence.

Independently reconstructed histories use deterministic chunk-wise exact comparison under explicit entry, payload-byte, work, witness-byte, name-depth, and cancellation limits. Cross-branch restore fails only when the target ExtensionState differs; branch allocation identity is not semantic. FullJournal has no extension-journal ancestry requirement, while any combined-state restore envelope may separately bind explicit logical/module-provenance target roots. Resource facts distinguish newly allocated checkpoint bytes, carried suffix/full bytes, shared nodes, retained shared-base bytes, comparison work, and checked counter arithmetic. Unit/property/model/mutation suites cover shared and independently rebuilt equal bases, unrelated Environment changes, divergent equal-length bases, digest-colliding unequal bases, empty/nested/repeated suffix and FullJournal restores, exact/one-over budgets, every fixed cancellation point, and recovery. Reduced-width models reach height/index/counter overflow boundaries. Named mutants use hash-only prefix acceptance, copy the whole history, reverse iteration, break branch isolation, accept unchecked overflow, bind the whole Environment root, reject an equal reconstructed base, or accept a divergent equal-length base; each is killed discriminatingly.

The real Environment lane includes portable restore, collision refusal, resource/cancellation refusal, exact restoration, and clean recovery. Versioned strict records contain identity-proof kind; descriptor/checkpoint/base IDs; digest/collision and exact-comparison facts; retained/shared/new bytes and nodes; limits/usage; extension/logical/module-provenance roots with explicit scope; publication/authority/cacheability; expected/actual outcome; first divergence; artifacts; cleanup; and final state. Validators reject missing/extra/duplicate/stale/swapped/wrong-collision/merged-stream or incomplete evidence, and retained bundles validate independently. FullJournal remains self-contained but still binds target extension name, descriptor and schema version, epoch/profile, exact ordered EntryIds, actual entry values, payload identities, and cumulative facts. It has no ancestor-base prerequisite; that exemption never permits a descriptor, epoch, target, or value mismatch. Suffix mode binds its exact ExtensionState base.

Portable means same-process semantic portability across independently reconstructed content-identical branches and snapshots. It does not claim durable bytes, cross-process restart, or address-stable identity. Durable encoding/restart remains an explicit downstream Ledger/CAS/codec handoff. Reported retained/shared/new bytes are versioned logical attributed footprint and canonical payload/node accounting, not allocator ownership, RSS, or a claim that shared bytes belong exclusively to one checkpoint.

title: fln-env: align module graph registration limits and cancellation with FL-INV-07
description:
Closed fln-amv.9.1 established the lossless persistent module-record DAG, but its API and closure evidence classify module/row/name-depth/payload operational exhaustion as ModuleGraphError refusal and define no cancellation contract. The fln-amv.9 epic requires resource exhaustion and cancellation to remain typed Inconclusive under FL-INV-07. Preserve every successful graph, conflict, invalid-input, cycle, order, duplicate, and sharing behavior while correcting this outcome seam before atomic module application consumes it.

Separate normative Invalid input (malformed epoch/name/request), semantic Conflict (nonidentical re-registration, self/cycle policy where defined), Complete insertion/idempotence, operational Inconclusive (configured budget, checked-arithmetic limit, cancellation, fallible reservation failure), and InternalFault (validated-state invariant failure). A semantic conflict is authoritative only after its required bounded lookup/comparison completes; unfinished work is Inconclusive, never rejection. Registration remains an immutable transition and never exposes a partial graph.

## Steps to Reproduce

Exercise the closed fln-amv.9.1 registration API at exact/one-over module, row, name-depth, payload, work, arithmetic, reservation, and cancellation boundaries. The baseline seam reports operational exhaustion as ModuleGraphError-style refusal and has no frozen cancellation outcome, so an unfinished lookup can be mistaken for invalid/conflicting input. Any partial graph exposure or cacheable rejection reproduces the defect.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every criterion in this issue's acceptance_criteria field is mandatory: the Complete/Invalid/Conflict/Inconclusive/InternalFault lattice, deterministic precedence and usage, one nonpublishing ModuleGraphAdmissionPlan, all eight flags and duplicate rows, single-charge .13 handoff, nonpublication/retry, independent models and mutants, productive 1-8-32 coverage, and strict semantic evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
Freeze versioned logical usage units and deterministic observation points for modules, direct rows, name components/depth, payload bytes, graph/index/PMap work, fresh/copied nodes, witness/diagnostic bytes, and reserved capacity. Complete reports exact totals. Refusal reports exact work through the deterministic checkpoint plus the triggering dimension and a witnessed lower bound on unobserved required work; it never scans an unobserved suffix solely to claim an exact total. A total precedence table covers malformed input, existing-record conflict/idempotence, checked overflow, cancellation, and simultaneous resource breaches; pairwise tie tests prove identical outcome, checkpoint, usage, authority, and cacheability across schedules.

Consume the bounded collision/PMap contract from fln-amv.13 exactly once. Typed allocation claims are limited to configured budgets, checked arithmetic, and demonstrably fallible reservations; no claim promises recovery from arbitrary global allocator abort. Every input-sized fallible reservation occurs before publication. Final root/cancellation revalidation precedes a single immutable publication. On any refusal the base graph, module rows, roots, sharing, authority, and cache state remain identical; an adequate-budget retry yields the same canonical graph as the unlimited mathematical model.

Unit/property/model tests cover every record field and graph operation, zero/one-under/exact/one-over/large boundaries for every dimension, cancellation at every fixed point, duplicate/conflict versus budget ties, counter overflow via reduced-width models, sparse/dense/all-collision graphs, productive 1/8/32 schedules, nonpublication, and clean retry. Named mutants map resource/cancellation to Invalid or Conflict, omit a checkpoint, check late, publish a partial row/index, charge the wrong resource/actual, saturate overflow, cache Inconclusive, or drift canonical identity; every kill is discriminating. Detailed schema-versioned local evidence records request/base/result IDs and roots, limits/usage/checkpoint, expected/actual outcome, authority/publication/cacheability, sharing/work, mutation/first divergence, recovery, and final state.

This bead closes on exhaustive local proof plus a stable handoff. fln-amv.9.4 owns the later real decoded-fixture positive/resource/cancellation/nonpublication/recovery matrix before the outer fln-amv.9 epic closes; this bead neither duplicates that lane nor waits circularly on it. All fln-env/hash/domain tests, focused model/mutation suites, fmt, check, warnings-denied clippy, UBS, scripts/check.sh, and predecessor gates pass. No refusal/rejection collapse, unbounded compatibility wrapper, mock-only proof, arbitrary-OOM recovery claim, partial insertion, or deferred cancellation behavior satisfies closure.

Preparation returns a versioned nonpublishing ModuleGraphAdmissionPlan bound to schema, request digest, base graph root/revision, exact module/direct-row inputs, expected result root, reservations, deterministic checkpoints, and phase-local usage. Final consumption revalidates base root, plan integrity, reservations, and cancellation immediately before one immutable graph publication. Atomic module application consumes this plan and its collision/PMap facts once; it must not redo lookup, canonicalization, reservation, or accounting. A plan is non-authoritative until consumed and cannot enter an accepted cache.

Boundary refactoring preserves all eight import-flag triples, exact duplicate rows, and repeated rows for the same module with differing flags, ordinals, or evidence. Unit/model/mutation tables cross these cases with idempotence/conflict, zero/exact/one-over budgets, and every cancellation point. Decoder work and bytes already accounted by fln-20n.1 are referenced by digest and charged only by their owner, not recharged as graph work.

Strict semantic evidence records plan/base/result/request identities, exact ordered rows and flags, phase/cumulative usage, reservation/revalidation, outcome precedence, authority/cacheability/publication, and recovery. Timing, host, and scheduler telemetry is absent from semantic comparison or emitted in a separate bounded non-authoritative stream referenced by digest. Add mutants that reuse a plan on another base, skip final cancellation, publish a plan directly, double-charge decoder/PMap work, drop a duplicate, or collapse any flag triple.

title: fln-env: extract and freeze the pinned attribute-state census
description:
Create the mechanical, pin-anchored contract for every observable attribute registration, assignment-state shape, and data query consumed by fln-env. Extraction is confined to the checked-in Tribunal fixture/census path under the Oracle-Only Law; no Reference implementation executes in a FrankenLean product. The generated inventory is the single row-ID namespace shared by W2 data state, module provenance, real attribute fixtures, and W6 executable handlers.

Each canonical row records stable row ID; exact SUITE.lock epoch; module and source/contract anchor; registration identity; extension/state kind; target constraints; payload shape and canonical identity; priority and tie ordering; scope, persistence, import, removal, replacement, and duplicate/conflict rules; observable query surfaces; whether observation is data-only or RequiresHandler; logical-root and ModuleProvenanceRootV1 participation; epoch migration behavior; opaque fallback; equivalence/claim class; and evidence grade. Missing, duplicate, stale, contradictory, or unclassified rows fail generation rather than being guessed.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every criterion in this issue's acceptance_criteria field is mandatory: exact-pin mechanical generation; complete known-row inventory plus parameterized OpaqueFallback; stable one-to-one W2/W6 row IDs; every field/order/root/handler classification; bounded and cancellable atomic generation; productive 1-8-32 determinism; named mutants; offline fixture-generation failure/restoration/recovery; separated semantic/telemetry records; and strict manifest validation.
acceptance_criteria:
The census is generated mechanically from the exact pin and checked contracts with a versioned canonical schema, deterministic row ordering, stable IDs, complete source provenance, and a manifest binding generator/toolchain/input/output hashes. Byte-identical regeneration is independent of locale, timezone, absolute path, traversal order, and scheduler; pin or contract drift fails before publication. Unknown/custom attributes instantiate the versioned parameterized OpaqueFallback row shape rather than disappearing or requiring an impossible finite enumeration.

Unit/property/model/mutation tests cover every field/tag and row class, duplicate/missing/conflicting rows, malformed and truncated extraction inputs, zero/exact/one-over limits for rows/names/depth/bytes/work/output, cancellation at every fixed observation point, checked arithmetic, atomic generation, and clean retry. Individually discriminating mutants omit a row or field, alter priority/tie/scope/query/handler/root/provenance classification, accept a stale pin, duplicate an ID, or reorder observable rows. Complete outputs have exact usage; Inconclusive reports deterministic consumed facts/lower bounds and never publishes a partial authoritative census.

A fixture-generation E2E script runs the real checked-in extraction path offline, includes positive, pin-drift/malformed/resource/cancellation/mutant, byte-restoration, and clean-recovery scenarios, and emits concise human logs plus strict schema-versioned NDJSON. Records include run/scenario/step and bead/claim/invariant IDs; pin/generator/contract/input/output hashes; row counts/classes; limits/usage; expected/actual classification; authority/publication/cacheability; mutation/first divergence; manifest-relative bounded artifacts; cleanup; and final state. Validation rejects missing/extra/duplicate/stale/swapped/path-dependent/wrong-mutant/surviving-mutant evidence and the retained bundle validates read-only offline. Contract generation checks, focused fixtures, fmt/check/test, warnings-denied clippy, UBS, scripts/check.sh, and predecessor gates pass. No hand transcription, source-regex substitute where environment extraction is normative, hosted handler execution, unclassified row, partial output, or mock-only contract satisfies closure.

The finite census is total for known rows at the pinned epoch and also defines one versioned parameterized OpaqueFallback class for future or custom attribute names/payloads. It does not pretend to enumerate an unbounded future namespace. The fallback preserves exact unknown name, payload, target, scope/import position, and provenance facts, returns data-only or RequiresHandler honestly from the applicable rule, and never invents a known-row ID.

Row IDs reconcile one-to-one with the W6 franken_lean-epx handler census/dispatch namespace: every known executable row has exactly one shared stable ID and handler classification; missing, duplicate, stale, or differently classified mappings fail generation. W6 consumes this generated namespace rather than creating an alias table. This is a contract handoff, not permission to execute handlers in W2.

Productive fixed-input 1/8/32 extraction and canonical reduction runs do actual partitioned work with recorded schedules and byte-identical canonical census/manifest output; a relabeled sequential loop fails the gate. Semantic NDJSON excludes host, timestamp, duration, scheduler, and allocator facts. Optional timing/host telemetry is a separate bounded non-authoritative stream linked by digest. Add mutants that enumerate only known names without fallback, misclassify fallback authority, allocate a new row ID for an unknown name, drift a W6 mapping, fake thread productivity, or merge telemetry into semantic evidence.

title: fln-env: implement canonical attribute registry, assignment state, and data queries
description:
Implement the immutable native W2 data substrate generated by the attribute census. Definitions and assignments preserve exact registration and target identity, payload bytes, priority and tie order, scope, persistence, import replay, removal/replacement, and duplicate/conflict behavior. Unknown/custom payloads remain byte-exact with an opaque grade. Attribute state reuses canonical extension and module identities and never creates a second import truth.

The execution boundary is explicit. W2 serves lossless storage, deterministic typed data queries, and generated dispatch metadata. A census row whose observable query invokes Lean code returns typed RequiresHandler with the exact row ID and honest provisional grade until W6 discharges it; retaining bytes is not executable compatibility. Root participation follows the generated per-field table: semantic state changes affect logical_root only where declared, while ownership/topology/evidence affects ModuleProvenanceRootV1, never by hidden coupling.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every criterion in this issue's acceptance_criteria field is mandatory: all census definitions/assignments/data queries and observable ordering; byte-exact opaque state and typed RequiresHandler; one import/root truth; collision-safe .13 PMap use; nonpublishing AttributeStatePlan; exact collision comparison; immutable snapshots and bounded updates; outcome/resource/cancellation laws; independent per-row models, productive 1-8-32 schedules, named mutants, recovery, strict evidence, and stable combined-state handoff.
acceptance_criteria:
Persistent definitions, assignments, indexes, and typed queries provide O(1) snapshots, bounded path-copy updates, exact branch isolation, stable conflict diagnostics, and typed Complete/Invalid/Conflict/Inconclusive/InternalFault outcomes. Limits cover rows, target/name depth, payload bytes, indexes, work, output/witness bytes, and fallible reservations; cancellation points and outcome precedence are frozen. Refusal publishes nothing and adequate-budget retry reproduces the independent canonical model.

For one fixed ordered input closure, construction-map insertion permutations and productive 1/8/32 scheduler partitions yield byte-identical state, diagnostics, roots, and evidence. Reordering assignment or import rows that the pin makes observable is a different input and must reproduce the corresponding distinct Reference result. Only census-declared commutative or independent reorderings are metamorphic invariants.

Unit/property/model/mutation suites exercise every census row and query class; registration, assignment, duplicate/conflict, removal/replacement, priority/tie, scope/persistence/import behavior; valid/missing targets; opaque rows; root/provenance participation; snapshot sharing; every exact resource/cancellation boundary; nonpublication; and recovery. Named mutants drop payload/priority/scope/import order/root participation/RequiresHandler classification, normalize observable order, publish partial indexes, cache Inconclusive, or decode opaque data; each is killed. Schema-versioned component evidence records row/attribute/target IDs, ordered inputs/results, handler class, roots and transition reason, limits/usage, sharing/work, expected/actual outcome, authority/publication/cacheability, mutation/first divergence, recovery, and final state.

The real no-mock decoded attribute fixture matrix is owned by the downstream attribute E2E child and must block fln-amv.8 epic closure, not this local child. This child closes on exhaustive local proof plus a stable row-ID/state/query handoff. All fln-env/hash/domain tests, generated-census drift checks, fmt/check/test, warnings-denied clippy, UBS, scripts/check.sh, and predecessor gates pass. No disconnected registry, hidden import truth, lossy opaque data, implicit handler execution, mock-only model, or deferred data behavior satisfies closure.

Persistent indexes consume the collision-safe PMap contract from fln-amv.13. Every operation returns a nonpublishing phase-local AttributeStatePlan bound to census/schema, exact base state/root, ordered request, expected deltas/root, reservations, and usage. Combined-state integration consumes that plan and its PMap/collision facts once; it does not rewalk state or double-charge canonical bytes, lookup, path-copy, fresh nodes, or reservations.

Where a digest accelerates opaque or understood payload equality, exact canonical value comparison remains decisive. Bounded injected equal-digest unequal-payload cases must produce Conflict after exact comparison; exhaustion before comparison completes is Inconclusive; an unequal value already stored under an allegedly validated identical identity is InternalFault. No production-hash collision or injectivity proof is claimed from the test seam.

Models and mutants cover sparse/dense/all-collision indexes, shifted branch shapes, every zero/exact/one-over PMap and exact-comparison limit, plan substitution/staleness, final cancellation, plan nonpublication, and clean consumption/retry. Add mutants that accept digest equality, omit exact opaque bytes, reuse a plan on another root, publish a phase plan, skip final cancellation, or double-charge PMap work. Versioned semantic evidence records plan/root/census/request identities, exact value-comparison class, collision claim type, phase/cumulative usage, reservations, authority/cacheability/publication, sharing, mutation, recovery, and final state; timing telemetry is separate.

title: fln-env: integrate attribute state with module provenance, checkpoints, and merge plans
description:
Integrate canonical attribute state into the single immutable committed Environment/module-provenance state. Exact decoded module replay owns attribute definitions and assignments through the same module contribution transaction, forward/reverse ownership indexes, completeness/evidence grades, invalidation rules, paired checkpoints, and provenance merge plans used by declarations and extensions. No parallel attribute import, root, or ownership truth is permitted.

Combined state distinguishes data-only rows from RequiresHandler rows without executing Lean handlers in fln-env. Opaque data remains byte-exact and conservatively grades invalidation. Attribute checkpoint and merge behavior uses the collision-safe exact extension-history identity contract. Semantic changes update logical_root exactly through the canonical Environment builder; module ownership/evidence updates ModuleProvenanceRootV1; operational facts affect neither.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every criterion in this issue's acceptance_criteria field is mandatory: one combined Environment/module/attribute/provenance truth; prepared module plus attribute plan composition; closure-wide private staging with no visible prefix; single-charge phase/cumulative accounting; final revalidation and one no-failure publication; exact checkpoints/invalidation/merge plans; authority and collision laws; independent closure models; productive 1-8-32 schedules; named cross-plane mutants; recovery; and strict evidence handoff to real fixtures.

One preflighted immutable transaction validates census row/epoch, target, actual payload bytes, module artifact and contribution identity, priority/scope/order, base logical and ModuleProvenanceRootV1 roots, completeness, both ownership indexes, checkpoint identity, resource envelope, and cancellation before publishing all attribute/Environment/provenance planes together. Missing/partial input remains non-authoritative; opaque-but-byte-complete input remains explicit and conservative. Conflict, Inconclusive, or InternalFault leaves every value/root/index/payload Arc and sharing relation unchanged; clean retry succeeds.

Paired checkpoints preserve actual assignment/contributor identities, ranges, payload Arcs, completeness, and both index directions. Three-way attribute merge returns a versioned bounded child plan consumed by fln-amv.11, with complete canonical conflicts, root/schema/epoch preconditions, exact usage, reservations, and no publication authority. Observable assignment/import order follows the census; only declared commutative changes reorder freely.

Independent combined-state models, exhaustive tables, generated fork/checkpoint/restore/merge/invalidation histories, productive 1/8/32 schedules, sharing/allocation counters, exact resource/cancellation boundaries, nonpublication, and recovery are mandatory. Named mutants drop an assignment, priority, scope, payload, contributor/reverse row, checkpoint binding, completeness grade, root transition, conflict, or semantic plane; publish early; accept a stale plan; or cache Inconclusive. Each mutant has a discriminating kill and versioned component evidence with census/module/attribute/target/plan IDs, ordered facts, roots/reasons, ownership/ranges/completeness/handler class, limits/usage, sharing/reservations, expected/actual outcome, authority/publication/cacheability, first divergence, cleanup, and final state.

The downstream attribute E2E child owns real pinned decoded fixtures and authoritative retained bundles. This child closes on exhaustive local integration proof plus a stable adapter/plan handoff. All fln-env/hash/domain tests, census and module-provenance gates, fmt/check/test, warnings-denied clippy, UBS, scripts/check.sh, and predecessor gates pass. No second truth, handler execution in W2, hidden root effect, digest-only payload equality, partial publication, or deferred combined-state behavior satisfies closure.

Composition uses the nonpublishing prepared module plan from franken_lean-module-provenance-atomic-apply-as7 and one bounded AttributeStatePlan from fln-attribute-native-state-m7l. A versioned PreparedCombinedModulePlan binds both to the same base roots, module/artifact/manifest/request, ordered payloads, expected deltas/roots, reservations, authority axes, and exact child usage. It sums each graph, declaration, extension, provenance, attribute, PMap, and index fact once, never rewalks or recharges a child truth, then revalidates and publishes all planes once.

A versioned StagedClosurePlan chains PreparedCombinedModulePlans in exact fln-amv.9.2 discovery/replay order. Later staged modules may read prior staged contributions inside the private candidate, but no Environment head, accepted cache, receipt, watcher, or query observes a prefix. All module and attribute planes become authoritative together after final closure-wide root/cancellation/reservation revalidation. After that point publication performs no input-sized allocation and has no failure/cancellation point. Failure at module N discards modules 1 through N-minus-1 from authoritative visibility. The downstream real fixture runner executes this protocol; it does not invent atomicity in the shell.

Freeze one total composite outcome precedence and phase/cumulative accounting schema. Validated-state invariant faults outrank Inconclusive/cancellation; unfinished semantic decisions remain Inconclusive; complete canonical conflicts precede publication. Complete reports exact phase and cumulative logical usage. Refusal reports deterministic checkpoints and witnessed lower bounds without scanning unseen work. Missing/partial products may exist only as quarantined authority=false, cacheable=false diagnostic candidates and never replace the authoritative head. Byte-complete opaque products may be exact-replay authoritative but force conservative semantic queries.

Equal-digest unequal-value candidates at module, declaration, extension, or attribute boundaries are decided by bounded exact comparison and yield typed Conflict; exhaustion before comparison is Inconclusive; inconsistency already inside validated state is InternalFault. Add independent closure models and mutants that publish a staged prefix, let later staged reads escape, compose different bases, skip a child plan or final revalidation, double-charge a child, fail after publication, promote partial authority, treat a collision as equal, alias logical/provenance roots, or publish attribute and module planes separately. Strict evidence records closure/combined/child plan IDs, batch position and ordered dependencies, root pairs, reservations, phase/cumulative usage, authority axes, expected/actual outcome, publication visibility, mutation/first divergence, recovery, and final state.

title: fln-env E2E: validate pinned attribute fixtures through the real module-import path
description:
Close the attribute epic through the one real decoded module-import transaction path. Reuse the generated census row IDs, fln-20n.1 fixture/oracle manifest by digest, fln-amv.9.4 decoder-to-transaction adapter, canonical committed state, and authoritative environment evidence mechanism. Do not create a second attribute-only decoder, import graph, fixture truth, or publication path.

Real pinned fixtures must cover every census state/query shape and observable ordering class. If the existing corpus lacks a duplicate, flag, priority/tie, scope, removal/replacement, opaque, or handler-class case, the Tribunal fixture generator creates a Reference-produced artifact at the pin; an absent case is a blocking evidence gap, not a skip. Reference execution stays inside fixture generation/oracle comparison.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every criterion in this issue's acceptance_criteria field is mandatory: immutable fln-20n.1 and fln-amv.9.4 baseline references; additive attribute manifests; every census/flag/duplicate/Name/order/opaque/handler class; real closure-wide publish-once and late-failure zero-prefix behavior; bounded-model collision separation; single-charge composite budgets; productive 1-8-32 semantic determinism; governed-byte restoration and recovery; strict closed-schema bundle validation plus validator mutants; and no unclassified skips or mock-only closure.
acceptance_criteria:
The no-mock matrix exercises row-by-row positive application; duplicate/conflict/removal/replacement; priority/tie; scope/persistence and observable import order; opaque data; RequiresHandler queries; missing/malformed/stale-epoch input; resource and cancellation Inconclusive; planted mutants; late-module failure with zero visible prefix; exact governed-byte restoration; and clean retry/publication. Full module closures stage in the exact fln-amv.9.2 discovery/replay order and publish once, so failure at module N exposes none of modules 1 through N-minus-1. Changed artifacts fork the last unaffected snapshot, invalidate, and replay rather than overwrite in place.

For fixed ordered inputs, repeated productive 1/8/32 runs produce byte-identical authoritative semantic NDJSON modulo registered run IDs. Timing and host telemetry live in a separate bounded non-authoritative stream referenced by digest; absolute paths, timestamps, and scheduler facts never enter semantic comparison. Logs contain census row, pin/epoch/profile, fixture/module/transaction/attribute/target IDs, exact ordered inputs/results, handler class and evidence/authority grade, before/after logical and ModuleProvenanceRootV1 roots with transition reasons, ownership/index/range/completeness facts, composite per-phase and cumulative budgets/usage, publication/cacheability, expected/actual outcome, mutation/signature/first divergence, manifest-relative stdout/stderr and artifact references, cleanup, and final state.

A strict additionalProperties=false validator requires exactly one terminal record and rejects missing/extra/duplicate/stale/swapped/merged-stream/path-escape/wrong-mutant/unrelated-failure/surviving-mutant evidence. Bundles stage, validate, then promote atomically; offline read-only revalidation binds manifests and all governed hashes. Named cross-boundary mutants reorder/drop decoder rows, substitute payloads, lose forward/reverse ownership, promote completeness/authority, accept stale roots/profile, alias roots, publish a prefix, or emit a wrong transition reason. All census, fln-env, fln-olean, module-provenance, contract-drift, fmt/check/test, warnings-denied clippy, UBS, scripts/check.sh, authoritative runner, mutation, and parent gates pass. No hand-authored oracle answer, network dependency, hosted handler, mock-only fixture, partial-prefix publication, normalized observable order, unclassified skip, or deferred row satisfies closure.

The closed fln-20n.1 oracle manifest is an immutable baseline imported by exact digest and never edited, relabeled, regenerated in place, or silently expanded. Newly generated attribute fixtures use a separate additive manifest that references the baseline digest and records its own pin, producer, inputs, outputs, row coverage, and governed hashes. The attribute bundle references the validated fln-amv.9.4 module bundle exactly once and adds only attribute-specific semantic records; it does not copy or reinterpret component evidence.

Fixture coverage explicitly includes all eight import-flag triples, exact duplicate rows, same-module repeated rows with differing flags, empty/anonymous/str/num/deep structural Name cases within configured limits, every priority/tie/scope/removal/replacement class, opaque fallback, and RequiresHandler. Corpus absence triggers checked-in Tribunal generation; any case still absent is a blocking evidence gap, never a skip or reduced denominator.

Injected equal-digest unequal-value scenarios run in a separate bounded_model seam and record that claim class. They never masquerade as observed/proven production-hash collisions or as no-mock Reference fixture evidence. Exact canonical inequality and nonpublication are the proven behavior; collision resistance remains separately classified.

Composite accounting charges decoder, module graph 46b, declaration j8h, extension/history, attribute x74, provenance/index, serialization/evidence, and aggregate coordination overhead exactly once. Every phase and cumulative limit, checkpoint, triggering dimension, precedence result, and lower bound is logged. Productive 1/8/32 proof records actual partitioned work and rejects fake thread labels.

Validator mutants substitute baseline/additive manifests, duplicate or omit the fln-amv.9.4 reference, cross-link semantic and telemetry streams, double-count child usage, fake thread productivity, accept a missing flag/duplicate/Name class, or relabel bounded_model as no-mock evidence. Each is killed discriminatingly.
