title: W1 contract extraction: canonical pin/target inventory schema and atomic publication
description:
## Description

Define the single canonical, schema-versioned intermediate consumed by every W1 contract extractor. ContractInventoryV1 separates mechanically observed facts from reviewed policy and joins them bijectively; extraction never invents support policy, and policy never substitutes for a raw fact. Every row binds inventory kind, extractor id/version, exact Reference epoch/commit/tree, authoritative source locator, target and ABI class when applicable, raw evidence hash, canonical row identity, and authority state.

Own the candidate-generation and publication protocol. Generation occurs in a fresh candidate location under fixed locale, timezone, path, and toolchain inputs. Publication is atomic and allowed only after schema validation, row uniqueness, policy bijection, cross-output roots, resource limits, and unchanged pre/post governed-input roots succeed. Cancellation, unavailable extraction authority, source-root change, partial traversal/output, ambiguous target facts, or resource exhaustion produces typed Inconclusive/InternalFault evidence, leaves the previously published generation untouched, and never yields a clean or authoritative inventory.

This task owns the common schema, raw/policy join, deterministic identities, target vocabulary, candidate lifecycle, and publication API. The ABI, OLEAN/ILEAN, and extern/builtin tasks own their domain facts. The terminal integration task owns cross-domain rendering, drift CI, and real handoff verification. No Reference code enters a release/runtime closure.

## Acceptance Criteria

ContractInventoryV1 and its policy join are closed, versioned, deterministic, target-aware, independently validated, and shared by all domain extractors. Candidate publication is failure-atomic and root-bound. Named unit/model/property suites, mutation cases, and a real filesystem candidate/recovery E2E prove complete traversal, deterministic two-run output, invalid/duplicate/stale/missing policy handling, target ambiguity, cancellation/resource refusal, source-root races, interrupted publication, cleanup, and exact restoration. Human logs and strict schema-versioned NDJSON carry run/scenario/step ids, inventory/schema/extractor versions, Reference and SUITE.lock roots, target facts, raw/canonical/policy roots, row totals, unresolved rows, resource facts, publication stage, authority, cleanup, and final published root. No partial, stale, mock-only, cross-target-projected, or unbound inventory can close this task.

Candidate publication is failure-atomic and root-bound. Named unit/model/property suites, mutation cases, and a real filesystem candidate/recovery E2E prove complete traversal, deterministic two-run output, invalid/duplicate/stale/missing policy handling, target ambiguity, cancellation/resource refusal, source-root races, interrupted publication, cleanup, and exact restoration. Human logs and strict schema-versioned NDJSON carry run/scenario/step ids, inventory/schema/extractor versions, Reference and SUITE.lock roots, target facts, raw/canonical/policy roots, row totals, unresolved rows, resource facts, publication stage, authority, cleanup, and final published root. No partial, stale, mock-only, cross-target-projected, or unbound inventory can close this task.

title: W1 contract extraction: target-indexed Lean ABI layout, exports, and ownership census
description:
## Description

Mechanically extract the complete pinned Lean C ABI inventory into the shared canonical ContractInventory schema. Facts include object-header field offsets and widths, alignment, endianness, packing, tag values by category, size-class and scalar-packing rules, every exported lean_* symbol and signature, and per-parameter/result ownership, effect, and lifetime conventions. ABI facts are indexed by certified target/ABI class; host pointer width, alignment, endianness, packing, or tag behavior may never be projected onto another target.

Keep raw observed facts distinct from reviewed policy. Every extracted symbol has exactly one supported status and ownership/effect policy row; the vocabulary preserves NativeSafe, RawPlatform, CompatWrapper, a clearly named native semantic implementation class matching observed Reference behavior, and Unsupported. The prior ReferenceSemanticAdapter wording must never mean runtime invocation, linking, spawning, or fallback to the Reference. Any fact requiring an unapproved external extractor is a load-bearing unknown that blocks authoritative publication.

Generate ABI contract rows and Rust constants from the canonical inventory only; no hand-copied layout value or parallel extractor is permitted. Preserve raw evidence and target provenance so every generated value can be independently traced.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites abi_target_matrix, abi_contract_inventory_model, and abi_policy_bijection cover every certified/supported target class, layout category, export, signature, ownership/effect row, unsupported row, and unknown-target outcome. Generated Rust compiles and two sealed cold runs are byte-identical. Mutations for host-ABI reuse on another target, offset/tag/signature/ownership drift, missing/duplicate/stale policy, unknown target normalization, handwritten generated-only change, unsupported-symbol promotion, and Reference runtime leakage are each detected with exact first-divergence evidence and clean recovery. A no-mock E2E regenerates the ABI inventory from the pinned inputs and exercises generated constants and symbol metadata through real repository validators/compile consumers; full G0-1 olean resurrection remains the downstream G0-1 obligation and consumes this published contract. Strict NDJSON records target/ABI class, pin and suite roots, extractor/schema versions, row/category totals, policy/root joins, unresolved facts, expected/actual values, authority, resources, cleanup, and final published root. Partial or cross-target evidence cannot pass.

title: W1 contract extraction: exact OLEAN and ILEAN format inventory
description:
## Description

Mechanically extract the pinned .olean and .ilean contracts into the shared canonical ContractInventory schema. Preserve header and epoch fields, constructor and field ordering, scalar encodings, relocation tables, compact encodings, sharing/back-reference behavior, ModuleData inventory, extension-entry encoding, validation-level semantics, and Lean.Server.Ilean fields. Every fact binds its authoritative pinned source locator and raw evidence; generated Markdown, constants, fixtures, and validators may only consume the canonical rows.

The extractor must distinguish absent, unsupported, corrupt, epoch-mismatched, and genuinely unknown facts. It must not infer host-specific layout into another target, normalize away format-significant ordering or sharing, use source comments as authority when executable/environment evidence is required, or silently accept a newer unknown field/version. Load-bearing ambiguity blocks publication with typed non-authoritative evidence.

This task owns exact format fact extraction and domain validation. It does not implement the production decoder/writer; those downstream codec beads consume this contract and retain their complete round-trip, corruption, resource, and mixed-producer obligations.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites olean_ilean_inventory_model, compact_encoding_contract, relocation_and_sharing_contract, and artifact_epoch_boundary cover every extracted field/category, ordering and back-reference rule, extension row, validation level, supported epoch, and typed unknown/corrupt case. Two sealed cold runs are byte-identical and every rendered output traces to one canonical row/root. Mutations to header/version, field or constructor order, scalar width, relocation, sharing/back-reference, ModuleData/extension rows, validation-level semantics, .ilean fields, source locator, policy join, and generated-only text are all killed, with a clean recovery run. A real no-mock E2E regenerates from the exact pinned tree, validates representative real pinned artifacts through repository contract consumers, rejects truncated/corrupt/unknown-epoch fixtures within explicit budgets, and proves failed candidates do not replace the published inventory. Strict NDJSON records artifact kind/epoch, pin and suite roots, extractor/schema versions, target facts where relevant, row/category totals, raw/canonical/policy roots, first divergence, resources, publication authority, cleanup, and final state. Production read/write parity remains owned by the downstream codec beads and is not falsely claimed here.

title: W1 contract extraction: pinned extern and builtin environment censuses
description:
## Description

Mechanically extract the complete extern and builtin inventories from a pin-verified Reference environment walk performed only in the constitutional fixture/census role. Preserve qualified names, generated declarations, universe parameters, binders, signatures, attributes, source/module provenance, extern names, and every builtin/native-Mirror partition input. Retain the raw environment snapshot and bind it to the exact Reference epoch/commit/tree and SUITE.lock root.

Observed rows and reviewed policy remain separate and bijective. Extern rows feed the native Golem intrinsic table. Builtin rows receive exactly one reviewed Mirror partition class (toolchain API, library code, or user-facing data), support/effect facts, and a stable row identity. Source regex or source-text heuristics cannot substitute for the required environment walk. The Reference is invoked only by the offline Tribunal/extraction harness; no generated table may link, spawn, call, or fall back to it at runtime or in release artifacts.

Unknown attributes, generated forms, duplicate names, source/environment disagreement, incomplete traversal, cancellation, source-root change, or resource exhaustion block authoritative publication and preserve the prior generation. This task owns census extraction, not native implementation or conformance levels; downstream intrinsic and Mirror beads consume the published rows and earn row-level evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites extern_builtin_census_model, reference_environment_walk_completeness, census_policy_bijection, and oracle_only_census_boundary cover all modules, declaration forms, generated declarations, universes/binders/signatures/attributes, extern mappings, partition classes, and typed incomplete outcomes. Two sealed cold walks are byte-identical and every canonical row traces to raw environment evidence. Mutations for source-regex substitution, omitted module/generated declaration/attribute, name or signature drift, duplicate/missing/stale policy, wrong Mirror partition, extern remap, source/environment disagreement suppression, partial walk promoted to pass, and Reference leakage into a release closure are all killed and followed by clean recovery. A no-mock E2E runs the real pinned environment walk, validates totality and raw/canonical roots, exercises generated intrinsic/façade input tables through real repository validators, and proves a failed candidate cannot replace the published census. Strict NDJSON records oracle kind, Reference/suite roots, extractor/schema versions, module/declaration/extern/partition totals, raw/canonical/policy roots, unresolved rows, process/resource outcome, authority, publication stage, cleanup, and final state. Downstream implementation and Mirror conformance obligations remain intact.

title: W1 contract extraction: canonical render/join, drift CI, and real handoff E2E
description:
## Description

Join the ABI, OLEAN/ILEAN, extern, and builtin domain inventories under one canonical inventory root and render every checked-in output from that root: ABI_CONTRACT.md, OLEAN_CONTRACT.md, Rust constants, façade/census tables, fixture manifests, and drift reports. Cross-output agreement, schema/policy totality, target scope, pin identity, and raw-evidence traceability are validated before atomic publication.

Own the contract drift CI and the real W1 handoff. This task publishes the stable handoff root and schemas consumed by G0-1, G0-3, W2 codecs/ABI, W5 intrinsics, and W6 Mirror work. title: W1: Typed operation outcomes and diagnostic-cause taxonomy core — FL-INV-07
description:
## Description

Define the versioned fln-core operation-outcome algebra separately from diagnostic causes. Every authoritative operation returns exactly one of: Complete with its domain result (including a legitimate rejection only where that domain defines one); Inconclusive with explicit cancellation, resource, unavailable-dependency, or incomplete-authority reason plus budget/usage facts and authority=false; or InternalFault with invariant, causal, and evidence facts. No generic conversion may map Inconclusive or InternalFault to acceptance, rejection, cacheable negative result, normal user error, or successful absence.

Define the stable diagnostic-cause vocabulary required across the product: SyntaxFailure, MacroFailure, ElaborationFailure, kernel rejection cause, ArtifactCorrupt, ArtifactEpochMismatch, AbiViolation, CapabilityDenied, PluginCrashed, BuildFailure, ProtocolFailure, ReplayDivergence, and InternalInvariantViolation, with structured cause chains, source identity/byte spans, related spans, severity, resources, evidence references, bounded details, and explicit truncation. Outcome authority and diagnostic cause are orthogonal: a resource refusal is an Inconclusive outcome carrying a cause, not a rejected theorem or malformed user program.

Own values, pure compatibility rules, and safe versioned serialization only. Unknown versions/variants and malformed/truncated encodings fail typed at the boundary. Authoritative internal matches are exhaustive with no wildcard/catch-all that silently swallows a new variant. Panics remain invariant failures and never become user diagnostics. Renderer and transcript conformance are downstream.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites operation_outcome_authority_model, outcome_cause_orthogonality, diagnostic_schema_versioning, bounded_diagnostic_payloads, and outcome_serialization_no_mock_e2e cover every complete/inconclusive/internal-fault branch, every diagnostic cause, nested cause/evidence/resource fields, old/current/unknown versions, malformed and adversarial payloads, bounded truncation, cache/publication authority, cancellation, and clean retry. title: W1: Faithful and sound diagnostic projection conformance — per epoch and frontend
description:
## Description

Implement and prove per-epoch projections from the shared typed outcome/cause model into every representative user-facing diagnostic surface. A renderer binds epoch, mode, frontend, human versus robot format, output channel, color/path policy, comparison class, exit class, and deterministic ordering policy. CLI, JSON, LSP/server, and embeddable-library projections remain views of the typed causal value; they never become a second stringly error authority.

Faithful projections reproduce pinned Reference diagnostics at each row-declared exact or semantic class. Incidental upstream ordering/text gaps remain explicitly named at their honest evidence level; a normalizer cannot erase a body, cause, severity, span, channel, or ordered diagnostic to pass. Sound mode may add richer Palimpsest bodies under the registered Behavior Note while preserving acceptance/rejection authority, source positions, severity, and stable typed causes; sound wording can never leak into faithful output. Unknown epochs/formats/variants fail typed, and bounded truncation retains its cause/evidence link.

fln-core owns values only. Frontend crates own rendering adapters; fln-conformance owns Reference transcript fixtures, comparison classes, and evidence. Future subsystems retain their adapter obligations through the verification registry rather than being falsely declared complete before they exist.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites diagnostic_projection_golden, diagnostic_ordering_model, frontend_exit_channel_matrix, mode_projection_isolation, and diagnostic_projection_no_mock_e2e cover every current cause/outcome, epoch/mode/frontend/format/channel combination, exact versus semantic comparison, positions/severity/related spans, cause/evidence retention, unknown versions, bounded adversarial details, and truncation. The real C-family transcript E2E runs Reference and FrankenLean projections in separate Tribunal processes, binds exact fixture/comparator/mode roots, exercises corrupt/aged/malformed/resource/cancellation/internal-fault inputs, proves retry/recovery, and retains strict typed/rendered/exit/root/final-state bundles. Mutants include catch-all swallowing, Inconclusive rendered as reject, InternalFault as user error, unknown epoch default, reordered ordered diagnostics, output-channel drift, sound-text leak, body/cause/evidence loss, unmarked truncation, normalizer comparison downgrade, and mock transcript substitution; all must die for the intended reason. Semantic NDJSON carries typed outcome/cause plus canonical expected/actual comparison facts; timestamps, duration, host, PID, and absolute paths remain linked telemetry and cannot perturb semantic roots. All downstream adapters remain registered and gate their owning beads.

title: W1: Benchmark evidence core — host profiles, raw attempts, statistics, and bundle validation
description:
## Description

Build the neutral benchmark-evidence substrate shared by Reference baselines and FrankenLean performance gates. It owns versioned host-profile qualification, workload/sample-plan manifests, immutable raw-attempt records, cache-state vocabulary, statistical calculations, semantic-versus-telemetry separation, bundle rendering/validation, and failure-atomic publication. It does not own target values, PG policy, Reference observations, or a subsystem bench adapter.

A host profile binds CPU SKU/topology and enabled cores/SMT, RAM, storage/filesystem, OS/kernel, power governor, thermal policy and sensors, virtualization/translation state, toolchain/binary hashes, target/profile/features, and clock/counter capabilities. A workload freezes inputs/order, warmup, sample count or stopping rule, invalidation rule, quantile algorithm, confidence interval, outlier policy, variance decision, cache layers, and resource bounds before results are observed. Cache state independently records filesystem/page cache, Reference or candidate artifact cache, build cache, imported-module residency, and warm daemon state.

Every attempt is immutable and typed valid, invalid-host, cancelled, resource-refused, target-failed, infrastructure-fault, or insufficient-sample. Invalid attempts never disappear or enter aggregates; resume cannot duplicate samples. Raw data, statistical summaries, and rendered reports share one content-addressed root chain. Benchmark observations are D7 benchmark evidence, repeatability/variance decisions are statistical, and targets are SLOs; none becomes PROVEN or a correctness result.

Canonical semantic records contain identities, sample plan, raw measurements in declared units, validity decisions, statistical result, and roots. Wall-clock collection metadata, timestamps, host/PID/absolute-path details, and profiling counters that are not comparison inputs remain bounded linked telemetry. Candidate publication occurs only after independent schema/statistics/root validation and unchanged input roots.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites benchmark_host_profile_model, benchmark_attempt_state_machine, cache_state_lattice, statistical_reference_vectors, benchmark_bundle_validator, and benchmark_core_no_mock_e2e cover all host/cache/workload/attempt states, exact quantile/confidence/dispersion algorithms, pre-registration, invalid exclusion, resume/deduplication, cancellation/fault/nonpublication, semantic/telemetry separation, and independent regeneration. Mutations for invalid sample averaging, dropped attempt, duplicate resume sample, post-observation stopping change, cache conflation, host-profile substitution, naked point-estimate gate, benchmark-as-proof promotion, telemetry in semantic root, silent raw edit, stale summary, partial publication, and profiler contamination are killed. The no-mock E2E runs a small real benchmark under a qualified local profile, plants valid/invalid/cancelled/insufficient attempts, recomputes the summary from raw data, detects tampering, restores, retries, and independently validates the final immutable bundle. Strict NDJSON records schema/workload/host/cache/sample/attempt ids, exact units and measurements, validity/reason, statistical method/result, pre/post roots, expected/actual, resources, publication authority, cleanup, and final state. Reference baselines and PG policy remain downstream owners.
acceptance_criteria: Named suites benchmark_host_profile_model, benchmark_attempt_state_machine, cache_state_lattice, statistical_reference_vectors, benchmark_bundle_validator, and benchmark_core_no_mock_e2e cover all host/cache/workload/attempt states, exact quantile/confidence/dispersion algorithms, pre-registration, invalid exclusion, resume/deduplication, cancellation/fault/nonpublication, semantic/telemetry separation, and independent regeneration. Mutations for invalid sample averaging, dropped attempt, duplicate resume sample, post-observation stopping change, cache conflation, host-profile substitution, naked point-estimate gate, benchmark-as-proof promotion, telemetry in semantic root, silent raw edit, stale summary, partial publication, and profiler contamination are killed. The no-mock E2E runs a small real benchmark under a qualified local profile, plants valid/invalid/cancelled/insufficient attempts, recomputes the summary from raw data, detects tampering, restores, retries, and independently validates the final immutable bundle. Strict NDJSON records schema/workload/host/cache/sample/attempt ids, exact units and measurements, validity/reason, statistical method/result, pre/post roots, expected/actual, resources, publication authority, cleanup, and final state. Reference baselines and PG policy remain downstream owners.

title: W1 Reference baseline: pinned 32-core x86-64 Linux lane
description:
## Description

Produce the complete pinned-Reference baseline lane on the designated bare-metal 32-core x86-64 Linux profile using the benchmark evidence core. Freeze exact CPU SKU/topology, enabled cores/SMT, RAM, storage/filesystem, OS/kernel, governor, thermal/isolation policy, Reference/Corpus/toolchain hashes, target/profile/features, and every cache layer. A VM, container with unverifiable host controls, laptop, oversubscribed proxy, or different core topology cannot silently substitute.

Measure the full required matrix: cold and warm full-Corpus build wall; per-file elaboration p50/p95/p99 and raw distribution; explicitly defined kernel recheck of the olean set (not module loading relabeled as checking); representative server worker import/attach and RSS; first-goal latency; interpreter micro-corpus; and bv_decide with the exact CaDiCaL binary identity. CaDiCaL is D4 oracle-side tooling inside the Tribunal baseline only and never a FrankenLean runtime dependency. Any Reference instrumentation binds patch/hash/overhead and is reported separately.

Pre-register workload order, warmups, samples/stopping, invalidation, quantile/confidence/dispersion, outlier and variance rules before measurement. Retain every valid and invalid/interrupted/thermal/cancelled/insufficient attempt immutably. Methodology and summaries regenerate from raw data; future timing values are not expected to be byte-identical.

## Acceptance Criteria

All required workload/cache/thread rows have sufficient valid raw attempts on the exact qualified Linux host and an independently validated immutable bundle. Named suites linux_host_qualification, reference_workload_manifest, reference_kernel_recheck_definition, oracle_tool_identity, baseline_resume_and_recovery, and linux_reference_baseline_no_mock_e2e cover host facts, cache states, workload semantics, pre-registration, invalid-attempt retention, resume/deduplication, instrumentation overhead, cancellation/fault/retry, and bundle regeneration. Mutations for proxy host acceptance, cache conflation, module-load-as-recheck, CaDiCaL identity loss, invalid attempt averaging, raw sample drop, post-observation rule change, duplicate resume, hidden instrumentation, telemetry-root drift, and partial publication are killed. The no-mock E2E executes the real pinned Reference/Corpus workloads on the designated host, preserves bounded stdout/stderr and raw samples, injects/recoverably classifies at least cancellation and host-quality invalidation, recomputes every summary, and independently validates final roots. Strict NDJSON records host/workload/cache/sample/attempt/tool identities, measurements/units, validity, statistical methods/results, resources, roots, cleanup, and final state. A missing or invalid host lane yields a BLOCKED evidence artifact and this task remains open.

title: W1 Reference baseline: cross-profile report and PG target-ratification join
description:
## Description

Join the complete Linux and Apple-silicon pinned-Reference baseline bundles into the W1 report and produce reviewed PG-1 through PG-M target-ratification proposals. The host lanes remain separate statistical populations; the join compares coverage/methodology and presents both, never merges samples or hides a missing profile. Every row binds exact workload, metric/unit, cache/thread/profile, raw and summary roots, host identity, validity/dispersion/confidence, and D7 claim class.

Classify every PG row by comparator kind: Reference ratio, absolute SLO, zero-tolerance correctness join, first-native baseline, or not Reference-observable. Baseline observations are benchmark claims, variance/repeatability decisions are statistical, and target thresholds are SLOs. A non-observable metric remains TARGETED with an owner and future evidence path; it is neither fabricated nor omitted. Correctness joins cannot be ratified by timing data.

Any target amendment is a reviewed decision proposal containing old and proposed target, both host/data roots, methodology/schema, rationale, affected user promise/gate, uncertainty, owner, and approval state. Measurement tooling cannot silently rewrite the plan or gate registry. Both valid host lanes are mandatory; a BLOCKED/missing/invalid lane leaves this task and the parent epic open.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites cross_profile_coverage_join, pg_comparator_classification, target_ratification_state_machine, claim_type_separation, report_regeneration, and baseline_report_no_mock_e2e prove exact row coverage, separate host populations, immutable raw-to-summary-to-report roots, honest non-observable rows, reviewed amendments, and failure-atomic publication. Mutations for dropped workload/host, cross-host averaging, benchmark promoted to PROVEN, SLO inferred as observation, non-observable row fabricated/omitted, correctness inferred from performance, silent target rewrite, stale/mixed host roots, invalid lane accepted, and rendered-only edit are killed. The E2E independently validates both real host bundles, recomputes every table, plants and repairs each join/claim/amendment error, emits the complete report and proposals, and proves an incomplete candidate cannot replace the prior report. Strict NDJSON records PG/workload/metric/comparator/claim ids, host/profile/cache/thread facts, raw/summary/report roots, observed values and uncertainty, old/proposed targets, approval state, limitations, expected/actual join, cleanup, and final state. No single-host completion, proxy, aggregate-only evidence, unreviewed threshold change, or omitted listed metric satisfies closure.

Mutations for dropped workload/host, cross-host averaging, benchmark promoted to PROVEN, SLO inferred as observation, non-observable row fabricated/omitted, correctness inferred from performance, silent target rewrite, stale/mixed host roots, invalid lane accepted, and rendered-only edit are killed. title: W1/W9/W12: Pinned option, CLI/Lake, and LSP wire census contracts
description:
## Description

Own the missing mechanically extracted compatibility contracts for three pinned public surfaces not covered by the ABI/OLEAN/ILEAN/extern/builtin epic: the option registry; lean/leanc/lake CLI and Lake behavior; and LSP plus $/lean wire schemas. This epic is a strict terminal join over three domain extractors and one cross-surface publication/E2E task. It uses the shared canonical raw-fact/reviewed-policy schema and Tribunal authority; no hand-copied flag, default, method, error shape, or lifecycle rule is authoritative.

Every row binds stable id/version, exact Reference epoch/commit/tree and Corpus/toolchain scope, authoritative source or observed-transcript anchor, platform/target where applicable, raw evidence/root, reviewed compatibility/effect/determinism policy, support/evidence state, and downstream consumer identity. Unknown/missing/duplicate/stale rows or source-versus-binary disagreement fail closed. The Reference is used only in the Tribunal extraction/oracle role and is absent from release/runtime closures.

The three inventories preserve: all options, types/defaults/scopes/precedence and semantic/cache/effect classifications; lean/leanc/lake verbs/flags/environment/config precedence/help/version/exit/channel/JSON plus Lake facets and trace/manifests; and LSP initialize/capabilities/methods/notifications/errors/legends/progress/cancellation/document versions plus every $/lean RPC request/response and lifetime. Epoch/platform deltas are explicit rows, never normalized away.

Candidate generation is deterministic and failure-atomic. The terminal task renders schemas/docs/generated tables/fixtures/drift reports from one canonical root, compiles or validates generated consumers, runs real Reference transcript E2E, and atomically publishes only after independent validation. W9/W12 consumers retain implementation and installed-editor/CLI parity obligations.

## Success Criteria

All domain children and the terminal join close under one authoritative pin and compatible schema/platform namespaces. Named inventory/model/property/mutation suites and real no-mock Reference transcript E2E cover every row, default/precedence/lifecycle/exit/error/channel behavior, unknown/unsupported versions, cancellation/resource failure, deterministic regeneration, drift, publication recovery, and independent validation. Mutants omit or alter option defaults/scopes, flags/precedence/exits/help/JSON, Lake facets, LSP methods/capabilities/legends/errors/document versions, $/lean lifetimes, source anchors, policy rows, platform/epoch roots, or publication stages; each fails for the intended reason and recovers. Strict semantic NDJSON carries exact inventory/fixture/expected/actual/root/authority/final-state facts, while host/time/PID/path/performance telemetry is separate. No handwritten census, incomplete transcript slice, mock-only protocol, hidden exclusion, or downstream promise without an immutable contract root satisfies this epic.
acceptance_criteria: All four children close beneath one authoritative pin and compatible schema/platform namespaces. The terminal PublicSurfaceContractV1 root is total, deterministic, independently validated, failure-atomically published, and consumed by downstream owners without transferring implementation claims. Named inventory, model, property, mutation, drift, publication-recovery, generated-consumer, and real no-mock Reference transcript suites pass. Semantic NDJSON and telemetry are separately linked and validators reject any incomplete, handwritten, stale, mixed-epoch, mock-only, telemetry-contaminated, or overclaimed evidence.

title: Pinned option census: names, types, defaults, scopes, precedence, and effects
description:
## Description

Mechanically extract and version the complete pinned option registry consumed by parser, elaborator, kernel/fuel, compiler, runtime, Lake, server, and front doors. title: W8/W12: Conservative epoch-bridge authority and migration handoff
description:
## Description

Own the cache epoch-bridge decision independently from federation transport and W12 migration implementation. Bridges are off by default. A candidate bridge binds old/new canonical contract and census roots, exact allowed deltas, mode/profile/product portability, schema translators, claim/evidence state, promotion cell, local revalidation, rollback generation, and limitations. Unknown or unallowlisted delta, missing source facts, target-sensitive mismatch, stale promotion evidence, or unavailable revalidation refuses reuse.

Emit a typed bridge/census/migration handoff consumed by the epoch ratchet and migration autopilot; do not edit projects or implement migration here.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites epoch_bridge_delta_model, bridge_allowlist_totality, local_revalidation_authority, bridge_promotion_recovery, and epoch_bridge_no_mock_e2e cover compatible and incompatible adjacent epochs, unknown deltas, schema changes, target-sensitive products, cancellation, stale evidence, rollback, and exact W12 handoff. Mutants enable by default, wildcard a delta, skip local check, trust remote signature/tier, reuse cross-mode, auto-promote, or publish partial translation; each is killed. Canonical semantic logs are separately linked from telemetry and exact old/current/new generation roots are asserted.
acceptance_criteria: Named suites epoch_bridge_delta_model, bridge_allowlist_totality, local_revalidation_authority, bridge_promotion_recovery, and epoch_bridge_no_mock_e2e cover compatible and incompatible adjacent epochs, unknown deltas, schema changes, target-sensitive products, cancellation, stale evidence, rollback, and exact W12 handoff. Mutants enable by default, wildcard a delta, skip local check, trust remote signature/tier, reuse cross-mode, auto-promote, or publish partial translation; each is killed. Canonical semantic logs are separately linked from telemetry and exact old/current/new generation roots are asserted.

title: W8: Coherent watch-mode generations, overflow rescan, and shared TUI/robot truth
description:
## Description

Own local watch mode independently from distributed execution. Bind every event batch to a coherent source snapshot and generation; coalesce deterministically, handle create/modify/remove/rename and editor save patterns, and recover watcher overflow or unknown event state by a complete rescan before publication. Never publish results from a stale or mixed generation. Human TUI and versioned robot output project the same authoritative state while decoration and telemetry stay separate.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites watch_generation_model, rename_coalescing_property, watch_overflow_rescan, robot_tui_state_bijection, and watch_real_filesystem_no_mock_e2e cover event storms, overflow, concurrent edits, atomic saves, symlinks/path identity, cancellation, crash, rescan, stale worker result, and restart. Mutants drop overflow, merge generations, publish before rescan, diverge TUI/robot truth, or lose terminal records; all fail. Productive 1/8/32 rebuild work is semantically identical. Strict semantic NDJSON and separate telemetry assert source/build roots, cleanup, and exact final state.

Mutants drop overflow, merge generations, publish before rescan, diverge TUI/robot truth, or lose terminal records; all fail. Productive 1/8/32 rebuild work is semantically identical. Strict semantic NDJSON and separate telemetry assert source/build roots, cleanup, and exact final state.

title: W8: Distributed elaboration — versioned tasks, leases, canonical merge, and local admission
description:
## Description

Own distributed build execution after Ledger-backed cross-module speculation and completed W3/W6 authority. Tasks bind full source/dependency/environment/mode/profile/epoch roots, schema, resource contract, lease and idempotency ids. Workers return typed products plus receipts; coordinator validates product-specific schemas, dependencies, authority, obligation drainage, and canonical CGSE merge before local publication. Crucible trust admits declarations only and cannot automatically admit diagnostics, build metadata, traces, or services.

Worker loss, duplicate/reordered responses, partition, Byzantine or stale product, cancellation, and coordinator crash produce typed nonpublication and exact retry/recovery.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites distributed_task_schema_model, lease_idempotency_dpor, product_admission_matrix, canonical_merge_property, and distributed_separate_host_no_mock_e2e cover real network and separate hosts, loss/partition/duplicates/reordering/corruption, stale roots, cancellation, resource refusal, restart, obligation leakage, and exact local re-verification. Mutants trust declaration receipts for other products, merge by arrival, omit roots, accept stale leases, double-publish, or use same-host-only evidence for release; all fail. Productive 1/8/32 plus multi-host runs preserve canonical semantic roots; telemetry remains separate.
acceptance_criteria: Named suites distributed_task_schema_model, lease_idempotency_dpor, product_admission_matrix, canonical_merge_property, and distributed_separate_host_no_mock_e2e cover real network and separate hosts, loss/partition/duplicates/reordering/corruption, stale roots, cancellation, resource refusal, restart, obligation leakage, and exact local re-verification. Mutants trust declaration receipts for other products, merge by arrival, omit roots, accept stale leases, double-publish, or use same-host-only evidence for release; all fail. Productive 1/8/32 plus multi-host runs preserve canonical semantic roots; telemetry remains separate.

title: W1/W10/W12: Certified platform and target support decision contract
description:
## Description

Define the single versioned platform-support decision consumed by codegen, plugin doors, distribution, epoch ratchet, and G6. Distinguish build host, target triple/features, object/runtime ABI, functional support, execution support, C4 plugin support, certified reproducibility, browser tier, installer availability, and R4 declaration. Resolve D17 Windows explicitly rather than allowing build success or functional CI to imply certification.

Every matrix cell binds exact toolchain/closure, target/profile/mode, owner, required real host, support/evidence state, exclusions, runtime allowlist, artifact class, and promotion criteria. Unknown or missing cells fail closed; one host cannot proxy another.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites platform_cell_schema, target_runtime_allowlist, support_level_lattice, d17_windows_decision, matrix_promotion_model, and platform_real_host_no_mock_e2e cover Linux, macOS, Windows, browser, supported/unsupported targets, cross compilation versus execution, plugins, installers, reproducibility, transitions, cancellation, and stale evidence. Mutants conflate host/target, functional/certified, C4/R4, static/runtime-allowlisted, or browser/native; omit cells; alias host proof; or promote from mock/cross-build only; all fail. Canonical semantic decisions are separate from host/performance telemetry and every promoted cell cites real evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Named suites platform_cell_schema, target_runtime_allowlist, support_level_lattice, d17_windows_decision, matrix_promotion_model, and platform_real_host_no_mock_e2e cover Linux, macOS, Windows, browser, supported/unsupported targets, cross compilation versus execution, plugins, installers, reproducibility, transitions, cancellation, and stale evidence. Mutants conflate host/target, functional/certified, C4/R4, static/runtime-allowlisted, or browser/native; omit cells; alias host proof; or promote from mock/cross-build only; all fail. Canonical semantic decisions are separate from host/performance telemetry and every promoted cell cites real evidence.

title: G6: Late Palimpsest producer-adoption join for W11/W12 edges
description:

Synthetic fixtures prove schema compatibility only and cannot close producer adoption.

Each edge binds producer/version, source and destination node identities, epoch/mode/profile, event generation, completeness grade, receipts, semantic roots, ordering policy, and typed drop/incompleteness facts. G6 consumes this join.

title: W1/W4 naming contract: reserve Quill for Frankensearch and rename FrankenLean parser/macro to Vellum
description:
## Description

Reserve Quill for the Frankensearch lexical engine and rename FrankenLean W4 parser, syntax, source-position, hygiene, macro, GrammarEpoch, and recovery subsystem to Vellum. This is a terminology-only contract migration: fln-parse and fln-syntax crate names, public Lean-compatible surfaces, semantics, dependencies, gates, and every existing feature remain unchanged.

Update the comprehensive plan, README/AGENTS architecture summaries, every Bead title/field, generated contract vocabulary, schema identifiers intended to expose subsystem names, telemetry/evidence labels, dashboards, diagrams, code/module documentation, test and fixture names, and user-facing prose. Do not add an internal Quill compatibility alias: there are no users, and aliases would perpetuate ambiguity. Historical receipts/comments may retain their immutable text but receive a correction note and must not define current vocabulary.

Add a suite-wide subsystem-name registry with project/repository owner, canonical spelling, scope, reserved aliases, and collision validation so future FrankenSuite plans cannot silently reuse a load-bearing subsystem name.

## Acceptance Criteria

Named suites subsystem_name_registry, reserved_name_collision_model, vellum_surface_inventory, generated_name_drift_guard, and vellum_naming_no_mock_e2e scan all governed source/docs/contracts/generated schemas/Beads/current evidence vocabulary and prove that current FrankenLean parser/macro references use Vellum while Quill resolves only to the explicitly cited Frankensearch lexical engine or immutable historical records. Mutants leave one title, plan section, schema label, dashboard row, generated artifact, lowercase variant, or new conflicting registry entry; each fails for the intended reason. A clean regeneration/check run is deterministic and produces canonical semantic NDJSON with file/field/old/new/authority/root/final-state facts, separately linked telemetry, independent validation, and exact recovery from interrupted candidate publication. No feature change, alias shim, code-path duplication, or silent historical rewrite satisfies closure.
acceptance_criteria: Named suites subsystem_name_registry, reserved_name_collision_model, vellum_surface_inventory, generated_name_drift_guard, and vellum_naming_no_mock_e2e scan all governed source/docs/contracts/generated schemas/Beads/current evidence vocabulary and prove that current FrankenLean parser/macro references use Vellum while Quill resolves only to the explicitly cited Frankensearch lexical engine or immutable historical records. Mutants leave one title, plan section, schema label, dashboard row, generated artifact, lowercase variant, or new conflicting registry entry; each fails for the intended reason. A clean regeneration/check run is deterministic and produces canonical semantic NDJSON with file/field/old/new/authority/root/final-state facts, separately linked telemetry, independent validation, and exact recovery from interrupted candidate publication. No feature change, alias shim, code-path duplication, or silent historical rewrite satisfies closure.
close_reason: Landed in commit 7689306. Quill reserved for Frankensearch; FrankenLean W4 subsystem renamed to Vellum across plan/README/AGENTS/closure-allowlist/crate docs; ci/SUBSYSTEM_REGISTRY.txt registry with collision + candidate-publication law; named suites subsystem_name_registry, reserved_name_collision_model, vellum_surface_inventory, generated_name_drift_guard (36 tests, all mutant classes killed for intended typed reasons, byte-deterministic scan); no-mock fln.e2e/2 lane vellum_naming_no_mock_e2e.sh green with sealed validated bundle (run vellum-naming-20260724T044910Z-2999995: 23 supervised steps, seeded stale-doc/registry-conflict/generated-drift/interrupted-candidate mutants killed, byte-verified restores, quarantine recovery, determinism byte-compare, final real recheck; artifacts retained under target/e2e/). Gates: fmt clean, check clean, clippy -D warnings clean, cargo test --locked fully green (89 suites incl. refreshed kernel-contract ownership binding), shellcheck clean, UBS reviewed (remaining findings are test-file panic! assertions, house pattern; token false-positives annotated ubs:ignore). Mutable bead fields migrated (p8a/26y/7xw/a2e/hly); correction notes on 17 beads with immutable Quill history. No alias shim, no feature change.

comments.text: Review sweep 1 — naming authority and feature preservation: confirmed Vellum uniquely names FrankenLean syntax/parser/macro work while Quill remains the Frankensearch lexical engine. The migration changes terminology only; crate identities, source/ABI/wire surfaces, semantics, tests, gates, and every feature remain intact.
